tests.name: Crop Production & Management Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Crop Production and Management CBSE Online Test 01 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Cutting mature crop manually or by a machine is called:
questions.explanation: The process of cutting the mature crop manually or by machine is called harvesting. In harvesting, crops are cut close to the ground.

questions.question: Process of separating grains from chaff is:
questions.explanation: The process of separating grains from chaff is called as winnowing.

questions.question: Cultivation of vegetables, fruits, and flower plants on large scale is known as:
questions.explanation: Cultivation of vegetables, fruits, and flower plants on large scale is known as horticulture. Horticulture is usually done in a small area.

questions.question: Any plant cultivated on large scale at a large stretch of land is called:
questions.explanation: When plants of the same kind are grown and cultivated at one place on large scale, it is called a crop. For example crop of wheat, rice, etc.

questions.question: Process of loosening and turning the soil is called:
questions.explanation: The process of loosing and turning of the soil is called tilling or ploughing. This is done by using a plough, made up of wood or iron.

questions.question: The science of practicing farming:
questions.explanation: The science of practicing farming is called agriculture. Agricultural practices are necessary for providing food to our growing population.

questions.question: Which of the following is a non-cereal crop?
questions.explanation: A cereal crop is any grass cultivated for the edible components of its grain. It includes maize, rice, wheat, barley, sorghum, etc. So, the gram is a non-cereal crop.

questions.question: Which of the following is a plantation crop?
questions.explanation: Plantation crop refers to monoculture crops which are cultivated on an extensive scale over a large. The main plantation crops include coconut, tea, cashew, rubber, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following is a legume?
questions.explanation: Those plants whose roots contain nodules in which nitrogen-fixing bacteria like rhizobium is present are called leguminous plants. It includes pulses like bean, pulses, gram, etc.

questions.question: The manual method of sowing seeds is also known as:
questions.explanation: The manual method of sowing seeds after the preparation of the soil is called broadcasting. It is done by hand. The mechanical method of sowing seeds involves seed drill to ensure the distribution of seeds at a proper distance.

questions.question: The rearing of honey bees on large scale is called:
questions.explanation: The rearing of honey bees on large scale is called apiculture. Apiculture generate extra income to the farmers with little investment and labour.

questions.question: Breeding, rearing, and managing animals for human use is called:
questions.explanation: The process of breeding, rearing, and managing animals for human use is called animal husbandry. Animal husbandry is done at home or farm.

questions.question: The medium of growth for plants is:
questions.explanation: Plants require soil for its growth. Soil provides all nutrients required by plants for their growth and metabolic activities including water and air.

questions.question: The agricultural implement used for removing weeds and for loosening soil is:
questions.explanation: Hoe is a simple tool that is used for removing weeds and loosening the soil. Hoe is made up of iron and wood.

questions.question: Which among the following crop is a tuber crop ?
questions.explanation: Potato is a tuber crop. The edible part of potato is underground stem having nodes in form of eyes on which new buds emerge when placed in soil.

tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Growing different types of crops in pre-planned succession:
questions.explanation: Growing of different types of crops in pre-planned succession is called crop rotation. Crop rotation is done to maintain the fertility of the soil. Leguminous plants are used between two cereal crops which provide nitrogenous nutrients to the plant.

questions.question: The method in which water enters the field through channels made between two rows of crops is:
questions.explanation: Furrow irrigation is a method of irrigation in which water enters the field through channels made between two rows of crops. Lots of water is wasted in this irrigation technique. So, it should be avoided.

questions.question: Cereals provide us mainly:
questions.explanation: The cereals are the main food providing seeds that include rice, wheat, barley, sorghum, and maize. They are the main source of carbohydrates essential to obtain energy. Starch and glucose are two common carbohydrates.

questions.question: Coconut provides us mainly :
questions.explanation: Fat is one of the nutrient that provides us double energy than carbohydrates. Groundnut,coconut,nuts etc provide us fat.

questions.question: Peas are rich in:
questions.explanation: Peas are rich in proteins. Pea is a leguminous plant having root nodules that fix atmospheric nitrogen. All the pulses including pea are the main source of proteins to human beings.

questions.question: Vegetables and fruits provide us:
questions.explanation: Vegetables and fruits are source of vitamins and minerals to human beings. Vitamins and minerals protect us from disease to remain healthy.

questions.question: _____  crops grown during winters:
questions.explanation: The crops grown during winter seasons are called Rabi crops. Their time period is generally from October to March e.g. Wheat, gram mustard, etc.

questions.question: The manure prepared from green plants is:
questions.explanation: The manure prepared from green plants is called green manure. During the preparation of green manure, plants are grown in the field and then ploughed and mixed with soil to increase fertility.

questions.question: Improper use of fertiliser can cause:
questions.explanation: Excessive use of inorganic fertilizers in field leads to soil alkalinity or soil acidity. So, plants can't grow such soil. These fertilizers contain acids that also harm the microbes present in soil.

questions.question: Process of watering crop plants in the field at different intervals is called:
questions.explanation: Irrigation is the process of watering crop plants in the field at different intervals. Irrigation fulfills the requirement of water to plants grown in the field for various physiological processes.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Tilling uproots all unwanted plants.
  2. Tilling loosens the soil and mixes fertilisers.

options.html: Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct
Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct

questions.explanation: The process of loosing and turning of the soil is called tilling. Tilling uproots all unwanted plants and loosens the soil and mixes the organic fertilizers with soil.

questions.question: After tilling, soil is _____ with the help of a wooden planck.
questions.explanation: After tilling, soil is levelled with the help of wooden plank as tilling makes the field uneven. Irrigation is difficult in uneven field.

questions.question: For farmers to decide which crop to be grown in the field depends upon:
questions.explanation: The decision of farmer to which crop to be grown in the field depends upon soil quality, water supply, and weather conditions along with the investment ability of the farmer.

questions.question: Some crops like paddy are first grown in a:
questions.explanation: Some crops like paddy are first grown in a nursery as they are too tender to grow directly in the field. The paddy plant is uprooted and transplanted into a larger filed for further growth.

questions.question: In the drip irrigation method:

options.html: droppers are used for watering the plant
droppers are used by farmers to irrigate the field
water drops are sprinkled on crop
drop by drop water is supplied

questions.explanation: In the drip irrigation method, drop by drop water is supplied on the plants to absorb maximum water. Drip irrigation is effective method of irrigation with less wastage of water.

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Crop Production and Management CBSE Online Test 03 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Waterlogging reduces _____ in the spaces between soil particles.
questions.explanation: The roots of the plant absorb air present between the soil particles. During waterlogging, these spaces are filled with water and may lead to the wilting of plants. In wilting, leaves become pale, and the plant dies with time.

questions.question: Stem of crop is cut into small pieces and used as:
questions.explanation: The stem of the crop plant is cut into small pieces and used as fodder (food for animals). The stem of paddy and wheat are the main fodder for domesticated animals of the farmer.

questions.question: Wilt is a disease caused in crops by:
questions.explanation: Wilt is a disease caused in crops by bacteria. Wilt disease affects the vascular system of plants to cause rapid killing of plants or its parts.

questions.question: Combine is a machine used for:
questions.explanation: A combine is a machine used for threshing to separate grain seeds from the chaff. Combine is in fact a combined harvester and thresher.

questions.question: Mixed fertilizers supply:
questions.explanation: Mixed fertilizers supply more than one essential element for plant growth. NPK fertiliser is the most common mixed fertilizer containing nitrogen, phosphorous, and potassium.

questions.question: The organisum added to waste, in the preperation of vermicompost is:
questions.explanation: Vermicompost is prepared by using redworms to enhance the composting process. The compost is effective manure used in agriculture field to increase nutrients as well as humus in the soil. The redworms make soil porous.

questions.question: The straw and husk of grains left out after threshing is:
questions.explanation: The straw and husk of grains left out after threshing is called chaff. Straw and husk are used as fodder for animals. Husk is separated from the seed by winnowing.

questions.question: The bacteria living in root nodules of leguminous plants is:
questions.explanation: The root nodules of leguminous plants contain Rhizobium bacteria. This bacteria is responsible for fixing atmospheric nitrogen into nitrates and nitrites that can be absorbed by plants. Nitrogen is a source of protein.

questions.question: Which of the following is a rabi crop?
questions.explanation: Crops which are grown during the rainy season are called Kharif crops. Paddy (rice), millets, and cotton are examples of Kharif crops. On the other hand, crops grown during the winter season are called rabi crops. Rabi crops include wheat, gram, and mustard.

questions.question: The process of introducing seeds into the soil is called:
questions.explanation: The process of introducing seeds into the soil is called sowing. Sowing is done by either broadcasting or by using a simple machine called seed drill. It is done after tilling.

questions.question: Which of the following is a bio fertiliser?
questions.explanation: Bio fertilisers are fertilisers obtain by microbes that increase the fertility of soil. Mycorrhiza combine with root of angiosperm plants to obtain nutrients which are essential for plant.

questions.question: Flood water standing in the field causes:
questions.explanation: The flood water standing in the field causes water logging. Water logging may leads to alkalinity of soil, which can be removed using lime or chalk powder.water logging also removes the air present in soil.

questions.question: Weedicides when sprayed in the field will destroy:
questions.explanation: Weeds are unwanted plants that grow along with crop plants. Weeds can be destroyed by using weedicides. Weeds compete with crop plants for nutrients and other essential substances and thus it is harmful to plants.

questions.question: Soil having big lumps is crushed for:
questions.explanation: After tilling or ploughing, the field contains large lumps of soil. These big lumps are crushed into smaller pieces by levelling with wood plank carried by animals or the machine.

questions.question: _____ is a common seedless fruit:
questions.explanation: Banana is a common seedless fruit. Banana is formed pathogenically without fertilization that leads to seed formation.

This is Science Class 8 Crop Production and Management CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
Select the method(s) of irrigation which can be employed in an uneven land.

  1. Moat
  2. Sprinkler
  3. Chain Pump
  4. Drip System
questions.explanation: Traditional methods of irrigation, such as moat and chain pump, are useful only in plains where the terrain aids the flow of water into the fields. However, both sprinkler and drip system of irrigation are very efficient even in an uneven land.

questions.question: A common weed is:
questions.explanation: A number of weeds grow in field along with crop plants. The most common weed is Amaranthus having broad leaves and profusely branched root and stem system. weeds hamper the productivity of plants.

questions.question: Soil can be loosened with the help of a:
questions.explanation: Soil can be loosened with the help of a plough is made up of wood or iron. Plough is carried by a pair of bulls and a man to direct the bull and operate the plough.

questions.question: Paddy crop is grown in rainy season because:

options.html: needs a microbe that grows along with crop in rainy season only
needs frogs for dispersal of seeds and spores
it needs waterlogging which is possible in rains

questions.explanation: Paddy is a Kharif crop sown in the rainy season between June to September because paddy requires more quantity of water. It is the staple food of India.

questions.question: Roots of plants can breathe easily in loose soil because:

options.html: lot of air remains trapped in loose soil
plants growing in loose soil have better exchange through aerial parts
loose soil traps fresh air produced by plants growing in it
microorganisms help to trap air in loose soil only

questions.explanation: The root of plants can breathe easily in loose soil because a lot of air remains trapped in loose soil. Water can also reach to the lower layer to be absorbed by the soil.

questions.question: Water logging can reduce soil fertility by:
questions.explanation: Waterlogging can reduce soil fertility by increasing salts in the soil. The salt present in the water gets accumulated although the water gets evaporated due to sun heat. This alkalanity affects the productivity of crops.

questions.question: Which of the following can never belong to category of plant pests?
questions.explanation: Pests are those organisms that harm the crop plants or seed grains during storage. Pests include insects, birds, rodents, or nematodes but never a leech that leaves in freshwater.

questions.question: Animals that provide us with milk are commonly called:
questions.explanation: The animals like cow, buffalo, and camel are reared to obtain milk. These animals are called milch animals or dairy animals.

questions.question: A tractor will be helpful in:
questions.explanation: A tractor will be helpful in tilling the field faster than manual tilling using a plough. Tractor is also used in carrying harvested crops and seeds from farms to the storehouse. But it is costly and not affordable by poor farmers.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Cross breeding is a method for crop improvement
  2. Self-breeding is called hybridization.

options.html: Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct
Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct

questions.explanation: Cross breeding is a method for crop improvement to obtain desired traits into the new variety. Hybridisation is technique of combining desired traits into single species by artificial cross breeding.

questions.question: In the field during fallow period:
questions.explanation: To maintain the fertility of soil after successive cropping, the field is left unused for a few years. This period is called fallow period.

questions.question: If new plants are grown from stem or root of parent plant then it will be:
questions.explanation: Vegetative propagation is the technique of growing new plants by using plant parts like stem, root, and leaves. It is a faster mode of asexual reproduction and is used to grow plants without seeds.

questions.question: Rainwater collected in check dams:
questions.explanation: Rain water collected in check dams increase ground water level. Check dam is important in the hilly areas where field is uneven and rainwater flows down quickly. Ground water is source of pure water.

questions.question: _____ have natural ability to make soil fertile:
questions.explanation: Beans have a natural ability to make the soil fertile. Bean is a leguminous plant in which root contains nodules having Rhizobium bacteria that convert atmospheric nitrogen into usable nitrogen. This type of plant is a rich source of protein.

questions.question: Nitrogen is essential for plant growth and its usable form for plants is:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen is essential for plant growth and its usable form for plants is nitrates. During nitrogen fixation, atmospheric nitrogen is converted into nitrates which can be absorbed by plants. Leguminous plants like beans help in nitrogen​ fixation.

questions.question: Organic farming includes:
questions.explanation: Organic farming involves growing crops without utilising inorganic fertilizers and pesticides. Organic manure and microbes are used in this method called biological methods.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Standing crops in the field are treated with chemicals called fumigants.
  2. Fumigation kills or repels pests without affecting stored grains.

options.html: Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct
Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct

questions.explanation: Seed grains stored in granary are treated with chemicals called fumigants. The use of this chemicals are called Fumigation. It kills or repels pests without affecting stored grains.

questions.question: Pests are organisms that:
questions.explanation: Pests are organisms that attack crops in the field, attack crops in granaries and damage the field crop. They may be insects, rodents or nematodes, etc.

questions.question: A banana plant is grown by planting vegetative part of it called:
questions.explanation: A banana plant is grown by planting vegetative parts of banana by vegetative propagation. The part of banana plant used for growing new plant is rhizome.

questions.question: Fields will need less frequent watering in:
questions.explanation: In humid weather, less frequent watering is required because in humid condition rate of transpiration is less in comparison to dry condition.

questions.question: Drip irrigation method:
questions.explanation: Drip irrigation method saves water during irrigation. In this system, the waterfalls drop by drop just at the position of the roots, and water is not wasted at all. It is a very useful method of irritation.

questions.question: Excessive use of ground water has:
questions.explanation: Excessive use of groundwater has depleted the water table. Groundwater is pumped out by tube wells using electric motors or diesel engines for construction or for irrigation. The upper limit of the portion of water completely saturated with water is called the water table.

questions.question: Overuse of chemical fertilisers has caused:
questions.explanation: Overuse of chemical fertilizers has caused a rise in soil salinity. These inorganic fertilizers also kill the useful microbes present in the soil.

questions.question: Maximum plant nutrients are to be found in:
questions.explanation: Top layer of soil contains maximum plant nutrients. Top fertile soil is the main source of nutrients for plants. These nutrients are formed by decomposition of dead and decaying plant and animals.

questions.question: Hand weeding method of weed removal is :
questions.explanation: Weeds are unwanted plants that grow along with crop plants. Hand weeding method of weed removal is an efficient but laborious method. The use of weedicides kills the weeds as well as microbes present in the soil. Often poor farmers have to choose this method to remove weeds.

questions.question: Broadcasting leads to:

options.html: equal distribution and proper use of seeds
unequal distribution and wastage of seeds

questions.explanation: Broadcasting is the spreading of seeds in the field manually. Broadcasting leads to unequal distribution and wastage of seeds as it is done by simply throwing the seeds by hand. The seeds don't go deep and eaten by birds and other animals.

questions.question: Seed drill helps into:

options.html: proper but irregular spacing between seeds

questions.explanation: Seed drill helps in proper and regular spacing between the seeds. In modern agriculture, seed drill is used for sowing seeds. It also covers the seeds by soil. It is a good method of sowing.

questions.question: Nowadays seed drills are driven by a:
questions.explanation: Nowadays seed drills are driven by a tractor. Sowing of seeds using seed drill ensures proper distances and depth and covering of seeds by soil. It saves time and labour. But it is costly for poor farmers.

questions.question:
Read the following:

  1. Manure is made of cow dung.
  2. Manure is considered better than fertiliser.

options.html: Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct
Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct

questions.explanation: Manure is made of cow dung and other domestic wastes. Manure is considered better than fertiliser because it does not kill the microbes in soil and provide nutrients for many years. It is made of organic waste so it doesn't pollute crops like fertilizers.

questions.question: Which of the following is nutrient specific?
questions.explanation: Fertilisers are nutrient specific and provide one or a few nutrients to the soil. On the other hand, manure is not nutrient specific. It contains all nutrients as well as humus.

questions.question: Formation of vermicompost manure involves addition of:
questions.explanation: The process of rottening and conversion of kitchen and animal waste into manure by adding redworms is called vermicomposting. Red worms are added to the waste, to enhance composting process.

questions.question: Organic farming will not include:
questions.explanation: Organic farming will includes use of manure, application of bio pesticides, and use of bio fertiliser. It will not include application of chemical pesticides.

questions.question: Blight of potato can be controlled by the use of:
questions.explanation: Blight of potato is caused by fungi, so it can be controlled by use of fungicides that kills the fungi. In this disease leaves of potato plant become blackish and reduce photosynthesis.

questions.question: The freshly harvested grains promote fungal growth because:

options.html: they are in contact with ground

questions.explanation: Freshly harvested grains contain lots of moisture that promote fungal growth. Seeds are dried in sunlight before storing to avoid the fungal growth.

questions.question: Continuous circulation of _____ through granaries keeps stored grains free of pests.
questions.explanation: Continuous circulation of nitrogen through granaries keeps stored grains free of pests. Nitrogen is an inert gas that does not help in the growth of microbes. So, grains remain pests free.

questions.question: The crop field is irrigated before sowing because:

options.html: moisture is necessary for seed germination
different irrigation systems prevent weed growth
irrigation works as bio fertiliser for seed germination
watering increases content of soil air for seed germination

questions.explanation: Crop field is irrigated before sowing because moisture is necessary for seed germination. The moisture present in the soil helps in the germination of seed.

questions.question: Which one of the following condition is not essential to grow maize?
questions.explanation: High temperature

questions.question: Rabi crop can be also named as:
questions.explanation: Rabi crop is grown in the month of October to March, which is also winter season in India. So, the rabi crop is also named as a winter crop. It includes wheat, gram, pea, etc.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Cereals are shrubs.
  2. Cereals are grown for their nutritious seeds.

options.html: Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct
Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct

questions.explanation: Cereals are grasses. Cereals are grown for their nutritious seeds containing mainly carbohydrates. Rice, wheat, and maize are main cereal crops.

questions.question: Loosening of soil helps to:

options.html: trap more free nitrogen for plant growth
settle nutrient at lower layers of soil

questions.explanation: Loosening of soil helps to mix fertilisers more uniformly. Loose soil help in germination of seeds and proper irrigation of the field.

questions.question: Same plant cultivated at a large stretch of land is called:
questions.explanation: When plants of same kind are grown and cultivated at one place on a large scale is called crop. For example: crop of wheat, crop of rice etc.

questions.question: Pests become resistant to poisonous chemicals:

options.html: if pesticides are sprayed in appropriate doses
if pesticides are sprayed at correct time
If pesticides are sprayed in very high doses
if pesticides are sprayed by an aircraft

questions.explanation: Pests become resistant to poisonous chemicals if pesticides are sprayed in very high doses for a longer duration. Pests change and develop resistant properties in course of time.

questions.question: Harvesting of crop may lead to:
questions.explanation: Nowadays harvesting is done with a machine called a harvester which separates seeds from chaff too. During this process, a lot of dust produced and causes​ air pollution.

questions.question: Hybridization is a technique used to develop:

options.html: new crop variety by cross breeding

questions.explanation: Hybridization is a technique used to develop a new crop variety by cross breeding method. In these process, two plants having desired characteristics is cross breed to get new varieties.

questions.question:
Fill in the blanks:
watering of fields : irrigation :: nurseries : _____
questions.explanation: The watering of fields is called irrigation. Nurseries are the small part of the field in which seeds are first grown and later transferred to other fields. The small field part is called seedbeds.

tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Field must be left fallow for some time.
  2. During field fallow only green manure plants should be grown but no crops.

options.html: Statement i) is correct and ii) is wrong
Statement ii) is correct and i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Regular growing of crops in the field leads to depletion of nutrients which can be restored by left the land fallow for some period of time. During fallow periods no crops or plant is grown.

questions.question: The type of crop shown in the following image is :-
questions.explanation: The crop shown in the image is of cotton plant which is grown to obtain cotton fibre. So, it is fibre crop. Cotton is used to form fabrics and also for other purposes.

questions.question: Vegetables may have coating of pesticides hence must be:
questions.explanation: Vegetables may have a coating of pesticides. So, vegetables must be washed properly before use. Pesticides are used to kill the pests that are non-biodegradable and remain on its surface.

questions.question: Chemical pesticides are poisonous hence following precaution should be taken:

options.html: consuming medicine before going to field.
applying antiseptic cream on exposed areas.
spraying with filters on the sprayer.

questions.explanation: During spraying pesticides, nose and mouth should be covered because chemical pesticides are poisonous and may be inhaled during breathing.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Paddy do not need standing water.
  2. Gram needs continuous irrigation hence planted in standing water.

options.html: Statement i) is correct and ii) is wrong
Statement ii) is correct and i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Paddy needs standing water so it is grown in rainy season and Gram needs very less water so it is grown in winter season.

questions.question: Weeds compete with crop plants for:
questions.explanation: Weeds are unwanted plants that compete with crop plants for water, sunlight, and minerals required by plants for proper growth. Weeds reduce the productivity of crop plants.

questions.question: Organic wastes can be decomposed to form ________, which can sustainably enhance the nutrient content of the soil.
questions.explanation: Composting is a process in which organic wastes are decomposed naturally to form a nutrient-rich compost. It is a natural and environment-friendly process, which forms a key feature in organic farming. Organic substances like animal feces, leaves, grass clippings, vegetables, eggshells, etc. can also be composted and used in farms.

questions.question: CAN & NPK are:
questions.explanation: CAN stands for Calcium, Ammonium Nitrate fertiliser and NPK stands for Nitrogen, Phosphorous, and Potassium fertilisers. Both CAN and NPK are mixed fertilisers.

questions.question: The method in which seeds are dropped in localized pits:
questions.explanation: The methods in which seeds are dropped in localized pits are called the dibbling method. This method is used in that case where the supply of seed is limited.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Traditional methods of irrigation use manpower or animal power.
  2. Modern methods of irrigation are based on economic use of water.

options.html: Statement ii) is correct and i) is wrong
Statement i) is correct and ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Traditional methods of irrigation use manpower or animal power to driven water wheels but modern method of irrigation is based on economic use of water using modern technique.

questions.question: The best way for protection against plant disease is:

options.html: putting a scarecrow in the field

questions.explanation: The best way for protection against plant disease is sowing disease resistance seed varieties.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Seed drill sows seeds non uniformly.
  2. Seed drill covers seeds with soil after sowing.

options.html: Statement (ii) is correct and (i) is wrong.
Statement i) is correct and ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Seed drill sows seeds uniformly at equal distance. Seed drill covers the seeds with soil after sowing to avoid loss of seeds by birds.

questions.question: Select the crop sown in june/july and harvest in September/October :
questions.explanation: Pulses are sown in June/July and harvest in September/October. Pulses are the main source of proteins in our diet. They also help in nitrogen fixation.

questions.question: Seeds that float over water should not be used for sowing because:
questions.explanation: Seeds that float over water should not be used for sowing because they are light, hollow, and damaged. These seeds do not germinate properly.

questions.question:
Match the following:

agricultural practice implement used
i) breaking crumbs a) drill
ii) harvesting b) spade
iii) tilling c) khurpa
iv) sowing seeds d) plough
v) digging soil e) soil plank
questions.explanation: Soil plank are used for breaking crumbs. Plough is used for tilling the field. Khurpa is used for digging soil. Seed drill is used for sowing seeds and sickle is used for harvesting the crops.

questions.question: The method which allows selective cultivation of only healthy seedlings is:
questions.explanation: Transplantation is the method that allows selective cultivation of only healthy seedlings. Healthy seedlings are selected in the nursery and planted in the main field.

questions.question: These days ploughs are made of iron instead of wood because:

options.html: iron plough is stronger than wood.

questions.explanation: Plough is used for tilling or ploughing. It loosens and makes it porous. These days ploughs are made of iron instead of wood because iron plough is stronger than wood.

questions.question: Which of the following group is that of horticultural crops?
questions.explanation: Horticulture is the practice of growing fruits and vegetables. Potato, balsam, papaya, onion, and crotons are horticultural crops.

questions.question: Animal those yield milk are also known as:
questions.explanation: Domestic animals those are kept or suitable for milk production are known as milch animals. For example cow, buffalo, camel, and goat.

questions.question: The image shows a:
questions.explanation: The image shown is of spice crop. Spices are used to add taste of food items. They do not contain nutrients at all.

questions.question: Birds can be kept away from field through:
questions.explanation: Birds can be kept away from fields through scarecrows, which is a hay-man or decoy in the shape of a human. It is usually dressed in old clothes and placed in open field to discourage and terrify birds.

questions.question: If a variety of sugarcane exhibits early maturation then it is.:
questions.explanation: If a variety of sugarcane  exhibits early maturation then it is good for production. Early maturation decreases the cost of production and time of the farmer.

questions.question: For a A high yielding variety (HYV) of cereal crop :
questions.explanation: A high yielding variety (HYV) of cereal crop must be dwarf and high yielding that can be grown with less labour and input.

questions.question: Overflooding of fields leading to accumulation of water in field will deplete:
questions.explanation: Over flooding of fields leading to accumulation of water in field decrease the soil air. The soil particles contain air that is absorbed by plants for respiration.

questions.question: The type of crop shown in the following image is :
questions.explanation: Pulses are rich source of protein and also provide nutrients to the soil.

questions.question: Raising crops by providing manures and biological methods refers to :

options.html: both organic farming and sustainable agriculture

questions.explanation: Raising crops by providing manures and biological methods refers to organic farming and sustainable agriculture. No inorganic fertilizer or pesticide is used in organic farming.

questions.question: Which of the following groups includes only rabi crops?
questions.explanation: Rabi crops are grown in the winter season. Pea, mustard, wheat, gram, and linseed are rabi crops. They are grown from October to March. So they are also called winter crops.

questions.question: The part of land of our country where agriculture depends on monsoon is-
questions.explanation: More than 50% of land in India depends on monsoon in our country. Agriculture depends on monsoon in this part of land.

questions.question: Due to easy solubility in water, fertilisers:

options.html: leach out fast causing water pollution

questions.explanation: Due to easy solubility in water, fertilisers are needed in a small quantity. They are easily absorbed by plants and leach out fast causing water pollution. They are also harmful for us.

questions.question: Fodder crop examples are:
questions.explanation: The plants that we use to feed the cattle are called folder plant.  grass, alfalfa,burseem etc are folder plants.

questions.question: Microbiology is the study of:
questions.explanation: The branch of biology in which we study about microbes or microorganisms, their growth, life cycle, and reproduction is called Microbiology.

questions.question: Some plants have nitrogen-fixing bacteria in their root nodules. What are these bacteria called?
questions.explanation: Leguminous plants like pea have nitrogen-fixing bacteria called Rhizobium in their root nodules. Rhizobium helps in converting atmospheric nitrogen into ammonia that can be utilized by the pea plant for its nutrition. Other bacteria like Nitrosomonas and Azotobacter are free-living soil bacteria that also help in nitrogen fixation.

questions.question: Pasteur discovered:
questions.explanation: Budding in yeast is an asexual means of reproduction. Fermentation is the anaerobic respiration in which alcohol is produced. Both these process are discovered by Luis Pasteur.

questions.question: _______ are non cellular infectious particles.
questions.explanation: viruses are non-cellular infectious particles. Viruses are placed on the border line of living and non-living. Viruses consist of genetic material and coating of protein surrounding it.

questions.question: Iwanowski discovered virus from:
questions.explanation: Iwanowski discovered virus from tobacco leaves and named it Tobacco Mosaic Virus (TMV). This virus contains genetic material surrounded by coat of protein.

questions.question: Organism responsible for amoebic dysentery is:
questions.explanation: Amoebic dysentery is caused by protozoa Entamoeba histolytica. This disease is characterised by loose motion with blood stain.

questions.question: The microorganism which helps in fermentation is:
questions.explanation: Fermentation is the process in which yeasts convert sugar into ethanol in absence of oxygen. Fermentation process is used to produce alcoholic beverage.

questions.question: Microorganisms are responsible for:
questions.explanation: Microorganisms like bacteria and fungi are responsible for biodegradation of dead and decaying materials into simple substances.

questions.question: Which two words are synonyms?
questions.explanation: Microorganism and microbe are two words having same meaning, very small organism that cannot be seen with naked eyes. So, microorganism and microbe are synonyms.

questions.question: Germ theory of disease was established by:
questions.explanation: Germ theory of disease was established by Robert Koch. This theory explained that disease are caused by one or more microbes.

questions.question: Scientists who study microorganisms are called?
questions.explanation: The scientist who study microorganisms are called microbiologists. Microbes are studied by culturing into suitable medium and making their slide.

questions.question: Microorganisms live as:
questions.explanation: Microorganisms are found everywhere on earth. They live solitary or in colonies. Most microbes are either pathogenic or beneficial to human beings.

questions.question: Microorganisms are:
questions.explanation: Microorganisms are either autotrophs that prepare their own food or heterotrophs that obtain their food from other organism. Autotrophic microbes includes unicellular algae, while most of bacteria and virus are heterotrophic.

questions.question: Protective covering that encloses microorganisms during unfavourable conditions:
questions.explanation: Cyst is the protective covering that encloses microorganisms during unfavourable conditions. Microbes survive under this cyst to continue perpetuation.

questions.question: Microorganisms can be seen using a _______
questions.explanation: Microorganisms are very tiny and cannot be seen with naked eyes. Microscope is required to study the microbes.

tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Microbes are present:
questions.explanation: Microbes are present everywhere. They are present in air, water, soil, inside the body of living organisms in form or parasite including thermal vent and polar ice caps.

questions.question: Which of the following microorganism lives alone:
questions.explanation: Microorganisms live either solitary or in groups. Amoeba is a protozoan that lives solitary or alone in dirty water having an irregular shaped body.

questions.question: Bacilli bacteria are:
questions.explanation: On the basis of body shape, bacteria having rod shaped body are called Bacillus. They may possess cilia or flagella for locomotion.

questions.question: Anaerobic bacteria will not need:
questions.explanation: Those bacteria that do not require oxygen for respiration are called anaerobic bacteria. Anaerobic bacteria produce either lactic acid or ethanol as product of respiration along with energy.

questions.question: Microorganisms are never found in a:
questions.explanation: Microorganisms are found in everywhere, including hot water springs, ice cold water, and marine water. They survive the unfavourable condition by formation of cyst.

questions.question: Fungi are:
questions.explanation: Fungi are plant like organisms because the cell of fungi contains cell wall. Chlorophyll is absent in the cell of fungi, hence they are heterotrophic organism.

questions.question: Which of the following is acellular?
questions.explanation: Those organisms whose body is not made up of cell are called acellular organisms. Virus do not contain cellular organelles and cell membrane. Virus consist of genetic material and protein coat only.

questions.question: Bacteriophage is a:
questions.explanation: Bacteriophage is a virus that infects and replicates within a bacterium. They are also called as bacteria eater.

questions.question: This bacteria helps in curd formation.
questions.explanation: Curd formation from milk requires lactic acid bacteria called Lactobacillus. These bacteria produce lactic acid bacteria by multiplying in number at appropriate temperature.

questions.question: Which of the following organism is used in cheese ripening?
questions.explanation: Cheese ripening is done by microbes called moulds. Characteristic taste and smell is produced by using different varieties of moulds to produce cheese.

questions.question: Yeast is not used in the preparation of ________
questions.explanation: Yeast is a kind of fungi that is used for the preparation of alcohol, bread and dhokla. Antibiotics are not produced using yeast. Antibiotics are used to prevent the growth of fungi and bacteria.

questions.question: Which is a viral disease?
questions.explanation: Dengue is a viral disease carried to healthy person by Ades mosquito. Malaria is caused by protozoa, plasmodium. Typhoid and tetanus are bacterial disease.

questions.question: Which is a fungal disease in human?
questions.explanation: Athlete’s foot is a fungal disease in human beings. Anthrax and tuberculosis are bacterial disease while amoebiasis is a protozoal disease.

questions.question: TMV causes disease in:
questions.explanation: Tobacco Mosaic Virus (TMV) causes disease in tobacco plant. The symptoms of this disease include yellowing of leaves, curling of leaf.

questions.question: BCG vaccine given to infants protects them from:
questions.explanation: BCG vaccine is given to infants within one month of birth. BCG vaccine protects the infants from tuberculosis, which is a bacterial disease that infects the lung.

tests.instructionHtml:

questions.question: Oldest method of food preservation is:
questions.explanation: Food is preserved before storing in future. Seeds and other food items are preserved by dehydrating in sunlight to reduce the moisture that decreases fungal infection.

questions.question: Which of the following fix atmospheric nitrogen?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen present in atmosphere cannot be used by plants directly; it is first converted into nitrate by nitrogen fixation. Some blue green algae can fix atmospheric nitrogen.

questions.question: Rhizobium can live in the root nodules of:
questions.explanation: Rhizobium bacteria is present in root nodules of leguminous plant to fix the atmospheric nitrogen into nitrate. Soya bean is leguminous plant out of the given options.

questions.question: Pathogens can spread by which medium/s:
questions.explanation: Pathogens are disease causing microbes. Pathogens can be spread from infected person to healthy person by insects, air, water, or food.

questions.question: Entry of a pathogen into the body of a healthy person is called:
questions.explanation: The entry of a pathogen into the body of a healthy person is called infection. Infection may or may not lead to establishment of disease depending upon the immunity of the individual.

questions.question: The group of microbes that help to clean up our environment are called:
questions.explanation: Decomposers are microorganisms that break down the dead and decaying materials into simple form and help to clean up our environment. Bacteria and fungi are main decomposer.

questions.question: Leeuwenhoek observed in a drop of pond water:
questions.explanation: Leeuwenhoek observed large number of living microorganisms in a drop of water when observed under microscope. These organisms are not visible to the naked eyes.

questions.question: Salting of food can inhibit growth of:
questions.explanation: Salting of food can inhibit growth of bacteria. Bacterial cell wall formation does not take place in salty condition and inhibit the growth of bacteria that spoil the food.

questions.question: Nitrogen fixing bacteria are:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen fixing bacteria are either free living or symbiotic. Symbiotic bacteria remain attached with plant body as in case of root nodule and free living bacteria are present in soil.

questions.question: Fish and meat can be preserved by:
questions.explanation: Smoking is the process of flavouring, cooking, or preserving food by exposing it to smoke from burning or smoldering material. Fish and meat can be preserved by smoking.

questions.question: Microbes are tiny organisms visible only under:
questions.explanation: Microbes are tiny organisms visible only under microscope. Microorganisms cannot be seen by naked eyes. They are unicellular or multi-cellular but very small in size.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a microbe:
questions.explanation: HIV, Yeast, and Amoeba are microbes but Yak is a herd animal found in mountain region. HIV, yeast, and amoeba are microscopic in size and can be seen only with microscope.

questions.question: Viruses show characteristics of:
questions.explanation: Viruses show characteristic of both living and non-living. They reproduce inside the host cell but become crystal like materials outside the living body.

questions.question: Which of following is cyanobacteria:
questions.explanation: Blue-green algae are also called as cyanobacteria. They are very primitive organism having ability of nitrogen fixation.

questions.question: Bacterium can never be:
questions.explanation: Those heterotrophic organisms that intake complex food and digest inside the body using enzymes are called holozoic organisms. Bacteria are either parasitic or saprophytic in nature.

questions.question: A pathogenic microorganism:
questions.explanation: The microorganisms that cause disease in healthy persons are called pathogenic microorganism. They enter the host through air, water, food, or direct contact.

questions.question: Microorganisms cannot be seen by naked eyes because:
questions.explanation: Microorganisms cannot be seen by naked eyes because they are very small in size. Their size is measured in nanometre (nm) or picometer(pm).

questions.question: Viruses live only:
questions.explanation: Viruses are living only inside host cell. They do not posses cellular mechanism for replication and use the host cell for replication. Outside host cell they are non-living

questions.question: Microorganisms are found at every place because:
questions.explanation: Microorganisms are found everywhere because they can form a hard covering called cyst to overcome unfavourable condition. In favourable condition cyst breaks open and they germinate to produce bacteria.

questions.question: Within a host cell, virus can:
questions.explanation: Within a host cell, virus can reproduce using the machinery of host cell because a virus lacks this machinery required for replication.

questions.question: An organism which can be seen only using an electron microscope is:
questions.explanation: Electron microscope is used to study the viruses because its magnification is much greater than light microscope. Single celled algae, bacteria, and unicellular fungi can be seen under light microscope also.

questions.question: Virus particles come out of host cell when:

options.html: host cell is ready to divide

questions.explanation: Virus replicate inside the host cell to multiply their number, virus particles come out of host cell when host cell bursts.

questions.question: Which of the following microorganisms can be polygonal in shape?
questions.explanation: Viruses may be polygonal in shape. Viruses contain genetic material and coat of protein outside. Fungi, bacteria, and algae are not found in polygonal shape.

questions.question: Which of the following is a disease caused by protozoans?
questions.explanation:
• Protozoans are eukaryotic, unicellular microorganisms. Most of them are free-living, found in soil and fresh water, while some are parasitic and cause diseases in plants and animals. Malaria is caused by the protozoan parasite, Plasmodium.
  • Tuberculosis (TB) is caused by a type of bacterium called Mycobacterium tuberculosis.
  • Polio is a highly contagious infectious disease caused by polio virus.
  • Typhoid fever is an acute infectious illness associated with fever that is most often caused by the Salmonella typhi bacteria.

questions.question: Which of the following never possess flagella to move?
questions.explanation: Flagella is used by microbes for locomotion. Bacteria, euglena, and Trypanosoma contains flagella but virus does not contain flagella for locomotion.

questions.question: Which of the following milk will be best for bacterial growth?
questions.explanation: Bacteria reproduce faster at temperature between 20-250C. Warm milk will be best for bacterial growth during formation of curd from milk.

questions.question: Decomposers increase soil productivity by adding to it?
questions.explanation: Decomposers are microbes that breakdown the complex organic matter present in dead and decaying matter of plant and animals. Decomposer increase soil productivity by adding humus to it.

questions.question: If a useful bacterium is living in human intestine it will:

options.html: help in removal of harmful microbes

questions.explanation: Bacteria may be useful of harmful to human beings. A number of bacteria live in human intestine that help in food digestion and production of vitamin K.

questions.question: Growth of moulds on food can make food:
questions.explanation: Growth of moulds on food can make food unfit for consumption. Moulds contain harmful chemicals that are harmful to human beings and may cause digestive problems.

questions.question: Which of the following diseases is caused by a bacteria?
questions.explanation:
• Tuberculosis is an air-borne disease caused by the bacterial species Mycobacterium tuberculosis. 
  • Polio, dengue, and chicken pox are viral diseases caused by Picornavirus, Flavivirus, and chicken pox virus respectively.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A virus particle will never contain:
questions.explanation: A virus particle will never contain Endoplasmic Reticulum (ER). Virus consist of genetic material either DNA or RNA and coat of protein surrounding it.

questions.question: Mosquito is not causal organism of malaria but it is:
questions.explanation: Malaria is a protozoal disease caused by Plasmodium. Plasmodium completes their life cycle in two hosts, mosquito and human beings. Female anopheles mosquito transfers the Plasmodium from infected person to healthy person.

questions.question: For pasteurization, milk should be:

options.html: packed and stored by sudden chilling

questions.explanation: Pasteurization is the process of preventing milk from spoiling. During this process milk is heated at high temperature for few minutes and then chilled suddenly. Pasteurized milk remain fit for a few days.

questions.question: Viruses do not multiply inside?
questions.explanation: Virus do not multiply inside non living cell because virus do not contain endoplasmic reticulum and other organelles required for replication of genetic materials.

questions.question: The microorganism which is never useful is:
questions.explanation: Viruses are never useful for human beings. They may cause disease in plants and animals. Some bacteria, fungi, and protozoa are useful for human beings too.

questions.question: The property of body to fight with pathogens is called:
questions.explanation: The property of our body to fight with disease causing pathogens is called immunity. Immunity protects us from spreading microbes inside the body. Immunity may be innate or acquired.

questions.question: Vaccines generally contain:
questions.explanation: Vaccines are generally weak or dead pathogen inoculated in our body orally or through injection. Our body produce antibodies against foreign organisms and prevent us in case of actual infection.

questions.question: Microbes are successful due to their:
questions.explanation: Microbes are evolved on earth first and still surviving on earth due to their tolerance ability and ability to adjust in different kinds of climatic conditions.

questions.question: The group of microbe regarded as aquatic green plants is:
questions.explanation: Algae are regarded as aquatic green plants because they contain chlorophyll that help in photosynthesis. Algae also contain cell wall and most of algae are found in water or moist condition.

questions.question: Which of the following have a cell wall?
questions.explanation: Cell wall is the outermost hard layer of cell. It is found in plants, fungi, and bacteria. Cell wall of plants is made up of cellulose and that of fungi is chitin.

questions.question: Who will not be able to consume solid food?
questions.explanation: Rhizobium is bacteria found in root nodule of leguminous plants, responsible for fixing of nitrogen. It will not consume solid food. On the other hand, Amoeba, Paramecium, and Euglena are holozoic and consume solid food.

questions.question: We study about microorganisms to:

options.html: develop strategies to kill them and make earth disease free
be aware of their positive and negative effects

questions.explanation: We study about microorganisms to know their positive and negative effects on us. Some microbes are useful and some others are harmful and cause many diseases of plants and animals.

questions.question: A well defined nucleus is absent in:
questions.explanation: All prokaryotic organisms lack well defined nucleus. Salmonella is a prokaryotic organism in which well defined nucleus is absent. All other organisms are eukaryotic.

questions.question: Viruses are unaffected by:
questions.explanation: Antibiotics are substance by WBC in response to microbes. Viruses are unaffected by antibiotics as they lack cellular mechanism on which antibodies acts.

questions.question: Microbes are unable to survive in solutions of _____ concentration.
questions.explanation: Microbes are unable to survive in solutions of high concentration because due to exo-osmosis. Plasmolysis takes place that kills the microbes.

tests.instructionHtml:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: TMV will grow:

options.html: in the soil surrounding tobacco roots
in the space between tobacco cells

questions.explanation: Tobacco Mosaic Virus (TMV) will grow inside the tobacco cells to cause mosaic in leaves. TMV virus has hexagonal head and long tail.

questions.question: Bacteriophages are:
questions.explanation: Bacteriophages are group of virus that infect the bacteria. Bacteriophage help in killing the bacteria.

questions.question: Which disease still doesn't have a vaccine?
questions.explanation: Vaccines are dead or killed pathogens to provide immunity against disease. Vaccines are available for jaundice, cholera, and tuberculosis but not for malaria.

questions.question: Microbial diseases are:
questions.explanation: Most of microbial diseases are communicable disease. Communicable disease or infectious disease can be transmitted from infected person to healthy person.

questions.question: Air tight packing of fruits and other materials:
questions.explanation: Air tight packing of fruits and other materials prevents microbial growth because microbes grow only in presence of Oxygen. Microbes do not enter inside the container too and remain fresh for long duration.

questions.question: The autotrophic filamentous microbes living in water or moist soil are:
questions.explanation: Algae is autotrophic filamentous microbes living in water or moist soil. Algae contain chlorophyll that is necessary for photosynthesis.

questions.question: The microbe used as single cell protein:
questions.explanation: Chlorella is unicellular algae that are used as single cell protein. It contains lots of proteins and vitamins and used by astronaut as food.

questions.question: Edible fungi are:
questions.explanation: Mushrooms are edible fungi having lots to protein and other nutrients. Mushroom grows as saprophytes and have umbrella like structure.

questions.question: A microorganism is spherical in shape, it can be:
questions.explanation: Bacterium coccus are spherical in shape. A virus may also be spherical in shape but Rhizopus is a filamentous fungus having long thread like structure.

questions.question: A microbe makes bakery products fluffy and spongy, it is:
questions.explanation: Yeast is used to make bakery products fluffy and spongy. Yeast undergoes anaerobic respiration in which carbon dioxide gas is produced that makes bakery products fluffy and spongy

questions.question: Suppose all soil bacteria are removed what will be the result?

options.html: all plants growing in that soil will become disease free
all grasses will appear more green

questions.explanation: Soil contains large number of bacteria, if all soil bacteria are removed that will result soil fertility decreased. A soil bacterium includes nitrogen fixing bacteria as well as decomposers.

questions.question: Soham observed some microbes under microscope. He saw a microbe irregular in shape and locomoting with false feet, it is:
questions.explanation: Amoeba is irregular shaped and locomoting with false feet called pseudopodia. Amoeba is a unicellular organism found in dirty water and viewed under microscope.

questions.question: A slipper shaped fresh water protozoan moves with the help of:
questions.explanation: Paramoecium is a slipper shaped freshwater protozoan. It contains small hair like projection all over the body that help them in movement called as cilia.

questions.question: Which is not caused by protozoans?
questions.explanation: Malaria, amoebic dysentery, and pyorrhoea are caused by protozoa but food poisoning is caused by either fungi or bacteria.

questions.question:
Match the following:

i) Penicillin a) pathogen
ii) blue green algae b) antibiotic
iii) virus c) malaria
iv) Plasmodium d) lack chlorophyll
v) fungi e) fertiliser
questions.explanation: Penicillin is an antibiotic, blue green alga is used as fertilisers, virus is a pathogen, plasmodium is malaria and fungi lack chlorophyll.

tests.instructionHtml:

questions.question: Disease completely eradicated from earth by vaccination is:
questions.explanation: Small pox is a disease that completely eradicated from earth by vaccination. This disease is widely spread in many countries including India. Large scale vaccination leads to complete eradication of this disease.

questions.question: Malarial parasite attacks our:
questions.explanation: Plasmodium is a Protozoa that cause malaria in human organism. This pathogen attacks our red blood cells and release toxic material to cause high fever and sudden chill.

questions.question: The scientist who established that microbes can cause disease?
questions.explanation: Robert Koch was first scientist who established that microbes cause disease. Most of the diseases are caused by microbes that enter inside the body.

questions.question: The organism which is non living outside living cell is:
questions.explanation: Human immune virus (HIV) is non living organism outside the living cell as viruses neither replicates nor respires outside the living cell.

questions.question: Which of the following microbe contain more than one cell?
questions.explanation: Baker’s yeast, Tobacco Mosaic Virus, and Slipper animalcule (paramecium) are unicellular organisms and contain only one cell. Bread mould (Rhizopus) is filamentous multi-cellular fungi.

questions.question: That group of microbes who can synthesize their own food?
questions.explanation: The group of organisms that can synthesize their own food are called autotrophs. They contain chlorophyll that captures solar energy for photosynthesis. Algae contain chlorophyll; hence they can prepare their own food.

questions.question: If denitrifying bacteria are removed from earth which element will get reduced in air?
questions.explanation: Denitrifying bacteria converts nitrogen containing organic materials into free nitrogen. If they are removed from earth no nitrogen will return back in atmosphere during nitrogen cycle.

questions.question: Yeast catalyses conversion of sugar into:
questions.explanation: Yeast catalyses conversion of sugar molecules into alcohols and carbon dioxide in absence of oxygen. The process of formation of alcohol by yeast is called as fermentation.

questions.question: Avoiding infection by eliminating microbes from the surroundings is called:
questions.explanation: Most of the disease in human beings are caused by microbes present in the surrounding. To avoid infection, microbes are eliminated using disinfectant to make the surrounding hygiene.

questions.question: We should not keep food in open because:

options.html: it will change the type of nutrients in food

questions.explanation: A number of pathogenic microorganisms are present in our surrounding along with insects that carry the germs. So, we should keep food in closed container to avoid food spoilage.

questions.question: What Leeuwenhoek observed under microscope were:
questions.explanation: Anton Van Leeuwenhoek observed bacteria and protozoa under microscope which were not visible by naked eyes due to small size.

questions.question: Viruses belong to _____ of living world.
questions.explanation: Viruses are not included in any kingdom of Robert Whittaker because they show property of both living and non-living. They do not possess cellular structure too.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Bacteria are unicellular organisms.
  2. Bacteria lack cell membrane but contain a cell wall.

options.html: Statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong.
Statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong.

questions.explanation: Bacteria are unicellular prokaryotic organisms not contain well defined nucleus. Bacteria contain both cell membrane as well as cell wall.

questions.question: Freezing ensures food preservation because:

options.html: kills microbes and its all spores
changes water inside food into ice

questions.explanation: Freezing ensures food preservation because at low temperature microbial growth does not take place and water inside the food is changed into ice.

questions.question: Watering the crops is called:
questions.explanation: irrigation

questions.question: What are pondscum?
questions.explanation: Pond scums are floating filamentous algae present in stagnant water bodies. Pond scum grows more if organic nutrients are present more in ponds.

questions.question: Biogas can be obtained by the action of:
questions.explanation: Biogas is produced by anaerobic fermentation of cow dung and other organic materials by the action of bacteria methanogen. These bacteria are present in cow dung that gives characteristic smell.

questions.question: Skin disease in humans caused by fungus.
questions.explanation: Fungus are non-chlorophyll containing microbes that cause a number of disease in plants and animals. Ringworm is caused by fungus in human beings.

questions.question: We can prevent cases of malaria by:

options.html: avoiding living in the vicinity of malaria patients
allowing water to collect in surroundings

questions.explanation: Malaria can be prevented by avoiding water from collecting in the surrounding. Stagnant water provides breeding ground for mosquito that carries plasmodium from infected person to healthy person.

questions.question: Hepatitis can be prevented by:

options.html: both drinking boiled water and vaccination

questions.explanation: Hepatitis is a viral disease transmitted through contaminated water and food. Hepatitis can be prevented by drinking boiled water and taking vaccine of hepatitis time to time.

questions.question: Which of the following group of microbes can be consumed as SCPs?
questions.explanation: Single cell proteins are excellent sources of protein required by human beings in concentrated form. Single cell protein is obtained from a group of algae.

questions.question: Which of the following is the simplest unicellular animal?
questions.explanation: Amoeba is considered as simplest unicellular animal that evolve on earth first. AIDS virus is acelluar. Azotoabactor and Agaricus are multicelluar organisms.

questions.question: Sleeping sickness is caused by a:
questions.explanation: Sleep sickness is caused by protozoa called Trypanosoma. This protozoa is transmitted by house fly form infected person to healthy person.

questions.question: Profuse growth of algae in water body will:

options.html: degrade taste, colour and odour of water
improve the quality of water and make it tasty
change the bad microbes of water into good ones
improve the rate of photosynthesis globally

questions.explanation: Profuse growth of algae in water body will degrade taste, colour and odour of water. The oxygen present in water will be absorbed by algae leading to change in colour, taste and odour of water.

questions.question: Scientific name of Baker's yeast.
questions.explanation: Baker’s yeast is used for baking cake, pasties, and other products. Baker’s yeast is actually scientific name of Saccharomyces cereviseae that release carbon dioxide gas on fermentation.

questions.question: To prepare jellies and ice creams there is use of:
questions.explanation: To prepare jellies and ice creams algae is used. Agar is obtained from algae that help in solidifying ice cream and jellies.

questions.question: Food and mouth disease occurs in cattle due to:
questions.explanation: Food and mouth disease is a common disease of cattle in which ulcers are formed in mouth as well as foot. The animals stop feeding. Foot and mouth disease is caused due to a virus.

questions.question: Potato blight is caused due to:
questions.explanation: Potato blight is caused due to fungi. In this disease foliage of potato plants develops black spot all over the plants to reduce photosynthesis.

questions.question: Which instrument is required to view viruses?
questions.explanation: Viruses are very minute acellular organisms. They cannot be viewed under light microscope. Electron microscope is required to view viruses. Electron microscope magnify the object over million times.

questions.question:
Match the following :

Disease Causal Organism
i) Food Poisoning a) Vibrio cholera
ii) Pneumonia b) Mycobacterium leprae
iii) Cholera c) Diplococcus pneumonia
iv) Malaria d) Clostridium botulinum
v) Leprosy e) Plasmodium falciparum
questions.explanation: Food poisoning is caused by Clostridium botulinum. Pneumonia is caused by Diplococcus pneumonia. Cholera is caused by Vibrio cholera. Malaria is caused by protozoa Vibrio cholera and leprosy is caused by Mycobacterium leprae.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Fibres are:
questions.explanation: Fibres may be synthetic like nylon, natural like cotton and wool or artificial fibre like rayon. These fibres are used to make different types of fabrics and number of household articles.

questions.question: PVC is:
questions.explanation: PVC stands for Polyvinyl Chloride. It is a thermoplastic and most widely used for the manufacture of sewage pipes.

questions.question: Which of the following set of features belong to thermoplastics?
questions.explanation: Thermoplastics are soft and less brittle in nature. Thermoplastics get deformed easily on heating and can be bent and reshaped into desired products easily. Example: polythene and PVC.

questions.question: Cellulose is used to prepare:
questions.explanation: Rayon is also called cellulosic fibre. Cellulose of wood pulp is used to prepare rayon which is also called artificial silk.

questions.question: The oldest synthetic plastic is:
questions.explanation: Bakelite is the oldest synthetic plastic which is poor conductor of heat and electricity. It is a thermosetting plastic. It is used to make electrical switches.

questions.question: Best alternative to wool is:
questions.explanation: Acrylic is best alternative to wool. Acrylic is soft, lightweight and warm. Acrylic fabrics are cheaper than wool and resistant to weathering which makes them more popular than natural fibres.

questions.question: Statement incorrect for synthetic fibres is/are:
questions.explanation: Synthetic fibres are long lasting and less expensive than natural fibres. They are prepared by a number of processes using raw materials of petroleum origin.

questions.question: First synthetic fibre formed from natural material is:
questions.explanation: First synthetic fibre formed from natural material is Rayon. It is prepared by chemical treatment of cellulose of wood pulp.

questions.question: First man-made fibre produced chemically is:
questions.explanation: Nylon is first man-made fibre produced chemically using coal, water, and air, no plant or animal raw material are used to make nylon. Nylon fibre is strong, elastic and light. It is lustrous and easy to wash.

questions.question: The lightest plastic material is:
questions.explanation: Polypropylene is the lightest plastic material which is used for packing and labelling. Laboratory equipment are also made from polypropylene.

questions.question: Which of the following fibres are made from petroleum products?
questions.explanation: All the synthetic fibres are made from petrochemicals, materials of petroleum origin. It includes terylene, decron, and acrylon, etc.

questions.question: A strand of a substance is called:
questions.explanation: A thin strand of a substance which can be changed into fabrics is called fibre. Fibre may be synthetic, artificial, or natural depending upon source of origin.

questions.question: Which one is artificial silk?
questions.explanation: Rayon is called as artificial silk because it is shiny and lustrous like silk. It is obtained from wood pulp containing cellulose.

questions.question: When plastics are burnt they release:
questions.explanation: When plastics are burnt, they release toxic gases that includes carbon monoxide, sulphur dioxide, etc into the environment, leading to increased air pollution.

questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect regarding plastics:
questions.explanation: Plastics can be recycled. Thermoplastics can be melted and reshaped into other products.

questions.question: ________ is blended with wool for making carpets.
questions.explanation: Nylon is blended with wool to make carpets. These blended carpets are easy to wash, dries out quickly, light, and strong. It also brings down the cost.

questions.question: _______ is blended with wool to give terry wool.
questions.explanation: Polyester is blended with wool to give terry wool. Terry wool is used to make winter dresses and other kinds of fabrics. It is durable and easy to wash.

questions.question: Fibre as soft as wool is:
questions.explanation: Acrylic is a synthetic fibre as soft as wool. They are polymers of polyacrylonitrile. Acrylic fibres are soft, lightweight and warm.

questions.question: Many fibres are twisted to form:
questions.explanation: Fibres are interlocked together to form long continous length of fibres called yarn. Yarn is used in knitting, crocheting, weaving, embroidery, etc.

questions.question: Raw materials for the synthetic fibres are derived from:
questions.explanation: Raw materials for the synthetic fibres are derived from petrochemicals, which are materials derived from petroleum using a number of processes. Therefore, these fibres are called as synthetic fibres.

questions.question: _________ is widely used for making non-stick pans and cookware.
questions.explanation: Polytetrafluroethylene (PTFE) is a synthetic polymer commonly called as Teflon. It is used as a non-stick coating for pans and other cookware.

questions.question: Name NYLON was derived from:
questions.explanation: Name NYLON was derived from New York and London, the hometown of the chemists working on the materials synthesis. NYLON is used for making bristles of tooth brush, in manufacture of tyres, tents, etc.

questions.question: Remoulding can be done of:
questions.explanation: Polyvinyl chloride (PVC) is a thermoplastic which can be melted by heating and can be transformed into a new shape and can retain that shape when cooled. It is used for making pipes and other materials which is flexible and durable.

questions.question: Unbreakable kitchenware is made from:
questions.explanation: Unbreakable kitchenware is made from melamine. Melamine is a thermosetting plastic. Heat does not affect the bonding of the polymer chain, so it can tolerate heat and cannot be melted and reformed.

questions.question: Which of the following is a thermosetting plastic?
questions.explanation: Melamine is a thermosetting plastic as they do not melt on heating. Once moulded into a shape, they are permanently set. They are used in the making of handles of cookware, plates, bowls, etc since they can tolerate and resist heat.

questions.question: Fibres are twisted to form:
questions.explanation: Long thin strand of cotton, nylon or other fibres are twisted to form thread. Thread is a type of yarn intended for sewing by hand or machine. Thread is a long continuous length of interlocked fibre.

questions.question: Identify the synthetic fibre which resembles wool.
questions.explanation: Acrylic is a transparent, synthetic fibre which resembles wool. In everyday products, it is particularly used for signs, sales displays, roof windows, lenses and screens. A more impressive use of acrylic is in the big aquariums around the world. The panoramic windows of large aquariums are made of glued acrylic blocks.

questions.question: The first pure synthetic fibre is:
questions.explanation: Nylon is the first non-cellulosic synthetic fibre. It is made from coal, air, and water and no natural raw material are used in the making of nylon.

questions.question: Woollen apparels can be made from:
questions.explanation: Wool and acrylic is used to make woollen apparels for winter season. Wool is a natural fibre obtained from goat, sheep or yak and acrylic is a synthetic fibre made from the polymer polyacrylonitrile where the properties of acrylic fibres are similar to those of wool, soft, lightweight and warm.

questions.question: Such plastic which gets deformed easily on heating is called:
questions.explanation: Thermoplastics which gets melted easily on heating and transformed easily into a desired new shape is called thermoplastic. PVC and polythene is example of thermoplastic.
