questions.question: Necklace : bead :: synthetic fibre: _______
questions.explanation: Necklace is made by joining large number of beads similarly synthetic fibres are manufactured by joining large number of monomers.

questions.question: The first true plastic was:
questions.explanation: The first true plastic was Bakelite, because it is purely synthetic, no natural material was used while making bakelite. It is a thermosetting plastic. Bakelite is a poor conductor of heat and electricity. It is used for making electrical switches.

questions.question: Uniform of firemen have a coating of:
questions.explanation: Uniform of firemen has a coating of melamine because it resists fire as it is a thermosetting plastic and can tolerate heat better than other plastics.

questions.question: The first thermosetting plastic was:
questions.explanation: The first thermosetting plastic was Bakelite. It is also known as the first true plastic as no natural raw material was used while making bakelite and it purely synthetic. Bakelite is a poor conductor of heat and electricity. It is used for making electrical switches.

questions.question: Synthetic polymers are:

options.html: bad conductor of heat and electricity.

questions.explanation: All synthetic polymer i.e; plastics are bad conductors of heat. They are thermal insulators as they are thermosetting plastics. They are heat resistant and cannot be melted. Plastics are also electrical insulators.

questions.question: Which of the following set of features belong to thermosetting plastics?
questions.explanation: Thermosetting plastics are type of plastic that are permanently set once moulded into a shape. The linear polymer chains are strongly bonded and it cannot be melted and reformed. Instead, when exposed to enough heat they will crack. That is why thermosetting plastics are strong and more brittle then thermoplastics.

questions.question: The thermosetting plastic formed by condensation of phenol and formaldehyde is:
questions.explanation: Bakelite is the first true plastic made from synthetic components.

questions.question: Which of the following material is a mixture of two fibres?
questions.explanation: Polywool is a blend of polyester and wool. The blend is designed to afford the advantages of both the wool and polyester fibres in one fabric, such as wrinkle resistance, increased absorbancy, increased strength, etc.

questions.question: Fabrics are made with:
questions.explanation: Fabrics are made with either yarn or thread. Thread is a long fibre made of cotton nylon, silk etc intended for sewing by hand or machine. Yarn is a long continuous length of interlocked fibre to make fabrics, knit sweaters, crochet etc normally it is made of wool.

questions.question: Which of the following is a thermoplastic:
questions.explanation: Nylon is a thermoplastic while melamine and Bakelite are thermosetting plastics. Nylon can be melted easily by heating and can also be moulded into a desired shape.

questions.question: A non biodegradable fibre will cause:
questions.explanation: Non-biodegradable fibre remains in the soil for many years. It cannot be decomposed by the action of fungi and bacteria. A single plastic bag can take upto 1000 years decompose. They cause pollution of soil and to air on combustion by releasing toxic fumes.

questions.question: Plastics are poor conductor of heat thus on bringing closer to flame:

options.html: they change colour but not shape
they change from thermoplastic to thermosetting plastic

questions.explanation: Plastics are poor conductor of heat thus on bringing closer to flame they melt. Thermoplastic easily get deformed easily on heating and can be moulded to a new shape but thermosetting plastics are more resistant to heat.

questions.question: The first man- made fibre obtained from natural source is:
questions.explanation: Rayon is a man-made fibre obtained from natural source. Rayon is obtained by chemical processing of cellulose of wood pulp or cotton.

questions.question: Which of the following is not found in artificial form:
questions.explanation: Artificial silk, rubber and wool are found but artificial jute is never found. Rayon is called as artificial silk and acrylic is synthetic wool. Artificial rubber as also found.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. fibres are made from fabrics.
  2. fabrics are made from fibres.

options.html: Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct
Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct

questions.explanation: Fabrics are made from fibres. Fibres are long thin strands that are twisted to form yarn or thread and weaved to obtain fabrics according to what type of fibre strands are used.

tests.name: Synthetic Fibres & Plastics Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Synthetic Fibres and Plastics CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Plastic means:

options.html: incapable of conducting heat and electricity
capable of conducting heat and electricity

questions.explanation: Plastic is derived from the Greek word meaning capable of being moulded or shaped. Plastic can be shaped in any form, can be recycled, reused, and coloured too.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Synthetic fibres are chemically inert.
  2. Synthetic fibres can be damaged by acids and alkalies.

options.html: Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct
Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct

questions.explanation: Synthetic fibres are chemically inert and they are resistant to action of acids and alkalis. On the other hand, natural fibres are attacked by acids and bases.

questions.question: All electrical wires used in household fittings contain a plastic covering because plastics are:
questions.explanation: All electric wires used in household fitting contain plastic covering because plastics are bad conductor of heat and electricity. It prevents short circuit and from electric shocks.

questions.question: Thermosetting plastics contain cross-linked polymeric chains so they can be used:
questions.explanation: Thermosetting plastics contain cross-linked polymeric chains so they can be used only once. The cross linked polymer chains make it strongly bonded even heat does not affect the bonding. They cannot be moulded on heating to change the shape and size.

questions.question: Fine hair like structures (fibre) are twisted together to make:
questions.explanation: Fine hair like structures (fibres) of cotton, nylon etc are twisted together to make thread. Thread are either used to form fabrics by weaving or sewing.

questions.question: Plastics that can be reclaimed from wastes are:
questions.explanation: Plastics that can be reclaimed from wastes are thermoplastics. As thermoplastic can be heated and melted and recast to different shapes and sizes.

questions.question: It was called miracle fibre and replaced silk, this fibre is :
questions.explanation: Rayon was called miracle fibre and replaced silk.It is the first man made fibre and developed as a substitute for silk. So,rayon is also called as artificial silk. Rayon is obtained by the chemical treatment of cellulose of wood pulp or cotton.

questions.question: This fibre is crease resistant and marketed under different names as terylene and terene. This fibre is:
questions.explanation: Polyester is crease resistant and marketed under different names as terylene. It resists wrinkles. It is actually made up of the repeating units of a chemical called an ester.

questions.question: PTFE is better known as:
questions.explanation: Polytetrafluroethylene (PTFE) is better known as Teflon. Teflon is used for coating non-stick cookware.

questions.question: Terylene, Terene & Dacron are made by repeating units of:
questions.explanation: Terylene, Terene, and Decron are made by repeating units of esters. They are also known as polyester. Esters are the chemicals that give fruits like smell.

questions.question: In a hot and humid weather, synthetic fibres:

options.html: stick to the body and feel uncomfortable
are very comfortable because absorb little water
absorb sweat of the body makes feel comfortable
very easy to handle because dry very quickly

questions.explanation: In hot and humid weather, synthetic fibres stick to the body and feel uncomfortable. They do not absorb sweat as compared to natural fibre.

questions.question: Synthetic clothes shouldn’t be worn while burning crackers because:

options.html: they may decrease the enjoyment due to their sticky nature
they may cause lung cancer at that time
they may cause severe burn injuries if catch fire
they may cause irritation in the presence of cracker fumes & smoke

questions.explanation: Synthetic fibres are obtained from petroleum products and catches fire and they also melts easily which is a major disadvantage. That is why it is advised not wear synthetic clothes while burning crackers.

questions.question: Main source of raw material for producing plastics is:
questions.explanation: Main source of raw material for producing plastics is crude oil or petroleum. They are formed by the polymerisation of the organic compounds.

questions.question: Plastic buckets are used to store water because:

options.html: plastics are poor conductor of heat
plastics are bad conductor of electricity

questions.explanation: Plastic buckets are used to store water because plastics do not react in water and are insoluble in water.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Plastics are environment friendly.
  2. Plastics are good conductors of heat and electricity.

options.html: Statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong.
Statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong.

questions.explanation: Plastics are non-biodegradable so, are not environment friendly. Plastics are bad conductor of heat and electricity therefore it is used for in making of electronic components such as circuit boards, electric switches etc

tests.name: Synthetic Fibres & Plastics Test 05
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Synthetic Fibres and Plastics CBSE Online Test 05 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A polymer is a large molecule composed of many repeated units. What is a polyester composed of?
questions.explanation: Polyester (Poly+ester) is a category of polymers, made up of the repeating units of a type of chemical called ester. Polyester fibres are strong, resistant to stretching, and shrinking. They dry quickly, are wrinkle resistant, and can be easily washed.

questions.question: We should never dispose off polythenes in the sewage system because:

options.html: it chokes drains and pollutes the soil.

questions.explanation: We should never dispose off polythene in the sewage system because polythene chokes drains leading to overflowing on roads and streets and also pollutes the soil since it takes long time to decompose.

questions.question: The 4th R added to 3R concept is:
questions.explanation: The only way to manage plastic is to follow the 3R's principle of reduce, reuse, and recycle. Later 4th R  i e Recover is also added to environmental friendly habits.

questions.question: The scratch proof and corrosion resistant low friction protective coating on metal surface is:
questions.explanation: Teflon is scratch proof and corrosion resistant low friction protective coating on metal surface. It is coated on cookware to make them scratch resistant.

questions.question: All kinds of fibres are:
questions.explanation: All kinds of fibres are polymers. Polymers are made up of number of repeated units of monomers all join together.

questions.question: A regenerated synthetic fibre is one which is made up of:
questions.explanation: Regenerated synthetic fibre is one which is made by chemical treatment of natural material. Rayon is obtained from chemical treatment of cellulose present in wood pulp.

questions.question: A nylon fibre is made entirely from chemicals hence it is:
questions.explanation: A nylon fibre is made entirely from chemicals hence it is true synthetic fibre. It is the first non-cellulosic polymer and does not used any plant or animal raw material. It is made from coal.air and water. Most of the synthetic fibres are petroleum products and formed by chemical treatment.

questions.question: We can control the physical properties of synthetic fibres by:
questions.explanation: The physical properties of fibre can be changed by rearranging the monomers, the inter and intrachain bonding etc.

questions.question: Bakelite do not melt on heating because such:

options.html: polymers are attached by strong linear chains
polymers are poor conductors of heat
polymers are joined by strong cross links

questions.explanation: Bakelite is a thermosetting plastic. And thermosetting plastics does not melt on heating because such polymers are joined by strong cross links.

questions.question: Which of the following will be a polymer:
questions.explanation: Polymers are repeated units of monomers. Artificial silk or rayon is the first made fibre also called cellulosic polymer. Rayon was developed as a substitute for silk. It is the only polymer here.

questions.question: _________ is the first fully synthetic fibre.
questions.explanation: Nylon was the first fully synthetic fibre made in 1931, without using any natural raw material. It was prepared from chemicals. Nylon fibres are strong, lustrous, elastic and used for making stockings, car belts, parachutes, ropes, etc.

questions.question: Nylon absorbs very little water hence it is most suitable for making:
questions.explanation: Nylon absorbs very little water hence it is most suitable for making fishing net, parachute, and tyre cords. Nylon is resistant to water, light, elastic, dries out quickly, and very strong.

questions.question: Plastics are classified as thermoplastic and thermosetting plastic based on their behaviour:
questions.explanation: Plastics are classified as thermoplastic and thermosetting plastic based on their behaviour on heating. Thermoplastic can be melted heated and harden again when cooled but thermosetting plastic does not melt when heated and cannot be moulded. Once moulded into a shape they are permanently shaped.

questions.question: Polythene is obtained by polymerization of:
questions.explanation: Polythene as the name suggests is polyethylene. Polythene is obtained by polymerization of ethylene monomers. Repeated units of ethylene join in a linear fashion to form polythene.

questions.question: Thermoplastics can be:
questions.explanation: Thermoplastics can be destroyed by heating. Thermoplastics are bad conductor of heat and electricity. It can be recycled since it can be melted on heating and reshaped to other products.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Nylon does not contain:
questions.explanation: Nylon is prepared by using coal, water, and air. It is first true synthetic fibre. Nylon is the first non-cellulosic polymer.

questions.question: Which of the following is not included in 3R concept:
questions.explanation: 3R’s are reduce, recycle, and reuse to manage plastics and develop the environ friendly habit. React is not included in 3R that creates good environmental practice.

questions.question: It is dangerous to burn plastics because:

options.html: fumes if inhaled cause immediate death

questions.explanation: Burning of plastics release a number of gases that includes carcinogenic substance that can cause cancer. So, burning of plastic is dangerous for health and also to the environment.

questions.question: To overcome disadvantages of synthetic fibres:

options.html: they are mixed with natural fibres
mixed with petroleum products and purified

questions.explanation: To overcome disadvantages of synthetic fibres, they are mixed with natural fibres. This mixing of natural and synthetic fibre is called blending. Terycotton and polywool are examples of blended fibres.

questions.question:
Identify the product based on the given features.

  1. It is a man-made product.
  2. It has a linear arrangement of monomer units.
  3. It is used for making goods like toys, combs, containers, etc.
questions.explanation: Thermoplastics are those plastics which can be re-molded on heating and can be bent easily. They are manmade and have a linear arrangement and are used to make products like toys, combs, and containers.

questions.question: Sooraj threw away plastic bags near his house. Next day he saw his cow dead close by those bags:

options.html: she had swallowed those bags and the bags had choked her throat. The cow died due to chokking and suffocation.
she had eaten food kept inside those bags

questions.explanation: she had swallowed those bags

questions.question: Atul was climbing mountain and he wondered about the strength of rope in his hand which was stronger than a steel wire. This rope would have been made up of:
questions.explanation: Nylon is synthetic fibre which is used to make rope and other household articles. The strength of rope made of nylon is stronger than a steel wire and it is also elastic and light, which makes it easier to carry. Strength is due to cross linking polymerisation.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Acrylic is costlier than wool.
  2. It is the best alternative of wool.

options.html: Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct
Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct

questions.explanation: Acrylic is cheaper than wool. Acrylic is a synthetic fibre made from the polymer polyacrylonitrile which is more durable and affordable. Acrylic is best alternative of wool. It is soft, light weight and warm.

questions.question: Littering of plastic materials leads to:
questions.explanation: Littering of plastic materials leads to solid waste pollution. Plastic is non-biodegradable material and remain undecomposed for long duration. Accumulation of plastics material on earth surface leads to solid waste pollution and cause a lot of environmental damage.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Acrylic fibres can be made into transparent sheets.
  2. Acrylic fibres are used to make thermal wears.

options.html: Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct
Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct

questions.explanation: Acrylic fibres can be made into transparent sheets which can be used as unbreakable window pans. Acrylic fibres are used to make thermal wears that resemble wool. It is an alternative of wool as it is also light weight, soft and warm.

questions.question: Bakelite is formed by condensation and polymerisation of:
questions.explanation: Bakelite is a thermosetting plastic. Bakelite is formed by condensation and polymerisation of phenol and formaldehyde. It is a poor conductor of heat and electricity. It is used for making electrical switches and handles of utensils.

questions.question: Cellulose of wood pulp is chemically treated to produce:
questions.explanation: Rayon is obtained by chemical treatment of cellulose of wood pulp. Rayon is also called as artificial silk.

questions.question: Making of base of magnetic recording tape needs:
questions.explanation: Base of magnetic recording tape is manufactured from polyester. Polyester is made up of the repeating units of a chemical called ester. PET and terylene are common polyesters.

questions.question: The statement incorrect about rayon is:

options.html: it is also called artificial silk
it is made entirely of chemicals
it is comfortable to wear in summers

questions.explanation: Rayon is prepared by chemical treatment of wood pulp which contains cellulose. It is also called a cellulosic polymer. It is the first man-made fiber. It is comfortable to wear in summer. It is also called as artificial silk.

questions.question:
Match the following:

i) Bakelite a) parachutes
ii) Nylon b) PET containers
iii) PVC c) sizing agent in paper industry
iv) Melamine d) telephone instruments
v) Polyester e) pipes
questions.explanation: Bakelite is used to telephone instruments. Nylon is used to make parachutes, PVC is used to make pipes, melamine is used to make sizing agent in paper industry and polyester is used to make PET containers.

questions.question: Dress materials made from synthetic fibres are:
questions.explanation: Dress materials made from synthetic fibres are non airy as monomers of these materials are closely packed. Although they are soak less water and are also resistant to acids and alkalis.

questions.question: We should not wear synthetic clothes in kitchen because:

options.html: they are non conductors of heat and electricity

questions.explanation: Synthetic fibres catches fire easily, therefore, we should not wear synthetic clothes in kitchen. On burning they melt easily and stick to the body surface to make burns more severe.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Plastic bags can choke the drains.
  2. Mosquitoes cannot grow in such choked drains.

options.html: Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct
Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct

questions.explanation: Plastic bags can choke the drains as it is non-biodegradable and remain unchanged for long duration. Stagnant water provide breeding ground for mosquitoes that spread malaria and other diseases.

questions.question: This symbol represents :
questions.explanation: The symbol shown above is for recycle. Plastics and metals that are non-biodegradable can be recycled.They are collected separately in container having symbols shown above.

questions.question: Dyeing refers to:
questions.explanation: Dying refers to applying colour on the surface of a fabric. Artificial or manmade fibres can be dye into different colours.

questions.question: It is a hard white solid and used as a sizing agent in the paper industry. This is:
questions.explanation: Melamine is a hard white solid and used as a sizing agent in the paper industry. Melamine is thermosetting plastics that can tolerate heat better than other plastics.

questions.question: A user of synthetic fibres feels uncomfortable in warm weather:

options.html: synthetic fibres are light yet strong
synthetic fibres provide resistance to fungal attack
synthetic fibres do not breathe well

questions.explanation: Synthetic fibres are more comfortable in warm weather as synthetic fibres do not breathe well. Air space is absent in synthetic fibre which make them unfit for summer and also they do not absorb sweat. They are water resistant.

questions.question: Which synthetic fibre is the jacket shown in image made up of ?
questions.explanation: The jacket shown in image is made up of nylon. Nylon is first synthetic fibre which is strong,elastic and light weight. It is easy to wash and dries out quickly.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Nylon is manufactured from coal, water, and air.
  2. Nylon is a natural fibre.

options.html: Statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong.
Statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong.

questions.explanation: Nylon is manufactured from coal, water and air so, it is a synthetic fibre. Nylon fibre is elastic, lustrous and easy to wash. It is used to make socks, ropes, tents, curtains etc. It is not a natural fibre since it is made of non-cellulosic polymer.

questions.question: The toy shown in the image is made up of a :
questions.explanation: The toy shown is made up of a thermoplastic. Thermoplastic is used to make toys which are flexible and can be moulded by heating. They are more soft then thermosetting plastics.

questions.question: Nylon is a:

options.html: both true synthetic fibre & regenerated fibre

questions.explanation: Nylon is a true synthetic fibre because nylon is prepared from coal, water, and air. It is made from non-cellulosic polymer. No natural substance obtained from plants and animals are used in preparation of nylon.

questions.question: The T- shirt shown in the image is made of :
questions.explanation: The T-shirt shown in the image is made of polyester fabric. Polyester is made up of esters,a chemical which gives fruity smell. Polyester is durable,does not shrink,easy to wash and affordable which make them more popular.

questions.question: The cap shown in the image must be made of :
questions.explanation: The cap is made of woollen like material called acrylic.They are soft, light weight and warm. Acrylic is available in variety of colours. They are more durable and affordable than pure wool.

questions.question: Yarn is twisted to form:
questions.explanation: Yarn is twisted to form thread. Thread is a type of yarn intended for sewing by hand or machine. Yarn is a long continuous length of interlocked fibre.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Recycled plastic can be produced from:
questions.explanation: Recycled plastic can be produced from used thermoplastic. Thermoplastic can be melted by heating and can be moulded into other desired products. This plastic can be used to produce recycled plastic articles.

questions.question: These telephone sets are made up of :
questions.explanation: Bakelite is used to make telephones and electrical switches. Bakelite is a thermosetting plastic.The linear polymer chain are cross linked and strongly bonded, so heat does not affect the bonding and also,they are very hard.

questions.question: These Japanese bottles are made of :
questions.explanation: Polyethylene terephthalate (PET) is used to make bottle production.It is a variety of polyester. These bottles are popularly known as Japanese bottles. These bottles are strong, water resistant and durable too.

questions.question: The bowls shown in image must be made of :
questions.explanation: The bowls shown above is made of plastics. The thermosetting plastic called melamine is used to make such bowls. Thermosetting plastics are resistant to heat and can tolerate heat better than other plastics. Thermosets are used to make things that will be warmed up.

questions.question: Sunita found that a plastic can be moulded into any shape one wants and a number of times, such plastic is:
questions.explanation: A plastic that can be moulded into any shape one wants and a number of times is polythene. Polythene is a thermoplastics and they can be melted by heating and changed into new products.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. We should not burn plastic materials.
  2. Fumes produced by burning plastics are carcinogenic.

options.html: Statement ii) is wrong and i) is correct
Statement i) is wrong and ii) is correct

questions.explanation: We should not burn plastic materials as it realeases toxic fumes into the environment. These fumes produced by burning plastics are carcinogenic that may cause cancer. The carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide releases in the environment also lead to air pollution.

questions.question: Choose the correct pair:
questions.explanation: Papers, leftover foodstuff, and cotton clothes are biodegradable as they get decomposed easily in the soil by the action of fungi and bacteria. But cans are non-biodegradable as it is made up of metal.

questions.question: Which plastic this symbol indicates?
questions.explanation: The symbol shown above indicates PET (Polyethylene terephthalate) that is of safe and not reactive to water and other liquid substances stored in them.It is a variety of polyester.

questions.question: Which plastic this symbol indicates?(it is the first of bad ones)
questions.explanation: The symbol shown above indicates PVC (polyvinyl chloride) plastic that cannot be used for storing food items. It is usually used to make pipes required for drainage system.It is a thermoplastic.

questions.question: This symbol is for another plastic to be avoided. This is :
questions.explanation: This symbol is used to indicate the worst quality of plastics called polystyrene. This kind of plastic is not suitable for storing food items or drinking water.

questions.question: Which of the following is incorrect regarding plastics?
questions.explanation: Plastics can be recycled. Plastics are non-biodegradable, resistant to weather, and durable. Thermoplastics can be melted by heating and reshaped into the desired products. It is used to make chairs, rope, bucket, and a number of other utensils.

questions.question: Crushing the plastic bottles and containers initiates its:
questions.explanation: Crushing the plastic bottles and containers initiates its recycling. Plastic bottles and other containers should be crushed to avoid reuse without sterilisation.

questions.question: PIC refers to:
questions.explanation: Plastic Identification code identifies the type of plastic, the product is made from. Plastic Identification Mark is marked on the plastic materials to make it easier for re-processors to identify and seperate used plastics for a range of new application.

questions.question: _____ of plastics releases foul smell and toxic gases:
questions.explanation: Incineration is a waste treatment process by burning. Incineration of plastics release foul smell and toxic gases into the environment that leads to air pollution.

questions.question:
Match the following:

i) Thermoplastic a) Polyester + cotton
ii) Rayon b) not easy to recycle
iii) Acrylic c) artificial silk
iv) Thermosetting plastic d) cheaper than wool
v) Polycotton e) can be recycled
questions.explanation: Thermoplastic can be recycled. Rayon is a called as artificial silk. Acrylic is cheaper than wool. Thermosetting plastic is not easy to recycle. Polycotton is a fabric made by mixing polyester and cotton.

tests.name: Materials Metals & Non Metals Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Materials Metals and Non Metals CBSE Online Test 01 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Gold : metal :: Silicon : _______
questions.explanation: Gold is metal and Silicon is a metalloid. Those elements that shows the property of both metal and non-metal are called metalloid.

questions.question: Mineral is:
questions.explanation: Mineral is a naturally occurring material obtained from earth crust. Mineral contain one or more element.

questions.question: Non metal that conducts electricity is:
questions.explanation: Non-metal are generally bad conductor of electricity but Graphite is an allotropes of carbon that is good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: Metals are:
questions.explanation: Metals are electropositive because metals contain 1,2 or 3 electron in outermost shell and have a tendency to lose electron and form a cation. Metals form electrovalent bond by loosing electron and become electropositive.

questions.question: When MgO is dissolved in water, Mg(OH)2 is obtained. The solution thus obtained is ______ in nature.
questions.explanation: An alkaline or basic solution turns red litmus paper to blue. Hence when a red litmus paper is dipped into this solution of Mg(OH)2, it turns blue indicating the basic nature of the solution.

questions.question: A metal is characterised by:
questions.explanation: Metal conducts electricity because they contain a free electron which can move through the metal and conduct electric current.

questions.question: Non metals occur as:
questions.explanation: Non-metals are found in nature in solid, liquid, and gaseous state. On the other hand, metals are found in solid state except mercury that is liquid at room temperature.

questions.question: Metals that react with both acids and bases are called:
questions.explanation: Metals that reacts with both acids and bases are called amphoteric. Amphoteric oxides are acidic as well as basic in nature.e.g zinc oxide

questions.question: Metalloids are also known as:
questions.explanation: Those elements that show the property of both metal and non-metal are called metalloid or semi metals. Silicon, germanium, etc are common metalloid.

questions.question: Metals are solid except:
questions.explanation: All metals are found in solid state at normal room temperature except mercury, which is liquid at room temperature.

questions.question: Metal oxides are:
questions.explanation: Metal oxides are basic in nature. When metal oxides react with water, they form bases.

questions.question: Bases turn:
questions.explanation: Bases turns red litmus blue but acids turn blue litmus red. Litmus is extracted from lichens that give different colour with different pH.

questions.question: Iron + oxygen + ......  {tex}\to {/tex} iron oxide
questions.explanation:
Iron + oxygen + water {tex}\to{/tex} iron oxide + hydrogen gas.
when iron react with oxygen and water, it gives a formation of rust and release hydrogen.

questions.question: The metal which can form a barrier layer of oxide on its surface is:
questions.explanation: Aluminium form a barrier layer of oxide on its surface which prevents the further corrosion of aluminium on heating in presence of water.

questions.question: Metal are hard but _____ can be cut with a knife
questions.explanation: Most of the metals are hard but potassium is very soft metal which can be cut with knife. It is stored in kerosene oil to prevent accidental fire.

tests.name: Materials Metals & Non Metals Test 02
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Materials Metals and Non Metals CBSE Online Test 02 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Oxides of non-metals are:
questions.explanation: Oxides of non-metal are acidic in nature. Non-metal oxide like SO2 and NO2, when dissolve in water they form sulphuric acid and nitric acid respectively.

questions.question: Metal oxides react with water to produce:
questions.explanation: Metal oxides react with water to produce hydroxides of metal which are basic in nature.

questions.question: If metallic Zinc is added to copper sulphate solution:
questions.explanation:
Zn (zinc) + CuSO4 (copper sulphate) {tex}\to{/tex} ZnSO4 (zinc sulphate) + Cu (copper) .
Zinc is added to copper sulphate solution, Copper is displaced by Zinc because zinc is more reactive than copper.

questions.question: Gas evolved when Calcium oxide reacts with water is/are:
questions.explanation:
When Calcium oxide reacts with water, hydrogen gas is evolved.
CaO (s) + H2O (l) {tex} \rightleftharpoons {/tex} Ca(OH)2 + H2

questions.question: Metals that react with oxygen include:
questions.explanation: Metals reacts with oxygen to form metal oxide, which are basic in nature. Iron, copper, and aluminium reacts with oxygen to form their respective oxides.

questions.question: Calcium disappears into water forming:
questions.explanation: When calcium hydroxide (lime water) is passed through carbon dioxide gas water turns milky due to formation of calcium carbonate, which is soluble in water.

questions.question: Which one of the following metals catches fire on reaction with air?
questions.explanation: Sodium and potassium both are extremely reactive and react with air as well as water vigorously. The reactions are highly exothermic and hence, the hydrogen gas evolved as a byproduct catches fire.

questions.question: Metal that reacts with steam only is/are:
questions.explanation: Iron and zinc do not reacts with cold or hot water; it reacts with only steam to form metal oxide with evolution of hydrogen gas.

questions.question: Brass is an alloy of:
questions.explanation: Homogenous mixture of two or more metal or non-metal is called alloy. Alloy of zinc and copper is called brass.

questions.question: Zinc reacts with hydrochloric acid to form:
questions.explanation:
Zinc reacts with hydrochloric acid to form zinc chloride with evolution of hydrogen gas.
The reaction between zinc and hydrochloric acid is
Zn + 2HCl  {tex}\to {/tex} ZnCl2 + H2
It is a single replacement reaction where zinc metal displaces the hydrogen to form hydrogen gas and zinc chloride, a salt.

questions.question: When Silver is added to Copper Nitrate solution result is:
questions.explanation: When silver is added to copper nitrate solution, no reaction takes place because silver is less reactive than copper.

questions.question: The most reactive metal is:
questions.explanation: Potassium

questions.question: That property of a metal by virtue of which it can be drawn into wires is:
questions.explanation: The property of a metal by virtue of which metal can be drawn into thin wires is called ductility. Gold is the most ductile metal.

questions.question: The metal alloyed with gold for making jewellery:
questions.explanation: Small quantity of copper is added to gold, used for making jewelleries. Pure gold is very soft and get deformed easily. Small quantity of copper make gold hard.

questions.question: The magnesium oxide reacts with sulphuric acid to form:
questions.explanation:
When magnesium oxide reacts with sulphuric acid, magnesium sulphate, and water is formed.
MgO + 2H2SO4 {tex}\to {/tex} 2MgSO4 + 2H2O

tests.name: Materials Metals & Non Metals Test 03
tests.instructionHtml:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which of the following metal/s is/are not ductile?
questions.explanation: Most of the metals are ductile and malleable but a few metals like arsenic and antimony are not ductile. They cannot be converted into thin wires.

questions.question: Metal used for galvanisation process is:
questions.explanation: Galvanisation is the process of forming a thin coating of other metal to protect it from rusting and enhance their look. Zinc is generally used for galvanisation process.

questions.question: If we add Iron to solution of Zinc Sulphate reaction will form:

options.html: slow reaction will occur to form Iron Sulphate
quick reaction will occur to form Iron Sulphate
there will not be any reaction

questions.explanation: When Iron is added to a solution of Zinc sulphate, there will not be any reaction between them because Zinc is more reactive than Iron.

questions.question: Metal having low melting point is:
questions.explanation: Most of the metals have high melting and boiling points but few metals like Potassium have low melting point and start melting at low temperature.

questions.question: Solder, an alloy used in electrical circuits is made up of:
questions.explanation: Solder is an alloy of lead and tin having a very low melting point. Solder is used in joining electrical circuits.

questions.question: Name of that property of metal by virtue of which it can be hammered into sheets is:
questions.explanation: The property of metal by virtue of which it can be hammered into thin sheets is called malleability. Gold is the most malleable metal followed by silver.

questions.question: Process by which metal can be isolated from its ore is called:
questions.explanation: The process by which metal can be isolated from its ore is called metallurgy. Metallurgy includes enrichment of ore, oxidation of enriched ore, reduction of ore, and refining of the metal.

questions.question: The property of electrical conductivity of metals is due to the presence of:
questions.explanation: Metals are good conductors of electricity due to the presence of free electron in their outermost orbits. Metal contain 1, 2, or 3 electron in their outermost orbit that can be transferred during bond formation.

questions.question: Zinc, copper, and tin constitute which alloy
questions.explanation: Alloy is a homogenous mixture of two or more metal or non-metal. Bronze is an alloy consisting of Zinc, copper, and tin.

questions.question: The metal amphoteric in nature is/are:
questions.explanation: Those metals that form basic as well as acidic oxide when react with oxygen is called amphoteric oxide. Aluminium and Zinc forms amphoteric oxide.

questions.question: Platinum being unreactive is a:
questions.explanation: Platinum is a noble metal as it is un-reactive to oxygen, water, acid, or base. Platinum is used to make jewelleries as it has shining surface and remain unchanged.

questions.question: Metals have tendency to loose electrons and therefore they form:
questions.explanation: Metals contain 1, 2, or 3 electrons in their outermost shell. During bond formation metals loose an electron and form positive ions.

questions.question: Which of the following metal will not react in atmosphere at all?
questions.explanation: Palladium is a less reactive metal present at the bottom of reactivity series. It do not react with air and moisture present in air.

questions.question: To prevent corrosion a metal should be coated with:
questions.explanation: Action of air, water, or acid on metal surface is called corrosion. To prevent corrosion, metal should be coated with a more reactive metal like zinc.

questions.question: Sodium is highly reactive therefore occurs as:
questions.explanation: Sodium is highly reactive metal. Sodium is never found in free state as it combine with other element to form compound.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Bells in temples are made from metals because they are:
questions.explanation: The property of metal to produce unique sound when hit by hard object is called sonority. The bells in temples are made from metals because they are sonorous in nature.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Iron is more reactive than Zinc.
  2. Zinc displaces Iron from aqueous salt solution.

options.html: statementt i) is wrong and statement ii) is correct
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong.

questions.explanation: Zinc is more reactive than iron. Zinc displaces iron from aqueous salt solution as displacement reaction takes place.

questions.question: A more reactive metal will have:

options.html: tendency to remain in free state

questions.explanation: More reactive metal have tendency to loose electron easily to form compound with other non-metals by forming electrovalent bond.

questions.question: A metal contains certain impurities within it. This metal should be:

options.html: if impurity is another metal like antimony then more ductile

questions.explanation: When some impurities is present in metals the ductility of metal decreases. Pure metal is more ductile than impure metals. ductility is the property of metal to convert into wires.

questions.question: A mixture of two or more elements in solid solution with metal as major component is:
questions.explanation: A mixture of two or more elements in solid solution having metal as major component is called alloy. Some common alloys are brass, steel, solder, etc.

questions.question: Copper is more reactive than:
questions.explanation: Copper is more reactive than Silver but less reactive than magnesium, zinc, and iron. Reactivity depends upon ability to loose electrons more easily.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Copper is considered a base metal.
  2. Copper oxidises react easily.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Copper is considered as base metal as it is used in a number of articles. Copper get oxides to copper oxide easily although copper is a less reactive metal.

questions.question: Rust formation is called:
questions.explanation: Formation of rust on metal surface like iron is also called as corrosion. Corrosion occurs in presence of water and oxygen

questions.question: Metal alloys can be:
questions.explanation: Metal alloy can be engineered into a number of form by changing the composition of different constituents and their proportion.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Non-metals combine to form alloys.
  2. Metals do not form alloys.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong.
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong.

questions.explanation: An alloy consists of two or more metal or a metal and non-metal in definite proportions. Two non-metals never form an alloy.

questions.question: Silver and Gold are least reactive metals hence:

options.html: they occur in free state in nature
they occur in combined state in nature
they react with each other only

questions.explanation: Least reactive metals are found in free state in nature. Silver and gold are least reactive metal hence always found in free state in nature.

questions.question: Home electrical systems are mostly wired with:
questions.explanation: Metals are good conductors of electricity and copper is a good conductor as well as cheaper than other metals given in options. it shows malleability as well as ductility.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) Nickel, Lead & Zinc are base metals.
ii) Base metals do not oxidise or corrode easily.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong.
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Nickel, lead and zinc are base metals used in variety of applications. Base metals also get oxidised or corrode but not easily.

questions.question: Zinc is less reactive than
questions.explanation: Zinc is less reactive than Sodium but more reactive than copper, gold, and silver. Zinc is used to galvanized iron articles to prevent corrosion.

questions.question: The metal which can be used in nuclear power plants to produce energy is:
questions.explanation: Radioactive metals are used in nuclear power plants to produce electricity. Plutonium and Uranium is used as fuel to nuclear power plant.

questions.question: These days precious metals are used for:
questions.explanation: Precious metals like gold, silver, and platinum are very costly. The rate of these metals are increasing faster than other things so, these days precious metals are used for investment purpose.

questions.question: Phosphorus immediately oxidises in air, so its ignition temperature is:
questions.explanation: Phosphorous catch fire or get oxidised immediately when come in contact of air. The ignition temperature of phosphorus is lower than room temperature.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) Nitrogen is an inactive liquid non metal.
ii) Fluorine is an extremely reactive gaseous non metal.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Nitrogen is an inactive gaseous non-metal. Nitrogen forms the maximum proportion of air. Fluorine is an extremely reactive gaseous non-metal due to smaller size.

questions.question: Common salt when react with sulphuric acid produces:
questions.explanation:
When common salt reacts with sulphuric acid, hydrochloride gas is produced along with sodium bisulphate.
NaCl + H2SO4 {tex}\to{/tex} NaHSO4 + HCl

questions.question: Displacement reactions are not shown by:
questions.explanation: The reaction in which more reactive element displaces the less reactive metal from its compound is called displacement reaction. Oxygen never shows displacement reaction.

questions.question: Carbonic acid formation involves reaction between:
questions.explanation: The reaction between carbon dioxide and water at high pressure results into formation of carbonic acid. In cold drinks, carbon dioxide is filled at very high pressure to form carbonic acid.

questions.question: Noble metals are different from base metals because:

options.html: they are non resistant to corrosion and oxidation
their economic value is low so not used nobly
they are resistant to corrosion and oxidation
they behave nobly with other elements

questions.explanation: Noble metals are different from base metals because noble metals are resistant to corrosion and oxidation. These metals are lustrous having shiny surface.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Ash of Magnesium forms Magnesium oxide with water.
  2. Magnesium oxide is a basic oxide.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: When Magnesium ribbon is burnt in presence of oxygen, magnesium ash is formed. Magnesium oxide reacts with water to form magnesium hydroxide. Magnesium oxide is basic in nature and form base when reacts with water.

questions.question: High density of most metals is due to:
questions.explanation: High density of most metals is due to tightly packed crystals lattice. Lattice of metals are closely packed due to high electrostatic force of attraction.

questions.question: The molecular formula of Sodium oxide should be:
questions.explanation: Molecular formula of sodium oxide is Na2O. The valency of sodium is one and that of oxygen is 2 that results into formation of compound Na2O.

questions.question: Mercury is used in thermometers due to:
questions.explanation: Mercury is used in thermometer due to its liquid nature and shiny color. It expands easily with an increase in temperature.

questions.question: Formula of sulphurous acid & sulphuric acid are _____ respectively:
questions.explanation: The formula of sulphurous acid is H2SO3 and that of sulphuric acid is H2SO4. Both are mineral acids.

questions.question: Sodium and Potassium can even float on water because:
questions.explanation: Sodium and Potassium floats on water surface due to very low density. The density of sodium and potassium metal is less than that of water.

questions.question: The acidic oxides react with water to form:
questions.explanation: Non-metal oxides react with water to form acids as non-metal oxides are acidic in nature.

questions.question: Metals are used for making wires of musical instruments due to:
questions.explanation: Metals are used for making wires of musical instruments because metals are sonorous in nature and produce specific sound when strike with hard object.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) Non metals are soft.
ii) Diamond is the hardest non metal.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Non metals are generally soft in nature but diamond an allotrope of carbon, which is also a non-metals is the hardest known natural substance.

questions.question: Sodium and Potassium are always kept in kerosene but Magnesium is not because:

options.html: Magnesium hardly reacts with cold water
Potassium hardly reacts with cold water
Sodium hardly reacts with cold water

questions.explanation: The ignition temperature of sodium and potassium is very low and catch fire if come in contact of oxygen. To prevent accidental fire, sodium and potassium is kept immersed under kerosene oil. Magnesium have high ignition temperature.

questions.question: If Chlorine is added to Potassium Iodide solution ____ is formed:
questions.explanation: If chlorine is added to Potassium Iodide solution potassium chloride is formed.

questions.question: ______ of non metals will dissolve in water to form acids.
questions.explanation: Oxides of non-metal dissolve in water to form acids. Oxides of Sulphur form sulphuric acid and that of nitrogen forms nitric acid.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) Metals like Zinc & Nickel take much longer to oxidise.
ii) Palladium & Platinum do not react with atmosphere at all.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Metals like Zinc and Nickel take much longer to get oxidised for corrosion. Palladium and Platinum do not react with atmosphere at all as they are very less reactive elements.

questions.question: At a higher temperature, the metal is:

options.html: same ductile as earlier it was

questions.explanation: The ductility of metal increase with increase in temperature, so metal is more ductile at higher temperature than at room temperature.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Non metals are poor conductor of heat.
  2. Diamond is a non metal and good conductor of heat.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Non-metals are poor conductor of heat and electricity but diamond is a non metal which is good conductor of heat and electricity.

questions.question: Tip of a matchstick has _____ in it:
questions.explanation: The tip of matchstick has phosphorus in it. When matchstick is rubbed on matchbox, it catch fire due to friction.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) Non metals are good conductors of electricity.
ii) Graphite is a non metal and good conductor of electricity.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Non-metals are bad conductor of electricity as free electron is absent in non-metals. Graphite is a non-metal but it is a good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: Tungsten is used in incandescent bulb because tungsten:
questions.explanation: Tungsten is used in incandescent bulb because tungsten has very high melting point and it has very high resistivity. It converts electrical energy into light energy.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:
i) Nitrogen is an inactive liquid non metal.
ii) Fluorine is an extremely reactive gaseous non metal.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Nitrogen is an inactive gaseous non-metal. Nitrogen forms the maximum proportion of air. Fluorine is an extremely reactive gaseous non-metal due to smaller size.

questions.question: Choose the correct ascending order of reactivity:
questions.explanation: The arrangement of common metals in ascending order of reactivity is called reactivity series. Copper is least reactive followed by iron, zinc, and aluminium.

questions.question: The non-metal used in food packaging and canning is:
questions.explanation: Nitrogen gas is very less reactive and prevents oxidation of food. Nitrogen gas is used in food packaging and canning.

questions.question: Which reaction will not release hydrogen gas?
questions.explanation: Magnesium oxide will not release hydrogen gas when react with water. Magnesium hydroxide is formed in this reaction.

questions.question:
Match the following :

non-metal uses
i) phosphorus a) fire cracker
ii) chlorine b) matches
iii) iodine c) lubricant in electric motor
iv) sulphur d) bleaching powder
v) graphite e) antiseptic
questions.explanation: Phosphorous is used in match stick. Chlorine is used in bleaching powder. Iodine is used in antiseptic creams. Sulphur is used in fire cracker and graphite is used as lubricant in electric motor.

questions.question: Select synonymous word to metal in free state:
questions.explanation: Least reactive metals are found in free state in nature, these metals are called as native metals but most of the metals are found in combined states.

questions.question: Desired characteristics can be obtained by

options.html: both making alloys & combining different ratios of metals

questions.explanation: Each metal have unique characteristic. Desired characteristic can be obtained from metal by either making allows or making compounds of metals.

questions.question: Steel is an alloy of:
questions.explanation: Alloys are homogenous mixture of two or more metal or non metal in a fixed ration. Steel is an alloy of iron and carbon.

questions.question: This image shows a liquid non-metal it is :
questions.explanation: Elemental bromine is a fuming red-brown liquid at room temperature Free bromine does not occur in nature, but occurs as colorless soluble crystalline mineral halide salts, analogous to table salts.

questions.question: Two non-metals of greatest significance to life on earth are:
questions.explanation: Water the basic need of life is made up of hydrogen and oxygen. So, hydrogen and oxygen are two non metals which are the gretest significance of life on earth.

questions.question: An element shown in the image is :
questions.explanation: The element shown above is of sulphur, which is a yellow coloured non-metal. It is used as antiseptic and fire crackers.

questions.question: The process of coating a thin layer of Zinc on surface of iron object can be called:
questions.explanation: The process of coating a thin layer of zinc on surface of iron object is called galvanisation. Zinc coating provide high resistant to corrosion.

questions.question: Nowadays base metal is used for making:
questions.explanation: Base metals like silver and copper is used for making coins. These metals have characteristic look and remain same for longer duration.

questions.question: Which non metal is required for proper functioning of thyroid:
questions.explanation: Thyroid gland is important gland that produce thyroxin hormone essential for metabolism of carbohydrates, proteins and fats. Iodine is essential for functioning of thyroid.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) All ores are minerals.
ii) Not all minerals are ores.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong.
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong.

questions.explanation: Minerals are naturally occurring substances obtained from earth crust but ores are those minerals from which metal can be extracted easily and conveniently.

questions.question: It is the best source of Iodine, it is :
questions.explanation: The best source of iodine for our body is iodized salt. Deficiency of iodine in our diet leads to mental retardation.

questions.question: Copper and Zinc mixture forms:
questions.explanation: The homogenous mixture of copper and zinc form an alloy called as brass. Brass is used to make utensils and statues.

questions.question: The element shown in the image is :
questions.explanation: Lead is a chemical element in the carbon group with symbol Pb and atomic number 82. It is a soft, malleable and heavy post-transition metal.

questions.question: Which of the following metals catch fire on reaction with air?
questions.explanation: Sodium and potassium both are extremely reactive and react with air as well as water vigorously. The reactions are highly exothermic and hence, the hydrogen gas evolved as byproduct catches fire.

questions.question: This food is rich source of :
questions.explanation: Spinach is a rich source of iron.

questions.question: Bronze alloy is made with:
questions.explanation: Bronze alloy is made with copper and tin. Alloy is homogenous mixture of two metal or a metal and a non metal.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) Solder is an alloy of Aluminium & Tin.
ii) It has very high melting point.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Solder is an alloy of Lead and Tin. Solder is used to join electrical joints in electrical circuit. Solder have very low melting point.

questions.question: This element is :
questions.explanation: The element shown is mercury. Mercury is the only metals which is found in liquid state.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) Calcium turns water milky.
ii) Calcium is insoluble in water.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide gas turns lime water milky due to formation of calcium carbonate. Calcium is insoluble in water.

questions.question: The fruit shown in the image is a rich source of :
questions.explanation: The fruit shown in picture is strawberry, which is a rich source of manganese. Manganese is a chemical element with symbol Mn and atomic number 25. It is not found as a free state in nature; it is often found in combination with iron, and with many minerals.

questions.question: Find the correct matched pair
questions.explanation: In the process of Electroplating iron with chromium, a thin layer of chromium is deposited on iron. Chromium metal is used because chromium metal is not attacked by the oxygen and water present in air i.e resistant to corrosion and it has shiny appearance.

questions.question: This image shows :
questions.explanation: Phosphorus is a chemical element with symbol P and atomic number 15. As an element, phosphorus exists in two major forms—white phosphorus and red phosphorus—but due to its high reactivity, phosphorus is never found as a free element on Earth.

questions.question: Plutonium is:

options.html: used to make pacemaker of heart

questions.explanation: Plutonium is a rare metal. It is used to make nuclear bomb and nuclear power plant. Plutonium is also used to make pacemaker of heart.

questions.question: This element shown in image is a pale yellow green gas at room temperature :
questions.explanation: The element shown is chlorine, which is a pale yellow green coloured gas at room temperature and in liquid state at low temperature and high pressure.

questions.question: Which reaction is not feasible?
questions.explanation:
Any metal will displace another metal only if it is more reactive than the other metal. The reactivity of different metals is given by the reactivity series. Following is the reactivity series.

As can be seen from above series, iron is less reactive than zinc, because of which it is enabled to displace zinc from ZnSO4.

questions.question: Wrought iron is the:

options.html: waste produced during extraction of iron

questions.explanation: Wrought iron is the pure form of iron. It is converted into steel by adding carbon in it. Wrought iron is used to make agricultural implements.

questions.question: The reaction X + Y {tex}\to{/tex} XY indicates that:
questions.explanation:
The reaction in which two or more substances combine together to form single compound is called combination reaction.
2 Na + Cl2 {tex}\to{/tex} 2NaCl

questions.question: This alloy is :
questions.explanation: This alloy is solder made by mixing lead and tin. Solder is used to join electrical joints in electrical circuit due to low melting point

questions.question: Stainless steel has composition that includes:
questions.explanation: Stainless steel is an alloy of iron, chromium, and nickel. It does not corrode in the presence of air and water. It is used to make surgical instruments and other utensils.

questions.question:
Match the following :
Element………………….property
i) Silicon………………….a) inert gas
ii) Helium…………………b) solid semi metal
iii) Aluminium…………….c) occurs as liquid
iv) Bromine………………d) noble metal
v) Silver………………….e) amphoteric
questions.explanation: Silicon is solid semi metal. Helium is an inert gas (a gas that is not chemically reactive, especially a noble gas). Aluminium is amphoteric ( aluminium oxide is amphoteric because it is able to behave as a base in the presence of an acid and behave as an acid in the presence of an alkali) . Bromine occurs as liquid and silver is a noble metal.

tests.name: Coal and Petroleum Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Coal and Petroleum CBSE Online Test 01 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Coke is obtained from:
questions.explanation: Coke is obtained by heating coal to a high temperature in the absence of oxygen or in limited supply of oxygen. It contains less impurities and high carbon content.

questions.question: Natural gas is a _____ resource:
questions.explanation: Natural gas is a non renewable resource of energy. Natural gas is a kind of fossil fuel that takes millions of years in its formation and cannot be reused again and again.

questions.question: Gaseous mixture containing more volatile components of petroleum is called:
questions.explanation: Natural gas is gaseous mixture containing more volatile components of petroleum. It is used in vehicle as compressed natural gas (CNG).

questions.question: Fossil fuels are:
questions.explanation: Dead remains of plants and animals are called fossils. Fossils used as source of energy are called fossil fuels. Fossil fuels are formed naturally.

questions.question: A thick black liquid formed by heating coal in the absence of air:
questions.explanation: Coal tar is a thick black liquid formed by heating coal in the absence of air. Coal tar is used to construct road and water proofing.

questions.question: Identify the gas that is generally produced when coal is burnt in the presence of a lot of air.
questions.explanation: Coal is mainly composed of carbon. When coal is burnt in the presence of air, carbon reacts with oxygen gas that is present in air (atmosphere) and mainly produces carbon dioxide gas (CO2).

questions.question: Such resources which can be continually replenished are called:
questions.explanation: Natural resources are either exhaustible or inexhaustible. Those resources which can be continually replenished are called inexhaustible resources.

questions.question: What is absent in coal?
questions.explanation: Coal mainly consists of carbon, hydrogen, and oxygen besides sulphur. Phosphorus is absent in coal.

questions.question: Coal type that contains lowest amount of carbon is:
questions.explanation: Carbon content in lignite coal is less than 50%, so, lignite coal is the inferior quality of coal. Anthracite coal is best quality of coal.

questions.question: Conversion of dead trees and other plants into coal is called:
questions.explanation: Conversion of dead trees and other plants into coal inside the earth crust due to high temperature and pressure is called carbonisation.

questions.question: Coke contains approximately _____ carbon.
questions.explanation: Coke is obtained from destructive distillation of coal. Coke contains approximately 98% carbon. It is used in blast furnace.

questions.question: Coal and petroleum can be categorised as:
questions.explanation: Coal and petroleum are nonliving natural resources. They are present in earth crust in limited quantity. Coal and petroleum is non replenishable natural resources.

questions.question: Type of coal that can serve as good heating fuel for domestic purpose is:
questions.explanation: Anthracite coal is used as fuel in domestic purpose for cooking food. It contain about 70% carbon content and provide sufficient heat for cooking.

questions.question: Coal was formed during:
questions.explanation: Coal was formed during carboniferous period. Lots of plants are present on earth that get buried inside the earth crust due to volcanic eruption.

questions.question: Coke is used in the manufacture of ______.
questions.explanation: Coke is a pure form or allotrope of carbon. It is used in the manufacture of steel, as a fuel and as a reducing agent in the extraction of various metals. Steel is an alloy of carbon and iron. Presence of carbon in the steel makes it harder and more durable than pure iron.

tests.instructionHtml:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The slow process of conversion of dead vegetation into coal is called _____.
questions.explanation: Plants contain mainly carbon, which gets converted into coal. Hence, the slow process of conversion of dead vegetation into coal is called carbonisation.

questions.question: That category of coal which has high heat value but very little moisture is:
questions.explanation: Bituminous coal has high calorific value to produce more heat per gram. It contains very less moisture content.

questions.question: The type of coal that can be used to produce coke is:
questions.explanation: Coke is produced by destructive distillation of bituminous coal. Bituminous coal contain very less amount of moisture in it.

questions.question: Coke is used to manufacture:
questions.explanation: Producer gas is mixture of carbon monoxide, hydrogen, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen. Producer gas is manufactured from coke.

questions.question: At the petroleum refinery, crude oil mixture is:
questions.explanation: Crude oil is obtained from earth crust that contains petrol, diesel, kerosene oil, etc. At the petroleum refinery, different types are separated by fractional distillation.

questions.question: Coal contains mainly:
questions.explanation: The constituent of coal is Carbon. Beside carbon; hydrogen, oxygen, and sulphur is also present in coal.

questions.question: Coal is classified according to:

options.html: layer of earth surface it is found

questions.explanation: Different kinds of coal are found in earth crust. They are classified into different categories on the basis of carbon content in it.

questions.question: When heated in air, coal burns to produce:
questions.explanation: Coal when heated in air mainly produces carbon dioxide gas and releases heat.

questions.question: Coal gas can be obtained from:

options.html: coal beds & destructive distillation of coal

questions.explanation: Coal gas can be obtained from destructive distillation of coal and from coal beds present above the coal mines.

questions.question: Coaltar contains about
questions.explanation: 200 substance

questions.question: A pure form of carbon is:
questions.explanation: A pure form of carbon is coke. Coke is obtained from destructive distillation of coal in absence of oxygen.

questions.question: Most inferior quality coal is:
questions.explanation: The quality of coal is determined on the basis of percentage of carbon in the coal and presence of moisture in it. Peat coal contains least amount of carbon with lots of moisture.

questions.question: Coal gas is a mixture of:
questions.explanation: Coal gas is a mixture of hydrogen, methane, and carbon monoxide. It is used as fuel in industrial application.

questions.question: Anthracite contains upto _____ percent carbon:
questions.explanation: Anthracite coal contains upto 92 to 98% carbon. It is a good quality of coal that is used in household fuel.

questions.question: Soft coal is _____ :
questions.explanation: Bituminous is often referred to as soft coal and it contains a soft tar-like substance called bitumen.
