questions.question: Destructive distillation of carbon is carried out in:
questions.explanation: Destructive distillation of coal is carried out in absence of air in close container to remove volatile substance to make coke.

questions.question:
Read the following statements and select the correct ones.

  1. Coal, petroleum and natural gas are fossil fuels.
  2. Coal and natural gas are exhaustible resources.
  3. Coke is used in the manufacture of steel.
  4. Fossil fuels are present in limited quantities.
questions.explanation: Fossil fuels are formed from the carbonisation of organic matter. These resources take a very long time to be formed and are not renewable easily. Hence, fossil fuels are present in limited quantities and are exhaustible. Coal, petroleum, natural gas, etc are examples of fossil fuels. Steel is an alloy of iron and the formation of steel requires carbon (typically in the form of coke).

questions.question: The component of coal which can be used for manufacturing steel is:
questions.explanation: Coke is used for manufacturing steel in a blast furnace. Coke contains lots of carbon so help in the production of high heat and it acts as an excellent reducing agent.

questions.question: Naphthalene balls can be obtained from:
questions.explanation: Naphthalene balls are used as insect repellent. Naphthalene ball is obtained from coal tar that is the residual product of fractional distillation of petroleum.

questions.question: The category of coal which is better known as brown coal is:
questions.explanation: Lignite coal is known as brown coal as this coal is brown in colour due to presence of less quantity of carbon content.

questions.question: An example of fossil fuel is
questions.explanation: Coal

questions.question: Natural gas widely used in automobiles is:
questions.explanation: CNG IS compressed natural gas used as fuel instead of other liquid fuels as it produces less harmful gases as compared to the other fuels.

questions.question: A better alternative to conventional fossil fuel usage as domestic fuel is use of:
questions.explanation: Biogas is a clean and renewable fuel produced by anaerobic respiration of organic matter.

questions.question: The constituent of petroleum used in ointments is:
questions.explanation: Paraffin wax is obtained from petroleum during fractional distillation. Paraffin wax is used in ointments as preservative and additive.

questions.question: Process of separating petroleum constituents is called:
questions.explanation: The process of separating petroleum constituents by fractional distillation is called refining to separate different constituent of petroleum like petrol, kerosene, diesel etc.

questions.question: Wastage and atmospheric pollution occurs if fuel undergoes:
questions.explanation: Incomplete combustion of fuel in automobiles causes wastage of fuel as well as atmospheric pollution by releasing harmful gases like carbon monoxide and particulate matter.

questions.question: Oil deposits are formed in the:

options.html: both porous rock sediments & non porous rock sediments

questions.explanation: Oil deposits are formed in the porous sedimentary rock. They are usually found in river basins or in the sea beds deep inside the earth crust.

questions.question: Coal is non renewable energy source because:

options.html: coal is a fossil fuel so needs death of organisms
coal is readily combustible so burns and is lost quickly
Burning of coal produces harmful gases
coal takes millions of years to get formed

questions.explanation: Coal and petroleum are non renewable source of energy because coal and petroleum takes millions of years to get formed. They cannot reproduce and are limited resources on earth.

questions.question: Coal gas can be used as a reducing agent due to presence of:
questions.explanation: Coal gas is used as reducing agent as well as excellent fuel due to presence of hydrogen atoms in them.

questions.question: Main product on complete combustion of coal is:
questions.explanation: Complete combustion of coal takes place insufficient presence of oxygen. The main product of complete combustion of coal is carbon dioxide gas along with other gases.

questions.question: Natural gas consists mainly of methane and it is a:
questions.explanation: Natural gas consists of 85–95% methane. Natural gas is considered as excellent fuel as it burns completely releasing carbon dioxide gas. Natural gas has also been promoted as a cleaner fuel than diesel, and it is replaced the fuel in many trucks and buses on city streets.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Anthracite and Paraffin both are purest forms of coal.
  2. Peat is most inferior quality coal.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Anthracite is the purest form of coal but paraffin is petroleum product. Peat coal is most inferior quality of coal as it contains less amount of carbon in it.

questions.question: Plants are the most important living natural resources because:

options.html: They hold water and provide shade to other organisms
They absorb carbon dioxide from atmosphere
They absorb minerals from soil which other organisms cannot
They make food for all other organisms

questions.explanation: Plants are most important natural resource; they make food for all other organism. Plants covert carbon dioxide gas into oxygen gas during photosynthesis process.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Animals & plants are living natural resources.
  2. Coal is formed from the living natural resources.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Animals and plants are living natural resources as we obtain different product from them. Coal is formed from dead plants due to carbonisation process in long course of time.

questions.question: Non living natural resources can be obtained from:

options.html: Atmospheric gases, Solid parts of earth and Water bodies

questions.explanation: Non living natural resources can be obtained from solid parts of earth in form of coal an metals, water bodies in form of common salt, and from atmospheric gases as oxygen.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) Renewable resources are those which will never run out.
ii) Those resources which are replaced within a reasonable period of time through natural processes are renewable.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Renewable resources are those which will never run out in due course of time as water and air. Those resources which are replaced within a reasonable period of time through natural process are renewable resources.

questions.question: Most renewable resources are exhaustible if:

options.html: both we disturb the natural method of their renewal & we use then faster than they are renewed
we disturb the natural method of their renewal.
we use them slower than their expiry period.
we use then faster than they are renewed

questions.explanation: Most renewable resources are exhaustible if we disturb the natural method of their renewal and we use them faster than they are renewed due to increased population.

questions.question: The non renewable resource that can be easily recycled is/are:
questions.explanation: Metals are non renewable natural resource that can be easily recycled by melting and recasting into desired shape. Natural gas, petroleum, coal cannot be recycled.

questions.question: Coke is useful in the production of:
questions.explanation: Coke is useful in the production of water gas, producer gas, and poly vinyl chloride (PVC). It is an excellent fuel due to high content of carbon in it.

questions.question: We need to conserve only:
questions.explanation: We need to conserve both renewable resources and non renewable resources as they are used at tremendous rate due to increase in population.

questions.question: Coal contains mainly
questions.explanation: The constituent of coal is Carbon. Beside carbon hydrogen, oxygen and sulphur is also present in coal.

questions.question: A faster depletion of natural resources has lead us to:
questions.explanation: A faster depletion of natural resources has leads us to imbalance in nature. Due to increased population and changed lifestyle we are using natural resource faster than ever.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Conservation is careless use of resources.
  2. We will endanger our survival if we practice conservation.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Conservation is the careful use of natural resources. We will endanger our survival if we do not practice conservation of our resources.

questions.question: The youngest form of coal is:
questions.explanation: The youngest form of coal is lignite as it formed the first. It is soft and ranges from black to brown in colour.

questions.question: When a fuel is burnt a large part of their _____ is released as heat:
questions.explanation: Burning of fuels results in the production of carbondioxide. The potential energy is converted to heat energy as energy in the universe remains constant.

  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Conservation is maintaining the quality of resources.
  2. Conservation is preventing wastage of resources.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Conservation of resources is the judicious use of resources and to preserve it for future generations without damaging the environment.

questions.question: The energy of fossil fuels is derived:
questions.explanation: Fossil fuels are obtained from the dead remains of plants and animals. These organisms depend on sunlight directly or indirectly for their food. Therefore energy of fossil fuels is derived indirectly from the sun.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Fuel is a substance which burns to give off heat.
  2. Fuel can be used to produce heat at an unreasonable cost.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Fuel produces heat on burning. A good fuel has low ignition temperature and high calorific value. Fuel produces heat at a reasonable rate.

questions.question: Coke is better fuel than coal because:

options.html: it is used for domestic cooking

questions.explanation: Destructive distillation of coal is carried out in the absence of air to remove volatile substance from it to form coke. Coke produces more heat but less smoke on combustion.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Destructive distillation of coal is carried out in the presence of air.
  2. Destructive distillation of coal is carried out in the absence of air.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Destructive distillation of coal is carried out in the absence of air to remove volatile substance from it to form coke and other products.

questions.question:
Coke is used in the manufacture of:

  1. producer gas by passing air through red hot coke
  2. water gas by passing steam over white hot coke

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Producer gas is produced by passing hot air through red hot coke. Producer gas is used as fuel in industry. Water gas is produced by passing steam over white hot coke.

questions.question: This product of distillation was earlier used for domestic cooking and lighting but not much used now, it is?
questions.explanation: Coal gas is used for domestic cooking and lighting in earlier time. Coal gas is obtained from destructive distillation of coal in absence of air.

questions.question: Crude oil is obtained by drilling through earth and:

options.html: both non porous rocks & impervious rocks

questions.explanation: Crude oil is obtained by drilling through earth and non porous rocks and impervious rocks in river basin or under the sea surface.

questions.question: Petroleum is a mixture of hydrocarbons and boiling point of hydrocarbon increases with:

options.html: decrease in the number of hydrogen atoms
increase in the number of carbon atoms
increase in the number of hydrogen atoms
decrease in the number of carbon atoms

questions.explanation: Petroleum is a mixture of hydrocarbons. As the chain length (numbers of carbons) increases the melting and boiling points gradually increase for these compounds. So, boiling point of Petroleum increases with increase in the number of carbon atoms.

questions.question: The components of petroleum which are separated during refining can be called:
questions.explanation: The different components of petroleum which are separated during refining of crude oil is called fractions as petrol, diesel, kerosene oil, gasoline etc.

questions.question: Choose the correct statement from the following:

options.html: Natural gas is stored under high pressure as compressed natural gas.
The disadvantage of natural gas is that it can not be used directly for burning in homes.
Natural gas can not be used for power generation.
It is difficult to transport natural gas through pipes.

questions.explanation: Natural gas is stored under high pressure as compressed natural gas.

questions.question: _____ is the household coal.
questions.explanation: Bituminous coal is called as household coal as this coal is used for domestic purpose in cooking and heating.

questions.question: All are natural sources of energy except:
questions.explanation: Solar energy, wind energy are natural sources of energy. Electrical energy is produced in thermal power plant or hydropower plant and it is not a natural source of energy.

questions.question: We can prevent energy crisis by using:
questions.explanation: Energy crisis can be prevented by using energy judiciously and looking for alternative sources of energy like solar energy, wind energy etc.

questions.question: The ultimate source of fossil fuels is:
questions.explanation: The sun is the ultimate source of energy present in fossil fuels. Fossils fuels are formed from plants and animals that utilise solar energy for growth and development by photosynthesis.

tests.name: Coal and Petroleum Test 06
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Coal and Petroleum CBSE Online Test 06 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.

  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Coal is produced inside the earth millions of year ago by:

options.html: aerobic thermal degradation of cellulosic material
anaerobic thermal degradation of cellulosic material

questions.explanation: Coal is produced inside the earth millions of years ago by anaerobic thermal degradation of cellulosic material present in plant due to high pressure and temperature.

questions.question: The destructive distillation of coal gives many useful products in the:
questions.explanation: The destructive distillation of coal gives many useful products in the absence of oxygen like coke, coal gas, and coal tar used for different purposes.

questions.question: CO is produced on:
questions.explanation: Incomplete combustion of coal takes place in absence or insufficient supply of oxygen. Carbon monoxide gas is produced on incomplete combustion in place of carbon dioxide gas.

questions.question: Peat is most inferior quality or variety of coal because:
questions.explanation: Peat coal is most inferior quality or variety of coal because peat coal contains minimum percentage of carbon in comparison to other coals

questions.question: During fractional distillation of crude oil the hydrocarbons that condense first are those with:

options.html: lightest in weight or least dense

questions.explanation: During fractional distillation of crude oil, the hydrocarbons that condense first are those with highest boiling point and get separated from column.

questions.question: Petroleum is a mixture of:
questions.explanation: Petroleum is a mixture of different hydrocarbons starting from methane (CH4) to higher hydrocarbons. Compounds of carbon and hydrogen are called as hydrocarbon.

questions.question: Coal is regarded as backbone of our national economy because:

options.html: supports our agriculture and crop production as manure
supports industries by providing raw material in its different forms
indirectly supports many industries and thermal power plants
is exported on large scale since our country has maximum reserves

questions.explanation: Coal is regarded as backbone of our national economy because coal indirectly supports many industries and thermal power plants to obtain energy.

questions.question: The given image shows :
questions.explanation: The given image shows a type of coal called anthracite. It is a medium category coal having about 70% carbon with moisture.

questions.question: The image shows a cheaper fuel with high calorific value it is :
questions.explanation: Petroleum coke is a cheaper fuel with high calorific value. It is obtained as by product in the fractional distillation of crude oil.

questions.question: Lead added to petrol engines works as:
questions.explanation: Lead is added to petrol engines as lead works as anti knock agent. It is used to engine knocking and increase the fuels octane rating.

questions.question: The image shows a common binder in road construction it is :
questions.explanation: Bitumen is used as common binder in road construction. It is also called as coal tar obtained from destructive distillation of coal to obtain coke.

questions.question: The following image shows :
questions.explanation: Paraffin wax is a white or colorless soft solid derivable from petroleum, coal or oil shale, that consists of a mixture of hydrocarbon molecules containing between twenty and forty carbon atoms

questions.question: _______ becomes coal when it is been buried long and deep enough.
questions.explanation: peat is an accumulation of partially decayed vegetation or organic matter that is unique to natural areas. Peat becomes coal when it is been buried long and deep enough.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Diesel is also known as gasoline.
  2. Diesel is used as a solvent for dry cleaning.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Finest quality of petrol is called as gasoline. It is used as fuel in aeroplane. Petrol is used as a solvent for dry cleaning of clothes in laundry.

questions.question:
Match the following:

i) Petroleum gas a) cattle dung
ii) Coke b) domestic fuel
iii) Coal gas c) conversion of wood to carbon
iv) Biogas d) used in metal extraction
v) Carbonisation e) an industrial fuel
questions.explanation: Petroleum gas is used as domestic fuel in form of LPG. Coke is used in metal extraction. Coal gas is used as industrial fuel. Biogas is obtained from cattle dung and carbonisation is the conversion of wood to carbon (coal).

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Coal and Petroleum CBSE Online Test 07 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Discovery and development of oil and gas resources which lie underwater is called:
questions.explanation: Discovery and development of oil and gas resources which lie underwater are called offshore drilling. Bombay high in Mumbai is one of such oil extraction centre.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Asphalt is not used for making roads.
  2. Paraffin wax is used to make shoe polish.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Coal tar is used for constructing road. It is a black thick fluid that is used as binder of the road. Paraffin wax is used to make shoe polish. They are obtain as by product in refining of petroleum.

questions.question: The vehicle shown in image is fitted with cylinder of :
questions.explanation: The vehicle shown in the image is fitted with cylinder of compressed natural gas. CNG is an excellent fuel as it does not produce harmful gases and particulate matters.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Kerosene is used as an illuminant.
  2. Carbon black is used for production of petroleum gas.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Kerosene is used as an illuminant in remote villages, where electricity is not available. Carbon black is used for production of polish and filler in tyres industry.

questions.question: Coke is used for:
questions.explanation: Coke is used for extraction of metals as it acts as excellent reducing agent as well as heat giving substance. It is obtained from coal.

questions.question:
CNG is a smokeless and environment friendly fuel:

  1. On burning CNG produces carbon dioxide and water.
  2. On burning CNG produces carbon dioxide, hydrogen, and ash.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: CNG is a smokeless and environmental friendly fuel as on combustion, CNG produces carbon dioxide and water along with large amount of energy to run vehicles.

questions.question: LPG finds its use as:
questions.explanation: LPG is also used as refrigerant in cooling devices such as air conditioner and refrigerator due to low boiling point.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Natural gas can be used for manufacturing carbon black.
  2. Carbon black is used in rubber industry as strengthening agent.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Natural gas is also used for manufacturing carbon black. Carbon black is used in rubber industry as strengthening agent. It is also used in ink and paint industry.

questions.question: Petroleum is also known as:
questions.explanation: Petroleum is also known as black gold as it is an important resource for industry, household, and all other aspects of like. It is also sold at a high price.

questions.question: Natural gas can be used for obtaining _____ on industrial scale:
questions.explanation: Natural gas can be used for obtaining hydrogen gas on industrial scale. Hydrogen is used in hydrogenation process as well as in manufacture of ammonia as fertiliser.

questions.question: Nowadays, addition of lead tetraethyl to petrol engines is banned because:

options.html: lead works as anti knock agent
lead works as stimulating agent for engine

questions.explanation: Lead was used in petrol as anti knocking agent. Nowadays, addition of lead tetraethyl to petrol engines is banned due to toxic nature of lead.

questions.question: The image is showing pellets used in rubber manufacture and is also used as a pigment, it is :
questions.explanation: Carbon black pellets is used in rubber manufacture and as pigment in ink and dye industry. It contain large percentage of carbon.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Petroleum fractions include fuel as well as non fuel products.
  2. Crude oil itself can be used for many useful purposes.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Petroleum fractions like petrol, kerosene, and diesel includes fuel as well as non fuel products like paraffin and coal, wax, etc. Crude oil itself cannot be used for any purpose without refining.

questions.question: Excessive use of coal & petroleum may lead to:
questions.explanation: Excessive use of coal and petroleum causes green house effect, acid rain, and energy crisis in future. Burning of fossil fuels release carbon dioxide gas. Carbon dioxide gas causes green house effect.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Petroleum occurs above natural gas on earth. 
  2. Natural gas consists mainly of butane.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Natural gas occurs above petroleum well in earth crust. Natural gas mainly consists of methane gas. LPG contains butane as it's main constituent.

General Instructions:

questions.question: In petroleum well, crude oil is found:
questions.explanation: In petroleum well, crude oil is found above the water surface as petroleum is lighter than water due to less density.

questions.question: That gas produced by burning of fossil fuels which is itself highly poisonous:
questions.explanation: Incomplete burning of fossil fuels produces carbon monoxide gas in place of carbon dioxide. Carbon monoxide gas is a poisonous gas that bind with haemoglobin to reduce oxygen carrying capacity.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Coke is a by product formed during processing of coal to form coal gas.
  2. Coal gas is used as a fuel in the coal processing plant.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Coke is produced by destructive distillation of coal in absence of oxygen along with coal gas as by-product. Coal gas is used as fuel in the coal processing plant.

questions.question: Naphthalene balls used to repel insects and moths are obtained from:
questions.explanation: Naphthalene balls used to repel insects and moths from cloths are obtained from coal tar. Coal tar is obtained as by product of fractional distillation of petroleum.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Coal is a mineral of plant origin.
  2. Process of conversion of dead vegetation into coal is called condensation.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Coal is formed by carbonisation of dead plants due to the high temperature and pressure of earth crust. The process of conversion of dead vegetation into coal is called carbonisation.

questions.question: Process of heating coal in the absence of oxygen is called:
questions.explanation: The process of heating coal in the absence of oxygen is called destructive distillation of coal. Coke and coal gas are obtained from destructive distillation of coal.

questions.question: Fraction of petroleum used for surface roads is:
questions.explanation: Bitumen is used for construction of surface roads. Bitumen is obtained as residual product of the fractional distillation of petroleum.

questions.question: Nowadays, a petroleum product is used for medalling roads in place of previously used material, they are:
questions.explanation: Bitumen and coal tar is used for medalling roads in place of previously used materials. These materials make roads more water resistant and durable.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Natural gas is easy to be transported through pipes.
  2. It occurs below the surface of petroleum deposits.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Natural gas is easy to be transported long distance through pipes. Natural gas is found alone or above the surface of petroleum deposits.

questions.question: Natural gas contains only traces of:
questions.explanation: The main constituent of natural gas is methane, only traces of ethane and propane is present in natural gas. Natural gas is used as excellent fuel for vehicles.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :
i) Bituminous coal is used to produce coke.
ii) It contains a lot of moisture.

options.html: statement i) is wrong but ii) is correct
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Bituminous coal is used to produce coke as this kind of coal contains lots of carbon. Bituminous coal contain very small amount of moisture.

questions.question: Natural gas is formed by decomposition of vegetation by:
questions.explanation: Natural gas is formed by decomposition of vegetation by anaerobic bacteria in absence of oxygen. During this anaerobic decomposition methane gas is produced.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Crude oil is free of impurities like salt, rock particles, and water.
  2. Crude oil is stored in big containers called oil tanks.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Crude oil or petroleum contains a number of impurities like salt, rock particles, and water. Petrol obtained by fractional distillation of petroleum is stored in containers called tanks.

questions.question: To save petrol/diesel while driving we must:

options.html: drive for a longer period of time
drive at a very fast speed
drive at a very slow speed

questions.explanation: To save petrol/diesel while driving, we must drive at a moderate speed and switch off the engine at red lights. Petroleum products are limited in quantity and takes millions of years for formation.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Car pool is useful to save natural resources.
  2. Regular maintenance of vehicles helps to save fuel.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Car pool is a useful way to save natural resources and reduce pollution level. Regular maintenance of vehicles also helps to save fuel.

questions.question: Gas necessary for combustion is:
questions.explanation: Combustion takes place only in presence of oxygen. Oxygen supports the combustion of substances in which heat is evolved.

questions.question: Combustion is a _____ chemical reaction:
questions.explanation: Burning of any substance in presence of oxygen is called combustion. During combustion heat is evolved so, combustion is exothermic chemical reaction.

questions.question: Substance that burns in oxygen to produce heat and light is called:
questions.explanation: Those substances that burn in the presence of oxygen to produce heat and light is called a combustible substance. Fossil fuels, wood, paper etc are examples of a combustible substances.

questions.question: Substance that does not burn in air is:
questions.explanation: The substance that does not burn in air is called non combustible substance. Wood, kerosene, and diesel is example of combustible substance but sand is non combustible.

questions.question: Spontaneous combustion is a:

options.html: both fast process and used in internal combustion engines

questions.explanation: A combustion in which a material suddenly bursts into flames, without the application of any apparent cause called spontaneous combustion. Spontaneous combustion is a fast process and complete in a few seconds.

questions.question: Cutting trees at large scale leads to:
questions.explanation: Cutting of trees at large scale leads to deforestation. Deforestation may cause desertification and loss of living natural resources.

questions.question: Amount of heat energy produced on complete combustion of 1kg of fuel is called:
questions.explanation: The amount of heat energy released on complete combustion of 1 kg of fuel is called calorific value of that fuel. Higher the calorific value better the fuel is considered.

questions.question: Substance which vaporises during burning forms
questions.explanation: Substance which get vaporise during combustion or burning forms flame. Each substance forms characteristic flame having different colour and heat region.

questions.question: Combustion is also called:
questions.explanation: Combustion is also called as burning. Burning of substance requires oxygen and combustible substance and ignition temperature.

questions.question: Lowest temperature at which a substrate catches fire is called its:
questions.explanation: The lowest temperature at which substance catch fire is called ignition temperature. The fuel with moderate temperature is considered better fuel for domestic use.

questions.question: A combustible substance is :-
questions.explanation: Those substances that start burning at ignition temperature in presence of oxygen is called combustible substance. Water, glass, and sand are non combustible substance while wax is a combustible substance.

questions.question: Water works as a fire extinguisher by removing :-
questions.explanation: Water works as a fire extinguisher because it removes oxygen and lower down the temperature below the ignition temperature.

questions.question: We can extinguish fire by:-

options.html: removing non combustible substances from surroundings
both cutting the supply of oxygen & lowering temperature below ignition temperature

questions.explanation: Fire can be extinguished by cutting the supply of oxygen and removing combustible substances from surrounding or lowering the temperature below the ignition temperature.

questions.question: Non combustible substances do not burn in:
questions.explanation: Non combustible substance does not burn in air and oxygen . Carbon is generally absent in non-combustible substance or present in very less quantity.

questions.question: The gas which supports combustion of coal is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen gas supports the combustion of combustible substances including coal. Combustion of coal release carbon dioxide gas as well as solid residue.

tests.name: Combustion and Flame Test 02
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Combustion and Flame CBSE Online Test 02 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: If methane is burnt insufficient oxygen the products formed are:
questions.explanation: If methane is burnt insufficient oxygen, the products formed are carbon dioxide and water. CNG and biogas contain mainly methane gas.

questions.question: Many harmful gases are produced during:
questions.explanation: Incomplete combustion of fuels release harmful gases like carbon monoxide. Carbon monoxide binds with haemoglobin and reduces the oxygen carrying capacity of blood.

questions.question: SI unit of the calorific value of a fuel is:-
questions.explanation: The amount of heat release when one kilogram of fuel is burnt completely is called calorific value. The SI unit of calorific value is joule per kilogram (J/kg).

questions.question: A material that burns is one that can combine quickly with:
questions.explanation: Those materials that combine with oxygen quickly burns faster. Oxygen help in combustion of substance by oxidising the carbon substance into carbon dioxide gas.

questions.question: Middle zone of candle flame is also known as zone of:
questions.explanation: The middle zone of candle flame is known as zone of partial combustion. This zone is generally yellow in colour and has less heat.

questions.question: Burning of a matchstick is an example of:
questions.explanation: Burning of a matchstick is an example of rapid combustion. Matchstick contains phosphorus that catches fire due to heat produce by friction.

questions.question: Fire caused by burning liquid fuels can be controlled by throwing:

options.html: both soil over it & sand over it

questions.explanation: Fire caused by burning of liquid fuels can be controlled by throwing soil and sand over it to avoid the contact of oxygen that supports the combustion.

questions.question: An ideal fuel is cheap, readily available, easily combustible, and easy to transport. It has high calorific value. It does not produce gases or residues that pollute the environment. Based on the above statements which of the following is closest to being an ideal fuel?
questions.explanation: There is probably no fuel that could be considered as an ideal fuel. We should look for a fuel which fulfills most of the requirements for a particular use. Compressed Natural Gas (CNG) contains methane which doesn’t produce a large amount of toxic gases or residue that pollute the environment. It is also easily combustible. Of the given options it is closest to being an ideal fuel.

questions.question: Burning of LPG is an example of:
questions.explanation: Burning of liquefied petroleum gas (LPG) is an example of rapid combustion. Rapid combustion starts very fast and lot of heat and light energy is produced.

questions.question: Some substances burn on their own when kept exposed in air for some time for example:
questions.explanation: Some substances burn on their own without getting heat from surrounding when kept exposed in air for some time because their ignition temperature is very low. Potassium and sodium catch fire when come in contact of oxygen.

questions.question: If a glass plate/slide is held into the luminous part of candle flame we see a circular blackish ring that represents:

options.html: Both burnt carbon particles & formation of coal

questions.explanation: When a glass plate or slide is held into the luminous part of candle flame we see a circular blackish ring that represents unburnt carbon particles.

questions.question: The colour of the hottest part of the flame is:
questions.explanation: Different zone is formed in the flame having different colour. The hottest part of the flame is blue in colour present on the upper part of flame.

questions.question: At its ignition temperature a combustible material:
questions.explanation: Combustible substance changes into vapour at its ignition temperature and starts burning to give heat and light.

questions.question: Sodium can catch fire without any:

options.html: both external heat sup & external oxygen supply

questions.explanation: Sodium can catch fire without any external heat supply when come in contact with oxygen. Sodium is kept under kerosene oil to prevent accidental fire and protecting the sodium metal.

questions.question: It is dangerous to extinguish fire caused by an electric short circuit with the use of:
questions.explanation: Fire caused by an electric short circuit should not be extinguished by using water because water is good conductor of electricity and may cause electric shock.

tests.name: Combustion and Flame Test 03
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Combustion and Flame CBSE Online Test 03 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Acid rain is occurs due to :-

options.html: both oxides of nitrogen & oxides of sulphur

questions.explanation: Acid rain occurs due to oxide of sulphur and oxide of nitrogen. Oxide of sulphur combine with water to form sulphuric acid and oxide of nitrogen form nitric acid with water.

questions.question: Low ignition temperature is the cause for:
questions.explanation: The temperature at which substance start burning is called ignition temperature. Low ignition temperature may cause fire hazards.

questions.question: Most common fire extinguisher is:
questions.explanation: Water is most common fire extinguisher as it cut the oxygen supply and lowers the temperature. Water is not used as fire extinguisher in case of electric fire. Soil and sand is also used as fire extinguisher.

questions.question:
Find the odd one out:

  • oxygen fire extinguisher
  • soda acid fire extinguisher
  • foam fire extinguisher
  • carbon dioxide fire extinguisher
questions.explanation: There is a different kind of fire extinguisher like soda acid fire extinguisher, foam fire extinguisher, and carbon dioxide fire extinguisher. Oxygen is not used as a fire extinguisher because it is one of the component of combustion only.

questions.question: When supply of air is insufficient burning of methane will produce:
questions.explanation: When supply of air is insufficient burning of methane will produce water and carbon monoxide. In presence of sufficient supply of air carbon dioxide is produced.

questions.question: Combustion of a substance releases heat and ______.
questions.explanation: Combustion or burning is an exothermic chemical reaction (in the presence of oxygen) that releases a significant amount of heat and light. Light is either in the form of a glow or a flame. Not all substances have a flame when burned. Flame depends on whether or not combustible vapours are released by the fuel. An example of combustion without a flame is the burning of coal. An example of combustion with a flame is a burning candle.

questions.question: All flames are:

options.html: broader at base and pointed at tip
broader at base and pointed in middle
broader in middle and pointed at base
broader at tip and pointed at base

questions.explanation: All flames are broader at base and pointed at tip. Flame is formed with combustible substance change into vapour and burns with flame.

questions.question: A soda acid fire extinguisher contains mixture of:

options.html: sodium bi carbonate and nitric acid
sodium bi carbonate and hydrochloric acid
sodium bi carbonate and sulphuric acid

questions.explanation: A soda acid fire extinguisher contains mixture of sodium bicarbonate and sulphuric acid. Reaction between sodium bicarbonate and sulphuric acid produces carbon dioxide gas that extinguishes fire.

questions.question: Ignition temperature is the lowest temperature at which a substance catches fire. Identify the correct option regarding the ignition temperature of a good fuel.

options.html: Ignition temperature equal to room temperature

questions.explanation: Ignition temperature is the lowest temperature at which a substance catches fire. We know, if the temperature of the fuel will be lower than the room temperature, then it can catch fire very easily which will be hazardous. So, for an ideal or a good fuel, the ignition temperature should be higher than room temperature.

questions.question: Combustion can be defined as a :-
questions.explanation: The process of burning of any substance in presence of oxygen is called combustion. Combustion is a chemical change as in combustion new substance is formed that cannot be reversed.

questions.question: The picture below shows two cases in which a person is trying to burn the paper cup. In case 1, the cup has water in it, and in case 2, it is empty and dry. Identify in which of the cases the paper will burn.

options.html: The paper doesn’t burn in both the cases.

questions.explanation: In case 2, the empty paper cup reaches its ignition temperature and catches fire. In case 1, the heat supplied to the paper cup is transferred to water in it. So, in the presence of water, the ignition temperature of the paper is not reached. So, the paper would not burn in case 1.

questions.question: A better fuel is one that leaves:
questions.explanation: A better fuel is one that leaves very little residue and does not produce harmful gases like carbon monoxide.

questions.question: Combustion of a fuel will remain incomplete in the absence of sufficient:
questions.explanation: The combustion of a fuel remains incomplete in the absence of sufficient oxygen. Incomplete combustion results into formation of carbon monoxide gas.

questions.question:
Choose the correct statement about inflammable substances from the following.
They have:

options.html: High ignition temperature and cannot catch fire easily.
Low ignition temperature and can catch fire easily.
Low ignition temperature and cannot catch fire easily.
High ignition temperature and can catch fire easily.

questions.explanation: Inflammable substances have low ignition temperature and can catch fire easily.

questions.question: From middle region of flame unburnt carbon particles arise due to:
questions.explanation: From middle region of flame, unburnt carbon particles arise due to low supply of air in this region.

tests.name: Combustion and Flame Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Combustion and Flame CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Ignition temperature of which of the following will be highest?
questions.explanation: The temperature at which a substance starts burning is called ignition temperature. A wet paper cup will have highest ignition temperature and catch fire at higher temperature.

questions.question: Combustion of coal releases particulate matter that may lead to:
questions.explanation: Combustion of coal releases particulate matter that may lead to respiratory diseases like asthma. Particulate matter inhaled through nostrils leads to asthma.

questions.question: The innermost zone of flame is least hot due to:
questions.explanation: The innermost zone of flame is least hot due to presence of unburnt carbon particles in them. Incomplete combustion produces less heat.

questions.question: If we want to fight fire we should do:-

options.html: we should cool down combustible substance below ignition temperature
we should cut off the supply of oxygen

questions.explanation: If we want to fight fire we should remove combustible substance. We should cool down temperature below ignition temperature and cut off the supply of oxygen.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Combustion of most fuels is believed to cause global warming.
  2. All the available fuels are ideal fuels.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Combustion of fossil fuels produces carbon dioxide gas. Carbon dioxide causes green house effect that leads to global warming. An ideal fuel should have low ignition temperature, easily available, and easy to store.

questions.question: A substance that has relatively low ignition temperature exhibits:
questions.explanation: Spontaneous combustion or spontaneous ignition is a type of combustion which occurs by self-heating (increase in temperature due to exothermic internal reactions), followed by thermal runaway (self heating which rapidly accelerates to high temperatures) and finally, autoignition.

questions.question: Burning of coal and diesel releases
questions.explanation: Burning of coal, petrol and diesel produces sulphur dioxide which goes into the air: It is an extremely suffocating and corrosive gas. It may damage our lungs.

questions.question: A paper cup containing water in it does not burn because

options.html: paper cup loses heat by combustion
paper cup loses heat by induction
paper cup is made of non metal
paper cup loses heat by conduction

questions.explanation: A paper cup containing water in it does not burn because paper cup loses heat by conduction and remain below ignition temperature.

questions.question: A carbon dioxide fire extinguisher cylinder is filled with:
questions.explanation: A carbon dioxide fire extinguisher cylinder is filled with liquid carbon dioxide at very high pressure and low temperature. The liquid gas changes into gas and extinguish the fire.

questions.question: During incomplete combustion, fuel gives out:
questions.explanation: During incomplete combustion, fuel gives out yellow flame with lots of smoke. The smoke contains mixture of carbon dioxide and carbon monoxide gas with particulate matter.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Substances having high ignition temperature take more time to burn while substance having low ignition temperature takes less time to burn.
  2. Substances having low ignition temperature take more time to burn while substance having high ignition temperature takes less time to burn.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: The substances having high ignition temperature take more time to burn while substance having low ignition temperature takes time to burn. Ideal fuel should have moderate ignition temperature.

questions.question: No external heat is required to start:
questions.explanation: Spontaneous combustion occurs itself with external heat. Spontaneous combustion occurs in those substances that have very low ignition temperature, lower than room temperature.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. White phosphorus ignites spontaneously at about 35{tex}^\circ {\text{C}}{/tex}.
  2. White phosphorus is never stored underwater.

options.html: Statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
Statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation:
1. White phosphorus is extremely flammable. Phosphorus will spontaneously ignite if exposed to air. Phosphorus ignites at approximately 86°F (30°C) in air; the ignition temperature is higher when the air is dry.
  2. To avoid accidental fire phosphorous is stored under water. This statement should be wrong. White phosphorus should be kept under water, as it is dangerously reactive in air, and it should be handled with forceps, as contact with the skin may cause severe burns.

questions.question: Explosion is a result of:

options.html: release of a large volume of gas
both rapid combustion & release of a large volume of gas

questions.explanation: Explosion is a result of rapid combustion and release of large volume of gas and energy takes place in short interval of time.

questions.question: It is due to complete combustion, that fuel burns with a:
questions.explanation: When complete combustion of fuel takes place, fuel burns with a blue flame without smoke. Lots of heat is produced during compete combustion.

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. The ignition temperature for dry paper is low as compared to wet paper.
  2. The ignition temperature for wet paper is low as compared to dry paper.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: The temperature at which a combustible substance starts burning is called ignition temperature. The ignition temperature for dry paper is low as compared to wet paper.

questions.question: Combustion of fuels is done to obtain:
questions.explanation: Fuel is burnt to obtain energy. Energy is used to drive a number of processes like running car, producing electricity, cooking food etc.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Substance doesn't catch fire until its temperature rises to ignition temperature.
  2. The ignition temperature of different substance does not vary according to the constituent and state.

options.html: Statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
Statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation:
1. The lowest temperature at which a substance catches fire is called its ignition temperature. A combustible substance cannot catch fire or burn as long as its temperature is lower than its ignition temperature.
  2. A combustible substance cannot catch fire or burn as long as its temperature is lower than its ignition temperature. The ignition temperature may vary from substance to substance.

questions.question: Temperature of water inside paper cup held above candle flame:
questions.explanation: The temperature of water inside paper cup held above candle flame rise due to heat transfer. But paper does not attain ignition temperature until all the water get vaporised.

questions.question:
Carbon dioxide released from extinguisher:

  1. is denser than air
  2. is lighter than air
  3. concentrates oxygen in vicinity
  4. dilutes oxygen in vicinity
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide gas is used to extinguish fire because carbon dioxide gas is denser than air and dilute the oxygen gas present in vicinity. Combustion do not takes place in absence of oxygen.

questions.question: Some combustion reactions occur suddenly with evolution of large amount of gas. Such a combustion is:
questions.explanation: Some combustion reactions occur suddenly with evolution of large amount of gas and energy in form of heat and light. Such sudden combustion is called explosion.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Substances that burn with flame are first converted into vapour.
  2. Wax first melts and changes into vapour before producing flame.

options.html: Statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
Statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: The substances which vapourise during burning, give flames. For example, kerosene oil and molten wax rise through the wick and are vapourised during burning and form flames. Charcoal, on the other hand, does not vapourise and so does not produce a flame.

questions.question: In the following cases fire can be extinguished by removing combustible substance except:
questions.explanation: Fire can be extinguished by removing combustible substance as in case of fire in wood log, a paper on fire and a firecracker but removing of combustible substance is not possible in case of fire in a building.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Ignition temperature of kerosene is lower than that of petrol.
  2. Ignition temperature is also known as critical temperature.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Ignition temperature of kerosene oil is higher than that of petrol. Ignition temperature is the temperature at which substance start burning.

questions.question: Some substances do not burn with a flame such as:
questions.explanation: Substances like wax, LPG, and kerosene burns with flame but charcoal do not produce flame. Flame is formed only when combustible substance first change into vapour state.

questions.question: If Bunsen burner is closed which colour flame is produced
questions.explanation: If hole in Bunsen’s burner’s is closed incomplete combustion of fuel take place due to presence of insufficient oxygen that produce yellow flame with lots of smoke.

questions.question: In a candle the part which serves as a fuel is:
questions.explanation: In a candle, the paraffin wax serves as a fuel. Matchstick provides the ignition temperature and surrounding air provides oxygen for combustion. The wick helps in vaporising the wax before burning.

questions.question: A flickering flame produces smoke due to:
questions.explanation: A flickering flame produces smoke due to partial or incomplete combustion of fuel due to insufficient oxygen in immediate surroundings.

questions.question: When a fuel undergoes complete combustion it produces:
questions.explanation: When a fuel undergoes complete combustion, it produces non luminous flame without any smoke. The colour of flame is blue in place of yellow as in case of incomplete combustion.

questions.question: At the base of flame, blue zone occurs due to:
questions.explanation: At the base of flame, blue zone occurs due to burning of carbon monoxide to form carbon dioxide in presence of oxygen. In this zone complete combustion takes place.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: When a candle is lighted, the wick:
questions.explanation: When a candle is lighted, the wick absorbs the liquid wax and forms a flame by changing the liquid wax into vapour form.

questions.question: Rubbing of matchstick on sides of matchbox raises temperature to ignition temperature by producing:
questions.explanation: When matchstick is rubbed on the sides of matchbox, the temperature of ignition temperature raises due to friction. The phosphorous present in matchstick catch fire easily.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Different substances have different ignition temperatures.
  2. Matchstick tail has a low ignition point.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Different substances have different ignition temperature. Petrol start burning at very low temperature but wood requires high temperature due to different ignition temperature. Matchstick tail has high ignition temperature.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. High ignition temperature can cause fire hazards.
  2. Low ignition temperature causes difficulty in ignition.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Low ignition temperature can cause fire hazards as in case of phosphorous and sodium and very low ignition temperature causes difficulty in ignition as in wood and coal.

questions.question: When a piece of rock from space enters earth, it burns because of
questions.explanation: When a piece of rock from space enters earth’s atmosphere it start burning due to friction between rock and atmospheric gases. These rocks are called meteors.

questions.question: Mohan was sitting in his drawing room watching television. Suddenly his television wires caught fire but to get rid off this fire he should not use:
questions.explanation: Electrical fire should not be extinguished using water because tap water is good conductor of electricity. Soil and sand should be used to extinguish electrical fire.

questions.question: Respiration takes place in living organisms is an example of:
questions.explanation: The process by which living organism obtain oxygen for releasing energy is called respiration. Respiration is slow combustion that takes place inside the mitochondria and cytoplasm.

questions.question: Within a living cell, food is burned to produce energy through:

options.html: addition of Carbon dioxide and water

questions.explanation: Within living cell, food is burned to produce energy through the process of respiration in which energy is released in form of ATP.

questions.question: Sunil wet a paper cup with water and then heated it over the flame:
questions.explanation: Due to presence of water in paper cup ignition temperature of wet paper cup gets increased and paper will not burn when placed over a flame until water gets evaporated.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Matchstick burns on striking a smooth surface.
  2. Striking generates enough heat to light the matchstick.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Matchstick burns on striking a rough surface having friction. The striking of matchstick over rough surface generates enough heat to light the matchstick. Matchstick contains phosphorus that catch fire easily.

questions.question: From the fire triangle(essential elements of fire) water removes:
questions.explanation: There are three essential requirements for producing fire. These are fuel, air (to supply oxygen), and heat (to raise the temperature of the fuel beyond the ignition temperature). Fire can be controlled by removing one or more of these requirements. Heat, oxygen and combustible substance forms the fire triangle that causes fire. Water removes the heat and oxygen when poured over burning substance that extinguishes fire.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Carbon dioxide is inflammable gas.
  2. Carbon dioxide is heavier than oxygen.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide gas is produced during combustion but carbon dioxide is non-inflammable substance. Carbon dioxide is heavier than oxygen that help in extinguish the fire.

questions.question: Find the odd one out:
questions.explanation: There is different kind of fire extinguisher like soda acid fire extinguisher, foam fire extinguisher, and carbon dioxide fire extinguisher but oxygen is not used to extinguish fire as it favours combustion.

questions.question: A person after fire casualty is wrapped with blanket to:

options.html: to increase the ignition temperature of body
to cut the supply of combustible substance
to cut the supply of carbon dioxide

questions.explanation: A person after fire casualty is wrapped with blanket to cut oxygen supply. Cutting of oxygen supply extinguish the fire due to absence of support of combustion.

questions.question:
Match the following:

i) water a) combustible substance
ii) stone b) hydrocarbons
iii) fuel c) burns without flame
iv) wood d) good conductor of electricity
v) charcoal e) non combustible substance
questions.explanation: Water is good conductor of electricity. Stone is non combustible substance, fuels are mostly hydrocarbons like methane, butane etc. charcoal burns without flame and wood is a combustible substance.

questions.question: This sign if present on a parcel indicates that it is :
questions.explanation: The sign shown above indicates that the substance inside is inflammable and catch fire easily. Hence , should be kept away from fire places.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. As the valve of carbon dioxide fire extinguisher is opened, vapour turns into liquid.
  2. Vapour carbon dioxide release from such fire extinguisher blankets the fire.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: As the valve of carbon dioxide fire extinguisher is opened, liquid inside the container turns into vapour. The vapour carbon dioxide gas released from such fire extinguisher blankets the fire to cut off supply of oxygen.

questions.question: Following image is of fire triangle, what should be placed on its three sides?
questions.explanation: In fire triangles air (oxygen), Heat (ignition temperature) and fuel (combustible substance) should be kept. Absence of any one components leads to extinguishing the fire.

questions.question: Geeta on a Diwali night heard several explosions and asked her father about the cause. He answered:

options.html: due to heavy pressure producing lot of fuel.
due to spark and incomplete combustion of combustible substance.
due to rapid combustion and very low pressure.
due to large amount of gas creating heavy pressure

questions.explanation: The explosion is caused due to formation of large amount of gas creating heavy pressure along with energy to create loud sound.

questions.question: Yellow flame leaves behind black residue which is also called:
questions.explanation: Yellow flame is formed due to incomplete combustion of fuel. Yellow flame leaves behind black residue which is also called as soot.

questions.question:
Water is never used to extinguish an electrical fire since :
i) It is bad conductor of electricity.
ii) It is good conductor of electricity.

options.html: statement ii) is wrong but i) is correct
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Water is never used to extinguish an electric fire because water is good conductor of electricity .

questions.question: A foam fire extinguisher is similar in its construction to:
questions.explanation: A foam fire extinguisher is similar in its construction to soda acid fire extinguisher except foam is produced along with carbon dioxide gas.

questions.question: Wax vapour burn completely in the:

options.html: not in any zone of flame

questions.explanation: Wax vapour burns completely in the non-luminous zone of flame. non-luminous zone is blue in colour. Luminous zone is yellow in colour with incomplete combustion of fuel.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. A combustible substance that produces heat at a high cost is called fuel.
  2. A combustible substance that produces heat at a low cost is called fuel.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: A combustible substance produces heat at low cost, should be easily available. A combustible substance that produces heat at a low cost is called a good fuel.

questions.question: Which of the following hydrocarbon is a fuel?
questions.explanation: Methane CH4 is a fuel. Methane is present in biogas and natural gas. This gas have high calorific value and do not leave any residue at combustion.

questions.question: A fuel will be considered good if:

options.html: ignition temperature is greater than room temperature
very costly and produce lot of smoke
ignites easily to produce lot of carbon monoxide
ignition temperature is lower than room temperature

questions.explanation: A fuel will be considered good if the ignition temperature is greater than room temperature. It should have a high calorific value and easy to transport.

questions.question: Which of the following can catch fire on their own?
questions.explanation: Sodium metal catches fire on its own. The ignition temperature of sodium is less than room temperature and catches fire when come in contact of oxygen.

questions.question: The least hot part of the flame is:
questions.explanation: The least hot part of the flame is black. This part is situated at the top of flame containing unburnt particles.

questions.question: A non inflammable substance out of the following is:
questions.explanation: A non inflammable substance out of the following is carbon dioxide. Carbon dioxide gas prevents the fire. LPG, CNG, and alcohol are inflammable substances and catch fire easily.

questions.question: During combustion temperature rises considerably because burning:
questions.explanation: During combustion temperature rises considerably because burning produces heat and light due to the breaking of carbon bonds in them.

tests.name: Combustion and Flame Test 08
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Combustion and Flame CBSE Online Test 08 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

questions.question: Anil was doing an experiment in his chemistry lab. He opened the air hole of Bunsen burner and adjusted it to get:
questions.explanation: The gas used in Bunsen burner burns completely and produce blue flame without any smoke.

questions.question: Which of the following fuels will give us maximum energy?
questions.explanation: Butter gives more energy on combustion than glucose, pulses, and rice. Pulses contain more protein and rice is also a source of glucose.

questions.question: Rahul brought a copper wire near the candle flame and found that it takes the least time to become red hot when introduced into:

options.html: equal time in all zones of flame

questions.explanation: The outermost zone of flame is hottest region because complete combustion of fuel takes place in this region. Copper wire near the candle flame of outermost zone become red hot faster.

questions.question: The type of combustion which can cause lot of air pollution is:
questions.explanation: Incomplete combustion of fuel causes lot of air pollution. During incomplete combustion, carbon monoxide gas is produced which is very harmfull for health of humans.

questions.question: Combustion can be defined as a:-
questions.explanation: The process of burning of any substance in presence of oxygen is called combustion. Combustion is a chemical change as in combustion new substance is formed that cannot be reversed.

questions.question: Rekha was studying about combustible substances and she concluded that every combustible substance when burnt produce:
questions.explanation: Every combustible substance when burnt produce heat. The quantity of heat depends upon the calorific value of fuel and physical state of fuel.

questions.question: Another name given to ignition temperature is:
questions.explanation: Ignition temperature is the temperature at which fuel starts burning. Ignition temperature is also called a kindling temperature.

questions.question: Fuels are present in:
questions.explanation: Fuels are present in all three states. In solid state, coal and wood, In liquid state kerosene and petrol and in gases state, CNG and LPG

questions.question: The contact number of a fire station or fire control room is:
questions.explanation: The contact number of a fire station or fire control room is 101 throughout India.

questions.question: In villages, many people still use coal or wood to cook food. For easy combustion of such fuels :

options.html: soil is used to make layers
sand is thrown on the stove

questions.explanation: Coal or wood is still used in a number of villages for cooking food. For easy combustion of coal and wood air is blown around to increase the supply of oxygen for easy combustion.

questions.question: If a combustible substance is present along with fire:

options.html: substance will not burn at all.
substance will not catch fire above its ignition point.
substance will not catch fire below its ignition point.

questions.explanation: If a combustible substance is present along with fire, the substance will not catch fire below its ignition point. Ignition temperature is necessary for combustion of fuel.

questions.question: Gas that has very high calorific value is:
questions.explanation: Hydrogen gas has the highest calorific value. Hydrogen gas is used as fuels in rockets.

questions.question: A Soda acid fire extinguisher releases _____ which puts out the fire.
questions.explanation: A soda-acid extinguisher was patented in the U.S. in 1881 by Almon M. Granger. Once the acid was mixed with the bicarbonate solution, carbon dioxide gas was expelled and thereby pressurized the water. A soda acid fire extinguisher releases water and carbon dioxide gas that lower the temperature and cut off the supply of oxygen.

questions.question: When the fuel burns incompletely a poisonous gas is released that can be very harmful our life:
questions.explanation: Incomplete combustion of fuels releases poisonous gas that can be very harmful for our health. Carbon monoxide gas combine with haemoglobin to reduce oxygen carrying capacity.

questions.question: Acid rain is caused by oxides of
questions.explanation: Acid rain is caused by a chemical reaction that begins when compounds like sulfur dioxide and nitrogen oxides are released into the air. These substances can rise very high into the atmosphere, where they mix and react with water, oxygen, and other chemicals to form more acidic pollutants, known as acid rain.

questions.question: Deforestation may lead to:
questions.explanation: Deforestation means clearing of forest and using that land for other purposes. Forest act as a natural absorber of rainwater, deforestation leads to a decrease in the water holding capacity of the soil or in other words we can say that infiltration of water is reduced so, there are floods.

questions.question: Large scale cutting of trees is named:
questions.explanation: Large scale cutting of trees is called deforestation. Deforestation leads to global warming, drought, and soil erosion.

questions.question: The existence of a variety of animals, plants, and microbes in a particular area is:
questions.explanation: Biodiversity is the presence of a variety of animals, plants, and microbes in a particular area. Biodiversity depends upon the climatic condition and availability of water.

questions.question: Biotic components of an ecosystem does not include:
questions.explanation: The living components of an ecosystem are called biotic components of ecosystem. Ecosystem includes microbes, plants, and animals. Minerals are nonliving component of an ecosystem so, minerals are not included in biotic components.

questions.question: A sustainable functional unit of biosphere is called:
questions.explanation: Ecosystem is the basic functional unit of biosphere. It is self contained ecological system  which consists of distinct biotic and abiotic component

questions.question: The largest among various protected areas for conservation of plants and animals:
questions.explanation: Wildlife sanctuaries, national parks, biosphere reserves, etc. are protected areas for conservation of plants and animals present in the area. Biosphere reserve is the largest among various protected areas for plants and animals. Biosphere reserves covers large areas of protected land for conservation of wildlife animal resource and traditional life of the tribals living in the area.

questions.question: A place in environment where an organism lives is
questions.explanation: Habitat

questions.question: Full form of IUCN is:

options.html: International union of conservation of nature and natural resources
International union of conservation of national parks and nature
International union of conservation of national parks and natural resources
International unity of conservation of nature and natural resources

questions.explanation: IUCN stands for International union of conservation of nature and natural resources. It is an international organization working in the field of nature conservation and sustainable use of natural resource.

questions.question: The regions of the surface and atmosphere of earth occupied by living organism is called:
questions.explanation: Biosphere have all the necessary things which are required to living organisms. It includes the lithosphere, hydrosphere, and atmosphere.

questions.question: Threatened species does not include:
questions.explanation: Those species that are less in number and going to extinct are called threatened species. Extinct species are those which are already lost from the earth for example dinosaurs.

questions.question: Those species which existed on earth once but are  not found anywhere now:
questions.explanation: Species that no longer exist anywhere on earth are called extinct species for example dinosaurs and dodo bird.

questions.question: Grazing of animals and crop cultivation is prohibited in:
questions.explanation: Grazing of animals and crop cultivation is prohibited in national park because National parks are constructed for the survival of particular plants and animals.

questions.question: Species confined to a specific geographical area of land is called:
questions.explanation: Those species of plants and animals which are found exclusively in a particular area of land are called endemic species. Endemic species are found in only a particular area due to climatic factors.

questions.question: Panchmarhi biosphere reserve has a national park named:
questions.explanation: Satpura national park is situated in Panchmarhi biosphere reserves in Madhya Pradesh. It is built to protect tigers and other animals.

questions.question: Animals showing seasonal movements are  known as:
questions.explanation: Some animals show seasonal movements due to unfavourable climatic changes. These animals are called migratory animals.

General Instructions:
