questions.question: India hold _____ position on the list of mega biodiversity countries:
questions.explanation: With a large landmass covering a range of ecosystems, India holds the eighth position on the list of mega biodiversity countries.

questions.question: The  source book which keeps a record about endangered species is called:
questions.explanation: Red Data Book contains information about endangered species. These species are present in less number and declining at an alarming rate.

questions.question: Which of the following is a biodiversity hot spot?
questions.explanation: Biodiversity hot spot is a region that is both a significant reservoir of biodiversity ( where a large number of different kinds of plants and animals are found) and threatened with destruction.

questions.question: Term biodiversity was coined by:
questions.explanation: Term biodiversity was coined by Wilson to represents the variation in plants and animals in a locality of diverse kind.

questions.question: Which wild animal species is extinct from the following species?
questions.explanation: Those species which are no more available anywhere in the world are called extinct species. White tiger is one of such species. Habitat destruction and hunting are important cause of extinction of white tiger.

questions.question: Our biosphere is made up of various ecosystems. All ecosystems are:
questions.explanation: Ecosystem is the functional unit of biosphere. All ecosystems are interconnected and interrelated with each other. Flow of energy and nutrients takes place through food chain in ecosystem.

questions.question: Those species which exist in small numbers are called:
questions.explanation: Those species which exist in small number are called rare species. These species are found in particular habit and may become extinct because of habitat destruction or other enviornmental disturbances.

questions.question: The species which are at the verge of the extinction
questions.explanation: Endangered

questions.question: Red data book provides information about:
questions.explanation: Red data book is the sourcebook that keeps a record of all endangered animals and plants. There are different Red Data Book for plants, animals, and other species. Extinct species are not included in the red data book.

questions.question: Species is a group or class of animals or plants capable of:
questions.explanation: Species is a group of population capable of interbreeding. Species refers to group or class of animals or plants having certain common and permanent characteristics that clearly distinguish it from other groups.

questions.question: A place where animals are protected out of their natural habitat:
questions.explanation: Zoo is a place where animals are protected out of their natural habitat. On the other hand sanctuary, national park and botanical garden provide protection to plants and animals in their natural habitat.

questions.question: The first reserve forest of India is:
questions.explanation: Satpura National Park is the first Reserve Forest of India. This reserve forest is located in Madhya Pradesh state.

questions.question: Identify the correctly matched pair from the following.
questions.explanation:
• Gir sanctuary is situated in Gujarat. Lions are the highly protected wild animals of this sanctuary. It was upgraded into a national park in 1975.
  • Sunderbans is known for protecting tigers.
  • Kaziranga national park is known for one-horned rhinoceros.
  • Tigers are the pride of Ranthambore National park which is located in Rajasthan.

questions.question: Project tiger was launched by Indian government on:
questions.explanation: Project Tiger is a tiger conservation programme launched in 1 April 1973 by the Government of India. The main aim of Project Tiger was to create a safe and ideal environmental conditions for the survival and growth of tigers and its prey to ensure the maintenance of a viable population of tigers in the country, Corbett national park was the first national park of India to be covered under Project Tiger on April 1st, 1973.

questions.question: Which of these is not included in the list of mega biodiversity countries?
questions.explanation: The megadiverse countries are a group of countries that harbor the majority of Earth's species and high numbers of endemic species. Conservation International identified 17 megadiverse countries in 1998. Many of them are located in, or partially in, tropical or subtropical regions. Argentina doesn't have high number of endemic species. it is not a country of great biological diversity so it is not included in the list of mega biodiversity countries.

questions.question: Saving paper will reduce the problem of:
questions.explanation: To make paper from trees, the raw wood must first be turned into "pulp Wood pulpis a watery “soup" of cellulose, wood fibers, lignin, and water. Large number of plants are cut for making paper, Saving paper will reduce the problem of deforestation. Paper can be recycled five to seven times for use. If each student saves at least one sheet of paper in a day, we can save many trees in a year. We should save, used paper and recycle it. By this, we not only save trees but also save energy and water needed for manufacturing paper.

questions.question: Soil erosion may lead to:
questions.explanation: The removal of the top fertile layer of soil is called soil erosion. It may lead to flood because the soil, silt, and gravels removed due to soil erosion get deposited in the river bed and reduces the volumetric water carrying capacity of the river and this extra volume of water then spreads out laterally from the river and cause a flood

questions.question: Saving paper will save:
questions.explanation: To make paper, the raw wood must first be turned into "pulp." Wood pulp is a watery “soup" of cellulose wood fibers, lignin, water, and the chemicals used during the pulping process. Wood can be turned to pulp in a couple of different ways. Paper is made from wood pulp for which a large number of trees are cut so we can save trees by saving paper.

questions.question: Variety of life forms include:
questions.explanation: Life forms in a habitat includes different kinds of plants (flora), different kinds of animals (fauna), and a large number of small microbes not visible to naked eyes.

questions.question: Animals found in a particular area are termed as:
questions.explanation: Animal found in a particular area called fauna and plants found in a particular area are called flora. Flora and fauna are characteristic of a particular habitat.

questions.question: Full form of WCU is:
questions.explanation: WCU stands for world conservation union. This international union is responsible for conservation of plants and animals world over.

questions.question: Creation of wildlife reserves and endorsement of laws are conservation measures that promote increased
questions.explanation: The creation of wildlife reserves and enforcement of laws are conservation measures that promote increased preservation of species. It is estimated that tropical forests are losing 2 – 5 species per hour or 14,000 – 40,000 species per year. If the current rate of species extinction goes on unabated, 50% of species are liable to die out by the end of the 21st century.

questions.question: Animals move from one place to the other for:
questions.explanation: Animals move from one place to the other for feeding and reproduction. They move in search of green fields for grazing. Some animals move for copulation to mate for reproduction.

questions.question: Which one of the following species is not included under the ‘Red List’?
questions.explanation: Endemic species are those species of plants and animals which are found exclusively in a particular area. They are not naturally found anywhere else. Endemic species are not included under red list because the red list contains those species whose continued existence is threatened. Each Red Data Book usually deals with a specific group of animals or plants (e.g., reptiles, insects, mosses).

questions.question: Non living things constitute:
questions.explanation: Non living things constitute abiotic components of ecosystem. Abiotic components includes soil, water, temperature sunlight, etc.

questions.question: Removal of fertile top soil causes:
questions.explanation: Removal of top fertile soil is called soil erosion. In absence of fertile soil, no plants grows and area slowly changes into desert and the process is called desertification.

questions.question: Rhizobium is ________ component of ecosystem:
questions.explanation: The living components of ecosystem that includes plants, animals, and microbes are called biotic components. Rhizobium is a bacteria that live in root nodule of leguminous plants.

questions.question: Cutting down trees is directly harmful to:
questions.explanation: Cutting down trees is directly harmful to land. In absence of tree, lands becomes infertile slowly-slowly and changes into desert in course of time.

questions.question: Which of the following groups indicates flora that exclusively belongs to pachmarhi biosphere reserve:

options.html: wild dog, barking deer, blue-bull, black bear

questions.explanation: Plants found in Pachmarhi biosphere includes teak, sal, ashok, and silver fern. This biosphere reserve is rich in different kinds of vegetation.

questions.question: The growing more and more of plants is called
questions.explanation: Afforestation

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Plant life of an area is called fauna.
  2. Animal community of a particular habitat is called vulnerable community.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Plant life of an area is called flora. Animal community of a particular habitat is called fauna.

questions.question: A miserly use of resources refers to:
questions.explanation: Proper use of resources is called conservation of recourses. Conservation of resources is essential for survival of life of on earth. Non-renewable resources should be conserved first.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Conservation provides individual commercial benefits.
  2. Conservation is same as preservation.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation:
1. conservation does not provide individual commercial benefits. Conservation helps in maintaining ecological balance.
  2. Conservation is the same as preservation. Both terms involve a degree of protection. Conservation is generally associated with the protection of natural resources, while preservation is associated with the protection of buildings, objects, and landscapes.

questions.question: Which of the following factors are responsible for wearing away of top soil?
questions.explanation: Wearing away of top soil is called soil erosion. Overgrazing, wind, and water are factors that are responsible for soil erosion.

questions.question: Different types of animals, plants and microbes in a particular area constitutes its:
questions.explanation: Biological diversity or biodiversity refers to the variety of organisms existing on the earth, their interrelationships, and their relationship with the environment. The biosphere reserves help to maintain the biodiversity and culture of that area.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. restocking of destroyed forest is done by planting new trees.
  2. reforestation is done by planting new trees.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Reforestation is restocking of the destroyed forest by planting new trees. The planted trees should generally be of the same species which were found in that forest.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. When we kill a small sized animal loss can be ignored.
  2. We have food webs in ecosystem that compensate for loss of species always.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: All the animals and plants are part of food chain. When a small sized animal is killed, a food chain is broken that may harm the other members which in turn disturbs the food webs in ecosystem.

questions.question: The term which represents richest biodiversity is:
questions.explanation: Global biodiversity represents richest biodiversity as it includes all the plants and animals presents on earth. So, global biodiversity represents richest biodiversity.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Global warming is a threat to biodiversity.
  2. Coral reefs will be all lost if global warming stops.

options.html: statement (i) is correct but (ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Due to increase in concentration of carbon dioxide gas in air, greenhouse effect occurs that leads to global warming, a threat to biodiversity. Coral reefs growth is affected due to global warming Higher sea temperatures from global warming causes major coral bleaching. Bleaching occurs when corals respond to the stress of warmer temperatures by expelling the colorful algae that live within them.

questions.question: Conservational biology is closely related to:
questions.explanation: The branch of biology that deals with nature surrounding and their effects on organisms is called ecology. Ecology closely related to conservational biology.

questions.question: Most biodiversity hotspots are located in the:
questions.explanation: Tropical areas are neither too hot nor too cool. This reason provides best suitable condition for growth of plants and animals. Hence, most biodiversity hotspots are located in the tropics or tropical region.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Conservation is replacing present resources with new ones.
  2. Conservation can be done ex situ as well as in situ.

options.html: statement (ii) is correct but (i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Conservation is protecting the resources from overuse and transfer the same to the next generation. Conservation can be done in situ at a native place or ex situ at different locations.

questions.question: Size of an animal will represent its:
questions.explanation: The size of animal will not represent its energy level, Social life and evolutionary trends. Some large sized animals have evolved earlier than small sized animals. Some small sized animals have more energy level than large sized animals.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Loss of biodiversity has contributed to poverty.
  2. To conserve biodiversity we must take lessons from the paleontological past.

options.html: Statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
Statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation:
1. It is the link between all organisms on earth, binding each into an interdependent ecosystem, in which all species have their role. It is the web of life. Together we all form part of the planet's ecosystems, which means if there is a biodiversity crisis, our health and livelihoods are at risk too.
  2. Paleontology is the scientific study of life that existed prior to, and sometimes including, the start of the Holocene Epoch (roughly 11,700 years before present). It includes the study of fossils to determine organisms' evolution and interactions with each other and their environments.

questions.question: The biosphere consists of a extensive network of inter-connected :-
questions.explanation: The biosphere consists of an extensive network of interconnected ecosystems in which flow of energy and nutrients takes place for its sustenance.

questions.question: Loss of corals due to environmental stresses has been referred to as:
questions.explanation: Loss of corals due to environmental stress has been referred as bleaching. Coral bleaching occurs when coral polyps expel algae that lives inside their tissues. Normally, coral polyps live in an endosymbiotic relationship with the algae and that relationship is crucial for the coral and hence for the health of the whole reef.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Honey bees are unimportant for ecological balance.
  2. Honey bees act as pollinators to a huge variety of agricultural crops.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Honey bees play an important role in ecological balance by pollination. Pollination is needed for plants to reproduce, and so many plants depend on bees or other insects as pollinators. When a bee collects nectar and pollen from the flower of a plant, some pollen from the stamens—the male reproductive organ of the flower—sticks to the hairs of her body.. Honey bee’s acts as pollinators to a huge variety of agricultural crops.

questions.question: Ecosystem stores an essential element to regulate climate of the world:
questions.explanation:
Ecosystem stores carbon element to regulate climate of the world. Carbon is the most essential elements that forms the part of living organism. All food items contain carbon element.
The carbon cycle is very important to all ecosystems, and ultimately life on earth. The carbon cycle is critical to the food chain. This occurs when decomposers (bacteria and fungi) break down dead plants and animals (decomposition) and release the carbon compounds stored in them.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Monarch butterfly is a migratory species.
  2. Monarch butterfly requires a large area to exist.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation:
Monarch butterfly is a migratory species that requires a large area to exist. This butterfly moves in large area and lay eggs at different places.. The monarch butterfly spend their winter hibernation in Mexico and some parts of Southern California where it is warm all year long.
The annual migration of North America’s monarch butterfly is a unique and amazing phenomenon. The monarch butterfly is the only butterfly known to make  two-way migration as birds, monarch butterfly cannot survive the cold winters of northern climates. Using environmental cues, the monarch butterfly know when it is time to travel south for the winter. Monarchs use a combination of air currents and thermals to travel long distances.

questions.question: By spraying pesticides ruthlessly in the field we may:
questions.explanation: Pesticides are used to kills the pests in agricultural field. These chemicals also kill the useful insect pollinators present in field.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Biodiversity declines from equator to the poles.
  2. Biodiversity increases from equator to the poles

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Biodiversity declines from equator to the poles due to changed environmental condition. Equator is best suitable place but poles are very cold and very few species survive there.

questions.question: To prevent loss of biodiversity we:

options.html: can discuss and prevent vehicular pollution

questions.explanation: To prevent loss of biodiversity we must regulate climate changes. Changes in temperature, rainfall, and other climatic factors influence the survival of organisms.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. A biodiversity hotspot is a protected area with only endangered species.
  2. Biodiversity hot spot is a region with high level of endemic species.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation:
Biodiversity hotspot is the area where large number of species of different kind are found. Biodiversity hot spot is a region with high level of endemic species.
Endemic species are plants and animals that exist only in one geographic region.

questions.question: Endemic flora of Pachmarhi biosphere reserve includes:
questions.explanation: Endemic flora of Pachmarhi biosphere reserve includes both sal and wild mango. Endemic species are present in particular locality only.

questions.question:
Read the following statements, which one of the following options follows:

  1. Genetic diversity is of higher level as compared to species diversity.
  2. Ecosystem diversity is of higher level as compared to genetic diversity.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: An ecosystem is made of all the plants, animals, and microorganisms in an area along with non-living components such as climate, soil, river deltas, etc. Biological diversity or biodiversity or ecosystem diversity refers to the variety of organisms existing on the earth, their interrelationships, and their relationship with the environment. Ecosystem diversity is of a higher level as compared to genetic diversity.

questions.question: Abiotic components of an ecosystem includes:
questions.explanation: The non living components of an ecosystem are called abiotic components. Rainfall is a non living component of an ecosystem. So rainfall is an abiotic component of an ecosystem.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Conservation protects natural resources.
  2. Conservation prevents wastage of natural resources.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation:
Conservation protects natural resources. We need to conserve our Natural Resources because:

  1. it is the main source of our daily needs  
  2. it is important to maintain ecological balance.

 Conservation prevents wastage of natural resources also.

questions.question: Even protected forests are unsafe due to our:
questions.explanation: Even protected forests are unsafe due to our greed. Human being exploits the natural resources for getting economic value without considering about future generation.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Biodiversity provides many ecological services.
  2. Biodiversity involve water purification process.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Biodiversity provides many ecological services such as pollination, formation of oxygen, and many other services. Biodiversity does not involve water purification process.

questions.question: One major cause of extinction of organisms can be:
questions.explanation: Disturbances in habitat of organisms for urbanisation and industrialisation causes extinction of organisms. Construction of dam is also a major cause of habitat loss.

tests.name: Conservation of Plants & Animals Test 06
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Conservation of Plants and Animals CBSE Online Test 06 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Biodiversity consists of all non-living components.
  2. Decrease in snake population will decrease the population of frogs too.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Biodiversity consists of all living components such as plants, animals, and micro organism. Decrease in snake population will increase the population of frogs as snake feeds upon frogs.

questions.question: Extinction of plants and animals will cause?
questions.explanation: Every organism is a part of one or more food chain. Extinction of plants and animals will cause damage to food chain .

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Siberian cranes are migratory birds.
  2. They normally move in winter from India to Siberia.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Siberian cranes are migratory birds. They normally move in winter from Siberia to India due to adverse climatic conditions in Siberia during that period.

questions.question: We need to protect forests to conserve:
questions.explanation:
We need to conserve forest because it-

  1. Releases Oxygen,
  2. Checks air pollution,
  3. Control moisture and prevents water pollution,
  4. Prevents soil erosion and increases soil fertility,
  5. Provides shelter to birds and nest.

questions.question: Causes of habitat destruction includes:
questions.explanation: Construction of dams engulfs large area of land having forest of habit of some other organisms.

questions.question: If we cut a few trees we must:
questions.explanation: Cutting of trees creates an ecological imbalance in nature and harm air, water, and land. so we should plant more trees if we cut a few trees

questions.question: A protected area that combines benefits of both conservation and sustainable use of resource is:
questions.explanation: Biosphere reserves are protected area that combines benefits of both conservation and sustainable use of resources. Different zone of biosphere reserves serves a different purpose.

questions.question: Finest teak of India is obtained from:
questions.explanation: Teak is one of the best quality of wood used for furniture due to durability and strength. Finest teak of India is obtained from forests of Satpura.

questions.question: We need to protect environment for:
questions.explanation:
We must protect environment because:
Global warming is a serious environmental issue. The continuous emission of carbon dioxide by thermal power generating units is the leading cause for the surge in greenhouse gases.
High-level of environmental pollution is detrimental for human and animal health.
Forests are fast depleting. It may cause soil erosion and drought situation.
Excessive use of pesticides and contamination of soil have negatively affected soil output. These fruits and vegetables are not very healthy for human health.

questions.question: Some people visited a forest and cooked food in an area where trees shed leaves result was:
questions.explanation: Forest fire may be caused due to cooking of food in the forest where trees shed leaves. Dry leaves catch fire easily and spread to large area resulting in forest fire.

questions.question: Species is a group of population capable of:
questions.explanation: Species is a group of living organisms consisting of similar individuals capable of exchanging genes or interbreeding.

questions.question: Crop failure occurred in Mohans field due to
questions.explanation: In India, most of the agriculture is rain dependent, due to no rainfall for long time results in drought.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Overexploitation of a wild species is a threat to wild flora.
  2. Introduction of other species into a habitat does not harm to native species.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Overexploitation of a wild species is a threat to wild flora. Introduction of other species into a habitat causes harms to native species due competition for food and water.

questions.question: We conserve biodiversity by:
questions.explanation: We conserve biodiversity by biosphere reserve, botanical garden, and sanctuaries. These are protected areas for the conservation of plants and animals present in that area.

questions.question: Some popular activities that are dependent upon biodiversity are:
questions.explanation: Gardening, aquarium, and herbarium are some popular activities that are dependent upon biodiversity of plants and animals.

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Exotic species create _____ for endemic species:
questions.explanation: Those species that are not native to particular habitat and brought from other places are called exotic species. Exotic species create competition for food, water, and other nutrients for endemic species.

questions.question: One of the most important causes of biodiversity is:
questions.explanation: Different climatic zone are found in different parts of world. Different species live in different climatic zone. This variation in climatic zone causes biodiversity.

questions.question: Most of the biodiversity hotspots are:
questions.explanation: Forest is considered as a biodiversity hotspot because of enormous number of species living in it. There are many varieties of birds, animals, insects, and plants inhabiting the forest.

questions.question: Which of these is a well known migratory species?
questions.explanation: Migratory species are species that move from one habitat to another during different times of the year, as they cannot live in the same environment all year round due to seasonal limitations in factors such as food, sunlight, and temperature. Albatross is a well known migratory species.

questions.question: Identify animal in the given image :
questions.explanation: The animal shown above is Mammoth. Mammoth is a extinct species not found anywhere in this world. This species had long curved truck and body like elephant.

questions.question: A habitat change may:

options.html: cause destruction of the new habitat
decrease the number of a given species
increase the number of a given species

questions.explanation: A habitat is an ecological or environmental area that is inhabited by a particular species. changes in habitats brought on by alterations in farming practices, tourism, pollution, ....etc decreases the number of a given species.

questions.question: Identify the picture of the extinct animal given below :
questions.explanation:
The image shown  is Tasmanian tiger. This tiger has got extinct from the earth.
 It had a head like a wolf, striped body like a tiger and backward facing pouch like a wombat. They  looked like a long dog with stripes, and a heavy stiff tail.

questions.question: Removal of fertile top soil will cause:
questions.explanation: Removal of fertile top soil is called soil erosion. In absence of fertile soil, no plants grows and area slowly changes into desert and the process is called desertification.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Tiger and lion both are big cats.
  2. Gir forest is a lion reserve.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Tiger and lion both are big cats because they belong to same family. Tiger lion and cat are quite similar in both structure and behaviour. The Gir Forest National Park and Wildlife Sanctuary is situated in Gujarat, India. It is famous for Asiatic Lions.  Gir forest is a lion reserve for protecting lions from extinction.

questions.question: Maintaining the organism in nature, refers to:
questions.explanation: Preservation of organisms is the process of maintaining the organism in nature. Preservation may be in situ or ex situ depending upon species to be conserved.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Function of ecosystem is to allow flow of energy through abiotic components.
  2. Ecosystem maintains transfer of organic matter.

options.html: Statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
Statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: The main function of ecosystem is to allow flow of energy only through biotic components  autotrophs, heterotrophs and decomposers. Ecosystem also maintains transfer of organic matter in the ecosystem through food  chain and food web.

questions.question: Which of the following tiger species is/are already extinct:
questions.explanation: Javan tigers got extinct due to the rapid increase in the human population leading to a reduction in the habitat of Javan tiger. The Caspian tiger became extinct in the mid-twentieth century due to the loss of natural habitat and overhunting.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Animals cannot be maintained outside their habitats.
  2. Plants can be maintained outside their habitats in botanical gardens.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Animals can be maintained outside their habitats by providing resembling habitat example in zoo. Plants can be maintained outside their habitats in botanical gardens.

questions.question: The existence of endemic species is under threat due to:
questions.explanation: The existence of endemic species is under threat due to increasing human population, habitat destruction, and introduction of exotic species into a foreign ecosystem.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. A species is exotic when no member of that species is still alive.
  2. Passenger pigeon became extinct in 1914.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: A species is exotic when new species are introduced from other area and harms the endemic species. Exotic species are species of plants or animals that are growing in a non-native environment. They have been moved by humans to areas outside of their native ranges. The passenger pigeon was driven to extinction by uncontrolled commercial hunting for their meat, Passenger pigeon become extinct around 1914.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Project tiger was launched to:

options.html: protect and preserve tigers of the world
protect tigers of India and neighbouring countries

questions.explanation: Project tiger was launched in 1973 in India. The main objective of Project Tiger is to ensure a viable population of tiger in India for scientific, economic, aesthetic, cultural, and ecological values.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Lion tailed macaque is endemic Indian species.
  2. Over collection of plants is responsible for their reappearance.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Lion tailed macaque is endemic Indian species  They are endemic to the Western Ghats of south India. Over collection of plants is not responsible for their reappearance. Collection of plant is done to conserve the plant species.

questions.question: Mohan saw a snake. He thought of killing it but his elder brother who was a science student, stopped him by recognising:

options.html: danger from female snake of that group
importance of non violence in human life
importance of snake in mythological stories

questions.explanation: All species of an ecosystem are important and play important role. Killing of snake breaks the food chain that controls the number of frog,

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Panchmarhi biosphere reserve maintains both biodiversity and culture.
  2. This biosphere reserve includes a wildlife sanctuary named Bori.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Panchmarhi biosphere reserve maintains both biodiversity and culture as the outer zone of this reserve accommodates a number of tribal communities. The Pachmarhi Biosphere Reserve is situated in Satpura Range of Madhya Pradesh  This biosphere reserve includes a wildlife sanctuary named Bori.

questions.question: When we kill a frog or lizard ruthlessly we:

options.html: create an imbalance in the abiotic environment
threaten the survival of neighbouring species only

questions.explanation: Frog eat grasshopper and lizard eat insects they play important role in food chain. killing frog and lizards ruthlessly will increase the number of grasshopper and insects. Grashoppers and insects may cause damage to crop in farms so killing lizards or insects ruthlessly means we are harming ourselves.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Overgrazing will pose a threat only to wild animals.
  2. Invasive species may cause extinction of some local species.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Overgrazing will pose a threat to both plants and animals. Soil erosion is also caused due to overgrazing. Invasive species may cause extinction of some local species by competition and by altering the environment.

questions.question: If you have saved at least one sheet of paper per day you can save:
questions.explanation: Paper is made from plants. If we save paper regularly, many tree can be saved. Trees are lifeline for survival of all animals directly or indirectly.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Humans can mimic natural ecosystems easily to support their needs.
  2. Human;s exploitation is not responsible for habitat destruction in ecosystem.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Natural ecosystem cannot be created easily that support life on earth. Human exploitation is mainly responsible for habitat destruction in ecosystem for their greed.

questions.question: The permanent solution to the problem of deforestation can be:
questions.explanation: Reforestation is the permanent solution to the problem of deforestation. Planting tree on deforested land is called reforestation. A permanent solution to deforestation would, therefore, be proper planning on part of humans to understand the importance of trees. Simply planting trees is not a solution to this problem. We need to find that where major deforestation has taken place and practice more afforestation in that region. Also, vigorous cutting of trees for the purpose of development should be avoided. Thus the best solution to deforestation is to not allow them to be cut.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Animals are kept under artificial settings in a Zoological park.
  2. In zoological park we protect both flora and fauna under natural conditions.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: In zoological park, we protect only fauna, the animals of different kinds. Zoological parks are confined areas were animals are kept within specific enclosures to conserve wildlife; maintain ecological balance and for breeding.

questions.question: Which of the following is multipurpose protected area that may also contain other protected areas in it:
questions.explanation: Biosphere reserve is multipurpose protected area that may contain protected areas in it. Biosphere reserves help ensure the environmental, economic, and social sustainability of the region, by encouraging wise use of natural and human resources. They provide practical ways to resolve land use conflicts and to protect biological diversity.

questions.question: The worst method of agriculture that threatens biodiversity is:

options.html: slash and burn method of agriculture

questions.explanation: Slash and burn method of agriculture is the worst method of agriculture that threatens biodiversity drastically as new forest are cleaned for agriculture. There are many problems that result from this method of growing crops, a direct consequence of cutting down forests for cropland is deforestation; sIt threatens biodiversity by loss of habitat and species; increases air pollution by release of carbon into the atmosphere—which contributes to global climate change, and an increase in temperature

questions.question: Preservationist applies the principle of:
questions.explanation: Preservation is considered better than conservation. Preservationist applies the principle of non interference in natural system (without interference by human beings).

questions.question: One of the cause of species extinction is:

options.html: more carbon dioxide in the atmosphere
more water vapour in the atmosphere

questions.explanation: Species extinction occurs due to more carbon dioxide gas in the atmosphere. More carbon dioxide leads to the greenhouse effect and global warming. Many species of plants and animals cannot tolerate the rising temperature due to globle warming and become extinct.

questions.question: A collapse in ecosystem function occur due to loss of:
questions.explanation:
Those species that are play crucial unique role in an ecosystem is called keystone species.
keystone species are the species whose presence and role within an ecosystem has a disproportionate effect on other organisms within the system. A keystone species& is often a dominant predator whose removal allows a prey population to explode and often decreases overall diversity.
Herbivores can also be keystone species. Their consumption of plants helps control the physical and biological aspects of an ecosystem.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A plant cell lacks:
questions.explanation: Haemoglobin is present in RBC’s to carry oxygen. Mitochondria, plasma membrane is present in both plant and animal cells.

questions.question: Number of cells found in unicellular organism is:
questions.explanation: Those organisms that are made up of only one cell are called unicellular organism. Amoeba, paramecium, bacteria, etc are unicellular organisms.

questions.question: All living organisms are made up of:
questions.explanation: Cell is the structural and functional unit of all living organisms. Unicellular made up of only one cell and multicellular are made up of more than one cells.

questions.question: Cells are capable of performing:
questions.explanation: Cells are capable of performing respiration, synthesis, and digestion of food to obtain energy. Cells is the functional and structural unit of living organisms.

questions.question: Robert Hooke observed under microscope:
questions.explanation: Robert Hooke observed dead cells of thin slice of cork. He observed small compartments and called it cell.

questions.question: Hen's egg is a:
questions.explanation: Hen’s egg is a single cell. Most of the cells are not visible with naked eyes. Microscope is needed to observe cells but Ostrich eggs and Hen’s eggs are also single cell.

questions.question: In unicellular organism all the functions are performed by the:
questions.explanation: In unicellular organism all the functions are performed by the single cell. Amoeba, paramecium, euglena etc are unicellular organism.

questions.question: A single cell that can change its shape is:
questions.explanation: White blood corpuscles can change its shape. This cell change the shape to engulf the pathogen to provide protection against disease.

questions.question: Cell responsible to receive and transfer message in human body parts is:
questions.explanation: Nerve cells are responsible to receive and transfer message in human body parts. Nerve cells together form the nervous system of body.

questions.question: Function of nerve cell of an elephant is to transfer messages while nerve cell of a rat:
questions.explanation: The sole function of nerve cell is to transfer messages from one part of the body to other part. Nerve cell of an elephant as well as nerve cell of a rat both transfer messages for control and coordination.

questions.question: Cell membrane is also called:
questions.explanation: Cell membrane is also called as plasmalemma or plasma membrane. It forms the outer covering of the cell made up of protein and fat. It is semi-permeable in nature.

questions.question: Unit of inheritance in living organisms is:
questions.explanation: The transfer of traits from one generation to next generation is called inheritance. Inheritance is carried out by gene located in nucleus of the cell.

questions.question: Living substance of a cell is collectively known as:
questions.explanation: The living substance of a cell is collectively known as protoplasm. Protoplasm is fluid present between nucleus and plasma membrane of the cell. It contain all the cell organelles.

questions.question: The thread-like structures present in the nucleus are _______
questions.explanation: Chromosomes are thin thread like structure present inside nucleus of the cell. Chromosome consists of DNA and protein. Genes are located on chromosome.

questions.question: The fluid present between cell membrane and nucleus is:
questions.explanation: The fluid present between cell membrane and nucleus is called cytoplasm. Cytoplasm is thick fluid medium in which different cell organelles floats.

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Cell Structure and Functions CBSE Online Test 02 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Function of vacuole is/are:
questions.explanation: Vacuole is clear fluid inside the cytoplasm. In plants cell, single large vacuole is present on the other hand animal cell contains many small nucleus. Main function of nucleus is secretary, storage, and excretory.

questions.question: Unicellular organisms reproduce by:
questions.explanation: Unicellular organisms generally reproduce by fission in which organism divides into two or more parts by simple mitosis division. Amoeba reproduce by binary fission while plasmodium by multiple fission.

questions.question: Cell has ability to extract _____ energy from food molecules:
questions.explanation: Cells has ability to extract chemical energy from food molecules. The energy present in food molecules like glucose undergo respiration to produce chemical energy in form of ATP.

questions.question: Which organelle is called the “powerhouse of the cell”?
questions.explanation: The mitochondrion is called the “powerhouse of the cell” because it is responsible for producing energy-rich ATP molecules. Cells use ATP for all their energy needs.

questions.question: Membrane of nucleus is porous so it:

options.html: allows leakage of genes into cytoplasm
allows movement of chromosomes out of nucleus

questions.explanation: The membrane of nucleus is porous so it allows transport of specific proteins. Nucleus is also called as brain of the cell.

questions.question: Which of the following doesn't have a cell wall?
questions.explanation: Plant cell, bacteria, and fungi have cell wall made up of different chemicals like cellulose, peptidoglycan, and chitin respectively but virus is acelluar structure without cell wall or membrane.

questions.question: Plasma membrane is a:
questions.explanation: Plasma membrane is a bilayered membrane made up of protein and lipid. It is selectively permeable to different substances.

questions.question: Which of the following is a locomotory organelle in protozoa?
questions.explanation: Flagella is a locomotory organelle in protozoa. Flagella is long hair like projection on the body of some protozoa.

questions.question: Power house of the cell is:
questions.explanation: Mitochondria is called as powerhouse of the cell because it contain enzyme for cellular respiration in which energy is released in form of ATP.

questions.question: Which of the following is selectively permeable?
questions.explanation: Cell membrane is a selectively permeable membrane because it allow some substance to cross but prevents the other.

questions.question: Plant cell wall is made up of:
questions.explanation: The cell wall of plant is made up of cellulose. Cellulose is a complex sugar that cannot be digested by human beings.

questions.question: Which of the following group is prokaryotes?
questions.explanation: The cell which lack well developed nuclear membrane is called prokaryotic organism. Blue green algae is an example of prokaryotic organism.

questions.question: Which of the following occurs only in animal cell?
questions.explanation: Centrosomes are present only in animals cell. Centrosome produces fimbriae that help in cell division. Chromosome and ribosome are present in both animal and plant cell.

questions.question: The plastid responsible for fruit colour is :
questions.explanation: The colour of the plant is due to presence of different kinds of plastids. Chromoplast provides all colours except green and white.

questions.question: Which of the following occurs only in plant cell?
questions.explanation: Dictyosomes are present only in plant cell. Centrosomes is present in animals cells only and mitochondria and ribosomes are present in both plant and animal cell.

tests.name: Cell Structure & Functions Test 03
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Cell Structure and Functions CBSE Online Test 03 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Structures present within a cell having a specific function are called:
questions.explanation: The structures present within a cell having a specific function are called organelles. Different organelles are responsible for particular function.

questions.question: Nucleus of the cell was described by:
questions.explanation: The nucleus is called the brain of the cell. Nucleus of the cell was described by Robert Brown first inside the protoplasm.

questions.question: Suicide bags of the cell are:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes are called suicide bag of cell because it contain powerful enzyme that can break the cell completely.

questions.question: Chloroplast is found in

options.html: Plant cell only and Animal cell only

questions.explanation: Plant cell only

questions.question: Which of the following organism contains vacuole in its cell?
questions.explanation: Amoeba is a unicellular organism having irregular shape. Amoeba contains vacuole which contain digestive enzyme and help in excretion.

questions.question: Structure that provides shape to the cell is:
questions.explanation: Cell membrane is the outermost covering of animal cell. Cell membrane provides shape to the cell. It is made up of protein and lipid.

questions.question: Which of the following cells is the longest?
questions.explanation: Nerve cell is the longest cell present in living organism. This cell help in transfer of message from one part of the body to others.

questions.question: Nucleolus contains:
questions.explanation: Nucleolus is located inside the nucleus of the cell. Nucleolus consists of protein molecules.

questions.question: The pigment that traps solar energy is:
questions.explanation: Chlorophyll is present in chloroplast pigment that impart green colour to leaves and trap solar energy for photosynthesis.

questions.question: A porous thin layer covering the cell is:
questions.explanation: Plasma membrane is a porous thin layer covering the cell. It is made up of protein and lipid. Plasma membrane or cell membrane is selectively permeable membrane.

questions.question: Smallest unit that transfers characters from parents to next generation:
questions.explanation: The smallest unit that transfers characters from parents to next generation is gene. Gene are small segment of DNA present in Chromosome that determine one traits.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Plant can be tree, shrub or herb.
  2. Such categorisation of plants is based on leaf structure.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Plant can be divided into tree, shrubs and herb on the basis of morphological feature like stem, roots, and size. Categorisation is not based on type of leaf but overall morphological features.

questions.question: Virus is:
questions.explanation: Virus is acellular organism placed at the borderline of living and non-living. Virus behaves like living when inside the host body but appear like crystal outside the host.

questions.question: Flowers of any plant are its:
questions.explanation: Flowers of any plant are its organs. Flower is reproductive organ of plant. Stem, root, and leaves forms the vegetative organ of the plant.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. all cells are microscopic.
  2. cells are structural and functional units of life.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Some cells like ostrich egg and hen’s egg are quite big in structure but most of the cells are microscopic and need microscope to visualise it. Cells are structural and functional units of life.

tests.name: Cell Structure & Functions Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Cell Structure and Functions CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: It is difficult to view cells in a:
questions.explanation: To view cells a thin section which is transparent should be used. When slide is placed on stage of microscope light should cross it. Section should be stained as white colour is not visible in microscope.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. In multicellular organisms cells are different in their shapes.
  2. difference in cell shapes is related to their location.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: In multicellular organisms cells are different in their shapes and size. The difference in cell shapes is related to their function in the organism. Different cells are specialised to perform specific function.

questions.question: Projections appear and disappear as Amoeba:
questions.explanation: Amoeba is a unicellular organism that forms finger like projections on the body that help the organism to moves and feed. This projection helps in catching food and movement in particular direction.

questions.question: Chromosomes become visible when the cell:
questions.explanation: Chromosomes become visible more clearly when the cell divides. During cell division chromosome present in the nucleus condense to form particular structure which can be seen easily through microscope.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Plasma membrane separates one cell from the other.
  2. Plasma membrane allows movement of materials both outwards and inwards.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Plasma membrane is the outermost layer of cell that separates one cell from the other. Plasma membrane allows movement of materials both outwards and inwards selectively.

questions.question: Each cell is:
questions.explanation: Cell is structural and functional unit of all living organisms. Cell is self contained and self maintaining. Each cell possess different organelles that perform different functions

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Nucleus controls all functions of a cell.
  2. Nucleus is present inside nucleolus.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Nucleus controls all functions of a cell. So, nucleus is called as brain of the cell. Nucleolus is present inside nucleus along with chromosome.

questions.question: Plasmid can provide:
questions.explanation: Plasmid contains extra nuclear genetic material that provides resistance to antibiotics of certain types. Plasmids are present in bacteria.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Cell extracts energy from inorganic molecules.
  2. Cell extracts energy from organic molecules.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Cell obtain energy from organic molecules like glucose and fats. These organic molecules undergo oxidation in which energy is released in the form ATP inside the mitochondria.

questions.question: Division of labour is a characteristic feature of:
questions.explanation: In multicellular organisms, different organs are responsible to perform different function, called division of labour.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Hydrophobic fat molecules are present in plasma membrane.
  2. plasma membrane allows movement of only selective materials.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Hydrophobic fat molecules are present in plasma membrane. Plasma membrane allows movement of only selective materials so, it is selectively permeable.

questions.question: Chromosomes float freely in the cytoplasm of a/an:
questions.explanation: Chromosomes float freely in the cytoplasm of a bacterial cell. Bacterial cell do not possess nuclear membrane and chromosome float in cytoplasm.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Protoplasm is the living substance of cell.
  2. Protoplasm includes cytoplasm and blood plasma.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Protoplasm is the living substance of cell. Protoplasm includes cytoplasm and nucleus of the cell. Different kinds of cell organelles are present in the cytoplasm.

questions.question: Large vacuoles can be seen within:
questions.explanation: Plant cell contain single large vacuole in the centre of the cell. In animal cells, many small vacuole are present in the cytoplasm.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Chloroplasts are found in non green parts of plant.
  2. Chloroplasts help the plant in preparing lipids.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Chloroplasts are found in green parts of plant such as leaves that contain chlorophyll necessary for photosynthesis. Chloroplast help the plant in preparing glucose by the process of photosynthesis.

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Cell Structure and Functions CBSE Online Test 05 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: In multicellular organisms, the work is:
questions.explanation: In multicellular organisms, the work is divided. Division of labour is essential for proper functioning of multicellular organism.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Cell wall is made up of living material.
  2. Cell wall allows substances to pass freely.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Cell is the outermost layer of plant cell. Cell wall is made up of non-living material called cellulose. Cell wall allows substances to pass freely.

questions.question: Hydrolytic enzymes help in breaking down various materials so they are present in:
questions.explanation: Lysosomes contain hydrolytic enzymes that help in breaking down various materials. Lysosome is called the suicide bag of the cell.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. RBCs are the longest cell in the body.
  2. RBCs are red due to the presence of haemoglobin.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Nerve cell is the longest cell in the body that transfer message from one part of the body to other. Red blood cells (RBC’s) are red due to presence of haemoglobin.

questions.question: The cell organelle found to be present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells is:
questions.explanation: Ribosome is called protein factory of the cell. Ribosomes are present in both prokaryotic and eukaryotic cells.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Muscles facilitate movements.
  2. Muscles carry messages from different parts of the body to brain.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Muscles facilitate movements in the body. It contain proteins that contract and relax to cause movement. Nerve cells carry messages from different parts of the body to brain.

questions.question: Cell grows in size by:
questions.explanation: Cell grows in size by digesting food materials and using them to form different cell organelles including cell membrane.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Vacuole is filled with cell sap.
  2. Vacuole is not bound by any membrane.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Vacuole is filled with cell sap. Single large vacuole is present in plant cell and many small vacuole in animal cells. Vacuole is bound by single layered membrane celled tonoplast.

questions.question: Which of the following group of cells need regular replacement in our body?
questions.explanation: Blood cells need regular replacement in our body. Blood cells undergoes replacement in our body after fixed interval of time.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Fertilised egg divides to produce more number of cells.
  2. Fertilised egg multiplies to increase the number of cells

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Fertilised egg divides to produce more number of cells by mitotic division. Fertilised egg multiplies to increase the number of cells to form embryo.

questions.question: The structure of a living organism is determined by:

options.html: types of cells in its body
property of membrane that bounds cells in its body
size of cells in its body
organisation of cells in its body

questions.explanation: The structure of a living organism is determined by organisation of cells in its body. Cell organise in particular way to form tissue and organ of the living organism.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. all life functions take place in the nucleus.
  2. Nucleus stores genetic material.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Different functions taking place in cell is performed by different organelles. Mitochondria produce energy while ribosome help in producing protein and nucleus control the different functions.

questions.question: Energy production in the cell is the responsibility of:
questions.explanation: Mitochondria is called the powerhouse of the cell as energy production in the cell is the responsibility of mitochondria. In mitochondria, energy is produced in form of ATP.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. skin cells keep dividing to heal injuries.
  2. Injury in the nervous system is repaired by division in neurons.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Skin cells keep dividing to heal injuries and replace the old cells. Injuries in nervous system cannot be repaired as nervous tissues do not divide.

questions.question: Cell wall of bacteria contains:
questions.explanation: Cell of bacteria consists of peptidoglycan. This kind of glucose forms rigid structure around the bacterial body.

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Name an Organelle which serves as a primary packaging area for molecules that will be distributed throughout the cell?
questions.explanation: Plastids

questions.question: The structure that is/are porous?
questions.explanation: Both nuclear membrane and plasma membrane are porous and allow the entry and exit of substances in and out of the cell.

questions.question: In a human being life starts with:
questions.explanation: In human being life starts with fertilised egg. Mitotic division in zygote leads to formation of many cells that organise in particular way to form embryo.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. All cells are similar in their basic structure.
  2. All cells are identical in their function.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: All cells are similar in their basic structure initially but later change in shape and size to perform different functions. Different cells perform specialised functions.

questions.question: Scientists use dyes to study:
questions.explanation: To study the details of cells scientist use dyes. Dyes impart colours to the cells to visualise the different organelles properly.

questions.question: Amit observed various types of cells. He reported largest vacuoles in a:
questions.explanation: In plant cell single large vacuole is present in the cytoplasm. In animal cell many small vacuoles are present. So, largest vacuole is present in plant cell. Virus is acellular and lack vacuole.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Size of cells is related to size of the animal body.
  2. Size of cell is related to the function of that cell.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Size of the cell is related to size of the animal body. Multicellular organism contains many cells arranged to form organs. Different cells are specialised to perform particular function in the body of organism but not the size.

questions.question: Shreya tried to observe sections of cells under microscope but transparent sections were not visible so she viewed after:
questions.explanation: Size of the cell is related to size of the animal body. Multicellular organism contains many cells arranged to form organs. Different cells are specialised to perform particular function in the body of organisms but not the size.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Genes are located on ribosomes.
  2. Genes transfer characters from one generation to the next.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Genes are located on chromosome inside the nucleus of the cell. Genes are responsible for transfer of character from one generation to the next generation.

questions.question: In a large animal like elephant life begins with :
questions.explanation: The entire living organisms either small or large as elephant begin their life with single celled zygote. Zygote formed by fusion of male and female gametes.

questions.question:
Match the following:

i) Plasma membrane a) highly rigid
ii) Nucleus b) prokaryotic cell
iii) Capsule c) semi permeable
iv) Nucleoid d) freely permeable
v) Cell wall e) contains genetic material

options.html: i - c, ii - e, iii - a, iv - b, v - d
i - e, ii - c, iii - d, iv - a, v - b
i - d, ii - a, iii - b, iv - e, v - c
i - b, ii - d, iii - e, iv - c, v - a

questions.explanation: Plasma membrane is semi permeable membrane. Nucleus contains genetic material inside the cell. Capsule is a highly rigid structure to overcome unfavourable condition. Nucleoid is present in prokaryotic cell and cell wall is freely permeable.

questions.question: For a particular species, the _____ of chromosomes is fixed:
questions.explanation: For a particular species, the number of chromosome is fixed. Human cell contains 23 pairs of chromosome in all cells. Chromosome carry gene to next generation via gametes.

questions.question: The correct sequence of layers from outside to inside of the bacterial cell:

options.html: capsule {tex}\to{/tex} cell wall {tex}\to{/tex} plasma membrane
plasma membrane {tex}\to{/tex} capsule {tex}\to{/tex} cell wall
cell wall {tex}\to{/tex} plasma membrane {tex}\to{/tex} capsule
cell wall {tex}\to{/tex} capsule {tex}\to{/tex} plasma membrane

questions.explanation: In bacterial cell, capsule forms the outermost layer followed by cell wall towards inside. Plasma membrane forms the innermost layer of the cell encircling the cytoplasm.

questions.question: Lining of nose have hair like projections called _____ and their movement keeps dust and germs away:
questions.explanation: Nose have hair line projections in the inner side called cilia. The movement of cilia keeps dust and germs away during inhaling the air from outside.

questions.question:
Match the following:

i) Cell a) Hydra
ii) Unicellular b) HIV
iii) Multicellular c) Paramecium
iv) Acellular d) Bacteria
v) Prokaryote e) Building blocks
questions.explanation: Cell is the building blocks of living organism. Bacteria are a unicellular organism but hydra is a multicellular organism. HIV virus that cause AIDS is acellular and paramecium is prokaryotic organism

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Cell Structure and Functions CBSE Online Test 07 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Extra chromosomal DNA is found in prokaryotic cells is called:
questions.explanation: Extra chromosomal DNA is found in prokaryotic cells is called as plasmid. It contains circular DNA. It forms an important tool in biotechnology.

questions.question: Muscles have the ability to:
questions.explanation: Muscles have the ability to contract and relax to cause movement in the body of organism. Muscles attached with bone are mainly responsible for movement in body of animals.

questions.question: Studying minute details of cell became possible due to:
questions.explanation: Electron microscope magnify the size of the cell more than thousands times. It becomes possible to study the minute details cells due to electron microscope.

questions.question: Cells that cannot reproduce is/are:
questions.explanation: Red blood corpuscles and nerve cells are not able to reproduce. RBC’s are replaced in blood periodically and nerve cell increase in length but not in number.

questions.question:
Match the following:

i) Nerve a) false feet
ii) Bacteria b) largest cell
iii) Ostrich egg c) branched cell
iv) Pseudopodia d) blood cell
v) Erythrocyte e) cell wall
questions.explanation: Nerve cells are branched cell. Ostrich egg is the largest cell. Pseudopodia is also called as false feet present in amoeba. Erythrocyte is blood cell also called as RBC’s.

questions.question: Amisha observed a slide and saw a pair of guard cells in it. She is observing slide of:
questions.explanation: Guard cells are part of stomata through which exchange of gases and transpiration takes place in leaves of plant. The slide was of course plant leaf.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Plasma membrane is non flexible.
  2. Plasma membrane creates channels or pores for movement of substances.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Plasma membrane is flexible outer layer of animal cell. Plasma membrane is semi permeable and help in exchange of substances in and out of the cell due to presence of channels or pores form movement of substances.

questions.question: Ribosomes are not found in:
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are present in all type of cell that helps in protein synthesis. It is present in amoeba, blue green algae as well as white blood cells. Tobacco mosaic virus (TMV) is acellular structure that lack ribosome.

questions.question: Materials can move across cell membrane by:
questions.explanation: Transport of material across the cell membrane make takes place by active transport using energy or passive transport due to concentration gradient. Diffusion is also means of transport of water across the membrane.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. chromosomes are found in eukaryotic nucleus.
  2. all chromosomes are of same length and shape.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Chromosomes are present in the nucleus of eukaryotic cells. Number of chromosome varies from species to species. All chromosomes are of different length and shape.

questions.question: The smallest possible cell is:
questions.explanation: Pleuro pneumonia like organism (PPLO) is considered as smallest possible cell having fluid like body. It causes disease in plants.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. cytoplasm is involved in synthesis of useful materials.
  2. cytoplasm recycles unwanted materials for the cell.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Cytoplasm is involved in synthesis of useful materials as it contains different organelles to perform a different function. Cytoplasm recycles unwanted materials for the cell.

questions.question: Cell organelle better known as protein factory of the cell are:
questions.explanation: Ribosomes are known as protein factory of the cell because ribosome provides site for protein synthesis using mRNA and t-RNA.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. A cell cannot live long without a nucleus.
  2. Red blood cell of human body lack a nucleus.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: A cell cannot survive long without a nucleus. Nucleus is called brain of the cell. Mature red blood cell of human lacks nucleus, hence called as red blood corpuscles.

questions.question: Which of the following organism will have a cell wall?
questions.explanation: Mushroom belongs to fungus family. Fungi contain cell wall made up of chitin. Cell is absent in animals cells as it is absent in dog, cat, and euglena.

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Cell Structure and Functions CBSE Online Test 08 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.
