questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Tiny holes are present in the cell membrane.
  2. Cell membrane is absent in the prokaryotes.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Tiny pores are present in cell membrane or plasma membrane through which exchange of materials takes place in and out of the cell. Cell membrane is present of all kinds of cells prokaryotic as well as eukaryotic cell.

questions.question:
Match the following:

(i) Prokaryotic cell (a) cytoplasmic structure
(ii) Vacuole (b) cell with nuclear membrane
(iii) Chlorophyll (c) porous
(iv) nuclear membrane (d) cell without nuclear membrane
(v) Eukaryotic cell (e) green pigment
questions.explanation: Prokaryotic cells are cell without nuclear membrane. Vacuole is present in cytoplasm. Chlorophyll is green pigments that help in photosynthesis. Nuclear membrane is porous. Eukaryotic cell is with nuclear membrane.

questions.question: We need cell division for:
questions.explanation: Cell division is necessary for growth, repair, and maintenance of cell. Cell divide to increase in number and repairs of dead and injured cells.

questions.question: Plants lack a skeletal framework in their body but this function is compensated by:
questions.explanation: Skeletal framework is absent in plant body but rigidness in cell body is due to presence of cell wall which is made up of cellulose.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Golgi bodies pack and secrete enzymes.
  2. Golgi bodies are also called dictyosomes.

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Golgi bodies pack and secrete enzymes. In plant cell, Golgi bodies is also called as dictyosomes. It consist of spherical vesicle and cylindrical tubules called cistern.

questions.question: The peel of a potato contains:

options.html: cell wall and cell membranes of those cells

questions.explanation: The peel of potato contains cells. Potato tuber is underground stem. The outer layer of plant cell is cell wall made up of cellulose.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Centrosomes are absent in plant cells.
  2. Centrosomes regulate cell division.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Centrosomes are absent in plant cells that help in cell division. Centrosomes produces centrioles that attach with centromere of chromosome and pool it to opposite ends.

questions.question: If paramecium:: cilia, then euglena::?
questions.explanation: Paramecium contains small finger like projections called cilia all over the body that help them in movement. Euglena contains long hair like projection on body called flagella.

questions.question:
Read the following statements :

  1. Group of cells is called tissue.
  2. Tissues are cell aggregates that perform specific function

options.html: statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong
statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong

questions.explanation: Group of cells having common origin and performing specific function is called tissue. Different types of tissue joins together to form an organ and organ system.

questions.question: If study of microbes is microbiology then study of cell is:
questions.explanation: The study of microbes is called microbiology and branch of biology in which we study about cell is called as cytology.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. All cells in our body keep dividing.
  2. Cell division is important for growth and replacement of cells.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: In early stage of development, all cells in the body are meristematic and keeps dividing but after maturity a few cells divide continuously throughout the life. Cell division is important for growth and replacement of cells.

questions.question: If cell membrane:: skin then Nucleus::?
questions.explanation: Cell membrane controls the entry and exit of substances in and out of the cell as skin protects the body. Nucleus is called brain of the cell as it controls the functioning cell.

questions.question:
Read the following statements:

  1. Amoeba and Paramecium contain contractile vacuoles.
  2. Such vacuoles help to accumulate excess food.

options.html: statement ii) is correct but i) is wrong
statement i) is correct but ii) is wrong

questions.explanation: Amoeba and paramecium contains contractile vacuoles that perform different functions. Such vacuoles help in excretion of undigested food materials.

questions.question: Vacuoles can store:
questions.explanation: vacuoles can store food, water, and wastes. Single large vacuole is present in plant cell but many small vacuoles are present in animal cells.

questions.question: Shubham was surprised to see the plastids providing colour to leaves. He discovered its name to be :
questions.explanation: Plastids provide colours to green colour to leaves. Plastids that provide green colour to leaf is called chloroplast that contains chlorophyll for photosynthesis. Chloromoplast impart all other colours except green.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Type of reproduction in which fusion of male and female gametes occur is called
questions.explanation: The fusion of male and female gametes takes place during sexual reproduction. Fusion results to the formation of zygote that is first cell of any living organism.

questions.question: In asexual reproduction
questions.explanation: In asexual reproduction only one parent is involved. The new organism is identical to parent. No gametes formation takes place in asexual reproduction.

questions.question: Human male germ cell is called
questions.explanation: Human male germ cell is called sperm. Sperm is produced inside the testes of male. Sperm contain head and long tail that help them to swim.

questions.question: Every month ______________mature egg(s) are released from ovary.
questions.explanation: In human female, at puberty one mature egg is released from ovary every month. Ovulation takes place in the middle of menstrual cycle.

questions.question: Internal fertilization do not occurs in
questions.explanation: The mode of fertilization in which fusion of male and female gamete takes place inside the body of female is called internal fertilization. In fish fertilization occurs outside the body of female fish.

questions.question: The transformation of larva into adults through drastic change is called
questions.explanation: The process of transfer of larva into adults through drastic change in their body is called metamorphosis. Larva changes into totally different shape and size through metamorphosis.

questions.question: The age between 9 years to 14 years is called
questions.explanation: The age between 9 years to 14 years is called puberty. During this age, a number of physiological and morphological changes take place in the children.

questions.question: Fusion of male and female gamete is called
questions.explanation: The process of fusion of male and female gamete is called fertilization. Fertilization may be internal or external depending upon organism.

questions.question: Budding is found in
questions.explanation: Budding is a mode of asexual reproduction in which a small outgrowth develop on body surface that enlarge and get separated from parent body. Budding takes place in hydra and yeast.

questions.question: During mitosis

options.html: One cell enlarge into one cell
One cell divide into four cells
One cells divide into eight cells
One cell divide into two cells

questions.explanation: Mitosis is the mode of cell division in which one cell divide into two cells. The number of chromosome remains same after mitosis division.

questions.question: Puberty ends when an adolescent reaches
questions.explanation: The age at which boys and girls start producing male and female gametes is called puberty. Puberty end when an adolescent reaches reproductive maturity.

questions.question: Process of formation of spermatozoa is called
questions.explanation: The formation of spermatozoa is called spermatogenesis. Spermatogenesis occurs in testes of male at puberty to form male gametes.

questions.question: The testes produce male gametes called
questions.explanation: Testes is primary sex organ of male in which sperms are produced at maturity. Sperms are male gametes produced in large number for fertilization.

questions.question: Fusion of male and female gametes produces
questions.explanation: The process in which fusion of male and female gamete takes place is called fertilization. Fertilization leads to formation of single cell called zygote.

questions.question: Which of the following can prevent sexually transmitted disease?
questions.explanation: The disease which can be transmitted from one person to other person during sexual intercourse is called sexually transmitted disease. Condom can prevent STD’s as well as unwanted pregnancy.

questions.question: Fallopian tube is also known as
questions.explanation: Fallopian tube is a long duct that transfer ovum from ovary to uterus. Fallopian tube is also known as oviduct.

questions.question: The thread like structure present in nucleus of cell to carry traits to next generation is called
questions.explanation: Chromosomes is thread like structure present in nucleus of cell to carry traits or genes from one generation to next generation. Each organism contains certain number of chromosome.

questions.question: In yeast, asexual reproduction takes place by
questions.explanation: In yeast, asexual reproduction takes place by budding. During budding small outgrowth is formed that enlarge and separate from parent body.

questions.question: Female reproductive system contains ______ of ovaries.
questions.explanation: Female reproductive system contains a pair of ovaries. Ovaries produce female gamete called ovum and secrete female hormone.

questions.question: Which sex organ of male produce sperms?
questions.explanation: Testis is the primary sex organ of male that produces sperm. A pair of testes is located in scrotum descending form abdominal cavity.

questions.question: How many mature egg/s are released every month from ovaries in human beings.
questions.explanation: In human female one mature egg is released every month from ovaries in the middle of menstrual cycle.

questions.question: The copulatory organ human male is called
questions.explanation: The process during which male release their sperm in genital organ of female is called copulation. The copulatory organ of human male is called penis.

questions.question: The unfertilized egg always has ________ chromosome.
questions.explanation: The unfertilized egg always has one X chromosome because egg is formed by meiosis cell division in which number of chromosome reduces to half.

questions.question: Development of baby takes place inside
questions.explanation: Development of baby takes place inside the uterus of female. Fertilized egg attach to wall of uterus with the help of placenta to supply nutrient and oxygen to foetus.

questions.question: Sperm and ovum are
questions.explanation: Sperm is male gamete while ovum is female gamete produce by meiosis cell division. Both sperm and ovum are single celled that fuse to produce zygote.

questions.question: Which of the following fertilize internally?
questions.explanation: When fertilization takes place inside the female body, it is called internal fertilization. In human beings, fertilization takes place in fallopian tube of female.

questions.question: First cell that begin the life of an organism is called
questions.explanation: Zygote is the first cell that begins the life of an organism. Zygote is formed by fusion between male and female gamete.

questions.question: The first cloned sheep was named
questions.explanation: The first cloned sheep was named Dolly. Cloning is the technique in which identical organism is produced asexually.

questions.question: Fertilization which takes place inside the female body is called
questions.explanation: The fertilization which takes place inside the female body is called as internal fertilization because development to new organism takes place safely inside the female body.

questions.question: In which of the following organism fertilization occurs outside the body?
questions.explanation: In frog, fertilization takes place outside the body of female. Female release their egg and male release sperm in water that fertilize the female gamete to produce zygote.

questions.question: In human beings, fertilization occurs inside
questions.explanation: In human beings, fertilization occurs inside the oviduct or fallopian tube. Oviduct carries egg from ovary to uterus.

questions.question: In 28 day human ovarian cycle, ovulation occurs on
questions.explanation: In 28 days human ovarian cycle, ovulation occurs on 14th day. As ovulation takes place in the middle of menstrual cycle.

questions.question: The stage of embryo in which all the body parts can be identified is called a
questions.explanation: The developmental stage of embryo in which all the body parts can be identified is called as foetus. Different organs differentiate at different time interval.

questions.question: The process of formation of ova is called
questions.explanation: Oogenesis is the process through which ova or the female gametes are formed and this female gamete is known as an ovum.

questions.question: The animals which give birth to young ones are called
questions.explanation: Those animals which give birth to young ones are called viviparous organism. Human beings, dogs, horse etc are example of viviparous organisms.

questions.question: Which of the following organ is called birth canal?
questions.explanation: Vagina is called as birth canal as fully mature baby after completion of pregnancy come out through vagina of female. Expansion of vagina is under control of hormone.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a viviparous organism?
questions.explanation: Cow, buffalo, and elephant give birth to young ones, so they are viviparous organisms. Hen lay an egg, hence hen is an oviparous organism.

questions.question: Reproductive cells are known as
questions.explanation: Cow, buffalo, and elephant give birth of young ones, so they are viviparous organisms. Hen lay egg, hence hen is a oviparous organism.

questions.question: First milk after parturition is called
questions.explanation: First milk after parturition is called colostrum. Colostrum contains a number of diseases fighting substance that provide immunity to children.

questions.question: Life cycle of which of the following organism shows metamorphosis?
questions.explanation: Life cycle of frog shows metamorphosis. During metamorphosis frog tadpole having fish like structure changes into frog, a totally new organism.

questions.question: Which one is larger in size?
questions.explanation: Ovum is larger than sperm. Ovum contains nutrients required for further growth of embryo after fertilization. Sperm is comparatively smaller having tail.

questions.question: Which of the following organism reproduce by budding?
questions.explanation: Budding is an asexual mode of reproduction in which a small outgrowth is formed on the body that increase in size and separate from parent body as new individual as in hydra.

questions.question: The mode of fertilisation in frog is
questions.explanation: The mode of fertilization in which fertilization of male and female gametes takes place outside the female body. In frog, fertilization takes place external.

questions.question: Binary fission is seen in
questions.explanation: The mode of asexual reproduction in which unicellular organism divide into two daughter cells. Amoeba reproduces by binary fission.

questions.question: The reproductive organs of female includes
questions.explanation: The reproductive organs of female include a pair of ovaries, a pair of oviduct or fallopian tube, and a uterus.

questions.question: The main function of testis is
questions.explanation: The main function of testis is to release sperm, male gamete. Along with sperm testis also release male hormone that produce secondary male characters.

questions.question: Fertilisation that takes place outside the female body is called
questions.explanation: The fertilization that takes place outside the female body is called external fertilization. Male and female gametes are released outside the body and fuse in external environment.

questions.question: The zygote divide repeatedly to give rise to an
questions.explanation: The fusion of male and female gamete leads to formation of zygote. Zygote divide repeatedly to give rise to an embryo.

questions.question: Vagina of the female reproductive system is
questions.explanation: Vagina of the female reproductive system is secondary sex organ. Vagina is called birth canal as fully mature baby comes out through vagina. Ovaries are the primary sex organ.

questions.question: The animals which lay eggs are called
questions.explanation: The animals which lay eggs are called oviparous animals. The animals that produce young ones are called viviparous animals.

questions.question: In which of the following organisms, multiple fissions occur?
questions.explanation: Multiple fission the mode of asexual reproduction in which single cell divides into more than two cells. Plasmodium reproduces by multiple fission.

questions.question: Internal fertilisation occurs
questions.explanation: Internal fertilization occurs inside the female body. Male gamete is transferred inside the female body during copulation where gametes fuse to produce zygote.

questions.question: A tadpole develops into an adult frog by the process of
questions.explanation: A tadpole develops into an adult frog by the process of metamorphosis. A tadpole resemble to fish having tail that undergoes morphological change to change into frog.

questions.question: The number of nuclei present in a zygote is
questions.explanation: Zygote is formed by fusion of male and female gametes. Each zygote contains one nuclei having 2n number of chromosome. Zygote is the first cell of life.

questions.question: The term used for bulges on the sides of the body of hydra is
questions.explanation: Hydra produces bulges on the side of the body called as bud. Bud develops into new individual.

questions.question: The process of producing exact copy of a cell or other living part or an complete organism is called
questions.explanation: The process of producing exact copy of a cell or other living part or a complete organism is called clone. Clone is identical to parent.

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary sex organ?
questions.explanation: Ovary is primary sex organ of female. Ovary produces ovum and female hormone estrogen and progesterone. Uterus, vagina, and fallopian tube are accessory sex organs.

questions.question: Ovulation occurs on the ______ day of menstrual cycle.
questions.explanation: Ovulation occurs on the 14-16th day of menstrual cycle. Each month one ovary produces one mature egg in the middle of menstrual cycle.

questions.question: Hormone responsible for milk ejection after the birth of baby is:-
questions.explanation: Once the baby is born, oxytocin promotes lactation by moving the milk into the breast. When the baby sucks at the mother's breast, oxytocin secretion causes the milk to release so the baby can feed. At the same time, oxytocin is released into the brain to stimulate further oxytocin production.

questions.question: The embryo development from the somatic cells is called
questions.explanation: The embryo development from the somatic cells is called embyroid. Somatic cells are diploid cells that form embryo without fertilization.

questions.question: Menstrual cycle is repeated at an average interval of
questions.explanation: Menstrual cycle is repeated at an average interval of 29 days. In the middle of menstrual cycle egg is released.

questions.question: Cessation of menstrual cycle in female is known as
questions.explanation: Cessation of menstrual cycle in female is known as menopause. Menopause occurs at the age of about 50 years.

questions.question: The process of delivery of the foetus is called
questions.explanation: The process of delivery of the foetus is called parturition. Parturition occurs at the end of pregnancy period of 40 weeks.

questions.question: The embryo at 16-celled stage is called
questions.explanation: The embryo at 16-celled stage is called morula. Implantation of embryo in female ovary takes place at this stage.

questions.question: Conversion of spematid into a spermatozoan is called
questions.explanation: Conversion of spematids present inside the testis into spermatozoa is called spermiogenesis.

questions.question: In most mammals, the testes are located in scrotal sac for
questions.explanation: In most mammals, the testes are located in scrotal sac for formation of sperm inside the testis. Testis require 2-3 degree less temperature than body temperature.

questions.question: The release of egg from ovum is described as
questions.explanation: The release of egg from ovum is described as ovulation. Ovulation occurs in the middle of menstrual cycle form each ovary each month.

questions.question: In the male human, sperm contain 22 pairs of autosomes and
questions.explanation: A human cell containing 22 autosomes and a chromosome is a sperm. A human sperm contains either an X or a Y chromosome, thereby determining the sex of the offspring after fertilization. XX = female. XY = male.

questions.question: Foetal ejection reflex in human female is induced by

options.html: Release of Oxytocin from pituitary gland

questions.explanation: After the completion of pregnancy period, foetal ejection reflex is induced due to release of oxytocin from pituitary gland to relax the vagina.

questions.question: The _________ is the sexual intercourse between male and female sex.
questions.explanation: The coitus is the sexual intercourse between male and female sex. During sexual intercourse, male gamete is released into female genital organ for fertilization.

questions.question: The period between fertilization and birth is called as
questions.explanation: The period between fertilization and birth of fully mature child is called gestation. Gestation period varies from animal to animal. In human beings, gestation period is 40 weeks.

questions.question: Which hormone controls metamorphosis in frogs?
questions.explanation: Metamorphosis is the morphological change in tadpole into adult frog. Metamorphosis requires hormone thyroxin to undergo metamorphosis.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct statement?

options.html: The mother is responsible for the sex of child
Ovulation occurs in the middle of menstrual cycle
Menstruation starts at the age of 25
Boys attain puberty earlier than girls

questions.explanation: Ovulation occurs in the middle of menstrual cycle i.e. on 14th day of menstrual cycle during which one ovum is released from ovary and travel towards uterus.

questions.question: Which one is the most likely reason of not occurring regular menstrual cycle

options.html: Maintenance of high concentration of sex hormones

questions.explanation: Irregular menstrual cycle may be due to a number of hormonal or physiological reasons but most likely reason of not occurring regular menstrual cycle is fertilization of ovum.

questions.question: The yellowish coloured milk secreted from the breast shortly after birth of the baby is called
questions.explanation: The yellow coloured milk secreted from the breast shortly after birth of the baby is called colostrum. This milk provide immunity to child to protect against a number of diseases.

questions.question: What is the number of sperms released in vagina in single insemination?
questions.explanation: During copulation insemination occurs in which male release about 400 millions of sperms present in semen.

questions.question: External genitalia of human female is called as
questions.explanation: External genitalia of human female are called as Vulva. Vulva are muscular fatty structure having fold of skin from outer side that leads to vagina.

questions.question: Which one is not correct about the function of placenta?
questions.explanation: Placenta is physical connection between mother and growing foetus for transport of nutrient and gases. It is not responsible for releasing progestron hormone.

questions.question: The secretory phase of in the human menstrual cycle is called as
questions.explanation: Secretory phase in human female menstrual cycle is called as luteal phase. During this phase blood and mucus comes out through vagina.

questions.question: Phenomenal and rapid increase of population in a short period is called
questions.explanation: Phenomenal and rapid increase of population in a short period is called as population explosion. Population explosion leads to shortage of food and other substances.

questions.question: Copper-T prevents from
questions.explanation: Copper –T is an intrauterine contraceptive device that helps in contraception. It is implanted in uterus by trained nurse or doctor. It prevents the fertilization of male and female gametes.

questions.question: Two hormones that regulate the menstrual cycle are
questions.explanation: Menstrual cycle in female is regulated by hormone estrogens and progesterone. Menstrual cycle is a 28-29 days cycle during which ovulation occurs in middle of this cycle.

questions.question: The opening of vagina is often covered partially by a membrane called
questions.explanation: The opening of vagina is often covered partially by a membrane called hyemen. This membrane broke during first sexual intercourse.

questions.question: Reproduction is essential life processes which help in
questions.explanation: Reproduction is essential life processes which help in continuity of race as the life span of each individual if fixed after which organism dies.

questions.question: Which of the following organism do shows binary fission
questions.explanation: Binary fission takes place in amoeba during which amoeba divides into two daughter cells by simple meiotic division.

questions.question: Fertilisation is external in
questions.explanation: The mode of fertilization in which fusion of male and female gametes takes place outside the female body. External fertilization occurs in fish and amphibians.

questions.question: Hydra reproduce asexually by formation of
questions.explanation: Hydra reproduces asexually by formation of small outgrowth on the body that enlarge and separate out as new species called budding.

questions.question: The living organism can be unexceptionally differ from non-living on the basis of
questions.explanation: The living organism unexceptionally differs from non-living on the basis of interaction with environment and evolution. Nonliving do not shows stimulus.

questions.question: All organism reproduce sexually after certain growth and maturity. That period of growth is called
questions.explanation: All organism reproduce sexually after certain growth and maturity. That period of growth is called growth phase. After growth phase reproductive phase started.

questions.question: In non-primitive mammals like cow, sheep, rats, dogs etc., such cyclic changes during reproduction are called
questions.explanation: The oestrous cycle is the reproductive system in most mammals. In non-primitive mammals like cow, sheep, rats, dogs etc., such cyclic changes during reproduction are called oestrous cycle.

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Reproduction in Animals CBSE Online Test 07 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Amoeba is called immortal because it _______

options.html: Completely divides into two daughter cells

questions.explanation: Amoeba reproduces asexually by binary fission in which amoeba divides into two daughter cells. Amoeba become immortal since there is no natural death of amoeba.

questions.question: Sexual reproduction is better mode of reproduction because offspring
questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction is better mode of reproduction because offspring have better chances of survival. Sexual reproduction leads to creation of variation that help in survival of species.

questions.question: Every sexually reproducing organism, including human being begin life as a single cell called
questions.explanation: Zygote is formed by fusion of male and female gametes. Every sexually reproducing organism including human beings begin life as single cell called zygote.

questions.question: The chances of survival of young one is greater in __________ organism
questions.explanation: Viviparous organism produce young one. The chance of survival of young one is greater than egg producing organism. As eggs can be destroyed by predator and harsh environmental condition.

questions.question: Insemination involves

options.html: Fusion of male and female gamete
Release of seminal fluid at the end of copulation
Passage of semen into prostate gland
Mixing of seminal fluid with sperm

questions.explanation: Insemination involves release of seminal fluid at the end of copulation. Seminal fluid contains millions of sperms for fertilization.

questions.question: Ovulation in the females normally takes place during the menstrual cycle
questions.explanation: Ovulation is the release of ovum from ovary which normally takes place during the middle menstrual cycle, 14th day of menstrual cycle.

questions.question: Which period of menstrual cycle is called risky period of conception?
questions.explanation: 10th to 17th day of menstrual cycle is called risky period of conception because ovulation takes place in the middle of menstrual cycle and coitus during this period may leads to pregnancy.

questions.question: Which one is not a sexually transmitted disease?
questions.explanation: The disease that can be transmitted from one partner to other partner during sexual intercourse is called sexually transmitted disease. AIDS, syphilis and genital herpes are sexually transmitted disease.

questions.question: In IVF technique, fusion of ovum and sperm take place in
questions.explanation: The couple which are not able to reproduce uses IVF technique in which eggs and sperms collected from donor or self is fertilized in culture medium and fertilized egg is implanted in uterus of female.

questions.question: During the process of cloning Dolly, a cell was collected from the __________ of a female Finn Dorsett sheep.
questions.explanation: During the process of cloning Dolly, a cell was collected from the mammary gland of a female Finn Dorsett sheep.

questions.question: Which one of the following technique is used in test-tube baby programme

options.html: Zygote Intra Fallopian transfer or Gamete intra fallopian transfer

questions.explanation: In test-tube baby programme either ZIFT (Zygote Intra Fallopian transfer) or GIFT (Gamete intra fallopian transfer) technique is used. ZIFT (Zygote Intra Fallopian Transfer), in which embryos up to 8 blastomeres are transferred into the Fallopian tubes.  GIFT is an assisted reproductive procedure which involves removing a woman’s eggs, mixing them with sperm, and immediately placing them into a fallopian tube.

questions.question: Viability period of human ovum is ______
questions.explanation: The periods for which human ovum remain viable is called viability period. This period is for 2 days. After 2 days ovum degenerate.

questions.question: Greatest biological problems faced by human being is
questions.explanation: Population explosion is the greatest problem that human being is facing nowadays due to increased medical facilities and lower mortality rate.

questions.question: The features that help to distinguish the male from female are called
questions.explanation: Male and female can be distinguished from each other externally by secondary sexual characters such as breast, mustache, beard, etc.

questions.question: The sex of child depends on the
questions.explanation: Male produce two kinds of sperms, half containing X chromosome and half contain Y chromosome. The sperm that fertise the egg of female decides the sex of child. X cromosome is responsible for the development of female and Y cromosome is responsible for male .

questions.question: Female hired to develop the in-vitro fertilized egg of another female to maturity is called
questions.explanation: Surrogacy is a method or agreement whereby a woman agrees to carry a pregnancy for another person or persons, who will become the newborn child's parent(s) after birth. The female hired to develop the in-vitro fertilized egg of another female to maturity is called surrogate mother.

questions.question: In human female, menstruation can be deferred by the administration of
questions.explanation: In human female, menstruation can be deferred by the administration of combination of estrogen and progesterone hormone as pills to avoid pregnancy.

questions.question: Inability to conceive or produce children even after two years of unprotected sexual co-habitation is called
questions.explanation: Inability to conceive or produce children even after 2 years of unprotected sexual cohabitation is called infertility. A large no of couples all over India are infertile. The reasons for this could be many-physical, congenital, diseases, drugs, Immunological, or even Psychological.

questions.question: Many people get acne and pimples on the face at puberty because of

options.html: Increased activity of sweat gland and oil gland

questions.explanation: Many morphological and physical changes take place in boys and girls at puberty. Many persons develop acne and pimples on the face at puberty due to increased activity of sweat gland and oil gland.

questions.question: It is misconception that

options.html: Sex is determined by vigor of male
Male are responsible of sex of child
Women are responsible for sex of child

questions.explanation: Misconception is wrong believing spread in society. It is a misconception that women are responsible for sex of child, although, father is responsible for sex of child.  There are two types of sex chromosomes X and Y chromosomes. Female cell contains the XX pair and the male contains XY pair. A human sperm contains either an X or a Y chromosome, thereby determining the sex of the offspring after fertilization. XX = female. XY = male.

questions.question: The growing foetus draws its nourishment through the
questions.explanation: The growing foetus gets its nourishment through placenta. Placenta is a tube like structure that connects foetus with mother uterus.

questions.question: Sperms are inserted by the male reproductive organ into the
questions.explanation: Sperms are deposited by the male reproductive organ into the vagina of female during copulation.

questions.question: Which of the following is true about asexual reproduction?
questions.explanation: Asexual reproduction involves only one parent. The new individuals are identical to their parents.

questions.question: The common duct formed by union of vas deference and duct from seminal vesicle is called
questions.explanation: The common duct formed by union of vas deference and duct from seminal vesicle is called urethra. Urethra is common duct for release of urine and semen during copulation.

questions.question: In mammals testis is located in
questions.explanation: In mammals testis is located in extra-abdominal cavity in scrotum. Testes require 2-3 degree less temperature than body temperature to produce sperm.

questions.question: The delivery of child or child birth is called
questions.explanation: The delivery of child or child birth is called parturition. Parturition occurs at the completion of gestation period through vagina.

questions.question: Human embryo completes development in
questions.explanation: Human embryo completes development in 266 days. A different organ of foetus is developed at different interval of time. Heart is formed first.

questions.question: Placenta is

options.html: Channel for providing essential requirement for growth of embryo

questions.explanation: Placenta is channel for providing essential requirement for growth of embryo. Through placenta nutrients and gases are transferred to foetus from mother.

questions.question: Seminal vessels is present at the junction of
questions.explanation: A seminal vessel is present at the junction of prostate and urethra. This vessel release fluid medium and nutrient for sperm movement.

questions.question: Which one is the correct sequence of steps involved in sexual reproduction
questions.explanation: During sexual reproduction gametogenesis occurs to produce gametes by meiosis cell division. Gamete is transferred to female body during copulation that leads to fertilization.

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Reaching the Age of Adolescence CBSE Online Test 01 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

questions.question: The age at which boys and girls start producing gametes is called__________.
questions.explanation: The age at which boys and girls start producing gametes is called puberty. A number of changes take place in the body at puberty. On average, girls begin puberty around ages 10 –11 and end puberty around 15 -17; boys begin around ages 11–12 and end around 16-17.

questions.question: The right meal for adolescents consists of

options.html: Vegetable cutlets, chips and lemon drink

questions.explanation: The right meals for adolescents include chapatti, dal, Vegetable etc. The meal should provide carbohydrates, proteins and fats along with vitamins for proper growth and development. One should take balanced diet during puberty.

questions.question: Which among the boys and girls puberty starts earlier?
questions.explanation: Puberty starts early in girls in comparison to boys of the same age. In girls, it starts at the age of around 12-13 years.

questions.question: Deficiency of which hormone cause diabetes?
questions.explanation: Deficiency of insulin hormone causes diabetes. Insulin converts the excess sugar in the blood into glycogen to lower the sugar level in blood.

questions.question: Which one is another terms for teenage?
questions.explanation: Adolescence is another term for teenage. The age between 12-19 years is called as teenage. During this period many physiological changes take place in the body.

questions.question: Adolescence begins around the age of______ years.
questions.explanation: Adolescence begins around the age of 11 years. The reproductive growth starts at this age to brings about much change in boys and girls.

questions.question: Glands without ducts?
questions.explanation: Endocrine glands are ductless glands which secrete hormones. Hormones are chemical messengers secreted by endocrine glands directly into the blood.

questions.question: Which gland release insulin hormone?
questions.explanation: Insulin hormone is released by the pancreas. Insulin controls the sugar level in the blood to avoid high blood pressure and heart disease.

questions.question: Thyroxin produced by
questions.explanation: Thyroid gland

questions.question: Reproductive phase in women continues for how many decades?
questions.explanation: Four

questions.question: AIDS is caused by a________.
questions.explanation: Acquired immune deficiency syndrome (AIDS) is caused by a virus called human immune virus (HIV). AIDS is a sexually transmitted disease.

questions.question: Pancreas secretes which of the following hormones?
questions.explanation: Insulin

questions.question: Hormone reaches here through the bloodstream?
questions.explanation: Hormones are released by endocrine glands. The hormone reaches to target site through the bloodstream.

questions.question: Which gland is called dual gland?
questions.explanation: The testis is called dual gland as testis produces sperms as well as male hormone testosterone.

questions.question: The most conspicuous visible change that occurs in boys during puberty is:
questions.explanation: The most clearly visible change during puberty is the sudden increase in height of boys and girls. Appearance of moustache and beard in boys and enlargement of the mammary gland in girls are other changes.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which of the following is a pancreatic hormone?
questions.explanation: Insulin is released from pancreas so, insulin is a pancreatic hormone. This hormone controls the sugar level in blood in case of excess sugar in blood.

questions.question: Which gland is called master gland.
questions.explanation: Pituitary gland is called as master gland because it controls the function of all other glands. Although, pituitary gland is itself under the control of hypothalamus.

questions.question: Height of a person depends on __________ inherited from parents.
questions.explanation: All the traits of a person depend upon the gene inherited by the person from their parents. Height is also controlled by a gene inherited by a person from their parents. Genes are carried by the chromosomes present in cells.

questions.question: Which of the following can prevent sexually transmitted disease?
questions.explanation: The disease transmitted from infected person to healthy person during sexual intercourse is called sexually transmitted disease. Condom provide protection against STD’s as well as pregnancy.

questions.question: Endocrine gland attached to brain is:
questions.explanation: Pituitary gland is an endocrine gland that remains attached to the brain. Pancreatic gland, gastric gland, and seminal gland are situated in the pancreas, stomach, and urethra respectively.

questions.question: Adam is:
questions.explanation: The protruded voice box in male is called as Adam’s apple. Voice box is situated just behind this protrusion to produce sound.

questions.question: During adolescence we should use:
questions.explanation: Adolescence is the most important phase of life during which lots of physical and mental changes takes place. Balanced diet should be taken during this period for proper growth and development.

questions.question: At puberty, the voice box begins to_______.

options.html: Increase male but decrease in female

questions.explanation: Voicebox begins to increase in size at puberty and appear as outgrowth in the neck in the male only.

questions.question: Puberty is the:
questions.explanation: A number of changes take place at puberty in boys and girls associated with starting to produce male and female gametes. In girls, ovulation starts at puberty.

questions.question: Thyroid gland is present in______.
questions.explanation: Thyroid gland is present in the neck of human beings. Thyroid gland release hormone thyroxin that helps in the metabolism of carbohydrate, fat, and protein.

questions.question: The thread like structure present in the nucleus of the cell to carry traits to the next generation is called
questions.explanation: Chromosomes are thread like structure present in the nucleus of the cell to carry traits from one generation to the next generation. Chromosomes are present in the nucleus of the cell.

questions.question: Moustache and beards in boys are
questions.explanation: A number of changes take place in boys at puberty that includes secondary sexual characteristics like the appearance of moustache and beard on face.

questions.question: The protruding part of the throat in male is called________ apple.
questions.explanation: The protruding part of the throat in male is called Adam’s apple. It contains a voice box or larynx that produce sound.

questions.question: Most of the drugs are:
questions.explanation: Drugs are addictive in nature and a person start taking it to make habitual to take it.

questions.question: The unfertilized egg always has _________ chromosome.
questions.explanation: Unfertilized egg always contains one X-chromosome. Gametes are formed by meiosis cell division. Female contain two X chromosome that separates during meiosis.

questions.question: Sebaceous gland is also known as
questions.explanation: Sebaceous gland is also known as oil gland. Sebaceous gland is present in skin that release oily substance to make skin moist.

questions.question: Leaves or fruits fall on the ground due to
questions.explanation: gravitational force

questions.question: A sperm would contain the following chromosome?
questions.explanation: Sperm is the male gamete formed by meiotic division inside the testis. Two kinds of sperms are formed by a male, half containing X and half containing the Y chromosome.

questions.question: Which of the following glands are called ductless glands?
questions.explanation: Endocrine glands are called ductless gland because it does not contain duct to carry hormone to the target site. The hormones release from endocrine glands reaches to target site through the blood.

questions.question: In general, boys and girls have a
questions.explanation: In general, boys and girls have a high pitch voice in comparison to adults. We easily identify the voice of children from adults due to their high pitch.

questions.question: The hormone which brings about characteristic changes in male at puberty is called_______.
questions.explanation: Testosterone hormone is released by the testis of male. This hormone brings about changes like the appearance of moustache, beard at puberty.

questions.question: Many people get acne and pimples on the face at puberty because of:

options.html: Increased activity of sweat gland and oil gland

questions.explanation: Many people get acne and pimples on the face at puberty because of increased activity of sweat gland and oil gland in their skin.

questions.question: An organ where a hormone shows its effect is called_________.
questions.explanation: Hormones are released from endocrine glands, which is a ductless gland. The organ where a hormone shows its effect is called a target site.

questions.question: At puberty, male begins to produce_________.
questions.explanation: Ovum to produce zygote.

questions.question: The age between 13 years to 19 years is called__________.
questions.explanation: The years during adolescence period are counted as ‘teen’, i.e., 13 to 19 years of age. Hence, this period is also called teenage.

questions.question: The features that help to distinguish the male from female are called___________.
questions.explanation: The sexual characteristics controlled by hormones which distinguish between sexually mature males and female but are not directly involved in reproduction are called secondary sexual characteristics.

questions.question: Which of the following is the secondary sexual characteristics of male only?
questions.explanation: Pimples, body hairs and oily skin are the common secondary sexual characteristics of male and female but moustaches and beard is a secondary sexual characteristics of male only.

questions.question: The changes which occurs at adolescence are controlled by___________.
questions.explanation: The changes which occur at adolescence are controlled by hormones released from primary sex organs like testis in male and ovary in female.

questions.question: The hormones released by testes in male is called___________.
questions.explanation: Testis is the primary sex organ of the male which release sperm as well as hormone testosterone that brings about a secondary sexual character in male.

questions.question: Progesterone level falls during
questions.explanation: Menopause is the stage of female life at which she stops producing ovum from ovary. During menopause progesterone level falls drastically.

questions.question: Which gland develops inside the breast of girls?
questions.explanation: Inside the breast of girls, mammary gland develops that produces milk after parturition. Mammary glands increase in size at puberty.

questions.question: Beginning of menstrual cycle at puberty is called
questions.explanation: Menarche meaning men "month" + arkhe "beginning") is the first menstrual cycle, or first menstrual bleeding, in female humans.

questions.question: In females, the reproductive phase of life begins at________.
questions.explanation: In females, the reproductive phase of life begins at puberty because at puberty female start producing ovum from their ovary.

questions.question: Which sex organ of male produce sperms?
questions.explanation: Testis is the primary sex organ of the male. It is located in scrotum outside the abdominal cavity. Sperms are produced by testis at puberty.

questions.question: Fusion of male and female gametes to produce zygote is called________.
questions.explanation: The process of fusion of male and female gametes to produce zygote is called fertilization. In human beings fertilization occurs in the fallopian tube of female.

questions.question: Loss of reproductive capacity in woman after age of 45 years is called__________.
questions.explanation: Loss of reproductive capacity in women after attaining the age of 45 years is called menopause. After menopause, the ovum is not produced and the menstrual cycle gets stopped.

questions.question: All human beings have ___________ of chromosomes.
questions.explanation: All human beings have 23 pairs of chromosomes as the names X and Y. A female has two X chromosomes, while the male has one X and one Y chromosomes.

questions.question: Which of the following is secondary sexual character?
questions.explanation: In secondary sexual characteristics, the body parts develop special features which make it easier to distinguish a boy and a girl. So, Development of the breast in females is secondary sexual characteristics.

questions.question: In male, sex chromosome is______.
questions.explanation: In the male, sex chromosome is XY along with 22 pairs of autosomes in the nucleus of each cell.

questions.question: The sex of child depends on the
questions.explanation: The sex of child depends on the sperm of father. If sperm carrying Y chromosome fertilize the egg, the sex of child will be male and sperm having X chromosome fertilize the egg the child will be female.

questions.question: Which of the following is a female hormone?
questions.explanation: Estrogen is a female hormone released from the ovary. Testosterone is a male hormone. Insulin and growth hormone is common to both male and female.

questions.question: Goitre is a disease of the:
questions.explanation: Goitre is a disease of the thyroid glands present in the neck. Neck swell due to goiter. Goiter occurs due to a deficiency of iodine in the diet.

questions.question: The hormone produced by thyroid gland is called_________.
questions.explanation: Thyroid gland produces thyroxin hormone. Thyroxin hormone helps in the metabolism of carbohydrate, fat, and protein in our body.

questions.question: Less or no secretion of insulin may cause________.
questions.explanation: Less or no secretion of insulin from pancreas causes diabetes. Insulin help in maintaining the sugar level in blood that affects heart functioning.

questions.question: Which hormone prepares us for an emergency situation?
questions.explanation: Adrenalin hormone is released from adrenal gland that prepare us from emergency situation by increasing supply of oxygen to vital organ.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which mineral is essential for formation of thyroxin?
questions.explanation: Iodine is essential for formation of thyroxin hormone. Thyroxin hormone is essential for metabolism of nutrients and mental development.

questions.question: The process of changing tadpole to frog is called__________.
questions.explanation: The process of changing tadpole to frog is called metamorphosis. During metamorphosis fish likes tadpole changes into adult frog.

questions.question: Reproduction is essential life processes which help in
questions.explanation: Reproduction is essential life process which helps in the continuity of race or species on earth as each individual has a fix life span.

questions.question: Secondary sex organs develops under the influence of____________.
questions.explanation: Secondary sex organs develop under the influence of the hormone. Testosterone released from testis brings about change in secondary sex organ of male and progesterone hormone brings a change in the female.

questions.question: The period of pregnancy is known as
questions.explanation: The period of pregnancy is known as gestation period. It ranges from fertilization of ovum to parturition. It is of about 40 weeks.

questions.question: Human beings are
questions.explanation: Human beings are non-seasonal breeder because in human being breeding takes place in all season unlike the dog and other animals that breeds in particular season.

questions.question: The release of egg from ovum is described as
questions.explanation: The release of ovum or egg from ovary is known as ovulation. Ovulation occurs at the middle of menstrual cycle.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a secondary sexual feature?
questions.explanation: Pimples, moustache and body hairs are a secondary sexual character in male and female. Ovulation is the release of ovum from ovary which is a primary sexual feature.

questions.question: Menstrual cycle, in human female is completed in
questions.explanation: Menstrual cycle in human female starts at puberty. Menstrual cycle is completed in generally 28 days. In the middle of the menstrual cycle, ovulation takes place.

questions.question: All organism reproduce sexually after certain growth and maturity. That period of growth is called
questions.explanation: All organism reproduce sexually after certain growth and maturity. That period of growth is called the growth phase which completes at puberty.

questions.question: Every sexually reproducing organism, including a human being, begins life as a single cell called________.
questions.explanation: Fusion of male and female gametes results into formation of zygote. Zygote is the first single cell from which all the sexually reproducing organism begin their life.

questions.question: Which of the following is a primary sex organ?
questions.explanation: Ovary is the primary sex organ of female. Ovary produces female gamete called ovum and release female hormone progesterone and estrogen.

questions.question: Ovulation occurs on the ___________ day of menstrual cycle.
questions.explanation: Ovulation is the release of ovum from the ovary of the female. Ovulation occurs on the 14-16th day of the menstrual cycle, the middle of the menstrual cycle.

questions.question: Hormone responsible for milk ejection after the birth of baby is:-
questions.explanation: Milk ejection start from mammary gland of female just after parturition of baby. Oxytocin hormone is responsible for milk ejection after the birth of baby.

questions.question: Menstrual cycle is repeated at an average interval of_______ days.
questions.explanation: Menstrual cycle is repeated at an average interval of 29 days if fertilization of egg does not take place. During this cycle, ovulation occurs in the middle of it.

tests.name: Age of Adolescence Test 06
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Reaching the Age of Adolescence CBSE Online Test 06 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Secondary sexual changes occurs
questions.explanation: Secondary sexual changes occur at puberty in which a number of physical and morphological changes occur that includes moustache and beard in male and enlargement of mammary gland.

questions.question: A Human female reaches menopause around the age of_______ years.
questions.explanation: A human female reaches menopause at around 50 years of age. After menopause, the menstrual cycle gets stopped permanently.

questions.question: Starting of first menstrual cycle is termed as__________.
questions.explanation: Menarche is starting of the first menstrual cycle in girls. Menstrual cycle starts at puberty in which ovulation takes place.

questions.question: In the male human, sperm contains 22 pairs of autosomes and
questions.explanation: A human cell contains 22 pairs of autosomes and 2 pairs of sex chromosomes. The sex chromosomes are the X and the Y chromosomes and human females have two X chromosomes while the human males have one X and one Y chromosomes.

questions.question: Sex hormones are released by:
questions.explanation: Sex hormones are released by testes in males called testosterone and in females by ovaries called progesterone and estrogen.

questions.question: Which of the following is a exocrine gland?
questions.explanation: Those glands that have ducts to pass secretion to target organ is called exocrine gland. Salivary gland is an example of exocrine gland. Pancreas, pituitary, and adrenal glands are endocrine glands.

questions.question: The XY condition of sex chromosome is present in ____________ child.
questions.explanation: The XY condition of sex chromosome is present in male child. When male chromosome containing Y chromosome fertilize the egg, XY condition is established.

questions.question: Menstrual cycle is controlled by__________.
questions.explanation: The cycle of events which begin from the release of an egg and end in shedding off egg is called the menstrual cycle. A menstrual cycle is usually 28 to 30 days. The menstrual cycle is regulated by hormones.

questions.question: The gland whose development is affected by iodine is________.
questions.explanation: Development of the thyroid gland is affected by iodine. Thyroid gland is located in the neck that produces thyroxin hormone.

questions.question: Voice is high pitched in_________.
questions.explanation: The voice of females is high pitched in comparison to male. Pitch of voice change with age.

questions.question: Which hormone controls metamorphosis in frogs?
questions.explanation: Metamorphosis in frogs is controlled by thyroxin hormone. Metamorphosis is a drastic change in the shape of organisms as a tadpole to frog.

questions.question: Menopause occurs in females at the age of ______ years
questions.explanation: Menopause is the stage of female life when a female stops the menstrual cycle. Menopause occurs at the age of 45-50 years in the female.

questions.question: Increase in size of the breast and oily skin are:
questions.explanation: At puberty, a number of changes take place in boys and girls. Increase in the size of the breast and oily skin are secondary sexual characters of girls.

questions.question: In menstrual cycle ovum is released during__________.
questions.explanation: In the menstrual cycle, the ovum is released during midway. The menstrual cycle starts at puberty and ends at menopause. In the middle of each cycle, one ovum is released from the ovary.

questions.question: A woman is not able to produce a child after menopause as:
questions.explanation: A woman is not able to produce a child after menopause because the menstrual cycle of such women get stopped and gamete is not released.

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Reaching the Age of Adolescence CBSE Online Test 07 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

questions.question: Chips and packed snacks should never replace regular meals as they do not have
questions.explanation: Chips and packed snacks should never replace regular meals comprising of bread and rice along with vegetables and pulses as these things do not have adequate nutritional value.

questions.question: Which of the following disease is sexually transmitted?
questions.explanation: The disease which is transmitted from one partner to another partner during sexual intercourse is called sexually transmitted disease. AIDS is a sexually transmitted disease.

questions.question: At puberty women start producing________.
questions.explanation: At puberty, the woman starts producing ovum in the middle of the menstrual cycle from the alternate ovary each month on 14th -16th days.

questions.question: Which of the following contain iron rich food?
questions.explanation: Green leafy vegetable like spinach contains iron rich food that is essential for haemoglobin formation in the blood.

questions.question: Which of the following minerals is an essential constituent of hemoglobin of blood?
questions.explanation: Iron is an essential constituent of hemoglobin of blood. Hemoglobin is essential for carrying oxygen in the blood.

questions.question: Walking and playing in fresh air keeps the body
questions.explanation: Walking and playing in fresh air keeps the body fit and healthy. Walking and playing increase the heartbeat and controls the blood pressure.

questions.question: In India legal age for marriage is _________ for boys and girls respectively.
questions.explanation: In India legal age for marriage is 21 years for boys and 18 years for girls.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct statement?

options.html: Boys attain puberty earlier than girls
The mother is responsible for the sex of child
Menstruation starts at the age of 25
Ovulation occurs in the middle of menstrual cycle

questions.explanation: Ovulation occurs in the middle of menstrual cycle. Father is responsible for the sex of child. Menstruation starts at the age of 13 years. Girls attain puberty at earlier than boys.

questions.question: AIDS is caused by dangerous virus called__________.
questions.explanation: Acquired immune deficiency syndrome (AIDS) is caused by a dangerous virus called human immune virus (HIV).

questions.question: Which of the following activity do not cause HIV infection?

options.html: Sharing syringe with HIV infected person
Sexual contact with HIV infected person
Shaking hands with HIV positive person

questions.explanation: Shaking hands with HIV positive person do not cause HIV infection. It is transmitted by sexual contact with HIV infected person, sharing syringe with HIV infected person and from infected mother to their child.

questions.question: We should not use drugs as they are_________.
questions.explanation: We should not use drugs as drugs are addictive in nature as a person start using it, it becomes difficult to give up the same that ruin the family and health.

questions.question: The period of life, where the body undergoes changes leading to reproductive maturity is called__________.
questions.explanation: The period of life, where the body undergoes changes leading to reproductive maturity is called adolescence.

questions.question: In girls, 77 percent of height is completed at the age of __________ years.
questions.explanation: In girls, 77% of height is completed at the age of 8 years. The physical growth in girls takes place faster in girls than in boys.

questions.question: Puberty ends when an adolescent reaches:
questions.explanation: Puberty ends when an adolescent reaches reproductive maturity and becomes able to reproduce physically and mentally as well.

questions.question: Initially girls grow faster than boys. At what age both reaches to their maximum height?
questions.explanation: Initially girls grow faster than boys. At the age of 18 years both boys and girls reaches to their maximum height.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Human body undergoes several changes during adolescence. These changes marks the onset of________.
questions.explanation: Human body undergoes several changes during adolescence. These changes mark the onset of puberty in male and female.

questions.question: Acne and pimples on face is due to secretion:
questions.explanation: Acne and pimples on face are due to secretion of oily glands during puberty. Acne and pimples occur in boys and girls at puberty.

questions.question: First menstrual flow is called_________.
questions.explanation: The first menstrual flow is called menarche that starts at puberty in girls. After menarche menstrual cycle takes place regularly till menopause.

questions.question: The change from larva to adult is called__________.
questions.explanation: The change from larva to adult is called metamorphosis. During metamorphosis, organism changes suddenly into new form of organism.

questions.question: The disease diabetes mellitus is the result of
questions.explanation: The disease diabetes mellitus is the result of under secretion of insulin hormone from the pancreas. Insulin controls the sugar level in blood.

questions.question: The gland which is exocrine and endocrine both is
questions.explanation: The pancreas gland is exocrine as well as an endocrine gland because the pancreas releases hormones as well as pancreatic enzymes for digestion of protein.

questions.question: During menstruation the level of progesterone in the body is
questions.explanation: During menstruation, the level of progesterone in body is low but it start increasing and becomes highest during ovulation.

questions.question: Manifestation of male secondary traits in females due to hormonal effects is known as
questions.explanation: Manifestation of male secondary traits in females due to hormonal effects is known as muscularity. It occurs due to presence of Y-chromosome beside XX chromosome.

questions.question: Table salt is often iodised for certain areas to prevent__________.
questions.explanation: Table salt is often iodized for certain areas to prevent goitre. Goitre disease occurs due to under secretion of thyroxin hormone which require iodine for secretion.

questions.question: Facial hair in some women due to effect of__________.
questions.explanation: Facial hair in some women occurs due to the effect of the hormone. Some woman starts producing testosterone hormone that causes secondary sexual character is the female.

questions.question: The development of a new individual from a single gamete without fertilization is called
questions.explanation: The development of a new individual from a single gamete without fertilization is called parthenogenesis. In honey bee, production takes place by pathogenesis.

questions.question: What will happen if the thyroid is removed from a tadpole?

options.html: It will continue in larval stage
The larva will produce a giant frog
The tadpole will grow into a dwarf frog

questions.explanation: If the thyroid is removed from a tadpole, it will continue in the larval stage as thyroxin hormone is necessary for the metamorphosis of a tadpole to frog.

questions.question: It is misconception that:

options.html: Male is responsible for the sex of the child
Sex is determined by vigor of male
Women are responsible for sex of child

questions.explanation: It is a misconception that women are responsible for the sex of the child. Male sperm is actually responsible for the sex of the child as it may contain X or Y sex chromosome.

questions.question: Ovary and testis release hormone as well as gametes, so they are called___________.
questions.explanation: Ovary produces ovum as well as produce hormone progesterone and testis release sperm as well as testosterone hormone. So, ovary and testis are called dual gland.

questions.question: A boy is 9 years old and 120 cm tall. At the end of growth period he is likely to be ______ tall.
questions.explanation: In boys, 75% of growth takes place at the age of 9 years. A boys having height of 120 cm at the age of 9 will likely to be 160 cm in height at the end of the growth period.

tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: A push or pull on an object is called
questions.explanation: A push or pull on an object is called force. Force can make stationary body in motion and moving body in rest.

questions.question: The motion imparted to objects is due to the
questions.explanation: The motion imparted to object is due to the action of force. If force acts on the object along the direction of motion, its speed increases. If force is applied on a direction opposite to the direction of motion, its speed decreases.

questions.question: S.I. unit of force is
questions.explanation: S.I. unit of force is Newton. When a force pushes a body of mass one kg to a distance of one meter per second, the force is equal to one Newton.

questions.question: In game of tug-of war, two teams

options.html: Push the rope in opposite direction
Pull the rope in opposite direction
Pull the rope in same direction
Pull the rope tied with tree

questions.explanation: In a game of tug-of war, two teams pull the rope in opposite direction. As the two teams are acting in opposite directions, they will balance each other and there is no net force acting on the rope. As soon as one team applies stronger force and pulls the rope toward itself, the team becomes the winner.

questions.question: Perpendicular force acting per unit area is called:
questions.explanation: The perpendicular force acting per unit area is called Thrust. S.I. unit of thrust is Newton.

questions.question: If the direction and magnitude of the applied force changes, its effect
questions.explanation: If the direction and magnitude of the applied force changes, its effect also changes. If two forces are acting in the same direction, they add up. If two forces are acting in opposite directions, the net force is the difference between the two.

questions.question: S.I. unit of pressure is
questions.explanation: S.I. unit of pressure is Pascal. One Pascal is equal to one Newton per meter square. Larger the force, larger will be the pressure exerted.

questions.question: The net force on an object is __________ if the two forces acting on it in opposite directions are equal.
questions.explanation: The force on an object is zero if the two forces acting on it in opposite directions are equal in magnitude. This kind of force is called a balanced force.

questions.question: An interaction of one object with another object ________ between the two objects.
questions.explanation: An interaction of one object with another object results in a force between the two objects. Force can change the shape, state of motion nd direction of the object.

questions.question: A force applied on an object may change its
questions.explanation: A force applied on an object may change its speed. When force is applied in direction of motion speed will increase and if the force is applied in a direction, opposite to its motion, the speed decreases.

questions.question: Force applied on an object in same direction
questions.explanation: The force applied on an object in same direction as the object is moving add the speed of the object but when applied in opposite direction, its reduces.

questions.question: The strength of a force is usually expressed by its
questions.explanation: Force is a vector quantity as it contains both direction and magnitude but strength of a force is usually expressed by its magnitude. It is a numerical value with an appropriate unit. S.I unit of force is newton (N).

questions.question: The state of rest is considered to be the
questions.explanation: The state of rest is considered to be a state of zero speed. When the position of a body with respect to its surroundings does not change with time, it is said to be at rest. There is no displacement at the time.

questions.question: Which change always occurs when force is applied
questions.explanation: Change in state of motion always occurs when force is applied on the body. Moving body may come to rest or resting body may come to motion.

questions.question: Which force enables us to perform all activities involving movement or bending of our body?
questions.explanation: Muscular force enables us to perform all activities involving movement or bending of our body. Muscular force is produced by the muscles in our body, due to the presence of contractile protein. It is contact force as our body has to directly or indirectly in touch with the object.

questions.question: Unlike poles of magnet
questions.explanation: Unlike poles of magnet attract each other while like poles of magnet repel each other. Magnetic force is the force exerted by a magnet on another magnet or magnetic material. It is a non-contact force.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of contact force?
questions.explanation: The forces in which to bodies acting on each other are in contact are called contact force. Muscular force is an example of a contact force as our body has to touch directly or indirectly the object.

questions.question: The force between two charged bodies is called
questions.explanation: electrostatic force

questions.question: The force responsible for changing the state of motion of objects is called
questions.explanation: The force responsible for changing the state of motion of objects is called friction. Friction force causes moving objects to slow down or stopped. It is an example of contact force in which two moving bodies are in contact.

questions.question: Electrostatic force is exerted by charged body on

options.html: Another charged body or uncharged body

questions.explanation: Electrostatic force is exerted by charged body on another charged body or uncharged body. Two charged body having same kind of charge repels each other. It is a non-contact force.

questions.question: Like poles of two magnet
questions.explanation: Like poles of two magnet repel when face each other while unlike poles of two magnets attract when face each other.

questions.question: Friction arises due to
questions.explanation: Friction arises due to contact between two surfaces. It arises when an object tries to move on the surface of another object. It is also a contact force.

questions.question: A force has
questions.explanation: A force has both magnitude and direction so, force is a vector quantity. The strength of a force is expressed by its magnitude. And the direction of force is away from us when we push something and towards us when we pull something. Thus, a force is expressed by its magnitude and direction. Force can change state and shape of body.

questions.question: Gravitational force is acting on
questions.explanation: Gravitational force is acting on earth, moon, and on all other planet. The strength of this force depends on the mass of the object. Our solar system exist because of the gravitational force.

questions.question: Which one is an example that shows that force can change shape and size of an object

options.html: Force applied to increase the speed

questions.explanation: Force applied on inflated balloon is an example that shows that force can change shape and size of an object. By inflating the balloon with air, it exerts a force and the balloon increased its size and shape.

questions.question: The speed of moving ball increase further if force is applied in
questions.explanation: Force can change the state of motion. The speed of moving ball increase further if force is applied along the direction of motion and the speed decreases if force is applied in a direction, opposite to its direction of motion.

questions.question: The force exerted by a charged body on another charged or uncharged body is known as
questions.explanation: The force exerted by a charged body on another charged or uncharged body is known as electrostatic force. It is a force exerted by charges at rest on each other. Electrostatic force is non-contact force. Example of electrostatic force - A plastic comb after running in dry hair and bringing it near small pieces of paper. The paper gets attracted to the comb.

questions.question: The tendency of a body to maintain its state of rest or uniform motion is called
questions.explanation: The tendency of a body to maintain its state of rest or uniform motion is called inertia. An object will resist of any change in its state of motion or will remain at rest unless acted on by an outside force. Mass is the measure of inertia.

questions.question: A force of 9.8 N is equivalent to
questions.explanation: A force of 9.8 N is equivalent to 1kg-wt. One Newton is equal to a force needed to accelerate 1kg of mass to 1 metre.

questions.question: Force of gravity is a
questions.explanation:
The force of gravity is a attractive force. Every object in universe attracts every other object. This attractive force is called gravity or the gravitational force.
Example- When a ball is thrown up, it comes down. This is because of the force exerted by the earth in pulls down the ball. It is a non-contact force.
