questions.question: The product of mass and velocity is called
questions.explanation: Momentum is the product of mass and velocity. (mass {tex}\times{/tex} velocity, P = mv). Momentum of a stationary body is always zero.

questions.question: The property due to which one body attracts every other body is called
questions.explanation: The property due to which one body attracts every other body is called gravitation. Gravitational force depends upon mass of the body and distance between them. It is a non-contact force.

questions.question: The S.I unit of force is named after famous scientist
questions.explanation: S.I. unit of force is named after famous scientist Sir Isaac Newton. One Newton is equal to 1 kilogram per second square.

questions.question: A vehicle stops on applying brakes because

options.html: Brakes apply a force in opposite direction
Brakes apply a force in same direction

questions.explanation: A vehicle stops on applying brakes because brakes apply a force in opposite direction due to the frictional force between them. The friction between two the surfaces creates opposite force to stop the vehicle. Friction arises when an object tries to move on the surface of another object.

questions.question: Pressure is the
questions.explanation:
The force per unit area is called pressure. Pressure is force acting on a unit area of a surface.
It can be expressed numerically as:
Pressure = Force/ Area. Pressure is directly proportional to force. So, larger the force, larger will be the force exerted.

questions.question: Fruits falls towards the earth due to
questions.explanation: Fruits fall towards the earth due to its gravitational force. Gravitational force of earth attracts the bodies towards its centre. The earth pulls all objects towards itself with a force. The gravitational force exerted by the earth on an object depends on the mass of the object.

questions.question: Gases exert pressure on the
questions.explanation: Gases exert pressure on the wall of container. The molecules are continously colliding with each other and with the walls of the container. The pressure exerted by the gas is due to the sum of all these collision forces.

questions.question: Gravity is the property of
questions.explanation: Universal law of gravitation states that every object in the universe attracts every other object with a force equal to product of their mass and inversely proportional to distance between them.

questions.question: The envelopes of air around the earth is called
questions.explanation: The envelopes of air around the earth is called atmosphere. The main gases in atmosphere include nitrogen, oxygen, and carbon dioxide along with other gases and nitrogen is the most abundant gas in the atmosphere. The atmospheric air also exerts a pressure called atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: Liquids and gases together called
questions.explanation: Liquids and gases together called the fluids. Fluids mean the substance that flows and conform to the shape of the container. Fluids show properties such as not resisting permanent deformation and the ability to flow. Flow of substance is due to less intermolecular force of attraction.

questions.question: Pointed tips of nails have

options.html: Less area of contact and less pressure
Less area of contact and more pressure

questions.explanation: Pointed tips of nails have less area of contact. More pressure will be created on nail with pointed tips with same force due to less area of contact.

questions.question: The pressure exerted by atmosphere is called
questions.explanation: The surface of the earth is surrounded by an envelope of air called atmosphere. As all fluids exert pressure, the atmospheric air also exerts pressure called atmospheric pressure.

questions.question: A body can not start moving or stop moving without
questions.explanation: A body starts moving due to applying force on it and if the force is applied in the same direction of motion, it will increase its speed.If force is applied in opposite direction of motion, the speed decreases and stop a moving object.

questions.question: Porter place a round piece of cloth on their heads to
questions.explanation: Porter places a round piece of cloth on their heads to increase surface area on which the force will act. As pressure is inversely proportional to area, the increase in area reduces the pressure and this helps the porter to carry the heavy load easily.

questions.question: To draw water from a well we have to ______ the rope.
questions.explanation: To draw water from a well we have to pull the rope in upward direction. The force required to pull the rope depends upon mass of the bucket with water and height in this case it is the depth of the water.

tests.name: Force and Pressure Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Force and Pressure CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Friction can also produce
questions.explanation: Two bodies moving in the opposite direction in contact create friction. Friction is produced by the interaction of the surfaces. Due to friction heat is produced. Two bodies when rubbed against each other become hot. Moving machine parts become hot due to frictional force.

questions.question: The force resulting due to the action of muscles is known as the
questions.explanation: The force resulting due to the action of muscles is known as the muscular force. Muscular force is the force produced by the muscles in our body. It is a contact force. We bend or move our body to push or pull or lift objects.

questions.question: The north pole of magnet __________ the north pole of another magnet.
questions.explanation: The north pole of magnet repel the north pole of another magnet because like poles of magnet when face each other repel and unlike poles get attracted when each other.

questions.question: To stretch the bow, the archer applies a force that causes a change in it
questions.explanation: To stretch the bow, the archer applies a force that causes a change in it shape. Force can change shape and size as well as state of motion of the arrow. It is an example of muscular force. It is contact force and the arrow moves towards the target, the force acting on it is due to gravity and that due to friction of air.

questions.question: Force has magnitude as well as
questions.explanation: Force has both magnitude as well as direction, so it is a vector quantity. Force actually depends on the direction the force is applied. It can be seem as push or pull.

questions.question: Force is a ______________ quantity.
questions.explanation: Force is a vector quantity as force contains both magnitude of the medium and direction of the movement the medium has taken. Forces is push or pull acting on body.

questions.question: The pressure inside our bodies is __________ to the atmospheric pressure.
questions.explanation: The pressure inside our bodies is equal to the atmospheric pressure of atmosphere. At high altitudes, the atmospheric pressure decreases but blood pressure remains the same.

questions.question: In which of the surfaces is the friction greatest?
questions.explanation: Friction is more in case of rough surface and less in case of smooth and polished surface. Friction is the force that causes objects to slow down and stop moving. Carpet have rough surface so produce more friction than glazed tiles and polished wood.

questions.question: The two common forces that are acting everywhere around us are
questions.explanation: Gravity and friction are two common forces that are acting everywhere around us. Gravitational force acts on all bodies in the universe and frictional force is acting due to air on all the objects

questions.question: The device used to measure atmospheric pressure is
questions.explanation: Atmospheric pressure is measured by using a device called barometer. There are two types of barometer: Mercury barometer and Aneroid barometer. Mercury barometer is used normally in school. It is a long tube filled with mercury in it.

questions.question: The device that measure weight is called
questions.explanation: Spring balance consists of spring fixed at one end with a hook to attach an object at the other. The scale markings are equally spaced. Weight is the force by which object is attracted by earth towards its centre.  Weight is measured using spring balance.

questions.question: As we go higher and higher, the atmospheric pressure
questions.explanation: As we go higher and higher, the atmospheric pressure decreases due to reduced air with increase in height.

questions.question: Sharp knife have
questions.explanation: Sharp knife have smaller area of contact. A small force on the sharp edges will result in large pressure which help us to cut objects easily.

questions.question: Which force is used by doctors to remove a very tiny iron filing from the eye of patient?
questions.explanation: Very tiny iron filing can be removed from the eye of patient by using magnet. Since iron gets attracted to magnet, magnet force will be used to attract iron.

questions.question: The two force acting on stationary object is
questions.explanation: Gravitational force and frictional force are two the forces that act on any stationary object. Gravitational force attracts the bodies towards the earth and frictional force keep them together.Frictional force always acts in the direction opposite to the direction of motion of the object.Friction arises between the two objects in contact and keep them together.

tests.name: Force and Pressure Test 06
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Force and Pressure CBSE Online Test 05 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The force responsible for keeping earth moving around sun is
questions.explanation: Gravitational force of sun on earth keeps the earth moving around the sun. The moon also revolves around the earth due to the gravitational force of the earth.

questions.question: When same force acts on large area, the pressure will be
questions.explanation: When same force acts on large area the pressure will be less because pressure is inversely proportional to area. If the area increase, the pressure decreases, and if area decreases, pressure increases.

questions.question: Sharp knife cuts better than blunt knife due to
questions.explanation: Sharp knife cuts better than blunt knife because sharp knife have less area of contact than blunt knife.A small force results in a large pressure, helping us to cut objects easily.

questions.question: Which one is not an example of contact force?
questions.explanation: Magnetic force is not an example of contact force because the magnets or the interacting bodies need not be in contact with each other for the magnetic force to act. It is a non-contact force. Whereas in contact force the interacting bodies are in physical contact with each other.

questions.question: While the arrow moves towards the target the two force acting on it is
questions.explanation: While the arrow moves towards the target, the two forces acting on it is gravitational force and frictional force. The forces acting on the arrow are due to gravity and that due to friction of air.

questions.question: If two forces act in the opposite directions on an object the resultant force acting on it is the
questions.explanation: If two forces act in opposite directions on an object, the resultant force acting on it is the difference between the two forces. The direction in which the object moves will depend upon the net force acting on the object. The net force will point towards the direction that pulls or push the object with a greater force. If acting in the same direction, the total force is equal to their sum.

questions.question: 1 Kgwt =
questions.explanation: 1 Kgwt is equal to 9.8N. Where N stands for newton (N). The S.I. unit of force is newton (N) named after the famous scientist Sir Isaac Newton. Kilogram weight is also a unit of force.

questions.question: The resulting force due to the action of muscles is known as
questions.explanation: The force resulting due to the action of muscles is known as muscular force.It is the same force that is used when we move or bend our body parts while lifting objects etc. Muscular force is an example of contact force

questions.question: Which of the following is an example that force may change the shape of an object?

options.html: Pulling a toy car to move
Hitting of iron nail into wooden box

questions.explanation: Making model using clay is an example that force may change the shape of object. The force applied by the fingers changes the shape of the clay.It is the muscular force that is applied on the clay.

questions.question: The force of friction always acts on moving object and its direction is
questions.explanation: The force of friction always acts on moving object and its direction is opposite to direction of motion. Friction is the force that resists motion when an object tries to move on the surface of another object. Friction tries to slow down and stopped the moving object. It is an example of contact force. Friction may develop heat.

questions.question: Friction opposes the __________ between two surfaces in contact.
questions.explanation: Friction opposes the relative motion between two surfaces in contact. It always acts in the direction opposite to the direction of motion of the object. Two moving objects in contact with each other are always in relative motion.

questions.question: A force which opposes the motion is called
questions.explanation: A force which opposes the motion is called friction. Friction always acts in the direction opposite to the direction of motion. This is the reason why moving objects slow down and stop.  Friction is an example of contact force.

questions.question: The frictional force exerted by fluids is also called
questions.explanation: The frictional force exerted by fluids is also called drag. Water and other liquids exert force of friction when objects move through them. The friction force exerted by fluids depends on its speed with respect to fluid and also on the shape of the object and the nature of the fluid.

questions.question: The threaded tyres of cars, trucks, and bus
questions.explanation: The threaded tyres of truck, cars and buses provide better grips with ground because threads in tyres creates more irregularity to the surface of the road and causes greater frictional force to act and form better grips.

questions.question: A body will remain in state of motion on rolling on ground if

options.html: Friction is acting on same direction

questions.explanation: Frictional force always acts in opposite direction of motion to slow down or stopped the body from moving. A body will remain in state of motion on rolling on ground if friction is absent or the body will not move at all as friction is due air also.

tests.name: Force and Pressure Test 06
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Force and Pressure CBSE Online Test 06 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Brakes of vehicle is stopped by using brake that works on property of
questions.explanation: Wheels of vehicle is stopped by using brake that works on property of friction. We deliberately increase friction by using brake pads in the brake system and arrest the motion of the rim and finally the wheel, which helps in slowing and stopping the vehicle.

questions.question: The force required to overcome friction at the instant an object start moving from rest is measure of
questions.explanation: Static friction is the friction that exists between a stationary object and the surface on which it is resting. Static force should be greater than frictional force in magnitude.

questions.question: The force by which earth attracts the other body is called
questions.explanation: The force by which earth attracts the other body is called gravitational force. The earth pulls all objects towards itself with a force. Gravitational force depends upon mass of the body and distance from the center of earth. It is a non contact force as the force exerted by the earth can act from a distance.

questions.question: In atmosphere, wind blows due to
questions.explanation: In atmosphere, winds blows due to unequal heating of air at two places. The sun heats the earth's surface unevenly. As heat is transferred to the air, we get regions of warm and cool air which can turn into regions of low and high pressure. This difference in pressure makes a force that causes the wind to flow.

questions.question: One Newton is equal to
questions.explanation: One Newton is equal to 1 kg m/s2. One newton is equal to 1 kilogram meter per second squared.

questions.question: Kabaddi players rub their hands with soil for a
questions.explanation: Kabaddi players rub their hands with soil for a better grip of their opponents. Soil increase the roughness and creates more friction while catching the player firmly such that not to slip.

questions.question: The upward force experienced by a body when submerged in a fluid is called
questions.explanation: The upward force experienced by the body when submerged in a fluid is called buoyant force. Pressure increases with depth as a result of the weight of the overlying fluid. So, the pressure at the bottom of an object submerged in a fluid is greater at the top of the object. This pressure difference results in a net upwards force on the object. Buoyant force depends upon nature of fluid and density of substance.

questions.question: Sliding is replaced in most machines by
questions.explanation: Ball bearings reduces friction of moving parts in a machine. So, sliding friction is replace by rolling friction. Ball bearings are spheres that are held in a track. They are used between a wheel and a fixed axle or between a wheel- axle and a vehicle shaft. They are used to provide better linear motion or rotation around a fixed axis.

questions.question: School bags have broader strips to
questions.explanation: School bags have broader strips to decrease pressure that is applied on the shoulder. The load remains the same but the area increases. Since pressure is inversely proportional to area, the greater the area, the lesser the pressure, and the pressure on the shoulder reduces in broad straps.

questions.question: Friction between any two objects is due to
questions.explanation: Friction between any objects is due to surface irregularities on them. Friction is the resistance to motion of one object moving relative to another. Friction arises due to strong atomic or molecular forces of attraction between the two surfaces at the points of contact. In rough surfaces, the actual area of contact is very small, so the pressure at the points of contact becomes very large. So rough surfaces have more friction than smooth or polished surfaces.

questions.question: One Pascal is equal to
questions.explanation: The S.I. unit of pressure is Pascal.One pascal is the pressure that results from the action of 1 N of force acting on a unit area.

questions.question: A motorcycle with worn out treads on tyres is more likely to skid due to
questions.explanation: A motorcycle with worn out threads on tyres is more likely to skid due to less friction on slippery road. Tyres have grooves to increase the friction, if these grooves are worn out the grip loses and the friction between the tyre and the surface of the road decreases. There will be less friction between the motorcycle and the road which is most likely to skid.

questions.question: A force of 5 N is applied on a body to create an acceleration of 2m/s. The mass of the body is equal to
questions.explanation: Here, force = 5 N and acceleration =2m/s . Force = Mass {tex}\times{/tex} Acceleration, Mass = Force/Acceleration. So, Mass = 5 N/2m/s = 2.5 Kg

questions.question: The weight of air in a column of the height of the atmosphere and area 10cm {tex}\times{/tex} 10 cm is equal to
questions.explanation: The weight of air in a column of the height of the atmosphere and area 10cm {tex}\times{/tex} 10cm is equal to 1000 kg. Atmospheric pressure is the weight exerted by the overhead atmosphere on a unit area of surface.

questions.question: The pressure inside our body is
questions.explanation: The pressure inside our body is equal to atmospheric pressure due to which we do not get crushed under the pressure of the atmosphere. The internal pressure developed by the fluids inside the body cells and the atmospheric pressure are equal. At high altitudes where the atmospheric pressure is less then the pressure inside of our body, we feel popping in the air.

tests.name: Force and Pressure Test 07
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Force and Pressure CBSE Online Test 07 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.

  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Pressure exerted by water at the bottom of the container depends on the
questions.explanation: The pressure exerted by water at the bottom of the container depends on the height of the column of water. Pressure of water increases with depth.

questions.question: A force of 200 N is applied to an object of area 2 sq. m. the pressure will be
questions.explanation:
Here, Force = 200N, Area = 2 sq m.
Pressure = Force/Area.
Pressure = 200N / 2  sq m =100 Pascal (Pa).

questions.question: Pressure is

options.html: Inversely proportion to area of contact
Directly proportional to area of contact

questions.explanation: Pressure is inversely proportional to area of contact and directly proportional to force acting on per unit area. Increased area of contact results in applying bigger force which results in lower pressure and less area of contact results in less force which in turn results in high pressure.

questions.question: Two boys exert force of 40 N and 60 N in opposite direction on an object. The resultant force is
questions.explanation:
Since the two forces are acting in opposite directions, the net force is the difference between the two forces. The net force will act in the direction of the larger force.
Force exerted by the first boy = 40N
Force exerted by the second boy = 60N
The resultant force exerted by two boys on same object will be : Force exerted by the second boy (60N) - Force exerted by the first boy(40N) = 20N

questions.question: It is easy to walk on sand with flat shoes because

options.html: Flat shoes have larger surface area
Flat shoes are made of plastic

questions.explanation: It is easy to walk on the sand with flat shoes because flat shoes have a larger surface areas. As pressure is inversely proportional to area. So, a larger surface area will produce less pressure on sand and it will become easy to walk.

questions.question: Same force produces more pressure on
questions.explanation: Same force produces more pressure on smaller area as pressure is inversely proportional to area of contact. So, high pressure in less area of contact and less pressure in more area of contact. Example: Cutting instruments like knife and scissors have sharp edges or less area of contact.

questions.question: 1N/ sq m is equal to
questions.explanation:
The S.I. unit of pressure is Pascal. One Pascal is equal to the pressure that results from the action of one Newton of force on one meter square area.
So,1Pascal = 1 N / 1 sq m.

questions.question: The lateral pressure exerted by liquid at same height is
questions.explanation: The lateral pressure exerted by liquid at same height is equal in all direction and pressure increases with depth.

questions.question: A box weighing 2 kg exerts a force of 20 N on the ground. The box covers an area of 2 sq. m on the ground. The pressure exerted by the box on the ground is
questions.explanation:
Here, Force exerted by the box on ground = 20N
Area of the ground the box covers = 2 sq m
Since, Pressure = Force/ Area.
Pressure exerted by the box on the ground = 20N / 2 sq m = 10Pa.

questions.question: Liquid pressure increases with
questions.explanation: Pressure exerted by liquid increases with an increase in depth. As the depth of liquid increases, the weight of liquid column pushing down from above increases, and hence the pressure also increases. To protect this pressure, sea divers uses a special suit.

questions.question: At high altitudes atmospheric pressure
questions.explanation: At high altitudes the atmospheric pressure decreases, as density of air decrease as we move upward. Due to the difference in the atmospheric pressure and the internal pressure developed by the fluids inside the body cells  sometimes popping of ears occurs at high altitudes.

questions.question: Which of the following effect is not produced by force?
questions.explanation: Force can change the state of motion, the shape, and size of the object, and change the state of motion but can never change the mass of the body. Force is the product of mass and acceleration.

questions.question: The force depends on
questions.explanation: Force depends upon mass and acceleration of the body. Actually force acting on a body is directly proportional to product of mass and weight. An object with a larger mass needs a stronger force to be moved along at the same acceleration as an object with a small mass.

questions.question: Rate of change in velocity is called
questions.explanation: The rate of change in velocity is called acceleration. Acceleration is a vector quantity and has both magnitude and direction. An object's acceleration is the net result of all forces acting on the object.

questions.question: The force required to move a stationary body is called
questions.explanation: Forces that cause a change in the motion of an object are called unbalanced forces. Unbalanced forces are not equal and opposite. Example: In a tug of war the team that pulls harder than the other team wins. This unbalanced force is required to overcome the gravitational force acting on the body.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: If the force applied on a moving body in direction of motion than
questions.explanation: If the force applied on a moving body is in the same direction of motion than the speed of the body increases. As both the forces are acting in the same direction, they add up to make a bigger force. This large force makes the speed increases. And if force acts in the opposite direction, speed decreases.

questions.question: When batsman hits the ball, it changes its
questions.explanation: When a batsman hits the ball, it changes the direction of ball as well as speed of the ball. Due to the force applied by the bat along the direction of motion the speed of the ball increases.

questions.question: How much force is applied on a body of mass 10 kg to increase its acceleration by 2m/s sq?
questions.explanation:
Here, Mass of the body = 10kg
Acceleration = 2m/s sq
Force= Mass {tex}\times{/tex} Acceleration =10kg {tex}\times{/tex} 2m/s sq = 20N.

questions.question: A body moving on leveled grounds need to apply constant force to
questions.explanation: Frictional force always works in opposite direction of motion. A body moving on leveled grounds need to apply constant force to overcome frictional force provided by ground.

questions.question: The direction in which swimmer applies the force to move forward is
questions.explanation: The swimmer applies backward direction to move forward. For every action there is an equal and opposite reaction, so when a person pushes the water backwards the water pushes him forward.

questions.question: When action and reactions forces are equal and opposite, They don't cancel

options.html: Because they are acting on two different bodies
Because they are in state of rest

questions.explanation: Action and reaction forces are equal and opposite to each other but don’t cancel each other because they are acting by two different bodies. Newton's Third Law of motion states that for every action force, there is an equal and opposite reaction force.

questions.question: A force produces an acceleration of 5m/s when applied on a body of mass 4kg. The magnitude of force applied is
questions.explanation:
Here, Acceleration = 5m/s sq 
Mass of the body = 4kg.
Force = Mass {tex}\times{/tex} Acceleration = 4kg {tex}\times{/tex} 5m/s sq = 20N.

questions.question: Which force is responsible for the stability of our universe?

options.html: Frictional force between planets and atmosphere

questions.explanation: The force of gravitation is responsible for the stability of our universe. Gravitational force acts on each and every object which attracts the bodies towards itself. The planets revolve around the sun on their own axis is because of the gravitational force of the sun. Gravitational force depends on the mass and the distance of the object.

questions.question: Truck or motor-bus has much wider tyres to
questions.explanation: Truck or motor-bus has much wider tyres to decrease the pressure on road because pressure is inversely proportional to area of contact,  and this increases the area of contact and reduces pressure.

questions.question: Lactometer is used to

options.html: Determine the fat concentration in milk
Determine the purity of sample of milk
Determine the presence of toxic substance in milk

questions.explanation: Lactometer is a simple device used to determine the purity of sample of milk. It consists of a cylindrical vessel made by a glass tube. One side of the glass tube looks like a bulb filled with mercury and another side is thin tube with scale marking.

questions.question: A perpendicular force of 50 N is acting on a circular plate of radius 7cm. The pressure acting on plate will be
questions.explanation:
Here, Force = 50 N
Radius of circular plate = 7cm
Area of a circle = {tex}\pi{/tex} r sq= 3.14 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 7 = 154 cm sq = 154/10000 m sq = 0.0154 m sq.
Pressure = Force/Area = 50N/0.0154 m sq = 3246.75 Pa

questions.question: At higher altitude, nose bleeding is a common problem because

options.html: Atmospheric pressure is less at higher altitude
Blood pressure decreases at higher altitude
Atmospheric pressure is more at higher altitude
Blood pressure increase at higher altitude

questions.explanation: Nose bleeding occurs at high altitudes is due to the difference in the atmospheric pressure and internal pressure developed by the fluids inside the body cells. At higher altitudes, atmospheric pressure is less, and pressure inside our body remains the same. The blood pressure in our body equal is to atmospheric pressure on earth.

questions.question: Muscular force: contact force :: magnetic force : ?
questions.explanation: Muscular force is example of contact force because the interacting bodies are in contact to each other. Magnetic force is an example of non-contact force as the interacting bodies need not be in contact with each other.

questions.question: Astronauts wear special suits when they go into space because pressure inside the suit is maintained at ______ .
questions.explanation: Astronauts wear special suits when they go into space because pressure inside the suit is maintained at atmospheric pressure on earth. As there is less atmospheric pressure in space to maintain the equilibrium astronauts wear special suits.

questions.question: A boy having area of 70 cm sq. exerts a pressure of 7N/cm sq. on the ground. The Force acting on the ground will be
questions.explanation:
Here, Pressure exerted by the boy on ground = 7N/cm sq
Area on the boy occupied = 70 cm sq
As, Pressure = Force/ Area
The force acting on the ground = Pressure {tex}\times{/tex} Area
= 7 N/ cm sq {tex}\times{/tex} 70 cm sq
= 490 N

tests.name: Friction Test 01
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Friction CBSE Online Test 01 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.

  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: What kind of substances are known as lubricants
questions.explanation: Decrease friction

questions.question: Friction depends on the ____________ of surface.
questions.explanation: Friction depends on the smoothness/ nature of the surface. Smoother surface offer less resistance as there will be less irregularities in surface which reduce the friction. As irregularities of the surface make the surface rough or smooth.

questions.question: Friction produces
questions.explanation: Friction produces heat. When two surfaces in contact rub each other produces friction resulting in the production of heat. All machines get warm due to this.

questions.question: Sliding friction is ______________ than static friction.
questions.explanation: Sliding friction is less than static friction.  When an object slides its surface didn't get enough time to be in contact with the surface of other due to which it becomes easier to slide the object.

questions.question: Sprinkling of powder on the carom board
questions.explanation: Sliding friction is less than static friction. Powder fill the rough surface of carrom board due to which friction between the carrom coins and the board reduces and coins slides easily.

questions.question: Fluid friction can be minimized by
questions.explanation: Liquid and gases are called fluid. Friction in fluid can be minimized by giving suitable shape called streamline shape. As streamlined shape is narrow at the ends and broader in the middle due to which it reduces the fluid friction and helps an object to move forward easily.

questions.question: The heat in metals is conducted by the process of
questions.explanation: The process of heat transfer in which particles transfer heat one after the other in contact is called conduction. The heat in metals is conducted by the process of conduction because molecules in solid are tightly packed. they just transfer their energy to adjacent molecule without leaving their place.

questions.question: In machine, friction between moving part is reduced by
questions.explanation: In machine, friction between moving part is reduced by using lubricants. These lubricants fill the irregularities of the surface and make it smoother.

questions.question: Friction opposes the __________ between two surfaces in contact.
questions.explanation: Friction opposes the relative motion between two surfaces in contact. Frictional force acts in opposite direction of motion to slow down the speed of moving body.

questions.question: A force which opposes the motion is called
questions.explanation: A force that opposes the motion is called friction. It generates when two surfaces are in contact with each other. Friction depends upon the type of surface whether smooth or rough. Rough surface offers more friction.

questions.question: Rolling friction is smaller than?
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: Thermos flask keeps hot liquid hot and cold liquid cold by
questions.explanation: The thermos flask keeps hot liquid hot and cold liquid cold by preventing heat loss. Thermos flask has double layered wall in which air is evacuated to prevent heat loss.

questions.question: The frictional force exerted by fluids is also called
questions.explanation: The frictional force exerted by fluids is also called as drag. This kinds of frictional force can be overcome by changing the shape of the moving body in fluids. Such shape is called streamlined shape

questions.question: Striking a matchstick produces fire by
questions.explanation: When a matchstick is rubbed on match box fire is produced due to friction. Matchstick contain phosphorous that catch fire easily at very low temperature.

questions.question: The substance which reduce friction are called
questions.explanation: The substances which reduce friction are called lubricants. Lubricants include oils, paints, and grease. Lubricant stick with the surface and fill the irregularities of it to reduce friction.

tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

questions.question: The threaded tyres of cars, trucks and bus
questions.explanation: The tyres of car, bus, and truck has treads to provide better grips with ground. Treads create more friction as when breaks are applied these treads increase the surface area with road that helps in better grips on road while moving.

questions.question: It is easier to hold a earthen pot than steel pot as
questions.explanation: It is easier to hold an earthen pot than steel pot as earthen pot has more irregularities which makes the surface rough than steel that provide more friction and better grips while holding.

questions.question: The shape of the airplane is like a
questions.explanation: Bird

questions.question: Which of the following is a contact force?
questions.explanation: Friction is a contact force because in friction both the bodies are in direct contact with each other moving in opposite direction.

questions.question: The shape of fish is streamlined to
questions.explanation: The shape of fish is streamlined that help in decreasing fluid friction. The narrower part of streamlined shape offer less resistance to friction due to which fish can move forward easily.

questions.question: In which of the following activities friction is useful?

options.html: Wearing and tearing of shoe sole

questions.explanation: Friction is called as necessary evils because friction is necessary for even walking. When we walk the surface of our feet and road comes in contact due to which friction generates thus helping in moving forward.

questions.question: A body will remain in state of motion on rolling on ground if

options.html: Friction is acting on same direction

questions.explanation: Friction presents in ground slowdown the speed of rolling body. A body will remain in state of motion on rolling on ground if friction is absent.

questions.question: Soapy water on floor would make it
questions.explanation: Soapy water on floor would make it difficult to walk on floor due to decrease in friction. The irregularities present on floor are reduced by soapy water which inhibit the contact of two surfaces thus reduces the friction drastically and make it slippery

questions.question: Which shape is useful for movement in fluid?
questions.explanation: Fluids also offer resistance due to fluid friction while a body is moving in it. Streamlined shape is useful for movement in fluid as it offer less resistance to friction .

questions.question: Which of the following will offer least friction to run a toy car?
questions.explanation: Wet marble will offer least friction to run a toy car as the roughness or irregularities in wet marble is reduced due to presence of water thus reduce the friction.

questions.question: Which of the following is characteristic of friction?

options.html: It can change the direction of motion
It works opposite to direction of motion
It works on only resting body

questions.explanation: Friction always works opposite to direction of motion. A moving ball slows down due to frictional force working opposite to direction of motion.

questions.question: Which of the following is known as friends as well as enemy?
questions.explanation: Friction is known as friends as well as enemy because friction is useful as well as harmful for us. Without friction it is impossible to walk on ground or move vehicle. On the other hand, friction decrease the efficiency of the object, machines, etc causing wear and tear.

questions.question: Mobil oil is used in scooters and bikes for which purpose?
questions.explanation: For reducing friction

questions.question: Brakes of vehicle is stopped by using brake that works on property of
questions.explanation: Brakes of vehicle is stopped by using brake as they work on property of friction. On applying brakes, the wheels connected to them they get expand and increase the contact area which slows down the speed of a vehicle.

questions.question: Friction is caused by the

options.html: Holes in two surfaces in contact
Irregularities on the two surface in contact
Smoothness on two surface in contact

questions.explanation: Friction is caused by irregularities on the two surfaces in contact. These irregularities interlock the two surfaces and hence cause friction.

questions.question: On applying force, an object start moving when

options.html: Applied force is less than static force
Applied force is more than static force
Frictional force and applied force act in same direction
Applied force is equal to static force

questions.explanation: On applying force, an object start moving when applied force is more than static force. Both applied force and static force works opposite to each other.

questions.question: Tyres of which vehicles are biggest treads among the following?
questions.explanation: Excavator

questions.question: On rough surfaces, friction is
questions.explanation: On rough surfaces, friction is more than smooth surface. Rough surfaces have more irregularities than smooth surface that interlock the two contact surfaces increasing the friction.

questions.question: Which of the following is used in powdered form as lubricant?
questions.explanation: Graphite

questions.question: Interlocking of irregularities on two surface in contact cause
questions.explanation: Interlocking of irregularities on two surfaces in contact cause friction. Rough surfaces have more irregularities that offer more friction.

questions.question: Increasing friction is necessary for which of the following?
questions.explanation: Bicycle handle bar

questions.question: The force required to overcome friction at the instant an object start moving from rest is measure of
questions.explanation: The force required to overcome friction at the instant an object start moving from rest is measure of static friction that opposes the motion because more friction is required to overcome the static friction.

questions.question: What air resistance do to falling objects?
questions.explanation: Air resistance slows down the falling object because of increase in the air friction.

questions.question: The bottom of stainless steel pan have copper because
questions.explanation: The bottom of stainless steel pan has a thick layer of copper because copper is better and uniform conductor of heat.

questions.question: Least amount of friction is required in which of the following sports?
questions.explanation: Ice Skating

questions.question: The force by which earth attract the other body is called
questions.explanation: The force by which earth attracts the other body is called gravitational force. Gravitational force acts on all the bodies by which they fall on the earth.

questions.question: It is difficult to walk on smooth surface due to
questions.explanation: It is difficult to walk on smooth surface due to less friction. Smooth surface have less irregularities so offer less resistance.

questions.question: Which of the following device is used to reduce friction?
questions.explanation: In moving part of machine, ball bearing is used to reduce friction because ball bearing works on the principle of converting static friction to rolling friction

questions.question: Which of the following have more friction?
questions.explanation: Rough surface have more friction than smooth surface. because rough surface have more irregularities than smooth surface

questions.question: In which of the following case, friction is useful?

options.html: Pushing of heavy box on floor

questions.explanation: Friction is useful for walking on road. because when two surfaces come in contact friction arises which is opposite in nature and help in walking.

questions.question: Why is it harder to start to slide a heavy box across the floor than to keep it sliding?

options.html: Sliding friction is less than rolling friction.
Kinectic friction is greater than the static friction.
Static friction is greater than kinetic friction.

questions.explanation: Static friction is greater than kinetic friction.

questions.question: Nail have pointed tips to
questions.explanation: Nails have pointed tips to decrease the area of contact and reducing the friction. Smaller area offers less friction.

questions.question: Tyres are threaded to
questions.explanation: Tyres used in vehicles are treaded to increase the friction because rough surface offer more friction. Friction between road and tyres are responsible for its movement. When brakes are applied treads increase the contact area and safely stops the vehicle

questions.question: Sliding  friction is slightly smaller than
questions.explanation: Static friction is slightly greater than sliding friction. Static friction oppose the movement of body in contact because static friction have more contact area but sliding friction have small area in contact.

questions.question: If an object started moving, it would never stop if
questions.explanation: Friction works in opposite direction of motion. If an object started moving, it would never stop if there is no friction on the surface.

questions.question: Rocket has a special streamlined body in order to:
questions.explanation: Reduce friction

questions.question: A ball rolling on ground slows down and finally stops because of
questions.explanation: A ball rolling on ground slows down and finally stops because of friction offered by ground. Frictional force works in opposite direction of motion of ball which slows down the speed of a ball.

questions.question: Friction can also produce
questions.explanation: Friction can produce heat if two surfaces of the bodies are in contact with each other. Due to friction, the machine parts get heated on using for longer duration.

questions.question: When you strike a matchstick against the rough surface, it catches fire due to
questions.explanation: When a matchstick is rubbed on rough surface heat is evolved due to friction. The phosphorous present in matchstick catch fire easily.

questions.question: Friction can be reduced by
questions.explanation: Friction can be reduced by lubrication. Lubricants are thick sticky substances that fill the irregularities of the surface and hence reduce the friction.

questions.question: Burning of the meteor in the atmosphere is due to:
questions.explanation: Frictional force

questions.question: When you are riding a bicycle, the brake pads
questions.explanation: Brake pads are fitted in bicycle. When you are riding a bicycle, the brake pads do not touch the wheel. On application of brake, it touch the wheel and cause friction that leads to slowing down of bicycle.

questions.question: The process by which heat is transferred from hotter end to colder end is called
questions.explanation: Conduction is a process in which molecules transfer their heat energy from hotter end to colder end in solids without actually changing their place.

questions.question: Glass is good conductor of light but bad conductor of
questions.explanation: Glass is good conductor of light but bad conductor of heat because glass permits the light but prevents the passing of heat as it is an insulator.

questions.question: Kabaddi players rub their hands with soil for a
questions.explanation: Kabaddi players rub their hands with soil for a better grip of their opponents. Soil particle have rough surface that crates more friction and better griping on the opponent

questions.question: The machine in which oil and lubricant cannot be used, friction is reduced by
questions.explanation: The machines in which oil and lubricant cannot be used to reduce friction are fitted with an air cushion to reduce friction during motion.

questions.question: A polished surface is
questions.explanation: Friction is caused due to presence of irregularities in surface in contact to form interlocking pattern. A polished surface have very less irregularities as it fills the irregular surface and make it free from friction.

questions.question: Sliding is replaced in most machines by
questions.explanation: Sliding is replaced in most machines by use of ball bearing because, it works on the principle of converting static friction into rolling friction

questions.question: Metals are good conductor of heat because

options.html: Particles are away from each other
Particles are very close to each other

questions.explanation: Metals are good conductor of heat because particles of metals are very close to each other. In solids, conduction occurs in which heat is transferred from one particle to other in contact without changing their place.

questions.question: In which of the following articles bearing is not used?
questions.explanation: All the machines like fans, bicycles, and cars have ball bearing to reduce the friction but television does not contain ball bearing as there is no motion in it.

questions.question: Which of the following will exert less friction?
questions.explanation: Gases offers less friction than liquid and solids because molecules are losely packed. So, object moving in air will exert less friction than object moving in water and moving of road or rail.

questions.question: Liquids and gases together called
questions.explanation: Liquids and gases together called fluids. The substance that flows are called fluid. Fluids offer less resistance than solid.

questions.question: The frictional force on an object in a fluid depends on its

options.html: Speed with respect to the fluid

questions.explanation: The frictional force on an object in a fluid depends on its speed with respect to fluid and shape of the moving body. Streamlined body offers less friction in fluids because it is narrow at the edges which help in reducing the friction

questions.question: All vehicles are designed to have shapes which

options.html: Make the fluid friction to zero

questions.explanation: All vehicles are designed to have shape which reduces fluid friction so that they can easily ply. Streamlined shape having narrower parts in front offer less resistance.

questions.question: Tyres are treaded to
questions.explanation: Tyres used in vehicles are threaded to increase the friction because rough surface offer more friction. Friction between road and tyres are responsible for its movement.

questions.question: It is difficult to write on polished surface with pencil because

options.html: Friction prevents the movement of pencil

questions.explanation: It is difficult to write on polished surface with pencil because polished surface have less friction. Friction is necessary for writing with a pencil. Friction between paper and pencil leaves mark on paper.

questions.question: Drag is the
questions.explanation: The frictional force exerted by liquid is also called as drag. Frictional force always works opposite to direction of movement of body.

questions.question: Boat and ship have pointed tips to
questions.explanation: Boat and ship have pointed tips to decrease the friction due to liquid. The streamlined body offers less friction in fluid as pointed tips help to overcome the fluid friction

questions.question: When we attempt to move any surface, we apply a force to overcome
questions.explanation: When we attempt to move any surface, we apply a force to overcome interlocking between the surfaces called static friction present due to irregularities on the surface.

questions.question: The sole of shoes becomes flat after using few months because of
questions.explanation: The sole of shoes becomes flat after using few months because of wearing due to friction. There is friction between road and sole of shoe constantly which leads to flatness of the shoe sole with time.

questions.question: Which shape will have minimum friction in a fluid?
questions.explanation: The objects with pointed tips will have minimum friction in a fluid. The pointed tips offer less resistance due to less area of contact.

questions.question: Frictional force is necessary in
questions.explanation: Frictional force is necessary in using matchstick. When matchstick is strike with matchbox friction is caused due to which heat is generated. The phosphorous in matchstick catch fire.

questions.question: Fluids are
questions.explanation: The liquid and gases together called fluids. They have nature to flow due to less intermolecular force of attraction bteween their molecules.

questions.question: What does air resistance do to falling objects?
questions.explanation: Air resistance slows down the falling object as it increase with the increase in surface area of the object and acts against the gravitational force due to which it slows down the speed of object to fall down

questions.question: Which of the following objects have low friction?
questions.explanation: Car tyres, ice skates, and wicketkeeper gloves have more friction as compared to goalkeeper gloves as more friction is not required in this case.

questions.question: The direction of friction is always __________ to the direction in which the object is moving.
questions.explanation: The direction of friction is always opposite to the direction in which the object is moving. This opposite force is useful in case of walking, cycling, etc but harmful in case of wearing and tearing of shoes.

questions.question: Friction between any two objects is due to
questions.explanation: The friction between any two objects is due to surface irregularities on them. These irregularities creates interlocking of the surfaces in contact to create friction.

questions.question: A force of ____________ acts when an object moves through air or water.
questions.explanation: A force of friction acts when an object moves through air or water. Fluids have drag force which inhibits the movement of objects in fluids. Frictional force always works in opposite direction of motion.

questions.question: Carpenters often grease screws before fixing them to
questions.explanation: Carpenters while fixing screws, grease the tips of screw to decrease the friction. Grease present on screw smooth the irregularities hence reduce the friction offered by wood articles.

questions.question: A body is rolling over the ground or sliding over the same ground. In which case, the friction will be more?

options.html: There is no friction in both case

questions.explanation:
Sliding friction is always more than rolling friction because during sliding of a body more surface area is in contact not in case of rolling 
so, a body rolling over the ground will offer less friction than body sliding over the same ground.

questions.question: Streamlining is
questions.explanation: Streamlining is making the tips thin. Streamlining shape reduce the friction in fluids like air and water as it has narrow tips which reduces the area of contact

questions.question: Ball bearing is used in cycles because

options.html: Rolling friction is less than sliding friction
Ball is used to make attractive
Ball bearing increase the wheel size
Rolling friction is more than sliding friction

questions.explanation: Ball bearing is used in cycles because rolling friction is less than sliding friction. Hence, most of the moving parts of machine have ball bearing to reduce friction.

questions.question: The SI unit of frictional force is
questions.explanation: The S.I. unit of force is Newton. All kinds of force are measured in terms of Newton which is equal to force that push a body of mass 1kg to a distance of 1meter.

questions.question: A car with worn out treads on tyres is more likely to skid due to
questions.explanation: A car with worn out treads on tyres is more likely to skid due to less friction. The treads in tyres produce more friction that prevents the vehicle from skidding by providing more grip on road.

questions.question: It is difficult to move a cycle with brakes on because
questions.explanation: It is difficult to move a cycle with brakes on because friction between wheels and road surface increases as both the surfaces interlock each other.

questions.explanation: Oils and lubricants help in reducing friction because the lubricants fill the irregularities in the surface of moving bodies and hence reduce friction.

questions.question: Striker moves longer distance on carom when

options.html: Carom board is neat and clean

questions.explanation: The striker of carom moves longer distance with same force when powder is sprinkled on board. The minute particles of powder fills the irregularities on surface that reduce the friction and striker easily cover more distance.

questions.question: Which of the following method is not used to reduce friction?
questions.explanation: Using ball bearing, lubricating and greasing reduce the friction between two surfaces while treading the tyres of vehicles increase the friction that is necessary for motion as it increases the contact area which increases the friction.

questions.question: The force of friction which comes into play when one body moves over the surface of other body is called
questions.explanation: Kinetic friction is the force of friction which comes into play when one body moves over the surface of other body.

questions.question: Rolling friction and sliding friction are two types of
questions.explanation: The friction develops when one body moves over the other body is called kinetic friction. Rolling friction and sliding friction are two types of kinetic friction.

questions.question: When the friction is more

options.html: Two bodies in contact are smooth
Both bodies are in liquid state
Two bodies in contact are rough
One body is rough and other is smooth

questions.explanation: The friction is more when two bodies in contact are rough. Rough surface have more irregularities that interlock the surfaces and leads to more friction.

questions.question: The frictional force that counter balance the applied force during stationary state is called
questions.explanation: The movement in a stationary body is possible only when the applied force is greater than the frictional force. The frictional force that counter balances the applied force is called static friction. In static friction both the surfaces interlock each other strongly.

questions.question: In which of the substance friction is less
questions.explanation: Gases offers less friction than liquid and solid because in air there in more intercellular space. Hence, air will offer less friction in comparison to kerosene, water, and ice.

questions.question: Friction offered by water is good for the
questions.explanation: The friction offered by water is good for the movement of ships and boat in water. In ships and boat applied force is directed in backward direction and due to opposite nature of frictional force ships or boat moves in forward direction.

questions.question: It is difficult to apply brakes to stop the moving ship because

options.html: There is no friction between solid and liquid
Friction exerted by water is very high
Friction exerted by water is very small
Friction exerted by water is absent

questions.explanation: It is difficult to apply brakes to stop the moving ship because friction exerted by water is very small to stop the moving ship having very high momentum.

questions.question: Friction of air can be observed if a falling object is
questions.explanation: Friction of air can be observed if a falling object is light. When a tiny paper or feather falls from height, it takes long duration due to friction of air on it.

questions.question: A coin and a feather are dropped from same height, which one will reach first on the ground?
questions.explanation: When a coin and a feather are dropped from same height, the coin will reach first on the ground. Due to more friction on feather as it is lighter and has more surface area as compare to coin.

questions.question: Which of the following living organisms have streamlined body?
questions.explanation: Fish and birds have streamlined body to reduce the friction offered by water and air respectively. They have pointed tips and broader middle portion that help in reducing friction.

questions.question: Which of the following method is applied in moving parts of machine to reduce friction?

options.html: Converting sliding friction into rolling friction
Converting rolling friction into sliding friction

questions.explanation: The moving parts of machine are fitted with ball bearing to convert sliding friction into rolling friction. As rolling friction is always less than sliding friction due to less area in contact..

questions.question: To walk on slippery ground is difficult because the

options.html: Frictional force is very high to walk
Frictional force is not enough to prevent slip
Ground offer very high resistance to walk
Habit of walking on slippery ground is not enough to us

questions.explanation: Slippery ground have very less friction to prevent slipping so, it is difficult to walk on such grounds. As it does not allow two surfaces to be in contact with each other properly.

tests.name: Friction Test 08
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Friction CBSE Online Test 08 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which of the following method is applied to increase friction of vehicles?
questions.explanation: Friction is necessary for movement of vehicle on road. To increase friction treading of tyres is done. Regular oiling of engine, cleaning of vehicle, and less inflating of the tyres reduce friction.

questions.question: The shoes of football player have spikes in their sole to
questions.explanation: The shoes of football players have spikes in their sole to increase friction with ground while running to prevent slipping.

questions.question: Sandpaper is used to clean the metal surface to remove rust as because

options.html: Sand paper has rough surface to decrease friction
Sand paper has smooth surface to increase friction
Sandpaper has rough surface to increase friction
Metal and sand paper reacts easily

questions.explanation: Sand papers are used to clean the metal surface to remove rust because sandpapers has a rough surface to increase the friction to removes the rust.

questions.question: In moving machine, friction
questions.explanation: In moving machine, friction decreases the efficiency of machine. Friction in machine causes its heating due to which it smooth the surfaces causing damage to machine.

questions.question: Friction involves
questions.explanation: Friction involves unnecessary expenses of energy because due to friction heat is generated which leads to more consumption of energy or fuel.

questions.question: Sonic boom is due to

options.html: Gravitational Force between Earth and plane
Friction between air and supersonic object

questions.explanation: When a body moves with speed greater than speed of sound craking sound is produced in air called sonic boom. Sonic boom is due to friction between air and supersonic objects.

questions.question: Moving ball slowly decrease its speed in a grassy field due to

options.html: Rolling friction due to grass only

questions.explanation: Moving ball slows down its speed in a grassy field due to friction between ball and grass. Rough surface offers more friction than smooth surface which slows down the speed of a moving ball.

questions.question: Friction is a

options.html: Contact force acting in same direction
Contact force acting in opposite direction

questions.explanation: Friction is a contact force acting in opposite direction of moving body. Friction is created when one body moves over the surface of another body in contact to each other.

questions.question: The force responsible for changing the state of motion of an object is
questions.explanation: The force responsible for changing the state of motion of an object is friction. Moving body slows down due to friction and finally come to rest.

questions.question: Force of friction always acts on all
questions.explanation: The force of friction always acts on all moving objects due to contact between the two surfaces. Object moving in air, water, or on surface experiences friction.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of contact force?
questions.explanation: Magnetic force, gravitational force and electrostatic force are example of non-contact force while frictional force is an example of contact force because it arise only when two surfaces are in contact to each other.

questions.question: Friction depends upon

options.html: Smoothness of surface and nature of surface

questions.explanation: Friction depends upon the smoothness of surface and nature of surface. Rough surface offers more friction than smooth surface

questions.question: The force required to keep the object moving with same speed is a measure of
questions.explanation: When an object moves on a surface, resistance is offered and moving object slows down. To keep object moving with same speed a constant force is required which is the measure of sliding friction.

questions.question: Which of the following is correct order in descending frictional force
questions.explanation: Rolling friction is always less than sliding friction and sliding friction is less than static friction. So, correct order is static friction, sliding friction, and rolling friction in decreasing order. In static friction more area of contact is there due to which it is very higher than the others.

questions.question: It is difficult to walk on an oily floor because

options.html: Force of friction is very high
Force of friction is very less

questions.explanation: It is difficult to walk on an oily floor because force of friction is very less on oily surface. The irregularities present on surface are filled with oils that prevent the interlocking of the two surfaces.

General Instructions:

questions.question: Sound cannot travel through
questions.explanation: Sound cannot travel through vacuum as sound require material medium for its propagation. Sound can travel through air, water, and solids.

questions.question: Sound helps us to
questions.explanation: Sound helps us to communicate with one another. We share our views and suggestions with other either by sound or through written medium.

questions.question: Sound is produced by
questions.explanation: Sound is produced by the vibration of an object. Vibration creates a disturbance in a medium which reaches to observer's ear through the air, water, or solid medium.

questions.question: Speed of sound increase with
questions.explanation: Speed of sound increase with increase in temperature, during propagation of sounds particles of medium travel faster at higher temperature.

questions.question: When a tightly stretched band is plucked it

options.html: Vibrate but do not produce sound
Produce sound but do not vibrate

questions.explanation: When a tightly stretched band is plucked it vibrate and produce sound due to disturbance in medium.

questions.question: Which of the following factor influence speed of sound most?
questions.explanation: Speed of sound depends upon nature of medium, temperature, humidity, and source of sound but nature of medium influence the speed of sound most. Sound travels faster in solid medium than air and water.

questions.question: Which of the following require material medium for propagation?
questions.explanation: Sound require material medium for its propagation which may be solid, liquid or gaseous in nature. Light can travel in vacuum as no medium is required.

questions.question: In most of the cases vibration of objects are not visible because
questions.explanation: The vibrations of object are not visible because in most of the cases amplitude of waves are very small. Amplitude is the maximum displacement from mean position.

questions.question: Loudness of sound depends upon its
questions.explanation: The maximum displacement from mean position is called its amplitude. The loudness of sound depends upon its amplitude.

questions.question: Vibrating part of flute is
questions.explanation: Sound is produced by vibration of body. In flute, the vibrating part is air column inside the flute.

questions.question: Sound of single frequency is called a
questions.explanation: The sound of single frequency is called tone. A number of tones together form a note of the sound.

questions.question: The shape of the outer part of human ear is like a
questions.explanation: Human ear consists of three parts- outer ear, middle ear, and inner ear. The shape of outer ear is like a funnel through which vibration reaches to eardrum.

questions.question: In which medium sound travels faster?
questions.explanation: The speed of sound depends upon the material medium in which sound is travelling. Sound travels faster in solid (metals) followed by water and air respectively.

questions.question: Which of the following travels fastest?
questions.explanation: The speed of light is 299,792,458 metres per second in air. Supersonic aircraft and rockets travel with a speed of about 350 m/s only.

questions.question: In human beings, sound is produced due to vibration of
questions.explanation: Vocal cords are situated in neck of human beings. Sound is produced due to vibration of vocal cords.

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Sound CBSE Online Test 02 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
