If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Pitch of sound is determined by its
questions.explanation: The number of vibration per second is called frequency. The pitch of sound depends upon frequency of sound.

questions.question: The time-period is
questions.explanation:
The time takes to complete one vibration is called time-period. Time period is inversely proportional to frequency.
Time period T= {tex}1\over frequency {/tex}

questions.question: Tympanic membrane is another name of
questions.explanation: Tympanic membrane is another name of eardrum. When vibrations of audible range reach to eardrum it starts vibrating to produce sound.

questions.question: Damage to which of the following parts of ear cause hearing impairment?
questions.explanation: Tympanic membrane which is commonly known as eardrum vibrates to produce sound which is interpreted by brain.

questions.question: The nature of sound waves is
questions.explanation: Sound wave is longitudinal wave as it consists of compression and rarefaction. The region of high density form compression and that of less density rarefaction.

questions.question: Voice box or larynx of human process
questions.explanation: Sound

questions.question: The SI unit of frequency is
questions.explanation: The hertz (symbol: Hz) is the derived unit of frequency in the International System of Units (SI) and is defined as one cycle per second. It is named for Heinrich Rudolf Hertz, the first person to provide conclusive proof of the existence of electromagnetic waves.

questions.question: Sound propagates maximum in
questions.explanation: Solid

questions.question: Speed of sound __________ with increase in temperature
questions.explanation: The speed of sound increases with increase in temperature. At higher temperature, the particles of medium have higher energy to vibrate faster.

questions.question: In dholak sound is produced due to
questions.explanation: stretched membrane

questions.question: One of the most common well-known periodic motion is that of
questions.explanation: The motion of object which repeats after fixed interval of time is called periodic motion. Simple pendulum is most common well-known periodic motion.

questions.question: The SI unit of wavelength is
questions.explanation: Wavelength is the distance between two successive compression or rarefaction and its SI unit is meter(m).

questions.question: Range of audible sound is
questions.explanation: The range of audible sound is 20Hz to 20,000Hz. The sound above the 20,000Hz is called ultrasound and less than 20Hz is called infrasound.

questions.question: The sound of frequency more than 20,000Hz is called
questions.explanation: The sound of frequency more than 20,000Hz is called ultrasound. Ultrasound cannot be heard by human.

questions.question: Ultrasound is used in
questions.explanation: Ultrasound is used in echocardiography to known the functioning of heart. Echocardiography is commonly known as ECG.

questions.question: Which of the following species use ultrasound for navigation and catching prey?
questions.explanation: Bats produce ultrasound to catch prey and navigate in dark. The ultrasound produced by bats return from the prey or wall that is received by bat in navigation.

questions.question: Infrasound have frequency
questions.explanation: Infrasound has frequency less than 20Hz. Whale use infrasound to communicate between themselves.

questions.question: Speed of sound in solids is ___ the speed of sound in liquids.

options.html: sometimes greater ans sometimes less than

questions.explanation: greater than

questions.question: What is the name of motion when an object repeats its motion after some time?
questions.explanation: The motion of body which repeat its motion after same interval of time is called periodic motion.

questions.question: Loudness of sound is measured in units of
questions.explanation: The loudness of sound is measured in Decibel (dB). Sound of loudness greater than 80 dB is harmful for our ears.

questions.question: The speed of object greater than speed of sound in air is called
questions.explanation: The object travelling greater than speed of sound in air is called supersonic. When supersonic plane passes through the atmosphere sonic boom is developed.

questions.question: Which of the following travels slowest in air?
questions.explanation: Light travels fastest in air followed by ultrasound, sound, and aircraft. So, aircraft travels slowest in air out of the following.

questions.question: Dolphins and whales communicate by
questions.explanation: Dolphins and whales communicate by propagation of sound through water using infra sound not audible to human beings.

questions.question: Speed of sound in air is about
questions.explanation: Speed of sound in air is about 334 m/s. Sound travels at the speed of 1500 m/s in water and even faster in solids.

questions.question: The device used to produce sound in laboratory experiment is called
questions.explanation: Tuning fork is used to produce sound in laboratory experiment. When hits with a hard surface the plank of tuning fork vibrates with certain frequency.

questions.question: Motion described by a simple pendulum is called
questions.explanation: Simple pendulum describes oscillatory motion as pendulum vibrates back and forth again and again at specific interval of time.

questions.question: Vocal chord vibrates when
questions.explanation: In human beings sound is produced by the vibrations of the vocal cord present in neck. Vocal cord vibrates when lung force air through slit between them.

questions.question: The region of high density during propagation of sound is called
questions.explanation: Sound propagates by producing longitudinal waves in air. Longitudinal waves consist of compression and rarefaction. The region of high density during propagation of sound is called compression.

questions.question: During propagation of sound air particles
questions.explanation: During propagation of sound in air, particle of air vibrate back and forth. Particle transfer the energy to nearby particles and return to their original position.

questions.question: How many vocal chords are stretched across the voice box?
questions.explanation: Vocal cords are responsible for production of sound in human beings. Two vocal chords are stretched across the voice box to produce sound.

questions.question: A moving swing has
questions.explanation: A moving swing has periodic motion because swing repeats the motion back and forth at definite interval of time.

questions.question: The object vibrating in the sound of sitar is
questions.explanation: The object vibrating in the sound of sitar is string of sitar. String of sitar is pulled to vibrate to produce sound.

questions.question: Speed of sound ____________ with increase in humidity.
questions.explanation: Speed of sound increase with increase in humidity. At higher humidity density of air increases that increase the speed of sound in air.

questions.question: The substance through which sound travels is called
questions.explanation: The substance through which sound travels is called medium. Medium may be solid, liquid, or gases. Sound travel fastest in solid medium.

questions.question: Speed of sound in water is about
questions.explanation: Speed of sound in water is about 1500 m/s. Speed of sound is less in air but more in solid like metals.

questions.question: Vibration of which of the following produce hearing sensation in our ear?
questions.explanation: Vibration of eardrum produces hearing sensation in our ear. The three bones attached with eardrum increase the frequency further.

questions.question: Upper end of windpipe is called
questions.explanation: Upper end of windpipe is called voice box. Voice box contains vocal cord that vibrate to produce sound. 'Larynx' is the other name for voice box.

questions.question: Explosive noise caused by the shock of waves from an aircraft traveling faster than the speed of sound is called
questions.explanation: Sonic boom is explosive noise caused by the shock of waves from an aircraft travelling faster than the speed of sound. Such aircrafts are called supersonic plane.

questions.question: The bouncing back of sound when it strikes a hard surface is called
questions.explanation: The bouncing back of sound when it strikes a hard surface is called reflection of sound. Reflection of sound follows same rule as reflection of light.

questions.question: Voicebox is also known as
questions.explanation: Voicebox is also known as larynx. Voicebox in male is slightly protruded out called Adam’s apple.

questions.question: Stethoscope works on the principle of
questions.explanation: Stethoscope is a medical device used to detect malfunctioning of heart or lung. It works on the principle of reflection of sound.

questions.question: Which of the following device works on principle of multiple reflection of sound?
questions.explanation: Bulb horns are used in heavy vehicle to produce louder sound to demand pathway. Bulb horns work on the principle of multiple reflection of sound.

questions.question: A concave board which is placed behind the speaker in large halls or auditoriums is called
questions.explanation: A concave board is placed behind the speaker in large halls or auditorium called sound board for reflection of sound, so that sound reaches to all corner of hall.

questions.question: SONAR is used to
questions.explanation: SONAR stands for sound navigation and ranging. SONAR is used to detect the submarine and other underlying object at the bottom of ocean.

questions.question: Sonic booms is produced by
questions.explanation: When an object moves with speed greater than speed of sound, sonic boom is produced due to friction between air particles. Such planes are called supersonic planes.

questions.question: Vocal cords in men are about ___ long.
The minimum distance from sound reflecting surface to hear an echo is about
questions.explanation: The speed of sound in air is about 344 m/s and minimum time-interval between two sounds require is 0.1second to hear two sound. So, 2 {tex}\times{/tex} distance = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time = 344 {tex}\times{/tex} 0.1 = 34.4 m. minimum distance from sound to reflecting surface is 17.2m.

questions.question: The technique of obtaining pictures of internal organs of the body using echoes of ultrasound is called
questions.explanation: Ultrosonography uses high frequency sound(ultrasound) waves for obtaining pictures of internal organs.

questions.question: The full name of SONAR is
questions.explanation: SONAR is acronyms of sound navigation and ranging. This device is used to locate underwater object by placing them in the bottom of ships.

questions.question: The loudness of sound of people talking quietly is about
questions.explanation: The loudness of sound is measured in decibel. The loudness of sound of people taking quietly is about 65 dB.

questions.question: The sound of same loudness can be distinguished by
questions.explanation: The sound of same loudness can be distinguished from each other due to difference in their pitch. Different people produce sound of different pitch.

questions.question: The quality of a musical sound depends on the
questions.explanation: The quality of musical sound depends on the shape of sound wave. Different musical instruments produce different shape of sound.

questions.question: To hear two separate sound clearly, the minimum time difference should be
questions.explanation: To hear two separate sounds clearly, the minimum time difference should be more than 0.1second. The sound that we hear remains to our brain for 0.1second called persistence of sound.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a bone present in human ear?
questions.explanation: Femur bone is not present in ear. Anvil, Hammer, and stirrups are present in the ears.

questions.question: Which nerve transmits sound impulse to brain form ear?
questions.explanation: Auditory nerve transmits sound impulses to brain from ear. Optic nerve, cardiac nerves are present in eyes and heart respectively.

questions.question: The part of ear which vibrates when outside sound fall on it is
questions.explanation: The tympanic membrane is the part of ear which vibrates when outside sound fall on it to create vibration.

questions.question: The function of three bones present in the middle of ear is to
questions.explanation: Three bones are present in the middle ear named as hammer, anvil, and stirrup. These bones are attached with eardrum one after the other to amplify the sound.

questions.question: Name the medium in which sound travels slower?
questions.explanation: The medium in which sound travels slower is gas.

questions.question: Sound can travel through
questions.explanation: Sound require material medium for its propagation. The medium may be solid, liquid or gases. Sound travel faster in solid medium than gaseous and liquid medium.

questions.question: The number of oscillations per second of a vibrating object is called its
questions.explanation: The number of oscillations per second of a vibrating object is called its frequency. Frequency is measured in Hertz (Hz).

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Noise pollution may cause
questions.explanation: Noise pollution may cause hearing impairment. Unpleasant sound having high altitude may harm the eardrum that leads to hearing problem.

questions.question: Time taken by an object to complete one oscillation is called
questions.explanation: The time taken by an object to complete one oscillation is called time-period. Time-period is reciprocal to frequency of sound.

questions.question: Shrillness of sound is determined by the ________ of vibration.
questions.explanation: Shrillness of sound is determined by the number of vibration per second called frequency. Higher the frequency more shrillness is sound.

questions.question: A pendulum oscillates 40 times in 4 seconds. The time period of vibration will be
questions.explanation:
Number of oscillation = 40, time taken = 4 seconds. 
Frequency = {tex}40÷4{/tex} = 10Hz (number of oscillations​ made per second)
Time period=1÷Frequency
Time period=1 {tex}\div{/tex} 10 = 0.1s

questions.question: Loud sound can be produced by
questions.explanation: Loudness of sound depends upon amplitude of the sound. Higher the amplitude, louder the sound will be.

questions.question: Plantation on the road side can
questions.explanation: Plantation on the road side can reduce noise pollution as plants are good absorber of sound and also reduce the carbon dioxide gas released by vehicles.

questions.question: We must never put a sharp, pointed things into our ear because it can
questions.explanation: We must never put sharp, pointed things into our ear because it can damage eardrum. Eardrum is thin membrane at the junction of outer ear and middle ear.

questions.question: Time-period of a wave is 0.01 seconds than its frequency is
questions.explanation:
Time-period of a wave is 0.01seconds then 
Frequency=1 {tex}\div{/tex} Time period
Frequency= 1 {tex}\div{/tex} 0.01
Frequency= 100 Hz

questions.question: The to and fro motion of an object is known as
questions.explanation: The to and fro motion of an object is known as vibration. Vibration is necessary for production of sound.

questions.question: An object oscillates 20 times in 1 second. The frequency of the wave produced will be
questions.explanation: The object oscillating 20 times in one second will have frequency of 20 Hz as number of oscillation or vibration per second is called frequency.

questions.question: The repetition of sound caused by the reflection of sound waves is called
questions.explanation: The repetition of sound caused by the reflection of sound waves is called Echo. Echo is produced in large halls or in hilly areas due to reflection of sound.

questions.question: The loudness above __________ becomes physically painful noise.
questions.explanation: The loudness of sound depends upon amplitude of the sound wave. The loudness above 80 dB becomes physically painful noise.

questions.question: A bird makes high pitched sound but a lion makes a ______________ roar.
questions.explanation: A bird makes high pitched sound but a lion makes a low pitched roar. Low pitched sounds are lauder then high pitches sound.

questions.question: Ultrasound equipment for investigating and tracking medical problems works at frequency
questions.explanation: Ultrasound equipment is used for investigating and tracking medical problems. The sound of frequency greater than 20kHz is called ultrasound.

questions.question: The sound which is pleasant to hear is called
questions.explanation: The sound which is pleasant to hear is called musical sound while sound which is unpleasant to hear is called noise.

tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which of the following health problem is not caused due to noise pollution?
questions.explanation: Noise pollution may cause a number of health problem like hypertension, anxiety, and lack of sleep. Diabetes is a hormonal problem not caused due to noise pollution.

questions.question: Which of the following sounds cannot be heard by human beings?
questions.explanation: Human ear can hear sounds of frequency between 20Hz to 20,000Hz. So, sound of frequency 10Hz cannot be heard by human beings.

questions.question: The police use high frequency whistles which can be heard by
questions.explanation: The police use high frequency whistles which can be heard by dogs but not by human beings.

questions.question: Motion of which of these object is known as periodic motion?
questions.explanation: The motion of object which repeats after equal interval of time is called periodic motion. The motion of Simple pendulum is periodic in nature.

questions.question: Lightening and thunder occurs together but lightening is seen earlier than sound as

options.html: Light do not require medium for propagation
Sound travels in absence of light

questions.explanation: Lightening and thunder occurs together but lightening is seen earlier than sound because light travel much faster than sound.

questions.question: The time period of a vibration 500Hz is equal to
questions.explanation: Frequency of sound = 500Hz. Time-period = 1/frequency = 1/500 = 0.002 seconds.

questions.question: Which of the following generally do not cause noise pollution?
questions.explanation: Noise pollution is caused by vehicles, machines, and loudspeakers. Sitar is a musical instrument that does not produce noise.

questions.question: A child with hearing loss may have
questions.explanation: Generally, a child with hearing impairment from birth have defective speech.

questions.question: Which of the following have voice of higher frequency and shriller?
questions.explanation: The voice of woman have higher frequency and shriller than man. We can easily identify the voice of female from male.

questions.question: Vibration is also known as
questions.explanation: Vibration is also known as oscillatory motion as vibrating body oscillates to and fro. Oscillatory motion has fixed time-period.

questions.question: When the amplitude of sound is large, the sound produced is
questions.explanation: The loudness of sound depends upon amplitude of sound. So, when the amplitude of sound is large, the sound produced is large.

questions.question: Voice of male is heavy compared to woman because
questions.explanation: Voice of male is heavy compared to woman because male voice has low frequency as compared to female and male vocal cord is larger than female.

questions.question: The sound which is unpleasant to ear is called
questions.explanation: Noise is the sound which is unpleasant to ear. The pleasant sound produced by musical instruments is called music.

questions.question: Which of the following method is used to reduce the noise pollution along roads?
questions.explanation: To reduce noise pollution along roads, planting trees around roads is used. Plants are a good absorber of sound and reduce carbon dioxide concentration.

questions.question: Different types similar sounds can be differentiated due to
questions.explanation: Different kinds of similar sounds can be differentiated due to difference in amplitude and frequency. Pitch of different persons is also different

tests.name: Sound Test 08
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Sound CBSE Online Test 08 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.

questions.question: When the amplitude is doubled than its loudness
questions.explanation: The loudness of sound depends upon amplitude of sound. When amplitude is doubled than loudness increase by four times.

questions.question: Which of the following vibrates with a low frequency?
questions.explanation: Drum has a thin membrane of skin that vibrates on beating. The number of vibration per second is less in the drum in comparison to whistle, flute, and sitar.

questions.question: Industrial noise can be reduced by

options.html: Removing all persons away from factories
Not using machine that produce noise
Installing industrial activity away from residential area

questions.explanation: Industrial noise can be reduced by installing industrial activity away from residential area and using safety for workers.

questions.question: The frequency of a sound wave is 200 Hz, how many times will it vibrates in one minute?
questions.explanation: Frequency of sound = 200Hz, time = 1minute = 60 seconds. Number of vibration = 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 200 = 12000times.

questions.question: Which of the following quantities are proportional to each other?
questions.explanation: Loudness of sound depends upon amplitude of sound waves. Loudness of sound is proportional to amplitude of sound.

questions.question: We are not able to hear the sound of pendulum although it is oscillating because
questions.explanation: Human ear can hear sounds of frequency 20Hz to 20,000Hz. So, we are not able to hear the sound of pendulum of frequency less than 10Hz.

questions.question: Sound travels faster in solid medium as compared to liquid and gases because

options.html: Particles are away from each other
Particles of solid are very close

questions.explanation: Sound travels faster in the solid medium as compared to liquid and gases because particles of solid are very close to each other.

questions.question: Loudness of sound is proportional to
questions.explanation: Loudness of sound is proportional to the square of the amplitude. When the amplitude is doubled, loudness increases four times.

questions.question: A person is exposed to a loud sound continuously he may get

options.html: Optical problems and swelling of pinna
Temporary or permanent impairment of hearing

questions.explanation: When a person is exposed to a loud sound continuously he may get temporary impairment of hearing along with other health hazards.

questions.question: Total hearing impairment is usually

options.html: Due to viewing TV at high volume

questions.explanation: Total hearing impairment is usually from birth. Hearing impairment may also occurs due to accidents or some other reasons.

questions.question: In a classroom, all the students speak together the sound produced will be called
questions.explanation: Unpleasant sound is called noise. In classroom, all the students speak together the sound produced will be called noise.

questions.question: Sound cannot be heard directly on the moon because

options.html: Hydrogen gas is present on moon
There is no air on the moon

questions.explanation: Sound requires material medium for its propagation. On the moon, the atmosphere is absent so, sound cannot be heard directly on the moon in absence of air.

questions.question: The relation between the speed of sound, frequency, and wavelength is
questions.explanation: Speed of sound is equal to product of frequency and wavelength. Speed = frequency × wavelength.

questions.question: Speed of sound in sea water is slightly more than distilled water because

options.html: Density of sea water is less
Density of distilled water is more
Density of sea water is more

questions.explanation: Speed of sound increase with increase density. So, the speed of sound in seawater is slightly more than distilled water because density of seawater is more due to presence of salts.

questions.question: If the thunder is heard by a man 4 seconds after the lightening is seen. How far is the lightening from man? (Speed of sound in air is 330m/s )
questions.explanation: Speed of sound = 330 m/s, time = 4 seconds. Distance= speed {tex}\times{/tex} time = 330 m/s {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 1320m. so, lightening occurs at a distance of 1320m.

questions.question: The substance through which current can flow easily is called
questions.explanation: The substance through which electric current can flow easily is called good conductor of electricity. Metals are good conductors of electricity.

questions.question: Which of the following object is generally electroplated?
questions.explanation: Electroplating is the process of depositing a thin layer of another metal like zinc to prevent corrosion and make more attractive. Cycle handle is electroplated.

questions.question: The passage of an electric current through a solution causes
questions.explanation: The passage of an electric current through a solution causes chemical effect that leads to deposition of metal on cathode.

questions.question: Which one is not a good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Plastic is not a good conductor of electricity. Mostly all metals are good conductors of heat and electricity. Copper, Silver is a metal, hence a good conductor of electricity. Even Graphite is an allotrope of carbon, it is also a good conductor

questions.question: Electroplating is done to

options.html: Make it corrosion free and scratch resistance

questions.explanation: Electroplating is done to make the metal corrosion free and scratch resistance. It also enhances the look of the substance.

questions.question: The materials that have high resistance are called
questions.explanation: The substances through which electric current cannot pass are called bad conductor of electricity. These materials have high resistance.

questions.question: Which of the appliances at your home does not run on electric current?
questions.explanation: Air conditioner, mixer grinder and television run on electric current but gas burner used at home does not run on electric current.

questions.question: The device used to check whether a particular material allow current to pass through is called
questions.explanation: Tester is an electrical device used to determine the presence or absence of an electric voltage (usually (AC) in a piece of equipment under test. A test light is generally simpler and less costly than a measuring instrument such as a multimeter

questions.question: Material which do not allow electric current to pass through them easily are called
questions.explanation: Materials like paper, plastic, wood do not allow electric current to pass through them easily are called poor conductor of electricity. Insulators don't conduct electricity at all.

questions.question: Which of the following liquid is good conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Seawater contains a small quantity of salt that allow the flow of electric current through them. Distilled water, alcohol, and sugar solution do not contain ions or charge particles.

questions.question: Battery is a

options.html: Combination of two or more cells

questions.explanation: Combination of two or more cells is called battery. In a battery cells are connected in series with opposite poles close to each other.

questions.question: The bulb glows when the electric current passes through it due to
questions.explanation: When electric current passes through the filament of bulb get heated and start emitting light and heat energy. So, heating effect of electric current is applied in bulb.

questions.question: Which categories of substances are generally good conductors?
questions.explanation: Metals are generally good conductor of electricity as they have free electron. Non-metals except graphite are bad conductor of electricity.

questions.question: A device which prevents or allow the current to flow through it
questions.explanation: Switch is a device for breaking and making the connection. Switch is combined with positive terminal of circuit.

questions.question: In battery two or more cells are connected in
questions.explanation: Battery is a combination of two or more cells connected in series. It supplies electric power to the electrical or electronic devices.

questions.question: When the liquid between two ends allow electric current then

options.html: Bulb of tester heat up only

questions.explanation: It happens because current gets a closed path to flow. The glowing of bulb indicates that current is flowing through the liquid

questions.question: Which metal is electroplated on iron for making “cans” for storing food and drinks?
questions.explanation: Tin is electroplated on iron for making cans for storing food and drinks as tin is less reactive than iron. Tin can preserve canned food longer than iron vessels

questions.question: When two or more cells are joined together it forms a
questions.explanation: When two or more cells are joined together in series, it forms a battery. Battery provides more current than single cell. It is mostyl used in handheld devices like mobile, laptops etc.

questions.question: In which type of cell zinc and copper plates are dipped in separate solution?
questions.explanation: In Voltaic cell, zinc and copper plates are dipped in separate solution which works as cathode and anode respectively to initiate flow of electron, as a result, electric current flows through the circuit.

questions.question: Electric current is said to be pass through a wire when

options.html: Both electron and neutron pass through it

questions.explanation: Electric current is said to be pass through a wire when only electron flow happens through the wire. Conventionally direction of electric current is opposite to the flow of electron

questions.question: What is the common name for manganese-dioxide-alkaline-zinc cell?
questions.explanation: Alkaline cell is commonly called as manganese dioxide alkaline zinc cell. They can be primary or rechargeable secondary battery as well.

questions.question: Electric current is measured by a device
questions.explanation: Ammeter is a measuring instrument used to measure the current in a circuit.  The S.I. unit of electric current is Ampere. Many types of ammeter is available in market like electrodynamic, moving coil, etc. but digital is mostly popular these days.

questions.question: Plastic wire is
questions.explanation: An insulator

questions.question: Electroplating is based on
questions.explanation: Chemical effect of electricity

questions.question: Potential difference is created in a electric circuit due to
questions.explanation: In a circuit, potential difference is created in an electric circuit due to cell or battery. This difference is responsible for flow of electrons from higher to lower concentration of electrons. Potential difference is measured in Volt.

questions.question: In electric circuit, current flows due to
questions.explanation: In an electric circuit, current flows due to the difference in potential difference. This difference is responsible for the flow of electrons from higher to a lower concentration of electrons Potential difference is created due to the presence of battery or cell.

questions.question: Heating effect of electric current is used in
questions.explanation: Bread toaster is an appliance that works on heating effect of electric current as the filament of bread toaster has high resistance to provide large amount of heat. In toaster, therefore, electrical energy is converted to heat energy

questions.question: Electric circuit provides a complete path for
questions.explanation: The close and continuous path of electric current is called electric circuit. Electric circuit provides a complete path for current to flow. If it breaks or open then current cannot flow. Switch is provided to close or open the path

questions.question: Fuse wire is used in electric circuit to prevent damage in
questions.explanation: Fuse wire is a safety device used in electric circuit to protect the circuit due to overflow of current. When overflow of current happens then the fuse wire melts and breaks the circuit, thus saving the appliance from getting huge flow of current and short circuit as well.

questions.question: Which of the following is a poor conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Tap water, iron nail, and steel spoon are good conductor of electricity as they have free electrons in them but air is poor conductor of electricity. Air is used as a insulating material

questions.question: The instrument needed to measure the current flowing through a circuit is?
questions.explanation: ammeter

questions.question: Which of the following solution is best conductor of current?
questions.explanation: Acid water is best conductor of electricity because acid produces hydronium ions in water after dissociation to conduct electricity. Distilled water is bad conductor of electricity as it doesn't contain any ion.

questions.question: Coming together of live wire and neutral wire in a circuit is called
questions.explanation: When live wire and a neutral wire in circuit come in contact with each other short circuit occurs. These may damage electrical appliances or may prove fatal to someone touching the device.  Insulation is provided on these wires to prevent short circuits.

questions.question: A safety device that protect electrical appliances from short circuiting and overloading is called
questions.explanation: Fuse wire is a safety device that protects electrical appliances from short circuiting and overloading. It is made of material having very low melting point. When overflow of current happens then the fuse wire melts and breaks the circuit, thus saving the appliance from getting huge flow of current and short circuit as well.

questions.question: Which of the following wire is used to protect us from electric shock?
questions.explanation: Earth wire is used in a plug for safety that protects us from electric shock. For any overloading, excess is passed to earth and thus saves from electric shock.

questions.question: In LEDs the longer lead is connected to
questions.explanation: Light emitting diode (LED) has two leads one longer and another slightly shorter. The longer lead is connected to positive terminal. This is done as  a convention so that user knows which leg to insert at which point of socket else short-circuit may happen

questions.question: Resistance is the
questions.explanation: Resistance is the obstruction in flow of current. The substance having higher resistance is bad conductor of electricity. High temperature or heating is a cause of high resistance apart from free electron concept

questions.question: The process in which any electrolyte gets decomposed when electricity is passed through it, is called
questions.explanation: electrolysis

questions.question: The process of depositing a thin layer of any superior metal over an object of a cheaper metal with the help of electricity is called
questions.explanation: electroplating

questions.question: Tap water is good conductor of electricity but distilled water is not because

options.html: Tap water don not contain salts

questions.explanation: Conductance of electricity depends upon the presence of ions or free electron to carry charge. Tap water contains small amount of salts and minerals as impurities to conduct electricity but distilled water is free from any impurities

questions.question: The electric conductor used for domestic use has insulation. The wire having red insulation is generally
questions.explanation: Electric conductor used for domestic use has insulation to protect from short circuiting as well as from getting electric shock. The wire having red insulation is generally live wire and with black insulation is neutral wire. This coloring has been done to recognise the wires and put them in right socket

questions.question: Short circuit is caused due to

options.html: Direct contact of live wire and earth wire
Direct contact of live wire and guy wire
Direct contact of neutral wire and earth wire
Direct contact of live and neutral wire

questions.explanation: Short circuit occurs when live wire and neutral wire come in contact of each other. Large amount of current flows and heat developed may cause fire. To prevent fuse wire is used

questions.question: We should never handle electrical appliances with wet hand because

options.html: Appliance will not work in presence of water
Small quantity of salt make it conductor

questions.explanation: Electrical appliances should not be handled with wet hand because small quantity of salt or minerals  makes the water to conduct electricity and one may get shock

questions.question: Chromium is used to electroplate iron articles as it is
questions.explanation: Chromium metal is used to electroplate iron articles because chromium is shiny as well as rust free. Iron with passage of time develops rust that corrodes it, to avoid rusting one can do painting or electroplating

questions.question: Which type of water is free from salt?
questions.explanation: River water, seawater, and drinking water contain small amount of salt that have them good conductor of electricity but distilled water is salt free as it is pure form of water. Thus distilled water cannot conduct electricity

questions.question: When electrodes are immersed in water and electric current is passed, the bubbles formed at positive terminal is
questions.explanation: When electrolysis of water is done by immersing two electrodes, the bubbles formed at positive terminal are Oxygen as Oxygen is negatively charged. So it gets accumulated at anode and Hydrogen at cathode. Thus making flow of electric current

questions.question: Live wire is covered with ____________ coloured insulation.
questions.explanation: Live wire is covered with red insulation in the electric circuit while neutral wire is covered with black insulation. This coloring has been done to recognise the wires and put them in right socket.

questions.question: Small amount of Sulphuric acid is added to copper sulphate solution during electroplating to
questions.explanation: Sulphuric acid is a good conductor of electricity due to complete ionization in water. Complete ionization helps in generating free ions that conducts electricity. Hence small amount of Sulphuric acid is added to copper sulphate solution to increase conductivity during electroplating.

questions.question: The substance that easily allow the current to pass through it is called
questions.explanation: The substance that easily allows the current to pass through it is called conductor. Metals are good conductors of electricity. Metals have less resistance which makes them good conductors

questions.question: A coating of ________ is deposited on iron to protect it from rusting.
questions.explanation: A coating of zinc is deposited on iron to protect it from rusting during galvanization process. Rust corrodes the iron metal. to prevent this rust galvanization is performed

questions.question: Electric bell works on the principle of
questions.explanation: Electric bell works on the principle of magnetic effect of electric current. When current flows from the armature, electromagnet is formed that attracts the gong. It is a temporary magnet so withdrawing current it behaves as a nonmagnetic

questions.question: Galvanizing is done on metal surface to
questions.explanation: Galvanization is the process of depositing a thin layer of another metal like zinc coating to iron or steel, to prevent rusting. It is a common industrial method to prevent corrosion

questions.question: Which of the following is the most essential for an electric circuit to work?
questions.explanation: electric current

questions.question: The process of depositing a thin layer of desired metal on another metal is called
questions.explanation: The process of depositing a thin layer of desired metal on another metal is called electroplating. It is done to protect the metal from corrosion. It is also done to look ornaments precious, like gold coating over silver jewellery

questions.question: In bulbs electricity is converted into
questions.explanation: In electric bulb, electricity is converted into light energy and heat energy. The coils have high resistance and high melting point. So electric current at first heats the coil to red hot and then it glows producing light energy

questions.question: Chromium plating is done on many objects like cars, kitchen stoves and bath fitting to

options.html: Increase the look to sell at higher price

questions.explanation: Chromium plating is done on objects like cars, kitchen stoves, and bath fitting to prevent corrosion and scratch resistance. The process is known as electroplating

questions.question: A conductor having very low resistance is called
questions.explanation: A conductor having very low resistance is called good conductor of electricity. Resistance is the obstruction in flow of current. Whereas in resistor and insulator resistance is quite high. Superconductor has ideally zero electrical resistance

questions.question: Which of the following liquid is poor conductor of electricity?
questions.explanation: Acid solution, alkaline solution, and common salt solution are good conductors of electricity due to presence of salt/ions but distilled water is poor conductor of electricity. Distilled water is free from any impurities hence no free moving ions to conduct electricity

questions.question: In an electric circuit bulb glows only when switch is in
questions.explanation: In electric circuit, bulb glows only when switch is in closed position. In close position, circuit gets completed and electric current flows when circuit is closed

questions.question: Tin cans, used for storing food, is made by electroplating iron on tin because

options.html: Tin is less reactive than iron

questions.explanation: The can used for storing food is made by electroplating iron by tin because tin is less reactive than iron. Foods can be preserved for longer days in tin cans

questions.question: An electric cell produces electricity from the
questions.explanation: An electric cell produces electricity from the chemical stored in it. Here chemical energy is converted into electrical energy

questions.question: Inside the torch, two or more cells are placed with
questions.explanation: Inside the torch, two or more cells are placed with positive-negative-positive-negative terminal. This is done to arrange them in series connection

questions.question: Which of the following lightening device uses comparatively less energy?
questions.explanation: Compact fluorescent lamp (CFL) is less energy consuming device in comparison to tube light, bulb, and filament rod. CFLs use one-fifth to one-third the electric power and last eight to fifteen times longer.

questions.question: An electric current can produce:
questions.explanation:
When an electric current is passed through a conducting solution, it causes chemical reactions. The resulting effect is called the chemical effect of current.
When an electric current is passed through a bulb, its filament gets heated to a high temperature and bulb starts glowing. The resulting effect is the heating effect of current. Whenever an electric current te passed through a circuit, a magnetic field is produced around it. The resulting effect is the magnetic effect of current. Thus, an electric current can produce chemical, heating and magnetic effects.

questions.question: Which of the following group contain only conductor?

options.html: Sea water, tap water, distilled water

questions.explanation: Copper and silver is metal, so good conductor of electricity. Graphite is allotrope of carbon is also good conductor of electricity. They all have free electrons which take part in flowing of electric current

questions.question: ISI marks to ensure that the appliance is safe and wastage of energy is
questions.explanation: ISI marks on appliances ensure that the appliance is safe and the wastage of energy is minimum. ISI mark is a certification for industrial products in India. The mark certifies that a product conforms to the Indian Standard.

questions.question: A battery is used to
questions.explanation: In electric circuit, a battery is used to maintain potential difference between two terminals of appliances. It is necessary to the flow of current as current flow from high potential to low potential of voltage.

questions.question: Metals are good conductor of electricity because it can
questions.explanation: Metals are generally good conductor of electricity because it contains free electrons in their outermost orbit. The free electrons are responsible to flow of current  when potential difference is applied, giving rise to electric current

questions.question: Two similar charges are brought close to each other, then they will
questions.explanation: When two similar charges are brought close to each other, then they will repel each other on the other hand unlike charges attract each other.   This is the fundamental property of charge

questions.question: Chemical effect of electric current is used in ______.
questions.explanation: Chemical effect of electric current is used in electroplating in which a thin layer of other metal is deposited on other metal.This is done to avoid corrosion in metals or rusting in iron.

questions.question: Which types of solution do not conduct electricity?
questions.explanation: Solution of glucose do not produce ions when dissolved in water, so solution of glucose is poor conductor of electricity. For electricity to flow free ions should be produced after dissociation.

questions.question: Solution of acids are generally
questions.explanation: Solution of acid when dissociates in water produces free ions that help in flowing of electric current.  Hence, solution of acids is good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: LEDs are
questions.explanation: LEDs stands for light emitting diodes. It consists of two leads one longer and one smaller. Longer is positive and short is negative terminals.

questions.question: The two rods that are dipped in solution for electrolysis is called
questions.explanation: During electrolysis, two rods are dipped in salt solution. One rod is called cathode and other is called anode and both are together called electrode. Cathodes are negatively charged and anodes are positively charged electrodes.

questions.question: The passage of an electric current through a conducting solution causes
questions.explanation: Passage of electric current through a conducting solution causes chemical reaction in solution called chemical effect of electricity. Conducting solution contains free ions that conducts electricity.

questions.question: The electrode connected to negative terminal is called
questions.explanation: The electrode connected to negative terminal is called cathode. The positively charged particles deposited at cathode end. Whereas anode is connected to positive terminal.

questions.question: The substance through which current can flow easily are called
questions.explanation: The substance through which electric current can flow easily are called good conductor of electricity. Metals are good conductor of electricity. Copper metal is used widely as an electric wire.

questions.question: During electroplating, the object to be electroplated is attached with
questions.explanation: During electroplating, the object to be electroplated is attached with cathode, and object by which electroplating is done is attached to anode. Because metals are cations and they get deposited in cathode (negative terminal).

questions.question: Conductor having very high resistance is
questions.explanation: The obstruction in flow of electric current is called resistance. Conductor having very high resistance is called insulator. Air, cloth, and rubber are good electrical insulators.

questions.question: When common salt is dissolved in water it become more conducting because

options.html: Water dissociate into hydrogen and oxygen
Sodium and chlorine ions are formed

questions.explanation: When common salt is dissolved in water it becomes more conducting because on passing electric current common salt (sodium chloride) dissociates into sodium and chlorine ions. These free ions are responsible for conduction.

questions.question: When LED is connected to a circuit the shorter wire is connected to
questions.explanation: LED stands for light emitting device. When LED is connected to circuit the shorter wire is connected to negative terminal and longer with positive terminal. Due to this convention two legs of LED are different in size.

questions.question: When electric current passes through the filaments of bulb it start emitting light due to
questions.explanation: The filaments of bulbs are made up of tungsten having very high resistance and high melting point. When current passes through the filament of bulb, it start emitting light due to heating effect of electric current. So in electric bulb, electrical energy is converting first to heat energy and then to light energy.

questions.question: In which material electricity does flow?
questions.explanation: Electricity does flows through Metallic materials as these are good conductor of electricity due to presence of free electrons.

questions.question: Which of the following liquid conduct electric current?
questions.explanation: Lemon juice contains citric acid that produces ions after dissociation in water. These ions conduct electricity.

questions.question: LED is better than bulb to use in tester as

options.html: LED glows at low electric current

questions.explanation: LED is used as tester in place of bulb because LED consumes less power and hence less electric current.

questions.question: Which effect of electric current is used for detecting the flow of current through solution when a torch bulb is used?
questions.explanation: When an electric current is passed through a conductor, the conductor becomes hot after some time and produce heat. This happens due to the conversion of some electric energy passing through the conductor into heat energy. This effect of electric current is called heating effect of current. The heating effect of current is used in various electrical heating appliances such as electric bulb, electric iron, room heaters, geysers, electric fuse, etc.

questions.question: Which of the following can not be used to detect the flow of current?
questions.explanation: As conductors are the materials which enable current to flow through them and we may get an electric shock that is why we cannot use conductors to detect the flow of current.

questions.question: Which of the following should be added to distilled water to make it conductor?
questions.explanation: Addition of acid, base, or salt or lime juice in distilled water makes it a good conductor of electricity as they provide ions on passing current.

questions.question: Electric supply is shut off before using water hoses in case of fire because

options.html: Water is good conductor of electricity
Water may damage the electric wiring
Bulb and tubes may got fused
Water is bad conductor of electricity

questions.explanation: As impure water is a good conductor of electricity.  if the main electrical supply is kept on, there are chances that the water may spill over it, and the chance to get an electric shock increases.

questions.question: When electric current is passed through acidified water then what is produced at positive carbon electrode
questions.explanation: When electric current is passed through acidified water, then oxygen gas is produced at positive terminal. Oxygen forms anions that are attracted towards positively charged electrode. while the hydrogen gas is produced at negative terminal.

questions.question: Chemical effect of electric current is used in
questions.explanation: Electroplating is a chemical process using which a metal is coated with a layer of another desired metal. Electroplating is done to make the metals shiny.

questions.question: The gas released on negative carbon electrode when current passes through acidified water?
questions.explanation:
When an electric current is passed through acidified water, it decomposes to give hydrogen and oxygen gas. The hydrogen gas is obtained at the cathode(negative electrode) and the oxygen gas is obtained at the anode(positive electrode).
The reaction for decomposition of water into oxygen and hydrogen is as follows:
2H2O(I) {tex}\to{/tex} 2H2(g) + O2(g)
Thus, the number of hydrogen molecules produced is twice the number of oxygen molecules. So, electrolysis of water produces 2 volumes of hydrogen gas and 1 volume of oxygen gas because the ratio of hydrogen and oxygen elements in water is 2:1 by volume. Therefore, the amount of gases collected in two test tubes is not equal.

questions.question: Which of the following is used for electroplating
questions.explanation: Chromium metal is used for electroplating because chromium is least reactive and provide shiny surface with scratch resistance.

questions.question: The chemical effect produced by an electric current depends on
questions.explanation: The chemical effect of electric current produced by an electric current depends on conducting solution and amount of current flowing through solution.

questions.question: Which metal is electroplated on iron for making cans?
questions.explanation: Tin is electroplated on iron for making cans for storing food and drinks as tin is less reactive than iron.

questions.question: In the process of refining copper metal a thin plate of pure copper is connected to
questions.explanation: Electrolytic refining is used to refine copper metal. A thin plate of pure copper is connected to cathode and thick impure copper plate to anode.

questions.question: During purification of copper, the electrolyte used is
questions.explanation: During purification of copper, the electrolyte is used is copper sulphate. A thin strip of pure copper is used as cathode and a thick impure copper as anode.

questions.question: When the free ends of tester are dipped into a solution, the magnetic needle shows deflection due to
questions.explanation: The magnetic needle responds to the flow of current between the free ends of a tester. When a tester is dipped into a "conducting" solution, the current will flow between the two terminals through the completed circuit and will lead to deflection in the magnetic needle. A conducting solution contains free ions, that can conduct electricity. For a "non-conducting" solution, the circuit will not be complete and hence there would be no current...

questions.question: While checking your tester, do not join its free ends for more than a few seconds otherwise

options.html: Cells of the battery heat faster
Cells of battery drain very quickly
Bulb of the tester get fused

questions.explanation: While we joined the free ends of the tester, it consumes power and as a result, the cells of bettery may drain quickly.

questions.question: Lemon juice is good conductor of electricity due to
questions.explanation: Lemon juice contains citric acid that gives ions on passing electric current.

questions.question: When minerals acids are added to water it produce ____________ to make it good conductor.
questions.explanation: When mineral acids like Hydrochloric acid, Sulphuric acid, etc. are added to water it produces hydrogen ions that provide conductivity to water.

questions.question: We should not use electric supply from mains or generator to experiment because

options.html: Flow of current can not be measured

questions.explanation:
These are some precautions we should take care of during the experiment.

  • Never touch a lighted electric bulb connected to the mains. It may be very hot and your hand may get burnt badly.
  • Do not experiment with the electric supply from the mains or a generator or an inverter. You may get an electric shock, which may be dangerous.
  • Use only electric cells.

questions.question: Close the continuous path of electric current is called
questions.explanation: The close and continuous path of electric current is called electric circuit. It consists of switch, connector and an electric appliance. In a continuous and close path of electric current the electron gets a path to flow to form an electric circuit.

questions.question: Distilled water bad conductor of electricity but vinegar is good conductor as it contain
questions.explanation: Distilled water is bad conductor of electricity because it do not contain salts but vinegar contain acetic acid that give hydrogen ions to conduct electricity.

questions.question: The water from taps, hand pumps, wells, and ponds is a good conductor of electricity because of
questions.explanation: Small amount of salts are present in water from taps, hand pumps, wells, and ponds. The salt present in these waters make them good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: Which of the following substance should not be added to distilled water to make it good conductor?
questions.explanation: Addition of acids, bases, and salts in distilled water makes it good conductor of electricity due to presence of ions in these substances but alcohol does not produce ions and it does not make any change in the pH value of distilled water.

questions.question: A device that converts the electrical energy into heat energy is
questions.explanation: Electrical energy is a form of energy that occurs as a result of either stored or moving charged particles. A toaster draws electric current (electrical energy) from a wall outlet and converts these moving electric charges into heat (thermal energy) in the filaments that turn red hot to cook your toast. The same has happened with Heater and geyser. As they contain coils having high resistivity.

questions.question: Jewellery maker electroplate silver and gold on less expensive metals to make it

options.html: To make it resistance to corrosion
To increase the cost of jewellery
To have the appearance of silver and gold

questions.explanation: Silver and Gold jewelry are electroplated with another less expensive metals to have appearance of gold or Silver as these metals are more expensive than other metals. Gold or Silver electroplating, when used in jewelry manufacturing, allows the manufacturer to provide the market with jewelry that appears to be made of pure gold or Silver at a cheaper price as the jewelry is only plated with small amount of gold or Silver.

questions.question: What serves as anode in a common dry cell?
questions.explanation: A common dry-cell battery is a zinc-carbon battery, which uses a cell that is sometimes called the Leclanché cell. The cell is made up of an outer zinc container, which acts as the anode. The cathode is a central carbon rod, surrounded by a mixture of carbon and manganese(IV) dioxide (MnO2).

questions.question: Boojho's uncle has set up an electroplating factory near his village. He should dispose off the waste of the factory:

options.html: according to the disposal guidelines of the local authority.

questions.explanation: In electroplating factory, the disposal of the used conducting solution is a major concern because it is a polluting waste, therefore it should be disposed off according to the disposal guidelines of the local authority.

questions.question: What is the common name for manganese-dioxide-alkaline- zinc cell?
questions.explanation: Alkaline cell is commonly called as manganese dioxide alkaline zinc cell.These batteries are the most commonly used worldwide in flashlights, toys, radios, compact disc players, and digital cameras. There are three variations: the zinc-carbon battery, the zinc chloride battery, and the alkaline battery. All provide an initial voltage of 1.55 to 1.7 volts, which declines with use to an end point of about 0.8 volt.

questions.question: The two leads of LED is are of
questions.explanation: LED stands for the light emitting diode. It consists of two lead one longer and other shorter. The longer lead is attached to the positive terminal while shorter with the negative terminal. A light-emitting diode (LED) is a two-lead semiconductor light source. It is a p–n junction diode that emits light when activated. When a suitable voltage is applied to the leads, electrons are able to recombine with electron holes within the device, releasing energy in the form of photons.

questions.question: During electrolytic refining of metal M, what would you take as cathode?
questions.explanation: During electrolytic refining, a thin strip of pure metal is used as a cathode and thick impure metal as the anode. So, during refining of metal M, the electrode joined at the cathode is made up of a thin sheet of pure metal M.

questions.question: When iron nail is placed in Blue colored solution of copper sulphate it change to green due to
questions.explanation:
It changes to green due to displacement of copper by iron. This happens because a highly reactive metal can displace a less reactive metal from its salt solution such a reaction is known as displacement reaction.
Copper Sulphate(blue) + Iron {tex}\to{/tex} Iron Sulphate(green) + Copper 
CuSO4 + Fe {tex}\to{/tex} FeSO4 + Cu
While,
Iron Sulphate + Copper {tex}\to{/tex} No Reaction

questions.question: It is not safe to carry out electrical repair works outdoor during rain because

options.html: Rainwater is a good conductor of electricity
It become difficult to grips the tools

questions.explanation: Rainwater contains dissolved salts, therefore rainwater can conduct electricity and electrician may get electric shock. Hence electric repair work can not take place for safety precautions.

questions.question: Which property of LED makes it more popular as source of light?

options.html: It looks very attractive and colourful
It is available in market at very low cost
It consumes less electricity and lasts longer
It is available in many colours and shapes

questions.explanation: An LED lamp is a light-emitting diode (LED) product that is assembled into a lamp (or light bulb) for use in lighting fixtures. LED lamps have a lifespan and electrical efficiency which are several times greater than incandescent lamps, and are significantly more efficient than most fluorescent lamps, with some chips able to emit more than 300 lumens per watt. Like incandescent lamps and unlike most fluorescent lamps (e.g. tubes and compact fluorescent lamps or CFLs), LEDs come to full brightness without need for a warm-up time; the life of fluorescent lighting is also reduced by frequent switching on and off.

questions.question: Which of the following is concern over the use of CFL

options.html: It contain mercury which is toxic
It can not be used everywhere

questions.explanation: CFLs (compact fluorescent lamps )  consume less electricity and lead to less emissions from power plants, they do contain mercury – a hazardous material. It is a highly toxic substance. Overall, in terms of mercury emissions,  compact fluorescent lamps are beneficial to the environment compared to other lamps.

questions.question: Rainwater and distilled water are bad conductors of electricity but rainwater sometimes conduct electricity due to

options.html: Presence of oxygen gas in water
Absence of salts in rain water
Presence of base in rain water
Presence of acid due to pollution

questions.explanation: Rainwater sometimes conduct electricity due to presence of acids in it. Distilled water which is a pure form of water is neither acidic nor basic in nature so distilled water does not dissolve into ions since conduction of electricity requires free ions, so distilled water does not conduct electricity. Rainwater being an impure form of water, contains many ionic species. These ions are responsible for electrical coductivity of rainwater.

questions.question: Which type of tester is most suitable for testing flow of very low current?
questions.explanation: As it cause deflection when current is very low and it cannot be tested by LED type and other kinds of testers.

questions.question: Which type of effect is produced by electric current passes through a conducting solution?
questions.explanation: The passage of an electric current through a conducting solution causes chemical reactions. As result bubbles of gas are formed, deposits of metal on electrodes may be seen and changes of color of solution may occur depending on what solution and electrode are used, these are some of the chemical effects of electric current.

questions.question: Name the resin which produces spark is
questions.explanation: Amber

questions.question: Interaction of charges is called
questions.explanation: Lightening

questions.question: Which of the following natural phenomenon is most destructive in terms of loss of life and property?
questions.explanation: Lightening, storms, and wind occurs in cooperatively smaller area and destructive on the other hand earthquake occurs over large as it includes one of the tectonic plate of which earth is made area and most destructive in terms of loss of life and property.

questions.question: Lightening is _________ on huge scale.
questions.explanation: Lightening occurs when two oppositely charged ions present in clouds come in contact with each other that cause electric spark.

questions.question: Sparkers can be seen on an electric pole. Which wires become
questions.explanation: Loose

questions.question: Building can be protected from lightening by using

options.html: Wooden logs at the top of building
Lightening rod on the levels of building
Lightening rod at the top of building

questions.explanation: Building can be protected from lightening by using lightening rod/ conductor at the top of building. Lightening conductor is placed at highest point of the building which can easily attract the free ions passing nearby and transfer it to earth.

questions.question: Some object can be charged by _________ with other objects.
questions.explanation: Some object can be charged by rubbing with other objects. During rubbing charged object transfer its charge to another uncharged body

questions.question: Electroscope is used to
questions.explanation: Electroscope is a device used to detect the presence of charge. It consists of two leaves like structure that expand when charged particle passes the charge from the top metallic nob.

questions.question: Like charges repel and unlike charge
questions.explanation: Like charges repel each other and unlike charge attract each other. This is called the attractive property of a magnet. If the North Pole of a bar magnet is taken close to the South Pole of another bar magnet, it attracts and clings. But if the North Pole of a bar magnet is brought near a the North Pole of another bar magnet it repels or moves away.

questions.question: The process of transferring a charge from a charged body to the earth is called
questions.explanation: The process of transferring a charge from a charged body to the earth is called Earthing.

questions.question: Two objects with like charges will
questions.explanation: repel each other

questions.question: When charge move, they constitute an
questions.explanation: all of these

questions.question: Earthing is the process of transferring charge from charged object to
questions.explanation: Earthing is the process of transferring charge from charged object to earth. Earthing of building is done to prevent it from lightening so that free flowing ions can be easily transfer to uncharged.

questions.question: The area where earthquake are likely to occur are called
questions.explanation: Seismic zone is area where chances of occurring earthquake are more than the others. It is the area from where earthquake arises as they are the weak zones of the earth's crust. Since earthquakes are caused by the movement of earth's plates, the boundaries of the plates are the weak zones where earthquakes are most likely to occur.

questions.question: Lightening strike could destroy
questions.explanation: Lightening strike could destroy life and property if it falls on building or tree because a flash of lighting carries a lot of electric energy which occurs due to interaction of free flowing ions causing high temperature and which is sufficient to cause fire.

questions.question: The power of earthquake is measured in terms of
questions.explanation: The power of earthquake is measured in terms of Richter scale. Earthquake if measure more than 7 on Richter scale is very destructive. The Richter Scale is a series of numbers from 1to 12 used to express the magnitude of an earthquake. The earthquakes having a magnitude of higher than 7 on this scale are really destructive.

questions.question: Lightening conductor is made up of
questions.explanation: Lightning conductor is used to protect the building against lightning. The top end of the conductor is fixed above the highest point of the building. From the top, the metal strip run along the outer wall of the building to the ground. The electric energy of the lighting passes through the metal strip and gets discharged safely into the ground through the buried metal plate. Since no electric energy produced by the lighting passes through the building, no damage is caused.

questions.question: _________ are used to protect the building against lightning.
questions.explanation: Lightning conductor is used to protect the building against lightning. It is placed at the top of building to pass free flowing ions from the surrounding to the lower end of the metal strip, which is joined to a metal plate and buried deep in the ground near the base of the building so no damage is caused to the building.

questions.question: An earthquake is the
questions.explanation: An earthquake is the sudden shaking of earth which lasts for a short time and is caused by violent movements of rocks deep inside the earth's crust. It causes immense damage to life and property if it's a magnitude higher than 7 on Richter Scale.

questions.question: Lightning is caused due to accumulation of
questions.explanation: Lightning is a natural phenomenon that occurs because of electric discharge between two or more clouds or within the cloud or between clouds and the earth. The meeting of negative and positive charges produces streaks of light and sound, which is known as lightning. The accumulation of charges can flow from cloud to cloud, or cloud to ground, or between two portions of the same cloud.

questions.question: The earthquake measuring 7 or more on Richter scale

options.html: Do not cause severe damage to life and property
Can cause severe damage to life and property
Can cause damage to old building
Can cause damage to aquatic life only

questions.explanation: Intensity of earthquake is expressed on the Richter scale. The earthquake having magnitude 7 or more than 7 on Richter scale can cause severe damage to life and property. They are destructive earthquakes.

questions.question: Which scientist showed that lightning and sparks from clothes is same phenomenon?
questions.explanation: Benjamin Franklin showed that lightning in the sky and tiny sparks of light from clothes are essentially the same phenomenon. In both cases, opposite charge come in contact with each other to create spark.

questions.question: Weathering is the

options.html: No change in weather for longer time

questions.explanation: Weathering is the breaking down of rocks into small pieces  by the action of moving water ,frost ,difference of temperature and winds to form soil.

questions.question: What are the weak zones of the earth's crust called where earthquakes are most likely to occur?
questions.explanation: The boundaries of earth’s plates where earthquake tends to occurs are called fault zone. Striking of plates occurs in this zone more frequently because these has rough ends which during motion when contact each other cause earthquake.

questions.question: The outermost layer of earth is called
questions.explanation: The outermost layer of earth is called crust. It is that layer which is compartively made of lighter rocks than the mantle and supports life on earth. The middle layer is called mantle and innermost layer is called core.

questions.question: Earthquake is caused by

options.html: Disturbance deep inside the earth crust
Disturbance in upper layer of earth crest

questions.explanation: Earthquake is caused by a disturbance in plates of earth deep inside the earth's crust when these moving plates slide past or collide with one another. It causes a huge loss of life and property.

questions.question: Benjamin Franklin showed lightening in
questions.explanation: On June 10, 1752, Benjamin Franklin flies a kite during a thunderstorm and collects ambient electrical charge in a Leyden jar, enabling him to demonstrate the connection between lightning and electricity. He also invented the lightning rod, used to protect buildings and ships.

questions.question: During lightning and thunderstorm safe place is
questions.explanation: No open place is safe during lightning and hence a home or any other building is the safest place during lightning.

questions.question: Which of the following state of India is not likely to be affected by earthquake?
questions.explanation: Jammu Kashmir, Gujarat, and Rajasthan are Indian states falling in same seismic zone and are very prone to earthquakes. Uttar Pradesh is not likely to be affected by earthquake as it is not in the seismic zone.

questions.question: The electric charges generated by rubbing are
questions.explanation: The electric charges generated by rubbing two substances are called static charge. Static charge is charge at rest but when there is rubbing between two objects it gets activated due to rise in temperature. The static electric charge remains bound to the surface of the object and does not move.

tests.instructionHtml:

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
