If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Instrument which measures and records the magnitude of earthquakes in term of shock waves it produces is called
questions.explanation: Seismograph is an instrument used to measure and record shock waves produced by an earthquake in the form of a graph. It also shows the duration for which the earthquake lasts in seconds and also the epicentre of the earthquake.

questions.question: Electric current is
questions.explanation: Electric current is movement of electric charges as free electrons flowing in conductor are responsible for the flow of charge due to difference in potential charge.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an insulator?
questions.explanation: Glass, air, and wood are bad conductors of electricity or insulators but copper is a metal and is a good conductor of electricity.

questions.question: Which of the following cannot be charged easily by rubbing?
questions.explanation: All the insulator objects like plastic scale, woollen cloth, and the inflated balloon can be charged by rubbing but a copper rod, made of metal, is a good conductor and cannot be charged by rubbing.

questions.question: In a thunderstorm, the charges accumulate near the upper edges of clouds are
questions.explanation: positively charged

questions.question: When a charged body is touched with our hand, than

options.html: The charged body loses its charge

questions.explanation: When a charged body is touched with hand than charged body loses its charge and becomes neutral as the excess of accumulated charge or static charges on it, gets transferred to the ground through our body. Our body acts as a conductor of electric current and charge flows through our body to the earth.

questions.question: During thunderstorm, we should hide under
questions.explanation: During thunderstorms we should hide under a shorter tree a short tree is less likely to be hit by lightning. On the other hand, a tall being near to the thunderclouds is more likely to be hit by lightning.

questions.question: Earthquakes are caused by the _______.

options.html: Movement of earth plates

questions.explanation: Earthquakes are caused by the movement of tectonic plates which slide past or collide with one another in lower layer of the earth.

questions.question: What is wind?
questions.explanation: Moving air is called wind. Air moves due to difference in air pressure of two places.Ai runder high pressure moves towards areas of low pressure. The greater is the difference in pressure ,the faster the air moves.

questions.question: Static charges can be produced by
questions.explanation: Static charge is charge that is not in motion but remains bound on the surface of the charged object. Static charge comes in motion due to raise in the temperature by rubbing or friction. Charge stored in battery or cell and rubbing two substances create static charge.

questions.question: Benjamin Franklin discovered that

options.html: Wind blows from high to low pressure.
Building can be protected by using lightning rod or lightning conductor at top of building.
Effects of flood on life of man

questions.explanation: Benjamin Franklin discovered that building can be protected by using lightning rod/ conductor at the top of building that carry the charge to the ground. If the lightning strikes, it will hit the top of lightning conductor rather than the building and is discharges safely into the ground.

questions.question: Reduced air pressure cause
questions.explanation: Reduced air pressure at a place cause increased wind speed because air from nearby area moves from high pressure area to low pressure area resulting in generation of wind.

questions.question: Violent storms accompanied by high wind speed is called
questions.explanation: A violent storm accompanied by high wind speed is called a cyclone. High wind speed uproots the trees, building, and electric poles causing huge loss of life and property.

questions.question: Warm air is _________ than cold air.
questions.explanation: Warm air is lighter than cold air. Warm air moves upward leaving low pressure below where cool air takes place.

questions.question: Air expands on
questions.explanation: Air expands on heating because particle of air gain kinetic energy that results into faster movement of particle of air.

questions.question: Rapid expansion of air due to lightening cause
questions.explanation: Thunder is caused during lightening because of the rapid expansion of air. Lightening provides energy to air due to which expansion of air occurs causing thundering sound.

questions.question: During lightening
questions.explanation: During lightening strikes of light is formed in the sky as two oppositely charged clouds strike and flow of electrons occur causing lightening strikes and raise in temperature.

questions.question: Sudden occurrence of light due to friction of clouds is called
questions.explanation: Due to friction charge is developed in clouds. Clouds having very high charge strike with other charged clouds due to which lightening occurs

questions.question: Thunderstorm occurs more frequently in
questions.explanation: Thunderstorm is the blowing of high speed wind followed by heavy rain. Thunderstorm occurs more frequently in tropical areas.

questions.question: Unlike charges
questions.explanation: Unlike charges attract each other but like charges repel each other.

questions.question: Pressure difference at two localities is created by
questions.explanation: Wind is caused due to unequal pressure at two localities. Unequal pressure is created due to unequal heating by sun rays.

questions.question: Lightening occurs when negatively changed clouds come in contact of
questions.explanation: Lightening occurs when negatively charged clouds come in contact of positively charged clouds. As flow of electrons cause raise in temperature due to which spark occurs causing lightening.

questions.question: During lightning which of the following is a NOT a safe place?
questions.explanation: Elevated Ridge

questions.question: Current electricity is
questions.explanation: Electricity is of two type’s - static electricity, the charge at rest, and current electricity, charge at motion.

questions.question: Wind direction is found by using
questions.explanation: Wind direction is found by using wind vane. Wind vane is made up of arrow like structure which are free to move towards the direction of wind.

questions.question: If a storm is accompanied by lightening, we should not take shelter under a high tree because

options.html: It has more chance of falling

questions.explanation: The layers of air surrounding the earth is called atmosphere. Atmosphere around the earth maintains its temperature as it prevents the loss of heat.If Thunder is accompanied by lightening the its advised not to take shelter under high tree because those contains positive charge due to which it can easily cause fire.

questions.question: Atmosphere around the earth maintains its
questions.explanation: The layers of air surrounding the earth is called atmosphere. Atmosphere around the earth maintains its temperature as it prevents the loss of heat.

questions.question: When plastic comb is rubbed with dry hair, it acquire
questions.explanation: When plastic comb is rubbed with dry hair, it acquires charge on it due to rubbing. The charged comb can attract small piece of paper.

questions.question: For escape of hot air room has
questions.explanation: Ventilators are placed just below the roof in the wall to escape the hot air from room. Hot air is lighter and moves upward and comes out through these ventilators.

questions.question: Wind generally blow area of
questions.explanation: Wind generally blows from area of high pressure to low pressure. Winds from high pressure rush towards low pressure to fill the gap.

questions.question: Lightening conductor is a device used to protect

options.html: Protect the building from effect of lightening
Protect us from effect of electric current
Protect the building from heat effect
Protect the building from effect of UV rays

questions.explanation: Lightening conductor is a device used to protect the building from effect of lightening. It is placed at the top of roof so that it can attract the free flowing electrons and pass them to earth.

questions.question: Which of the following natural phenomenon can not be predicted?
questions.explanation: Raining, cyclone and extreme cold can be predicted as position of wind and clouds can be known using satellite but earthquake occurs due to movement of plates in earth which cannot be predicted.

questions.question: A major earthquake that occur on 26 January 2001 in
questions.explanation: A major earthquake occurs on 26 January 2001 in Bhuj district of Gujarat in which large number of people dies and huge loss of property is reported due to its high magnitude.

questions.question: Wind air is generated due to
questions.explanation: Wind air is generated due to uneven heating of earth. Uneven heating leads to high and low pressure area that causes movement of air from high pressure to low pressure.

questions.question: High speed wind can be destructive due to
questions.explanation: High wind speed can be destructive due to large amount of kinetic energy present in it that uproots the tree and buildings.

questions.question: A sudden shaking or trembling of the earth which lasts for a very short time is called
questions.explanation: A sudden shaking or trembling of the earth which lasts for a very short time is called earthquake which is caused due to the movement of tectonic plates, dislocations, or faults of the earth's crust.

questions.question: Earthquake is caused due to

options.html: Disturbance in the upper most layer of crest
Movement of electric waves in the earth crest
Disturbance deep down the earth crest

questions.explanation: Earthquake is caused due to disturbance in plates deep down the earth crest that leads to shaking or trembling of some parts of earth.

questions.question: When an uncharged body B is brought into contact with a positively charged body A:
questions.explanation: B acquires negative charge

questions.question: Which of the following will not cause tremor in earth?
questions.explanation: Tremor in earth may be caused by volcano eruption, meteor hitting the earth and underground nuclear explosion because all these involved disturbance in earth that cause earthquake

questions.question: Which of the following is not under seismic zones?
questions.explanation: The area in which chances of earthquake is more is called seismic zones. Rann of Kutch, Rajasthan, and Indo-Gangatic plain fall under seismic zone. Western Punjab is not under seismic zone.

questions.question: Which power of earthquake on Richter Scale is most destructive?
questions.explanation: Magnitude of earthquake can be measured on Richter scale. The power of earthquake on Richter scale more than 7 is most destructive.

questions.question: Lightning is usually accompanied by
questions.explanation: Lightening is usually accompanied by thunder. The energy generated during lightening passes through the air that causes thunder due to sudden raise in temperature.

questions.question: The lightning conductor was first established by a scientist named:
questions.explanation: Benjamin Franklin

questions.question: Losing static electricity as charges move off an object is called
questions.explanation: Losing static electricity as charges move off an object is called electric discharge. Discharged object is electrically neutral and doesn't allow electricity to pass.

questions.question: An object becomes charged when the atoms in the object gain or lose
questions.explanation: An object becomes charged when the atoms in the object gain or lose electron. The object that gains electron becomes negatively charged and one who loose electron becomes positively charged.

questions.question: A device used to test whether an object is carrying charge or not is called
questions.explanation: Electroscope is a device for detecting electric on an object. By using an electroscope, we can tell whether an object is electrically charged or not.

questions.question: During lightning actually ________ take place.
questions.explanation: During lightning, electric discharge take place between oppositely charged clouds. It is actually a great electric spark in the sky produced by the electric charges in the sky.

questions.question: During thunderstorm which action may be done?

options.html: Switching ON and OFF of electrical switches

questions.explanation: During lightning and thunderstorm we should avoid using telephone, switching electrical switches as all these are connected through wires from where free electrons can easily pass. Mobile phone can be used.

questions.question: To protect tall building from the damage of lightning, what should be done?

options.html: Have a roof top garden with tall tree

questions.explanation: To protect the tall building from the damage of lightning , lightning conductor should be installed at the top of the building so that it can attract free flowing ions and pass them down to the earth. If the lightning strikes,it will hit the top of the lightning conductor rather than the building and gets discharged safely into the ground through the buried metal plate.

questions.question: During thunderstorm it is safer to
questions.explanation: During thunderstorm it is safer to stand in open field as tall tree and building may attract the lightning towards itself because they carry positive charge.

questions.question: Seismograph is an instrument used to record
questions.explanation: Seismograph is an instrument used to measure and record the magnitude of an earthquake in terms of the shock waves it produces. It helps in locating the epicentre of earthquake.

questions.question: The centre of cyclone is known as
questions.explanation: The centre of cyclone is known as eye of the storm. Cyclone is a storm which develops on the sea and has high speed winds swirling around a low pressure centre called the eye of the storm.

questions.question: A wind vane is used to find the
questions.explanation: Wind vane is an instrument used to find the direction of wind at a place. It is made of arrows pointing towards different directions and rotates freely. The arrow of the wind vane moves until it points to the direction from which the wind is blowing.

questions.question: A balloon, when taken near candle, burst because
questions.explanation: A candle flame produces heat. When a balloon is taken near candle, the heat causes the air molecules inside the balloon expands that leads to bursting of balloon due to high pressure inside it. The heated air exerts pressure on the walls of the balloon and due to uneven expansion the ballon bursts.

questions.question: In Japan cyclone is known as
questions.explanation: Cyclones a huge revolving storm caused by very  high speed winds blowing around a central area of very low pressure in the atmosphere along with rainfall. A cyclone is called "typhoon" in Japan and Philippines.

questions.question: Cyclone warning is issued
questions.explanation: Cyclone warning is issued 24 hours in advance to warn the people living near coastal areas so that people can make arrangements to shift the essential household goods and domestic animals, etc. To safer places. This warning is issued by the Meterological Department through radio and television media.

questions.question: Dark funnel shaped cloud reaches to earth from sky are called
questions.explanation: A tornado is a violent storm with a column of rapidly rotating winds, having the appearance of a dark, funnel-shaped cloud that reaches from the sky to the ground. It causes much damage from the sheer force of its high speed winds.

questions.question: Sparkers can be seen on electric pole. Which wires become
questions.explanation: Electrical wires are made up of copper which is a metal, metals conduct heat and electricity. Because of this phenomena the wire undergoes expansion and contraction every single day, thus if the wires were tightly held in the long run the wires might get snapped because of the contraction it faced.

questions.question: Rapid volume of air due to lightning cause
questions.explanation: During lightning, the air gets heated up and expands suddenly. This rapid expansion in the volume of air sends a disturbance through the air producing loud sound. This loud sound is heard as thunder.

questions.question: Natural disaster can not be predicted as
questions.explanation: Natural disaster cannot be predicted in advance because natural disaster occurs suddenly although a warning or predication of its occurrence is nowdays is given with the help of the pictures sent by weather satellites.

questions.question: It is easier to row a boat in the direction of wind because

options.html: Air exerts pressure in opposite direction
Air exerts pressure in direction of sailing
Fresh air help the sail in easy breathing

questions.explanation: It is easier to row a boat in direction of wind because the wind exerts a force on us in the same direction in which our boat is moving and makes our boat to move easily and faster.

questions.question: Which among the following is not a natural phenomenon?
questions.explanation: Earthquake, cyclone and lightning are natural phenomenon as it occurs naturally but cooking is a man-made process.

questions.question: Wind blows faster when

options.html: There is no difference in pressure
Pressure difference between two places is less
Pressure difference between two places is more

questions.explanation: Moving air is called wind. Wind blows faster when pressure difference between two places is more. Air moves from the regions of high pressure to regions of low pressure in the atmosphere.

questions.question: Which one of the following is most destructive?
questions.explanation: Earthquake, Tornado, Typhoon, and storm all are destructive but an earthquake of more than 7 magnitudes measured on the Richter scale is most destructive, causing huge losses to life and property.

questions.question: Increase in level of carbon dioxide in air causes
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide gas is a greenhouse gas. Increase in the level of carbon dioxide gas in the atmosphere leads to greenhouse effect that causes rise in earth's temperature, which leads to global warming.

questions.question: The boundaries of earth’s plates, where earthquake tends to occurs are called
questions.explanation: The boundaries of earth’s plates where earthquake tends to occurs are called fault zone. In this zone there are about a dozen large,irregularly shaped plates which slide over, under and past each other. Movement of these plates occurs in this zone causing earthquakes.

questions.question: Lightening occurs due to
questions.explanation: Lightening is a discharge of electricity. A single stroke of lightning can heat the air around it to 30,000°C (54,000°F)! This extreme heating causes the air to expand explosively fast. The expansion creates a shock wave that turns into a booming sound wave, known as thunder.

questions.question: Windmill is used to
questions.explanation: Windmill is used to generate electricity when the vanes of the windmill moves with the speed more than 15km/hr. The energy made by windmills can be used in many ways like it can be used to grind grains,pump water and sawing wood.

questions.question: Skyscrapers contain ___________ to prevent lightning  at the top of building.
questions.explanation: Skyscrapers can be protected from lightning by providing a  lightning conductor at the top of the building. Lightning conductor consists of a long thick metal rod /strip having sharp spikes at its upper end. The lower end of the metal strip is connected to a large copper plate buried deep inside called the earthing, which discharges the lightning safe into the ground.

questions.question: Which state in India is badly affected by cyclone recently?
questions.explanation: Orissa is a coastal state in India that is badly affected by cyclone. Bihar, Jharkhand and Karnataka is not affected by cyclone as they are not situated near the coastline but far away from the sea.

questions.question: High speed winds are accompanied by
questions.explanation: High speed winds are accompanied by reduced air pressure. Winds blows faster if pressure difference between the two regions is more. Air moves from the regions of high pressure to the regions of low air pressure.

questions.question: Which of the following is not due to pressure change?
questions.explanation: Earthquake occurs due to movement in tectonic plates of earth crust, volcanic eruptions, or by explosions. Earthquake is not due to pressure change.

questions.question: If a storm is accompanied by lightning, we should not take shelter under a high tree because

options.html: It has more chance of falling thunder

questions.explanation: If a storm is accompanied by lightning, we should not take shelter under a high tree because When a cloud passes over a tall tree, it induces an opposite charge on them. If the charge built is large, it leads to an electrical discharge in the form of a lightning strike, which may shatter the tree or set it on fire causing severe burns and death of the person standing under it.

questions.question: Earthquake activities are monitored by using
questions.explanation: Earthquake activities cannot be monitored as it occurs suddenly without any sign. Satellite can monitor wind and cloud activities only.

questions.question: Instrument to measure waves of the earthquake is called
questions.explanation: Seismograph is an instrument which detects and records the intensity of seismic waves generated by an earthquake. The graphical record of the intensity of seismic waves is called seismograms.

questions.question: Rearranging charges in uncharged metal object without contact with charged object is called
questions.explanation: Rearranging charges in uncharged metal object without contact with charged object is called induction because it does not required any contact but charge jumps from high end to the lower end.

questions.question: The like charges

options.html: In contrast to the attractive force between two objects with opposite charges, two objects that are of like charge will repel each other. That is, a positively charged object will exert a repulsive force upon a second positively charged object. This repulsive force will push the two objects apart.

questions.explanation: Repel each other

questions.question: Which natural disaster is very rare in India?
questions.explanation: Thunderstorm, cyclone, and earthquake are common natural disaster in India but tornado is common in Japan.

questions.question: Two objects repel each other. What charges might have?

options.html: Both may be having either negative or both positive charges.

questions.explanation: Objects with same charges repel each other i.e. Like charges repel each other. A positive charge repels another positive charge and a negative charge repels another negative charge.

questions.question: What makes the balloon inflated when placed in hot water?

options.html: Increase temperature make the balloon lighter
Increased temperature makes the balloon softer
Increased temperature makes the balloon heavy

questions.explanation: When a balloon is placed in hot water it gets inflated as an increase in temperature inside the balloon leads to an increase in air pressure inside the balloon and the volume of air on heating and it occupies a bigger space which leads to expansion and the balloon inflates.

questions.question: Which of the following is negatively charged?
questions.explanation: The electron is a subatomic particle that carries a negative electric charge. An electron has a mass that is approximately 1/1836 that of the proton.

questions.question: Electrical charges produced by rubbing are called
questions.explanation: When two insulators are rubbed vigorously over each other, electrical charge is produced. This kind of charge is called as static charge which gets activated due to rubbing.

questions.question: What happen when glass rod is rubbed with silk and attain positive charge?

options.html: Electron moves out from glass rod
Proton moves out from glass rod

questions.explanation: When glass rod is rubbed with silk, glass rod becomes positively charged due to movement of electron from glass rod to silk. The materials like glass lose electrons more easily and hence gets positively charged on rubbing . On the other hand materials like silk gain electrons more easily and hence gets negatively charged during rubbing. Whether an object will lose or gain electrons during rubbing depends on the nature of the material of the object.

questions.question: Flash point term is related to
questions.explanation: Flash point, the lowest temperature at which a liquid (usually a petroleum product) will form a vapour in the air near its surface that will “flash,” or briefly ignite, on exposure to an open flame. The flash point is a general indication of the flammability or combustibility of a liquid. Flash point term is related to study of climate.

questions.question: Lightning is seen little earlier than thunder sound because
questions.explanation: Light travels at the speed of 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108 m/s while sound travels at a speed of 332 m/s i.e., light travels much faster than sound that is why lightning is seen earlier and thunder is heard later.

questions.question: Which of the following is not caused by an earthquake?
questions.explanation: Earthquake may cause floods, landslide and Tsunami but not the thunder because earthquake occurs due to movement of the earth plates whereas thunder is caused due to striking of clouds.

questions.question: The point at which a movement occurred which triggered an earthquake is called
questions.explanation: Epicentre is the point at which a movement occurred which triggered an earthquake. It is the point on earth's surface directly above the focus. Focus is the place inside the earth's crust where the earthquake is generated.

questions.question: Electrons and protons attracted each other because

options.html: They are negatively and positively charged and opposite charges attract each other

questions.explanation: Electron have negative charge and proton have positive charge, opposite charges attract each other, so Electrons and protons attracted each other

questions.question: Waves produced on the surface of earth by tremors are called
questions.explanation: The waves generated on the surface of earth due to sudden movement in a part of earth' s crust are called seismic waves or shock waves. Seismograph is an instrument used to detect and record obtain the intensity of seismic waves generated by an earthquake.

questions.question: In ancient times, people thought that earthquake and lightning is due to
questions.explanation: In ancient time, people thought that earthquake and lightning g is due to wrath of God visiting them. Later it gets clear that it is due to moving plates of the earth's crust which slide past or collide with one another earthquakes occur and lightning is an electric spark in the sky between oppositely charged clouds.

questions.question: Light is a form of
questions.explanation: Light is a form of energy that enable us to see the different things present around us. When it strikes the earth's surface it converts to heat which is in the form of photons. These helps in transforming to electromagnetic energy

questions.question: Splitting of white rays of light into seven colours is called
questions.explanation: Splitting of white ray of light into seven colours when passes through a prism is called dispersion of light. Different colours of light bend through different angles with respect to the incident ray as they pass through the prism, thus emerge along different paths and seen as dispersed

questions.question: ___________ help us to see the objects.
questions.explanation: Light enable us to see the objects around us. During day sun generates sufficient amount of light to illuminate the objects. Reflected light after incidenting on the surface enters the eye to form image on retina which helps to see the objects.

questions.question: In human eyes, image is formed on
questions.explanation: In human eyes, image is formed on retina. Retina acts as a screen in human eye on which inverted image is formed which is passed to brain through optical nerve and it interprets the image.

questions.question: Object that emit light is called
questions.explanation: The objects that emit light of their own are called luminous object. A luminous object is one that gives off light or that glows of its own. Sun, stars, bulb, tube, etc are example of luminous object.

questions.question: Which type of lens is present in our eyes?
questions.explanation: Human eye contains transparent biconvex lens made up of crystalline protein which is elastic and flexible through which ray of light enters and focussed on the retina to form real image

questions.question: Speed of light is fastest in
questions.explanation: Speed of light is fastest in air and vacuum. Speed of light is almost 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108m/s in vacuum /air. Speed of light decreases with increase in density of the medium. Light travels slowest in diamond.

questions.question: Band of seven colours obtained on screen after dispersion is called
questions.explanation: The band of seven colours obtained on screen when white light passes through a prism after dispersion is called spectrum of white light. These colours are arranged as Violet, Indigo, Blue, Green, Yellow, Orange, and Red

questions.question: What is the name of object through which light can pass?
questions.explanation: The object through which light can pass easily is called transparent object. Glass, water, and thin plastic sheets are transparent object. When light encounters transparent material almost all of it passes directly through them.

questions.question: Rainbow is a
questions.explanation: Rainbow is a natural spectrum that is caused by reflection, refraction, and dispersion of light in water droplets suspended in air to form spectrum of light appearing in the sky. It always appear in the sky directly opposite to the sun.

questions.question: Which of the following travels faster?
questions.explanation: Speed of light in air is 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108m/s which is much faster than speed of sound (nearly 340m/s), jet airplane, and supersonic planes (765m/h).

questions.question: Braille system is used by
questions.explanation: Braille system is used by blind people to read and write. It consists of raised dots or bumps that are felt by blind people with a finger to read and write . It is named after Louis Braille, the French man who invented it.

questions.question: _________ refers to collection of rays.
questions.explanation: Beam is a collection of rays It is a directional projection of light energy radiating from a light source. A beam of light is visible only when part of it is scattered by the objects

questions.question: When a ray of incident light strikes the surface of a plane mirror at an angle of 60o with the mirror. The angle of reflection is:
questions.explanation:
Here, the angle to the mirror surface (the angle between the incident ray and the mirror) = 60o
Therefore, the angle of incidence (the angle between the incident ray and the normal) = 90o - 60o = 30o
Now, we know that according to the law of reflection, the angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection.
Therefore, the angle of reflection will be 30o.

questions.question: What makes the object visible?
questions.explanation: Light makes the object visible. After reflection of a light from the surface of object enters the human eye and form image on the retina which helps to see the object. In absence of light, we are not able to view the object present in our surrounding.

questions.question: Mirror reflection gives us
questions.explanation: Clear image is formed when reflections are caused by smooth, regular, and shiny surfaces, such as mirrors. As mirror has regular smooth shiny reflecting surface through which regular reflection occurs thus formed clear and bright image of an object in a mirror

questions.question: Angle of incidence is always
questions.explanation: Angle of incidence of a ray is always equal to angle of reflection. The angle of reflection of a ray is the angle measured from the reflected ray to the normal. whereas angle of incidence is measured between the ray incidenting on the surface and a normal that intersects the surface at the same point.

questions.question: From a source, light travels as rays which are
questions.explanation: From a source, light travels as rays which are parallel beam of light. A light beam is a directional projection of light energy radiating from a light source to all the directions. One that are intercepted on earth's surface, correspond to a very small angle for which all the rays considered roughly as parallel.

questions.question: When an object 40 cm high is placed in from of a plane mirror at a distance of 100 cm then the size of the image will be
questions.explanation: When an object 40 cm high places in front of a plane mirror at a distance of 100 cm then the size of the image will be also 20 cm.

questions.question: Velocity of light in vacuum (air) is
questions.explanation: {tex}3 \times {10^8}m/s{/tex}

questions.question: What will be the angle of reflection if the angle of incidence is 0o?
questions.explanation: When angle of incidence is 0o then angle of reflection is also 0o.

questions.question: A number of rays from different direction assemble at point are called
questions.explanation: When a number of rays from different direction assemble at one point are called as convergent rays.

questions.question: Angle of incidence is equal to the angle of reflection.
questions.explanation: During reflection of light, the angle of reflection is always equal to the angle of incidence according to the law of light.

questions.question: Light travels in which direction?
questions.explanation: Light travels in straight line. This kind of travelling of light is called as rectilinear propagation of light.

questions.question: Which of the following object will produce regular reflection?
questions.explanation: Regular reflection is produced by smooth and polished surface like mirror. The reflected rays of light are always parallel to each other in regular reflection.

questions.question: Lateral displacement produced by a glass slab is directly proportional to:
questions.explanation: lateral displacement produced by a glass slab is directly proportional to its thickness of the slab.

questions.question: Which of the following have reflecting surface?
questions.explanation: Mirrors have reflecting surface because one side of mirror is painted with silver bromide and another is smooth shining through which reflection occurs.

questions.question: __________ refers to collection of rays.
questions.explanation: Collection of rays originating from a source of light and moving parallel to each other is called beam of light.

questions.question: Which of the following substance change the direction of light?
questions.explanation: Mirror is a reflecting object that changes the direction of light falling on it. Lens, thin plastic sheet, and water are transparent and allow light to pass through it.

questions.question: To a fish, the plane surface of the earth appears to be enclosed within an angle of
questions.explanation: 97o

questions.question: Power of a lens is 2D, its focal length is
questions.explanation: When the power of a lens is 2D, then its focal length is 50 cm.

questions.question: Which type of mirror always forms virtual, erect and image of same size?
questions.explanation: Plane mirror always forms virtual, erect, and image of same size. The image is laterally inverted too.

questions.question: Rainbow is formed due to

options.html: Dispersion of light by water droplets
Reflection of light by water droplets

questions.explanation: A rainbow is a natural phenomenon that is caused by reflection, refraction, and dispersion of light in water droplets resulting in a spectrum of light appearing in the sky. It takes the form of a multicoloured arc.

questions.question: Process of bouncing back of light in same medium is called
questions.explanation: The process of bouncing back of light rays in same medium after hitting the surface is called as reflection of light.

questions.question: In regular reflection

options.html: Reflected rays are parallel to each other
Reflected rays are perpendicular to each other
Refracted rays are parallel to each other

questions.explanation: In regular reflection, reflected rays are parallel to each other because of reflection from the smooth surface

questions.question: Which source of light is called ultimate source of light?
questions.explanation: Sun is called as ultimate source of light because all source of energy is directly or indirectly derived from Sun on earth

questions.question: Myopia can be corrected using
questions.explanation: concave lens

questions.question: Which type of mirror is used to make Periscope?
questions.explanation: Periscope is a device to see object which are not in direct line of vision. It is placed in submarines too. Plane mirror is used to make periscope.

questions.question: The most popular resource for visually challenged person is called
questions.explanation: Braille system is most popular resource for visually challenged person. It consist of dots of different shape and size that can be felt by fingertips to understand the literature.

questions.question: In convex mirror, the reflection of light takes place at
questions.explanation: In convex mirror, the reflection of light takes place at bulging out surface. It always form virtual, erect, and diminished image.

questions.question: Braille system has __________ dot pattern.
questions.explanation: Braille system has 63 dot pattern that can be felt with finger tips to understand the matter written on it.

questions.question: Which of the following instrument is used to obtain coloured pattern and designs?
questions.explanation: Kaleidoscope is used to obtain coloured pattern and design. Kaleidoscope works on the principle of multiple reflection of light through which different patterns are formed after reflection through plane mirrors kept at different angles.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an optical aid?
questions.explanation: Contact lens, magnifier, and bifocal lens are optical instruments as light is necessary for their working. Hearing aid is used by deaf persons to enhance their hearing capability.

questions.question: Solar cooker is used to
questions.explanation: A solar cooker is an insulated device which trap and  uses the energy of direct sunlight to cook food.

questions.question: The amount of light entering the eye ball is controlled by
questions.explanation: The amount of light entering the eye ball is controlled by Iris. Iris is responsible for changing the size of pupil according to light condition.

questions.question: Which type of image can be obtained on screen?
questions.explanation: Real and inverted image can be obtained on screen only. Real image is formed by actual meeting of rays on the screen

questions.question: Focal length of eye lens is changed by action of
questions.explanation: Focal length of eye lens is changed by action of Cilliary muscles to see the near and far object using same lens. Ciliary muscles are capable of adjusting the eyelens

questions.question: How many cells are there in a Braille character?
When light bounces off form surface the surface is
questions.explanation: When light bounces off from surface, the surface is called reflector. Mirror is excellent reflector as it reflects all the rays of light falling on it.

questions.question: Light sensitive cell present in retina are
questions.explanation: In human eye, image formation takes place at retina. Retina consists of two types of light sensitive cells called rods and cones.

questions.question: A concave mirror is a part of a sphere of radius 20 cm. Its focal length is
Transparent front part of eyeball is called
questions.explanation: The transparent front part of eye ball is called cornea. Pupil is situated just behind the cornea through which rays of light enters the eye ball.

questions.question: Concave mirror is used in headlight of vehicle as

options.html: It forms the strong beam of light
It reflect the parallel beam of light

questions.explanation: Concave mirror is used in headlight of vehicle as concave mirror reflect the parallel beam of light from their focus. Concave mirror is also called as converging mirror.

questions.question: Rainbow is the natural phenomenon showing
questions.explanation: Rainbow is a natural phenomenon that occurs due to dispersion of light through water droplets present in the atmosphere after the rain shower to form a spectrum.

questions.question: Shaving mirror are usually
questions.explanation: Concave mirror forms virtual, erect, and magnified image when object is placed between focus and pole. So, it is used as a shaving mirror to see a larger face.

questions.question: The colour of eyes is due to colour of
questions.explanation: The colour of eyes is due to colour pigmentation present in the diaphragm of iris present around the pupil that controls the amount of light entering the eye ball.

questions.question: Splitting of white light into seven colours is called
questions.explanation: Splitting of white beam of light into seven colours when passes through a prism is called dispersion of light.

questions.question: The light sensitive sense organ of human body is
questions.explanation: The light sensitive sense organ of human body is eye. Eye is also called as photoreceptor as it receive the light stimulus.

questions.question: White light consist of
questions.explanation: White light consists of seven colour of light. These colours separate out when white light pass through a prism.

questions.question: Sunlight is referred as
questions.explanation: Sunlight is referred as white light although sunlight consists of seven colours of light.

questions.question: A stick in water appears broken due to
questions.explanation: refraction of light

questions.question: The object which emits their own light are called
questions.explanation: The object which emits their own light is called luminous bodies. They may be natural or manmade sources of light.

questions.question: Dentist use concave mirror because as it forms
questions.explanation: Dentist use concave mirror because concave mirror forms erect and magnified image of teeth to view its minute details.

questions.question: Which of the following works on the concept of multiple reflections?
questions.explanation: A kaleidoscope is an optical instrument with two or more reflecting surfaces tilted to each other in an angle, so that one or more objects on one end of the mirrors are seen as a regular symmetrical pattern when viewed from the other end, due to repeated reflection.

questions.question: Which type of mirror is used as concentrator in solar furnace?
questions.explanation: Concave mirror is used as concentration in solar furnace. As the solar light coming from infinity meet at focus to produce more heat.

questions.question: Speed of light
questions.explanation: Speed of light changes with medium. Light travels faster in rarer medium and slower in denser medium.

questions.question: Refractive index of diamond with respect to vacuum is 2.5, and then the velocity of light in diamond is
questions.explanation: {tex}1.2 \times {10^8}m/s{/tex}

questions.question: A drop of water appears like pearl due to
questions.explanation: total internal reflection of light

questions.question: When you stands in front of a small mirror. The image formed will appear to be of
questions.explanation: Plane mirror always forms virtual, erect,  image of same size as object. So, when you stand in front of small mirror image will be of same size.

questions.question: Which of the following object can acts as both converging and diverging mirror?
questions.explanation: Steel spoon can acts as both converging and diverging mirror. Its inner scoop side behaves as a concave mirror and outer bulged side as a convex mirror

questions.question: A bundle of light rays is called a
questions.explanation: A bundle of light rays is called a beam of light. When source of light emits light, parallel beam of light together forms beam of light.

questions.question: When a concave mirror from a real and enlarged image of an object?

options.html: when the object is placed at F
when the object placed between F and C
when the object is placed at 2F
when the object places between F and P

questions.explanation: when the object placed between F and C

questions.question: Which of the following physical quantity do not require medium for propagation?
questions.explanation: Light is the physical quantity that do not require medium for propagation. Sound require medium for propagation. Light can travels in vacuum also.

questions.question: Which property of light enables us to see our face in mirror?

options.html: Light is a form of energy

questions.explanation: Reflection of light enables us to see our face in mirror as mirror forms image behind the mirror which is virtual and erect.

questions.question: What is the name of phenomenon in which light change its direction after hitting the surface?
questions.explanation: The phenomenon in which light changes its direction after hitting the surface is called reflection of light. Light reflects from the regular and shiny surface eg.mirror.

questions.question: Which of these explain the image of building or tree in river or pond?
questions.explanation: The image of building or tree in river or pond is formed by reflection of light form the water surface of ponds or river which acts as a reflector.

questions.question: A red rose and a white rose are placed in front of a source of light. The colour of the shadow will be

options.html: Black for red rose and white for white rose
Red for red rose and white for white rose

questions.explanation: The colour of the shadow is always black in spite of different object. So, a red rose and white rose always form black colour of shadow.

questions.question: Small patches of sunlight under the tree are the
questions.explanation: The small patches of sunlight under the tree are the image of sun formed by small gaps between tree leaves.

questions.question: Burning candle cannot be seen through a bended tube because

options.html: Light is a form of energy
Light can bend in metallic pipe

questions.explanation: Light travels in straight line. So, burning candle cannot be seen through a bended tube. This property of light is called rectilinear propagation of light.

questions.question: Mirror is a reflecting surface but glass is a
questions.explanation: Mirror is a reflecting surface but glass is a transparent object. Transparent object do not form image as reflection of light do not take place.

questions.question: Plane mirror always forms image
questions.explanation: Plane mirror always forms image behind the mirror as image is virtual, erect and of same size.

questions.question: Which of the following capital letter look the same in a plane mirror?
questions.explanation: In plane mirror lateral inversion takes place in which left side appear right and right side appear left. So, letter M appear same in plane mirror.

questions.question: From a source light travels as rays which are
questions.explanation: From a source light travels as rays which are parallel beam of light. Light always travel in straight line.

questions.question: On a sunny day, we are not able to see the shadow of Aeroplane flying in the sky because

options.html: Aero plane is a transparent object
Very large and faint image is formed

questions.explanation: On a sunny day, we are not able to see the shadow of an aeroplane flying in the sky because very large and fait image is formed on the ground.

questions.question: When two mirrors are placed parallel to each other

options.html: Large number of image are formed

questions.explanation: When two mirrors are placed parallel to each other, large numbers of images are formed due to repeated reflection form opposite side placed mirror.

questions.question: When an object is placed at a distance of 15 cm from a plane mirror. The image formed is at distance of

options.html: 15 cm in front of the mirror

questions.explanation: Plane mirror form virtual, erect and image of same size behind the mirror. The object distance is equal to image distance. Object placed at a distance of 15 cm form image at distance of 15 cm behind the mirror.

questions.question: A narrow beam of light is made up of
questions.explanation: A light ray is a line (straight or curved) that is perpendicular to the light's wavefronts; its tangent is collinear with the wave vector

questions.question: Light always travels in straight line. This property is called
questions.explanation: Light always travel in  a straight line. This property of light is called a rectilinear propagation of light. In a homogeneous transparent medium light travels in a straight line called rectilinear propagation of light.

questions.question: In a plane mirror image formed is
questions.explanation: In plane mirror, image formed is virtual and erect and of same size as the object.

questions.question: A toy is placed at 10 cm in front of a plane mirror. What is the distance of image from the mirror?
questions.explanation: The distance of image from plane mirror is 10 cm because the image formed by plane mirror has same distance, colour, size

tests.name: Light Test 07
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Light CBSE Online Test 07 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Questions can be answered in any order in given time.
  • For moving to previous question click on "PREVIOUS" at the bottom of the screen.
  • Students can mark the question for review too.
  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: We can see an object when it

options.html: Refract or absorb the rays of light
Reflect or emit rays of light

questions.explanation: When we see an object when it reflects or emit rays of light. Reflected light enters the eyes and image is formed on the retina.

questions.question: The mirror used as rear view mirror in vehicle is
questions.explanation: Convex mirror is used as rearview mirror in vehicle as a convex mirror always forms a virtual, erect, and diminished image that gives a wider view field to enable drivers to see the traffic behind.

questions.question: In which type of mirror image is always laterally inverted?
questions.explanation: Plane mirror always forms image which is laterally inverted. The left side object appear right side and visa versa.

questions.question: The path of the light is visible in
questions.explanation: The path of the light is visible in presence of dispersing medium like dust particle or prism or other substances in the medium.

questions.question: Dentist use concave mirror because it forms
questions.explanation: Dentist use concave mirror because concave mirror forms erect and magnified image of the object placed between pole and focus.

questions.question: In plane mirror, image formed is

options.html: At same distance from the mirror

questions.explanation: In plane mirror, the image formed is at same distance from the mirror and of same height as the object which is virtual and erect too.

questions.question: Objects are visible, when reflected light
questions.explanation: Objects visible to us only when ray of light enters our eye after reflection forming an image on the retina

questions.question: Which mirror always forms virtual, erect, and image of the same size. The image is laterally inverted too.
questions.explanation: Plane mirror always forms virtual, erect, and image of the same size. The image is laterally inverted too.

questions.question: The image formed on cinema screen is an example of
questions.explanation: The image formed on cinema screen is an example of real image as it is obtained on screen. The inverted image is re-inverted by the projector to appear erect.

questions.question: Normal is
questions.explanation: Perpendicular at the point of incident of light to the surface is called normal. Normal is an imaginary line.

questions.question: The word ambulance is written as below on patient carrying vehicle because Rear view mirror of ahead vehicle

options.html: Forms erect and laterally inverted image

questions.explanation: The word ambulance is written as shown above because rear mirror ahead vehicle forms erect and laterally inverted image which make driver ahead to see and gave passage to it

questions.question: Real image is always
questions.explanation: Real image is always inverted and can be obtained on screen. Real image is formed by actual meeting of rays.

questions.question: Human eye can see maximum ________ separate pictures in a second.
questions.explanation: Human eye can see maximum 16 separate pictures in a second. The images formed on retina remain up to 1/16th second. This phenomenon is called persistence of vision.

questions.question: Videography and cinematography is based on principle of
questions.explanation: Videography and cinematography is based on principle of persistence of vision in which image formed on retina remain for 1/16th second. So, picture moving at the speed with 24 frames in a second appear to be connected with each other.

questions.question: Different colours are due to ability of the object to
questions.explanation: Different colours are due to ability of the object to reflect the rays of light. The pigments present in objects absorb some light of the required wavelength and reflect the others.

tests.name: Light Test 08
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Light CBSE Online Test 08 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Danger signals are made red coloured because red light
questions.explanation: Danger signals are made red coloured because red light have longer wavelength that scatter less. Red coloured light can be seen from distance due to less scattering.

questions.question: Sky appear blue coloured during day because

options.html: Blue coloured light can be scattered by small particles
Sun light is made up of blue light only
Blue colour of light is visible in sunlight only

questions.explanation: Sky appear blue coloured during day because blue coloured light can be scattered by small particles present in atmosphere due to its shorter wavelength.

questions.question: A person want to obtain virtual image of same size, he should use a
questions.explanation: Virtual image of same size is formed by plane mirror only. A person want to obtain virtual image of same size should use plan mirror.

questions.question: Which of the following phenomenon is based on the persistence of vision principle?
questions.explanation: Cinematography is based on the principle of persistence of vision. Colour blindness, hemophilia, and myopia are defects of vision.

questions.question: A person is not able to view the nearby object clearly but can see far object distinctly. He is suffering from
questions.explanation: A person suffering from Hypermetropia or farsightedness is not able to view the nearby object clearly but can see a far object distinctly. It can be corrected using a convex lens of proper power.

questions.question: The phenomenon of bending of rays of light when passes from one medium to another medium is called
questions.explanation: The phenomenon of bending of rays of light when passes from one medium to another medium where its speed is different is called refraction of light. In Refraction of light when it passes from a rarer medium to a denser medium it bends towards the normal to the boundary between the two mediums.

questions.question: Diverging mirror is used as a rare view mirror in vehicle as it

options.html: Always form real and magnified image
Always form erect and diminished image
Always form erect the magnified image
Always form real and diminished image

questions.explanation: Convex mirror is called as diverging mirror which is used as rearview mirror in vehicle because it always form erect and diminished image and also cover wider area. As parallel beam of light diverges after reflection from a convex mirror and appears to meet at a point behind the mirror forming virtual, erect, and diminished image.

questions.question: Which type of mirror is used as shop security image?
questions.explanation: Convex mirror is used as shop security mirror because it covers a larger area and forms an erect image of the object to view a larger section of the shop.

questions.question: Which type of mirror always form image behind the mirror?
questions.explanation: Plane mirror and convex mirror always form image behind the mirror which is virtual and erect. The image formed by plane mirror is same in size while that of convex mirror is smaller sized.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of virtual image?

options.html: Image formed on retina of eyes

questions.explanation: The image formed by a plane mirror is always virtual, erect, and of the same size as the object. Image formed on retina, pinhole camera, and cinema screen are real images.

questions.question: Diffused reflection of light

options.html: does not obey the laws of reflection
May obey or may not obey laws of reflection
Occurs from plane surface like mirror

questions.explanation: Diffused reflection of light does not obeys the law of reflection of light as reflected rays are not parallel to each other.

questions.question: The elementary particles that defines light is the
questions.explanation: a particle representing a quantum of light or other electromagnetic radiation. A photon carries energy proportional to the radiation frequency but has zero rest mass.

questions.question: Light consists of electromagnetic waves that

options.html: Do not require material medium for propagation
Can propagate in rarer medium only

questions.explanation: Light consists of electromagnetic waves that do not require material medium for its propagation. It can travel in vacuum.

questions.question: The defects of eyes due to absence of cones in retina is
questions.explanation: Colour blindness is the defect of vision due to absence of cones in retina. Cones are responsible to see the colour of the object. Some people do not contain certain cones which enable them to see colour.

questions.question: In figure given below, the image of a clock in plane mirror is shown the correct time is
questions.explanation: Plane mirror forms virtual, erect and laterally inverted image of the object. So, the correct time shown is 9:25.

  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

questions.question: The Sun,other stars, Moon, planets and other distant objects in space are called
questions.explanation: The Sun, other stars, Moon, planets, and other distant objects in space are called Celestial bodies. Solar system is the sun and planets, Galaxy is group of stars only and Universe is the vast space including galaxies, dust, gas, and all forms of matter.

questions.question: Which is the farthest planet of solar system?
questions.explanation: Neptune

questions.question: Size of moon keeps changing when viewed from earth. This change is called
questions.explanation: The size of moon keeps changing when viewed from earth. This change is called phases of moon. The part of moon on which sunlight falls is visible to us.

questions.question: Sky is not clear in cities due to
questions.explanation: In cities a number of industries and vehicle emits large amount of dust and smoke that makes sky not so clear.

questions.question: What is the time-period of revolution of Halley's Comet:
questions.explanation: Halley’s Comet is visible from earth after about 76 years. This is the time-period of revolution of this comet.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a celestial body?
questions.explanation: Star, moon, and planets are celestial bodies as they are located in sky but artificial satellite made by human beings revolves around the earth in fix orbit for fix interval of time.

questions.question: Star appear small because
questions.explanation: Stars appear small because stars are very far from earth. Star appears as small balls as light from the star reaches to us in very small amount due to distance.

questions.question: ISRO stands for
questions.explanation: ISRO stands for Indian space research organisation. It is the space agency of the Indian government headquartered in Bengaluru, Karnataka.

questions.question: The sun and other stars are huge bodies made up of
questions.explanation: Sun and other stars are huge bodies made up of hot gases. They emit light due to nuclear fusion of hydrogen into Helium.

questions.question: The sun makes life possible by providing us with
questions.explanation: The Sun is the ultimate source of light and heat that supports life. Life is possible on earth due to Sun. The energy required for all kinds of living organism come from Sun.

questions.question: Water in liquid form is found only on
questions.explanation: Water in liquid form is found only on the earth. Water is essential for survival of life. On earth water is present as solid, liquid and gaseous state.

questions.question: The path of planet around the sun is called its
questions.explanation: Planets revolve around the Sun on a fixed path. The path of the planet around the Sun is called its orbits.

questions.question: Sun is
questions.explanation: Sun is a huge ball of gases emitting heat and light due to nuclear fusion. The size of Sun is very much larger than earth

questions.question: The brightest object in clear night sky is
questions.explanation: Moon is the brightest object in clear night sky. Moon reflects the light of sun as it does not have its own light.

questions.question: Moon is the
questions.explanation: Moon is the natural satellite of earth as moon revolves around the sun in a fixed orbit. Moon reflects the light of the Sun.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The various shapes of bright part of moon is called
questions.explanation: The various shapes of bright part of the moon is called phases of moon. The shape of the moon keeps changing every day.

questions.question: Which planets has huge red spots
questions.explanation: Jupiter is called red planet due to presence of a huge red spot over it. This planet is easily visible from the earth.

questions.question: The celestial body nearest to the earth is
questions.explanation: Moon is the natural planet of the earth and distance between them is 384,400 kms.

questions.question: Neptune have very low temperature due to
questions.explanation: Neptune is the eighth planet  from the Sun. Due to far distance Neptune have very low temperature as very less light of sun reaches there.

questions.question: A piece of rock which burns as it enters through the Earth's atmosphere is
questions.explanation: A piece of rock which burns as it enters through the earth's atmosphere due to friction is called meteoroids.

questions.question: The first outside orbit of the earth planet is
questions.explanation: Mars

questions.question: Millions of stars together form a
questions.explanation: Million of stars together make a galaxy. Our solar system falls under the milky way galaxy.

questions.question: Small celestial body revolving around a planet is called
questions.explanation: Satellites are small celestial body revolving around a planet. Moon is the natural satellite of earth.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a planet?
questions.explanation: Mars, Saturn and Mercury are planets that revolve around the Sun. Moon is the natural satellite of Earth.

questions.question: Day and night occurs on the earth due to
questions.explanation: Day and night occurs on the earth due to rotation of earth on its own axis. The part facing the sun is day and other part is night.

questions.question: In a solar system, the planets between which the earth lies are
questions.explanation: In solar system, planets revolve around the Sun in a fixed orbit. Venus is the second planet in the solar system, Earth is third and Mars is fourth.

questions.question: Heavenly bodies that emit heat and light throughout the year are called
questions.explanation: Heavenly bodies that emit heat and light throughout the year are called stars. Stars are a huge ball of gases that emit heat and light.

questions.question: The distance travelled by light in one year is called
questions.explanation: The distance travelled by light in one year (around 9,460,000million km) is called light year. Light year is unit of large distance.

questions.question: The number of planets in our solar system is
questions.explanation: There are eight planets in our solar system. They are Mercury, Venus, Earth, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn, Uranus, and Neptune.

questions.question: The path along which celestial bodies revolve around the Sun is called
questions.explanation: Orbit is a gravitationally curved path of an object about a point in space.

tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Stars and the Solar System CBSE Online Test 03 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Small celestial bodies characterised by small head and a long tail is called
questions.explanation: The comets appear as a body with a small head and long tail because as it approaches the Sun, it warms up and gases evaporate from its surface which forms a glowing tail that streams away from the direction of the Sun. Eg: Halley's Comet.

questions.question: Which one is the brightest planet of solar system?
questions.explanation: Venus is the brightest planet of solar system. It is also known as morning star and evening star.

questions.question: The largest planet of solar system is
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet(radius-69,911km) of solar system. Jupiter has a large number of satellites. It also has faint rings around it.

questions.question: Which one is called red planet?
questions.explanation: Mars planet is called red planet due to the presence of iron oxide (rust) in soil which gives its reddish appearance. It is situated between Earth and Jupiter.

questions.question: Which one of the following does not have their own light?
questions.explanation: Moon does not have its own light. Moon reflects the light of sun and appears as luminous bodies. Star, Sun, and Constellation have their own light.

questions.question: The brightest and hottest planet of solar system is
questions.explanation: Venus is the brightest object in the sky as it has a thick cover of clouds that reflects light and is visible low in the sky in the morning or evening in different times of the year. Venus has an atmosphere that consists of carbon dioxide(97%), nitrogen, and clouds of sulphuric acid that traps heat leading to the surface temperature around 460°C . There are many volcanoes on Venus.

questions.question: The distance of stars, are expressed in terms of
questions.explanation: We measure distances on Earth in kilometres but the distances between stars and galaxies are so great that such units (metres and kilometres) are not convenient to use so we use light year. The distance travelled by light in one year(around 9,460,000million km) is called a light year.

questions.question: The Earth completes it's journey around the Sun in
questions.explanation: Earth completes its revolution around the Sun in 365.25 days that makes a year. The rotation of earth on its own axis takes 24 hours.

questions.question: Which planet is visible from the earth for most part of the year?
questions.explanation: Mars is visible from the earth for most of the year. Mars is closer to earth (distance between them is almost 78 million kms) and can be seen throughout the year.

questions.question: The moon takes about _________ days to go around the earth once.
questions.explanation: Moon is the natural satellite of earth. The moon takes about 27.33 days to go around the earth once and its the same time it takes to complete one rotation on its axis.

questions.question: Pole star is also known as
questions.explanation: Pole star is also known as Dhruva Nakshatra in Sanskrit. This is the only star whose position appears fixed, its seen as a faint star located directly above the North Pole.

questions.question: The group of stars that form a recognizable shape are called
questions.explanation: The group of stars that form a recognizable shape is called constellation. Ursa Major, Ursa minor, polar bear, etc are constellation.

questions.question: Heavenly bodies that revolves around the planet are called
questions.explanation: A satellite is an object that revolves around a planet. A natural satellite is known as a moon and many planets in the Solar system have moons. Artificial satellites are sent into space by humans to learn more about the Earth and the universe.

questions.question: Which planet has beautiful rings to encircle it?
questions.explanation: The planet Saturn has beautiful rings, which consist mainly of dust and ice to encircle it. These rings are not visible with the naked eye.

questions.question: Heavenly bodies can be seen with the help of
questions.explanation: Heavenly bodies can be seen with the help of telescope. Telescope is an optical device that forms enlarge sized image of far away objects.

tests.instructionHtml:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Another star close to earth after the sun is
questions.explanation: The Sun's nearest neighbour, Alpha Centauri, is actually a triple-star system —three stars bound together by gravity. Alpha Centauri A and B are two bright, closely orbiting stars with a distant, dim companion, Proxima Centauri. Our own Sun is the closest star to our planet.

questions.question: Maximum number of moon is present around which of the following planet .
questions.explanation: Planets are celestial bodies that revolve around the Sun in a  fixed orbit which has natural satellites(moon) revolving around them. Jupiter has maximum moons (63 moons) while Uranus has 27 moons, Mars has 2 moons (phobos,demios) and Venus has no moon.

questions.question: Sun is a
questions.explanation: A star is called a "sun" if it is the center of a planetary system. A large number of planets have been found that orbit other stars, thus making these stars officially "suns."

questions.question: When the brighter part of moon is getting bigger, the moon is
questions.explanation: The shape of moon keeps changing. When the brighter part of moon is getting bigger, the moon is called as Waxing and when the brighter part is getting smaller is called waning.

questions.question: Which constellation is known as Saptarishi?
questions.explanation: Ursa Major constellation is known as Saptarishi. It consists of seven stars that rotate around the pole star during night.

questions.question: The full moon appears when the earth is

options.html: on same side of Moon and Sun
Between the Sun and the Moon

questions.explanation: The full moon appears when the earth is between the Sun and Moon. During full moon complete spherical moon is visible from earth.

questions.question: The scientist who discovered that the day and night are caused by the rotation of the earth was
questions.explanation: Arya Bhata was the Indian scientist who discovered that the day and night are caused by the rotation of the earth around the Sun on its own axis.

questions.question: Which one is the natural satellite of earth?
questions.explanation: The Moon is Earth's only natural satellite. It is one of the largest natural satellites in the Solar System.

questions.question: The piece of rock that fall from the sky and burn due to friction, emitting light are called
questions.explanation: The piece of rock that fall from the sky and burn due to friction of atmospheric air to emit light streak in the sky is called meteors.

questions.question: How long does it take the light from the sun to reach earth?
questions.explanation: Light travels with the speed of 3, 00,000 km/second. It takes the light form the Sun to reach earth about 8 minutes and 20 seconds.

questions.question: The educational satellite launched by India is
questions.explanation: India has launched many satellites for different purpose like weather forecast, communication, navigation etc..EDUSAT is an educational satellite launched by India.

questions.question: In how many days does Mercury completes one revolution around the sun?
questions.explanation: Mercury takes about 88 days to complete one revolution around the Sun. It has shortest orbit among all the planets.

questions.question: Planets appear bright because they
questions.explanation: Planets do not have their own light. Planets appear bright because they reflect the light of Sun.

questions.question: Revolution of Earth around the Sun causes
questions.explanation: The revolution of earth around the Sun causes seasonal change. Seasonal changes are due to difference in distance of Sun from the earth.

questions.question: Most of the planet rotates from
questions.explanation: Most of the planets rotates from west to east around the Sun but only Mars rotates from east to west.
