  • Students can make changes in their choice of options before clicking the "SUBMIT" button.
  • After completing all the questions, click "SUBMIT" button. A message will be displayed on the screen "Do you really want to submit ?". If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: The Pole Star forms a part of _____________.
questions.explanation: The Little Bear is visible throughout the year in North India. It constitutes seven stars and the tail star is the pole star which is the only star whose position appears fixed and has been used for centuries to locate the North Pole.

questions.question: Meteors are also known as
questions.explanation: Meteors are also known as shooting stars because due to friction of atmosphere meteors start burning and form a streak of light in the sky like a shooting star.

questions.question: Asteroids are present between the orbits of the following planets.
questions.explanation: Large number of asteroids are present between the vast orbit space of planets, Mars, and Jupiter. These asteroids are pieces of rocky materials which are parts of a planet that broke up or that was never formed and smaller than planets but revolve around the Sun.

questions.question: EDUSAT and INSAT were satellites launched by
questions.explanation: EDUSAT AND INSAT are artificial satellites launched by Indians for a specific purpose. EDUSAT is an educational satellite while INSAT is a weather forecasting satellite.

questions.question: Artificial satellite is mainly used for
questions.explanation: Artificial satellites are manmade satellites mainly used for communication and weather forecasting purpose. Satellites revolves around the Earth,Moon or other objects in the Solar System in specific orbits which gives more informations about the Earth and the universe.

questions.question: The only planet that have life is
questions.explanation: Earth is the only planet that has life. Life is possible due to the presence of liquid water (70%of earth's surface), right temperature(570C to  -890C), and an atmosphere containing nitrogen, oxygen, and a small amount of other gases like carbon dioxide and argon.

questions.question: Venus planet can be visible

options.html: Just before sunrise and just after sunset

questions.explanation: Venus planet is the brightest object visible low in the sky other than Sun and Moon in the morning and evening at different times of the year. As it's visible just before sunrise and just after sunset, it is also known as the 'Morning Star ' or 'Evening Star'.

questions.question: The size of moon gradually changes, the half-moon is called
questions.explanation: The word "gibbous' refers to phases where the moon is more than half illuminated. So half moon is known as Gibbous moon.

questions.question: Which of the following is the largest entity related to space?
questions.explanation: Universe is the largest entity as it is the vast expanse of space that contains all the galaxies, gas, dust, and all other forms of matter known to us. The Universe keeps expanding as galaxies are moving away from each other.

questions.question: The cluster of billions of stars is called
questions.explanation: Cluster of billions of stars is called a galaxy. Our solar system belongs to Milky Way galaxy. There are 100 billion galaxies in the universe and are of different shapes-spiral, elliptical, ring-shaped etc.

questions.question: Lunar eclipse occurs on a
questions.explanation: Lunar eclipse occurs on full moon night. When Earth comes between Sun and Moon (full moon ), the shadow of Earth falls on Moon and it appears dark so cannot be seen from Earth.

questions.question: When the moon is not visible at night in a clear sky, the moon is called
questions.explanation: When the moon is not visible at night in clear sky, the moon is called new moon because the Moon is between the Earth and the Sun so its fully dark and cannot be seen. The size of moon gradually increases after new moon.

questions.question: One light year is equal to
questions.explanation:
The distance travelled by light in one year is called one light year. one light year is 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108 m/s {tex}\times{/tex} 365 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 = 9.46 {tex}\times{/tex} 1015 m.
Here, (3 {tex}\times{/tex} 108) m/s is the speed of light and (365 {tex}\times{/tex} 24 {tex}\times{/tex} 60 {tex}\times{/tex} 60) are the time in seconds in one year

questions.question: Earth is rotating from
questions.explanation: Earth rotates around the sun from west to east on its own axis. The rotation of earth causes day and night.We see the sun rise in the East in the early hours; as we rotate East we see the sun climb overhead; finally we see the sun sink to the West as we continue moving East.

questions.question: Where is the asteroid belt located?
questions.explanation: The asteroid belt is a region of space between the orbits of Mars and Jupiter where most of the asteroids in our Solar System are found orbiting the Sun. That places it between 2.2 and 3.2 astronomical units (AU) from the Sun. The belt is about 1 AU thick.The asteroid belt probably contains millions of asteroids.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Moon have no atmosphere because there is
questions.explanation: Moon is very small compared to earth and has no atmosphere because there is no water and air on it. Days are very hot and nights are very cold. Gravitational force of moon is also very less.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a constellation?
questions.explanation: Leo, Libra, Cancer are constellations. Constellations are group of stars arranged in a particular shape. Venus is a planet that revolves around the sun.

questions.question: The approximate distance between the Sun and  the Earth is
questions.explanation: Speed of light is 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 105km/s and sun’s light reaches the earth in about 8 minute 20 seconds (500 seconds). Distance = speed {tex}\times{/tex} time = 3,00,000 {tex}\times{/tex} 500 = 150,000,000 km

questions.question: Earth and other planets revolve around the Sun due to
questions.explanation: Earth and other planets revolve around the sun due to the gravitational force of Sun. Every object in the universe attracts every other object, the force of attraction is called gravitational force. They revolve around the Sun in an anti-clockwise direction along its orbits.

questions.question: Which of the following natural phenomenon was discovered by Arya Bhatta?

options.html: Day and night occurs due to rotation of earth
Day is longer than night in winter

questions.explanation: Arya Bhatta discovered that day and night occurs due to rotation of earth on its own axis. The part of earth in direct line of sun has day and other part have night.

questions.question: The star nearest to the Solar System is ________ light years away.
questions.explanation: Proxima Centauri is the star closest to the solar system ,is more than 40,000,000 million km from the Sun.Light from it takes 4.2 years to reach us ; Proxima Centauri is therefore 4.2 light years away from the Solar System.

questions.question: The star which appears stationary to us and situated in the north direction is called
questions.explanation: Pole star is the only star that appears stationary to us and is situated in the north direction in the sky. Other stars appear to rotate around the pole star.It is the tail of the constellation -Ursa Minor ,which has been used for centuries to locate the North Pole.

questions.question: The scientists who practice astronomy are called
questions.explanation: The scientists who practice astronomy( study of celestial bodies and the space that contains them) are called Astronomers. Claudius Ptolemy (85-165 A.D., Greek) was an astronomer who used Hipparchus' extensive observations to develop a model that predicted the movements of the Sun, Moon, planets, and stars. Aristotle (384-322 B.C., Greek), the great philosopher, proved that the Earth is spherical, and believed that it was at the center of the universe.

questions.question: From the Earth, the Moon appears to complete one revolution in
questions.explanation: The moon completes one revolution around the earth in 29 1/3 days. But as the moon revolves around the earth, the earth moves ahead in its own orbit around the sun. So from the earth, the moon appears to complete one revolution (between one New Moon to the next New Moon) in 29.5 days. The Lunar calendars used by astrologers follow this idea.

questions.question: Satellite are heavenly bodies which
questions.explanation: Satellites are heavenly bodies which revolve around the planets. The natural satellite of planets are called moon. Earth has one moon and Jupiter has the maximum number of satellites  (63 moons). Artificial satellites are sent by humans to space to learn more about Earth and the universe.

questions.question: Which of the following is not correct?

options.html: Planets rotates in its own axis.

questions.explanation: Planets do not have their own light but they reflect the light of Sun.

questions.question: Venus is brighter in appearance due to
questions.explanation: Planet Venus is brighter in appearance due to its thick cloudy atmosphere. The reflection of light occurs more than other planets due to this cloudy atmosphere of different gases.

questions.question: The first planet to be discovered with the help of telescope was
questions.explanation: Uranus is the seventh planet from the Sun. It was discovered by William Herschel in 1781 using a telescope. It has the third-largest planetary radius and fourth-largest planetary mass in the Solar System.

questions.question: Which property of the pole star makes it useful for sailors?

options.html: It appears to be in a  fixed position.
It is visible during day and night.
It appear to be the closest star to Sun.

questions.explanation: Pole star appears to be a fixed position and is seen as a faint star located directly above the North Pole. Thus pole star is useful for sailors to know the direction.

questions.question: Which of the following is a larger unit of distance?
questions.explanation: Light year is larger unit of distance. One light year is equal to distance travelled by light in one year which is almost 9,460,000  million km.

questions.question: The Sun is seen at different positions in the sky at different times of the day because of ____________.

options.html: The rotation of the Earth on its own axis.
The revolution of the Earth around the axis.
The movement of the Moon around the Earth.
The movement of the Sun in the space.

questions.explanation: The Sun, the Moon, the planets, and the stars all rise in the east and set in the west. But it appears to rise and set because of the Earth's rotation on its axis. As the Earth rotates toward the east, it looks like the sun is moving west.

questions.question: The first Indian satellite to the Moon.
questions.explanation: India's first satellite was launched in 1975 and named  Aryabhata, the prominent Indian astronomer and mathematician of the 5th century bearing the same name. The 360-kilogram satellite built by ISRO was launched by Russia from their Kapustin Yar rocket launch and development site using a Kosmos-3M launch vehicle.

questions.question: Indian astrology is based on the relative position of the
questions.explanation: Indian astrology is based on the relative position of planets in the sky at a particular point of time. In Astrology, there are nine planets or Grahas. These include Mercury, Venus, Mars, Jupiter, Sun, Moon, Saturn Rahu, and Ketu, which are astronomical points formed where the moon's orbit intersects the apparent path of the Sun around the earth.

questions.question: Which planet is called blue planet?
questions.explanation: Earth is called blue planet because earth appear blue from sky as 70%  Earth's surface is covered with liquid water.

questions.question: Rotation of earth on its own axis causes
questions.explanation: We have day and night because the Earth rotates. It spins on its own axis, which is an imaginary line passing through the North and South Poles. The Earth completes one rotation in 24 hours. The Earth's side facing Sun will have day and other having night.

questions.question: Earth appear blue from the sky due to presence of
questions.explanation: Earth appear blue from the sky due to presence of water on earth. About 2/3rd part of earth is surrounded by water which gives blue color.

questions.question: The atmosphere of Uranus contains gases like
questions.explanation: Uranus has hydrogen and helium in the upper layer like the other gas giants. It also has an icy mantle that surrounds its rock and iron core. The upper atmosphere of water, ammonia and methane ice crystals gives Uranus, its distinctive pale blue color.

questions.question: The Sun is a
questions.explanation: The Sun is a star that emits heat and light. Sun appear larger than other stars due to its closeness from earth.

questions.question: Ozone layer around the earth protects it from
questions.explanation: Ozone layer around the earth in the atmosphere prevents us from harmful UV rays. Ozone prevents the harmful ultraviolet rays from entering the atmosphere of earth.

questions.question: Shooting star is actually
questions.explanation: Meteorite is also called shooting star because due to friction of air in atmosphere meteorite starts burning that appear as streak of light in the sky.

questions.question: The number of days between full moon to new moon is
questions.explanation: There is an interval of 15 days between full moon and new moon. After full moon, the size of moon start decreasing and after 15 days on new moon, moon is not visible. A complete cycle from one full moon to the next one is 29.5 days and referred as lunar cycle.

questions.question: Phases of moon occurs due to
questions.explanation: Phases of moon occurs due to shadow of Earth on Moon. As Moon revolves around the Earth and Earth revolves around the Sun, the shape of the Moon appears to change when viewed from the Earth. Different phases of Moon are Full Moon, New Moon, Gibbous Moon, and Crescent Moon.

questions.question: Orbit is the

options.html: Path along which a celestial body revolves

questions.explanation: Orbit is the path along which a celestial body revolves around the sun. Different planets revolve around the Sun in a fixed orbit.

questions.question: The only source of light and heat for all the planets is
questions.explanation: The Sun is a ball of gases (mostly Helium and Hydrogen) that contains 99.8% of the mass of the Solar system. Due to this, massive pressure leading to a nuclear reaction (fusing of helium and hydrogen atoms) which results in the production of intensive heat and light, which is the source of energy for the whole solar system. The temperature at the core of the Sun is around 15,000,0000C and the surface temperature is around 55000C.

questions.question: Venus is the brightest and hottest planets due to
questions.explanation: The presence of carbon dioxide (97%), nitrogen, and clouds of sulphuric acid in Venus's atmosphere trap heat. Due to the greenhouse effect, the temperature on the surface of Venus is around 4600C, making it the hottest planet in the solar system. The thick cover clouds reflect light making its brightest planet too.

questions.question: Which of the following gas is called greenhouse gas?
questions.explanation: A greenhouse gas (sometimes abbreviated GHG) is a gas in an atmosphere that absorbs and emits radiation within the thermal infrared range. The primary greenhouse gases in Earth's atmosphere are water vapor, carbon dioxide, methane, nitrous oxide, and ozone. Carbon dioxide (CO2) and other greenhouse gases act like a blanket, absorbing IR radiation and preventing it from escaping into outer space. The net effect is the gradual heating of the Earth's atmosphere and surface, a process known as global warming.

questions.question: Gravitational force of the heavenly body like Earth and Moon depends upon
questions.explanation: Gravitational force of the heavenly bodies like Earth and Moon depends upon the mass and radius of the object. The pull of gravitational force is directed towards the centre of the Earth. The size of gravitational force depends on the mass of the object being pulled by the Earth. The Moon is a smaller object than the Earth and of lower mass. As a consequence, its gravitational field only pulls with a force of about a fifth of that of the Earth.

questions.question: Which planet shows the slowest rotation around its axis?
questions.explanation: Venus has the slowest rotation around its axis, over 243 Earth days for one rotation. It rotates from east to west unlike other planets.

questions.question: Telescope is a device used to study about ____________.
questions.explanation: Heavenly bodies can be seen with the help of telescope. Telescope is an optical device that forms enlarge sized image of far situated objects. Uranus was the first planet discovered using telescope.

questions.question: NASA stands for
questions.explanation: NASA stands for National aeronautics and space administration. It is an independent agency of the USA government responsible for the civilian space program, as well as aeronautics and aerospace research.

questions.question: Which of the following planets takes the longest time to complete one revolution?
questions.explanation: Neptune takes longest time to complete one revolution as it has longest orbit. Neptune is farthest planet from the sun(4498 million km away)and it takes 165 years to complete one revolution.

questions.question: In the orbit between Jupiter and Mars, a large number of rock and metal pieces are found. They are called
questions.explanation: Asteroids revolve around the Sun and are present between orbit of Jupiter and Mars as large number of rock and metal pieces.This region where most asteroids are found are called the Asteroid belt which is about 550 million km.

questions.question: The pieces of rock which revolve around the Sun, sometimes enter the Earth's atmosphere and start burning due to
questions.explanation: Pieces of rock revolving around the sun that enter the earth’s atmosphere and start burning due to friction from air of atmosphere and appear as streak of light in the sky.

questions.question: The streak of light in the night sky are commonly called
questions.explanation: Shooting star is a common name for the visible path of a meteoroid as it enters the atmosphere, usually burns up due to friction with the atmosphere becoming a meteor.

questions.question: INSAT 3B or Kalpana is
questions.explanation: Artificial satellites are man made satellites revolving around the earth. INSAT-3B is an Indian communications satellite which was built and is operated by the Indian Space Research Organisation. INSAT-3A, a multipurpose satellite was launched by Ariane in April 2003 and it provides communication, weather, and search and rescue services.

questions.question: In which constellation, pole star is present ?
questions.explanation: Pole star is at the end of tail in Ursa minor constellation. Other stars of this constellation appear to revolve but pole star which remain fixed. It is located directly above the North Pole so used for centuries to locate North Pole.

questions.question: Which one is called the red planet?
questions.explanation: Mars planet is called red planet due to the presence of iron oxide (rust) in its soil which gives a reddish appearance. It is situated between Earth and Jupiter.

questions.question: The study of the objects in the sky is known as
questions.explanation: The study of celestial bodies and the space that contains them is known as Astronomy and the scientist who studies astronomy are called astronomers.

questions.question: Which of the following planet have the greatest radius?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest and fifth planet of solar system. So, Jupiter planets have greatest radius of about 69,911 km. It has the most number of moons (63 moons) too.

questions.question: An astronaut standing on the surface of the moon throws a ball upwards. The ball would

options.html: Directly fall down from the point it is released.
Keep going up never to come back.
Go up and then come back to the surface of the moon.

questions.explanation: The ball will go up and then come down because at moon,acceleration due to gravity is less than of Earth.

questions.question: Addition of harmful substances in water to make it unfit for human consumption is called?
questions.explanation: Whenever harmful substances such as sewage, toxic chemicals, silt, etc., get mixed with water, the water becomes polluted and unfit for human consumption. This is known as water pollution.

questions.question: The substance that pollutes the natural substance is called
questions.explanation: The substances that pollute natural substances like air, water and soil are called pollutants. Exhaust and sewage from industries, vehicular emission, non-biodegradable materials, silt, etc. are some common pollutants.

questions.question: Water pollution is caused by

options.html: Excess drawing of water from water bodies
Addition of untreated sewage in water bodies

questions.explanation: Water pollution is caused by addition of untreated domestic sewage and industrial wastage in water bodies. They make water unfit for human consumption.

questions.question: Which of the following area is likely to be most air polluted?
questions.explanation: Residential area, park, and crop fields are very less polluted areas while industrial areas have a large number of factories that emit harmful gases to cause air pollution.

questions.question: Which one is not a natural source of water
questions.explanation: Ponds, rivers, and lakes are a natural source of water as they are present naturally on earth. On the other hand, Canals are a man-made sources of water, especially for irrigation purposes.

questions.question: Which of the following is not responsible for air pollution?
questions.explanation: Air pollution is caused by combustion of fossil fuels, waste(gases) from factories and burning of coal. Freezing of ice is not responsible for air pollution.

questions.question: Which is not produced by burning of fossils fuel?
questions.explanation: Burning of fossil fuels produce carbon dioxide gas, Sulphur dioxide gas, and Nitrogen oxide gas. Oxygen gas is not produced by burning of fossil fuels.

questions.question: The purest form of water is
questions.explanation: Rain water is purest form of water as during cloud formation, evaporation of water takes place in which impurities are left in water bodies. Spring water, subsoil water, and hand pump water contains some salt dissolved in it.

questions.question: Which one is not an organic waste?
questions.explanation: Vegetable peels, cotton cloths, and paper are organic wastes as all of them are obtained from plants. DDT is an insecticide, used to kill insects and pests, and is prepared in laboratory.

questions.question: Which one is a common source of drinking water?
questions.explanation: River and lake are common sources of drinking water. Sea and ocean water are salty and cannot be used for drinking purpose.

questions.question: The process of waste treatment is called
questions.explanation: The process of waste treatment is called sewage treatment. During this process impurities are removed by filtration, sedimentation, and chlorination, thus converting the sewage to drinkable water.

questions.question: Bacteria that decompose organic wastes are called
questions.explanation: Bacteria that decompose organic wastes are called anaerobic bacteria. Oxygen is not required by these bacteria for respiration.

questions.question: Percentage of nitrogen gas in air is
questions.explanation: Nitrogen gas is present in atmosphere in maximum amount that is about 78% followed by oxygen and carbon dioxide.

questions.question: After the treatment, the treated water is released into
questions.explanation: Sewage treatment is done to remove different kinds of wastes present in water. After the treatment, the treated water is released into water bodies.

questions.question: Which gas is produced on burning of fossil fuels
questions.explanation: On burning of fossil fuels carbon dioxide gas is mainly produced. Some amount of sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxide is also present in it.

questions.question: A number of people are suffering from respiratory diseases due to
questions.explanation: Polluted air contains a number of harmful gases as well as particulate matter floating in the air. These pollutants irritate the respiratory tract and cause diseases.

questions.question: The Taj Mahal is being affected by
questions.explanation: Air pollution

questions.question: Most polluted river in the world is
questions.explanation: Ganga

questions.question: Addition of harmful substance in water leads to
questions.explanation: Addition of harmful substance in water leads to water pollution. Harmful substances may be organic wastes, biological substances, and inorganic wastes. Untreated waste from industries is a common water pollutant.

questions.question: Pollution of water is responsible for
questions.explanation: All of these

questions.question: People living near sea or ocean also face lacks of water because

options.html: Sea water is banned by government

questions.explanation: People living near sea or ocean also feel scarcity of water because sea water is salty and cannot be used for drinking purposes, washing clothes or any other household chores.

questions.question: There is acute shortage of water due to

options.html: Lack of technique to purify water

questions.explanation: The acute shortage of water is due to growing population and overuse of water. The sources of water remain limited but population has grown many folds.

questions.question: Sea water is unfit for drinking because it contain
questions.explanation: Sea water is unfit for drinking because it contain large amount of dissolved salts. Sea water cannot be used for even washing the clothes as foam is not formed in such water.

questions.question: Air is a mixture of various gases. One of the gases is 21% part of the air and is essential for the survival of human beings. This gas is:
questions.explanation: Oxygen makes up about 21% of air by volume. It is essential for the survival of human beings.

questions.question: Last stage in treatment of sewage is
questions.explanation: Sewage treatment involves primary, secondary, and tertiary treatment during which different kinds of impurities are removed. The last stage in treatment is chlorination in which microbes are killed by chlorine.

questions.question: Presence of soluble salts in sea water makes it
questions.explanation: Presence of soluble salts in sea water makes it Saline and thus unfit for human consumption( can cause edema ). It can not be used for washing clothes and for bathing purposes. Seawater is denser than fresh water and distilled water both.

questions.question: Water is the most common _______.
questions.explanation: Water is the most common solvent as it dissolves almost all kinds of solute. Water is also called a universal solvent.

questions.question: Algal bloom is caused due to

options.html: Presence of inorganic matter in water
Absence of carbon dioxide in water
Presence of organic matter in water

questions.explanation: Due to presence of large amount of organic wastes in water, large number of algae grows in it called algal bloom to deplete the dissolved oxygen in water.

questions.question: Gases and fine dust particle together make the
questions.explanation: Smoke is the combination of fine dust particles along with gases like carbon dioxide in air.

questions.question: Most of the rivers in India are polluted due to
questions.explanation: In India, main reason for pollution of rivers is addition of untreated sewage directly into water bodies that contain a number of pollutants.

tests.name: Pollution of Air and Water Test 03
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Pollution of Air and Water CBSE Online Test 03 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.
  • There will be total 15 MCQ in this test.
  • The test will consist of only objective type multiple choice questions requiring students to mouse-click their correct choice of the options against the related question number.
  • For every question there will be only one correct option out of the four given options.
  • Each question carries one mark. There is no negative marking for incorrect choice.
  • For moving to the next question, click on "Next" at the bottom of the screen.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Open drain cause ____________ condition.
questions.explanation: Open drain cause unhygienic condition as it attracts a number of harmful insects and microbes that may leads to diseases.

questions.question: Which of the following substance is present in least amount in air
questions.explanation: Nitrogen gas is present in maximum percentage in air followed by oxygen and Carbon dioxide. Helium gas is present in very trace amount.

questions.question: The components of air which are harmful to living beings are
questions.explanation: Air contains gases like nitrogen, carbon dioxide, oxygen along with other gases. Dust particles and water vapour are also present in the air. Dust and smoke present in the air are harmful to living organisms and can cause respiratory diseases.

questions.question: Jaundice affects which organ?
questions.explanation: Jaundice is caused by the build-up of a substance called bilirubin in the blood and tissues of the body. Any condition that disrupts the movement of bilirubin from the blood to the liver and out of the body can cause jaundice.

questions.question: Which of the following do not act as air pollutant?
questions.explanation: Forest fire, volcanic eruption and burning of fossil fuels acts as pollutant as carbon dioxide and other gases are released in these processes. Electrical heater do not act as air pollutant as it does not release any harmful gas.

questions.question: Which of the following is present in polluted air
questions.explanation: Polluted air contains poisonous gases like carbon monoxide and carbon dioxide etc. Suspended dust particles are also present in polluted air.

questions.question: Solid parts of sewage can be used as
questions.explanation: Solid parts of sewage can be used as manure as they contain large amount of organic matters. Solid wastes can be used in biogas plant to obtain energy.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a pollutant of air?
questions.explanation: Sulpher dioxide, carbon dioxide, and nitrogen oxide are pollutant of air as they may cause acid rain. Hydrogen oxide is not a pollutant.

questions.question: Air is a mixture of
questions.explanation: Pure air is a mixture of several gases that are invisible and odourless. It consists of about 78% nitrogen, 21% oxygen, and less than 1% of argon, carbon dioxide, and other gases — as well as varying amounts of water vapour.

questions.question: Disease causing micro-organisms are called
questions.explanation: Disease causing micro-organisms are called pathogens. These pathogens are present in polluted air, water, and food particles.

questions.question: We feel suffocated in a room having burning substance due to
questions.explanation: Incomplete combustion of substances produces carbon monoxide gas that is responsible for suffocation in a room having burning substance. Oxygen is consumed in combustion.

questions.question: Drainage system failure in a city may cause
questions.explanation: Drainage system is set up to remove sewage from cities to nearby water bodies. Failure of drainage system in cities may cause water logging on street.

questions.question: Which of the following is a green house gas?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide is a green house gas as it forms a blanket around the earth that prevents the heat escaping out of atmosphere to cause increase in temperature of earth.

questions.question: Boojho wishes to contribute in reducing air pollution. Which vehicle should he use for going to school?
questions.explanation: Boojho, if wishes to contribute in reducing air pollution, should use school bus for going to school. A school bus carry many children together, thus vehicular emissions are reduced.

questions.question: Sewer system is useful in
questions.explanation: Sewer system is setup in cities to remove domestic sewage water. Sewer system is useful in all seasons as sewage is produced in all seasons.

tests.name: Pollution of Air and Water Test 04
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Pollution of Air and Water CBSE Online Test 04 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

  • Students must not use calculators and any other unfair means while taking the test.
  • The duration of the test is 30 minutes. You will not be able to submit the test after the time is over.

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which component of air is used for fire extinguishers?
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide extinguishes fire by displacing oxygen, or taking away the oxygen element of the fire triangle. The carbon dioxide is also very cold as it comes out of the extinguisher, so it cools the fuel as well.

questions.question: Which is not produced by burning of fossils fuels?
questions.explanation: Burning fossil fuel produces carbon dioxide gas, Sulphur dioxide gas, and Nitrogen oxide gas. Oxygen gas is not produced by the burning of fossil fuel.

questions.question: Which of these diseases may happen due to drinking of contaminated water?
questions.explanation: Cholera is caused by a Bacteria that is transferred to a healthy person through contaminated water. Malaria is transmitted by mosquito. Smallpox occurs due to direct contact.

questions.question: In cities, water supply is through
questions.explanation: In cities, water supply is through a network of pipes. Portable water is generated by water work that is supplied to the residential areas through a network of underground pipes network.

questions.question: Global warming is caused due to increased level of
questions.explanation: Global warming occurs due to an increased level of greenhouse gases. Main greenhouse gas includes carbon dioxide and methane.

questions.question: Soap do not form foam with
questions.explanation: Soap does not form foam with hard water because hard water contains calcium and magnesium salt that combine with soap ions to form scum.

questions.question: Which one is the most common cause of contamination of water in rural areas?

options.html: Addition of manure in field near water bodies
Defection in or around water bodies
Soil present around the water bodies

questions.explanation: Defection in or around water bodies is the most common cause of contamination of water in rural areas, as toilets are not available in a rural areas to all households.

questions.question: Which of the following disease is caused by ultraviolet rays?
questions.explanation: Ultraviolet rays cause skin cancer in human body and animals. UV rays enters the earth's atmosphere due to depletion of ozone layer.

questions.question: River water is unfit for drinking because it has
questions.explanation: River water without water work is unfit for drinking because it has suspended impurities and pollutants that may cause disease in human beings.

questions.question: Finer hair inside the nose
questions.explanation: Fine hairs are present inside the nostril that filters the dust particles while inhaling the air. The mucus inside also prevent the entry of dust particles.

questions.question: Agricultural chemicals include
questions.explanation: Agricultural chemical includes fertilizers and pesticides. Fertilizers is used to increase the nutrients and pesticides to kills the pests.

questions.question: Water management is the process of

options.html: Judicious and careful use of water

questions.explanation: Water management includes judicious and careful use of available water. As soft water is available on earth is less than 1% of total water present on the earth surface.

questions.question: Pollution is affecting the
questions.explanation: pollution of air, water, and the soil is affecting the quality of our daily life. Polluted water causes water-borne disease while polluted air cause respiratory problems.

questions.question: Accumulation of harmful non-biodegradable chemicals in food chain is called
questions.explanation: Biomagnifications is the accumulation of harmful non-biodegradable chemicals in food chain at each trophic level. Accumulation of DDT starts from use of pesticide that enters the water bodies and water animals.

questions.question: Sewage treatment plants removes
questions.explanation: Sewage treatment plants removes solid, liquid, and chemical wastes present in water. It occurs as primary, secondary, and tertiary treatment to make water suitable for animals.

General Instructions:

questions.question: Chocking of sewage is caused due to
questions.explanation: Chocking of sewage is caused by plastics and other non-biodegradable items that enter the sewage system. Chocking leads to overflowing of sewage on roads.

questions.question: Water treatment is the process of removing harmful substance from
questions.explanation: Water treatment is the process of removing harmful substances from sewage which includes solid, liquid, and chemical wastes.

questions.question: Which of the following is not a way to conserve water?
questions.explanation: Replace is not a way to conserve water. At our individual level, we should consciously save water and not to waste it. Reduce, reuse and recycle should be our mantra.

questions.question: Water-wise habits includes

options.html: Bath with one bucket of water
Wash the flour one time in a day

questions.explanation: Water-wise habits include measures to save portable water from its misuse. Turning off tap while brushing and repairing of leaky taps are water-wise habits.

questions.question: Biological Oxygen Demand is _____________ in polluted water.
questions.explanation: Biological oxygen demand is the absence of dissolved oxygen in polluted water essential for survival of aquatic organisms. BOD is more in polluted water.

questions.question: Algal bloom causes
questions.explanation: Growth of large number of alga in water bodies due to presence of excess of nutrients causes low dissolved oxygen level in water bodies. It becomes difficult to respire in such water.

questions.question: Incomplete combustion of fuel such as petrol and diesel gives?
questions.explanation: Carbon monoxide is produced by the incomplete combustion of fuels like wood, coal, kerosene, petrol and diesel.

questions.question: The type of pollution which is likely to affect Taj Mahal in Agra to a greater extent is:
questions.explanation: Air pollution is responsible for the yellowing of Taj Mahal in Agra. Polluted air contains oxides of sulphur and nitrogen that mix with water during rain to cause acid rain that acts on marbles to make it yellow.

questions.question: Anaerobic bacteria is used for
questions.explanation: Fermentation bacteria are anaerobic, but use organic molecules as their final electron acceptor to produce fermentation end-products. Streptococcus, Lactobacillus, and Bacillus are some of the examples of anaerobic bacteria.

questions.question: Water from bathroom, kitchen and toilet are called
questions.explanation: Water from bathroom, kitchen and toilet are called waste water. These water together with other household wastage forms the sewage.

questions.question: Inorganic substance present in sewage includes
questions.explanation: Sewage includes organic as well as inorganic substances. The main inorganic substance present in sewage includes nitrate and phosphates.

questions.question: Network of big and small pipes called sewers to join together to form
questions.explanation: In cities, there is a network of big and small pipes connected together to remove sewage from domestic and industrial area and is called as sewerage.

questions.question: Which gas is responsible for acid rain?
questions.explanation: Fossil fuel contains small amount of sulphur that combine with oxygen during combustion to form oxide of sulphur (SO2). SO2 reacts with rain water to form Sulphuric acid to cause acid rain.

questions.question: Chemicals used for disinfecting water are
questions.explanation: Disinfection is the process of killing harmful microbes present in water. Chlorine and Ozone is used as disinfect in drinking water.

questions.question: Waste water should be used after
questions.explanation: Waste water should be used after removing pollutants present in waste water. Sewage treatment is used to remove harmful pollutants involving filtration, sedimentation and chlorination.

General Instructions:

If student click on "NO", the will be redirected to the test page again and if they press "YES", the result will be displayed on the screen with details of questions attempted.

questions.question: Which of the following gas is most harmful to us?
questions.explanation: Out of helium, nitrogen, carbon monoxide, and carbon dioxide, carbon monoxide is most harmful to us as it combines with hemoglobin to reduce oxygen carrying capacity.

questions.question: ___________ Plants are planted along the sewage treatment plant.
questions.explanation: Eucalyptus plants are generally planted along the sewage treatment plant because it grows very fast(also in contaminated water) and cover large area. They absorbs waste water and releases pure water vapour.

questions.question: Aeration of water during water treatment removes the
questions.explanation: Aeration is the addition of oxygen to water. Aeration of water during water treatment removes the anaerobic bacteria that grow only in absence of oxygen.

questions.question: The process during waste water treatment, in which germs are killed is
questions.explanation: During tertiary treatment chlorination is done that kills the germs present in water to make it fit for human consumption.

questions.question: In a network of sewer system, Manhole are provided at

options.html: Junction of two or more sewers

questions.explanation: In Sewer network system, manhole is provided at junction of two or more sewers. Manhole is a deep, generally cylindrical structure.

questions.question: WWTP stands for
questions.explanation: WWTP stands for Waste water treatment plant. It is the physical infrastructure used for treating waste water. The treated water can be used for consumption, household chores, and recharging water table.

questions.question: Treatment of waste water do not involves
questions.explanation: Treatment of waste water involves physical treatment, chemical treatment and biological treatment. Hormonal treatment is not a part of sewage treatment.

questions.question: Absence of safe drinking water may lead to
questions.explanation: Waterborne diseases are caused by pathogen present in water. Absence of safe drinking water may lead to water born disease as people start using unsafe water.

questions.question: The water that is purified and fit for drinking is called
questions.explanation: The water that is purified and fit for drinking is called as portable water. Portable water contains small amount of useful salts.

questions.question: Water in a river is cleaned
questions.explanation: In natural water bodies, there is a biological system of water cleaning. But addition of large amount of pollutants in water makes this natural system fail to clean the water.

questions.question: River Yamuna is polluted near Delhi due to
questions.explanation: River Yamuna is polluted near Delhi due to addition of untreated sewage in large quantities. All the sewage enters the Yamuna and pollutes it.

questions.question: Water is chlorinated to
questions.explanation: During water works, water is chlorinated to kill the germs present in it to make it fit for human consumption.

questions.question: Water can be conserved by
questions.explanation: Rainwater harvesting is a technique of collection and storage of rainwater into natural reservoirs or tanks, or the infiltration of surface water into subsurface aquifers (before it is lost as surface runoff)

questions.question: Sea water is unfit for drinking because it contains
questions.explanation: Seawater is unfit for drinking because it contain a large amount of dissolved salts. Seawater cannot be used for even washing the clothes as the foam is not formed in such water.

questions.question: Which of the following is a respiratory disease caused by polluted air?
questions.explanation: Asthma is a respiratory disease caused by polluted air. Blood pressure, jaundice, and goiter is not caused by polluted air.

questions.question: In winter, smoke and fog together mix to form
questions.explanation: In winter, smoke and fog together mix to form smog that decrease the visibility and affect the air and road transportation.

questions.question: Gaseous pollutant like Sulpher dioxide and nitrogen oxide is mainly released from
questions.explanation: Small amounts of sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxide is released from petroleum refinery. These gases combine with water during rain to cause acid rain.

questions.question: Presence of SO2 and NO2 in air cause _____________ during raining.
questions.explanation: Presence of sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxide gas in air causes acid rain. Acid rain causes corrosion of monuments having marble.

questions.question: Which of the following gas is responsible for ozone layer depletion?
questions.explanation: Chlorofluorocarbon gas is responsible for ozone layer depletion. Ozone layer present in stratosphere combine with CFC,s to release free oxygen.

questions.question: Ozone layer protects us from harmful
questions.explanation: Ozone layer present in the stratosphere protects us from harmful ultraviolet rays. Ultraviolet rays may cause skin cancer and other diseases in human beings.

questions.question: Chlorofluorocarbon gas is used in
questions.explanation: Chlorofluorocarbon gas which is mainly responsible for depletion of ozone layer is used in refrigerator and air conditioners.

questions.question: Mathura oil refinery and leather industry around Agra are mainly responsible for
questions.explanation: Oil refinery release sulphur dioxide and nitrogen oxide gas which is carried by wind to other places. These gases combine with rain water to form Sulphuric acid and nitric acid and cause acid rain. This rain corrodes marble of Taj Mahal.

questions.question: Sulpher dioxide combine with water to form
questions.explanation: Sulphur dioxide combines with water to form Sulphurous acid.  SO2(g) + H2O(l) {tex}\to{/tex} H2SO3(aq)

questions.question: CNG is called clean fuel because

options.html: Combustion do not produce Carbon dioxide
Combustion of it produces less Carbon dioxide

questions.explanation: Compressed natural gas is called clean fuel because combustion of CNG produce less amount of CO2 without any solid residues. It can be used easily too.

questions.question: We cannot survive a few minutes without
questions.explanation: Oxygen is crucial to sustaining life. Though it varies from person to person, on average, 3 minutes is the maximum time where the person can stay alive without oxygen.

questions.question: Green house effect is responsible for
questions.explanation: Greenhouse effect is responsible for global warming. Greenhouse gases form a blanket in atmosphere that prevents the escape of heat to cause increase in temperature.

questions.question: In which process of plant carbon dioxide is converted into oxygen?
questions.explanation: During photosynthesis, plants convert carbon dioxide gas into oxygen along with the formation of glucose.

questions.question: The average temperature of earth is increasing slowly due to
questions.explanation: The average temperature of the earth is increasing slowly due to the greenhouse effect that leads to global warming.

questions.question: Which of the following is most polluted stretch of river Ganga?
questions.explanation: Due to the presence of large number of leather industries near Kanpur, large amount of wastes are released in river Ganga that make most polluted stretch of river Ganga.

questions.question: Kyoto protocol is about the

options.html: Reduction in emission of greenhouse gas
Increase in the emission of greenhouse gas

questions.explanation: Kyoto Protocol is an international treaty that extends the 1992 united nations framework convention on climate change to reduce the emission of greenhouse gases.

tests.name: Pollution of Air and Water Test 08
tests.instructionHtml:
This is Science Class 8 Pollution of Air and Water CBSE Online Test 08 . Students are instructed to read all the points carefully before clicking the START TEST button.

General Instructions:

questions.question: An ambitious plan to save the river Ganga, launched in 1985 was
questions.explanation: An ambitious plan to save the river Ganga, launched in 1985 is the Ganga Action Plan. This multicrore project although failed to clean the river Ganga.

questions.question: World largest eco-friendly, CNG bus service is
questions.explanation: World largest eco-friendly, CNG bus service is Delhi Transport Corporation. This service is started to reduce the air pollution in Delhi.

questions.question: WWF stands for
questions.explanation: The World Wide Fund for Nature is an international non-governmental organization founded on April 29, 1961, working in the field of the biodiversity conservation, and the reduction of humanity's footprint on the environment.

questions.question: Which of the following is not an alternative source of energy?
questions.explanation: Solar energy, wind energy, and hydropower are alternative sources of energy. Petrol is a conventional source of energy along with coal.

questions.question: The substance like sewage, toxic chemicals, and silt forms
questions.explanation: The substance like sewage, toxic chemicals, and silts form water pollutants. These pollutants are present in domestic and industrial sewage along with hospital wastes.

questions.question: Van Mahotsav is celebrated in month of
questions.explanation: Van Mahotsav is an annual tree-planting festival in India. This movement was initiated in the year 1950 by India's Union Minister for Agriculture, Kulapati Dr.K M Munshi. It is now a week-long festival, celebrated on different days in different parts of India, but usually between 1 July to 7 July.

questions.question: Adding some material to water that spoils its quality and change the smell and colour is called
questions.explanation: Addition of materials to water that spoils its quality and changes the smell and colour is called water pollution. The changes may be biological, chemical, or physical.

questions.question: Dry leaves should be ___________ to reduce air pollution.
questions.explanation: Mulching is one of the simplest and most beneficial practices you can use in the garden. Mulch is simply a protective layer of a material that is spread on top of the soil.

questions.question: Which of the following is common toxic chemical that get accumulated by biomagnifications?
questions.explanation: DDT is common toxic chemical that get accumulated by biomagnifications. DDT is non-biodegradable insecticide that accumulates at each trophic level.

questions.question: Which of the following substance is present in least amount in air?
questions.explanation: Nitrogen gas is present in maximum percentage in air followed by oxygen and Carbon dioxide. Helium gas is present in very trace amount.

questions.question: Amount of water recommended by United Nation per person per day is
questions.explanation: Amount of water recommended by United Nation per person per day is 40 litres. The recommendation includes water on the basis of everyday use like bathing, sanitation, consumption, cooking, etc.

questions.question: In water works, first step is
questions.explanation: In water works, first step or primary treatment is sedimentation in which solid wastes are settled at bottom.

questions.question: If a person consumes contaminated water, he may suffer from a disease called
questions.explanation: a medical condition with yellowing of the skin or whitening of the eyes, arising from excess of the pigment bilirubin and typically caused by obstruction of the bile duct, by liver disease, or by excessive breakdown of red blood cells.

questions.question: Traffic police use _________ to prevent themselves from inhaling polluted air.
questions.explanation: Traffic police use masks to prevent themselves from inhaling polluted air. On roads due to high traffic, large amounts of gaseous wastes are released.

questions.question: Water is the most common ________.
questions.explanation: Water is the most common solvent as it dissolves almost all kinds of solute. Water is also called as universal solvent.
